name: #Disclaimer
description:
The benefits and services provided by Northwestern University’s student groups are available to all students without regard to any category protected by the law. The University believes different perspectives, experiences and backgrounds are critical to a dynamic and
vibrant University community, and as such all students are welcome and encouraged to join each group. Any student
interested in exploring establishing a new affinity group should contact SOA.
summary: Read a statement about Northwestern University's student groups.

name: 01 - Intro to Wildcat Connection
description:
�� Welcome Wildcats!

If you’re ready to dive into the dynamic world of student involvement and explore campus opportunities, Wildcat Connection is your go-to hub for all things student-led.

 

Wildcat Connection is your gateway to a vibrant community of student-led initiatives, clubs, and activities designed to enhance your university experience. Whether you’re looking for new events to attend or looking to start a student organization on campus, this page is your guide to everything you need to know. 

Here's what you can do with Wildcat Connection:

  1. Find Organizations: Discover clubs matching your interests from academics to hobbies. Find their social media pages or contact them directly! 
  2. Check Events: Stay updated on upcoming socials, workshops, and more.
  3. Access Resources: Get tools for organization management and event planning.
  4. Start a New Org: Bring your ideas to life by starting a new organization.
  5. Submit Event Proposals: Host your own events hassle-free.
  6. Explore the Library: Get inspiration and guidance from our resource library and news updates 

To get you started, we’ve put together a list of all the important links. Ready to dive in? Check out the links below!

  • Organizations Directory - https://northwestern.campuslabs.com/engage/organizations
  • Events Calendar - https://northwestern.campuslabs.com/engage/events
  • Create a New Event - https://northwestern.hosted.panopto.com/Panopto/Pages/Embed.aspx?id=d9159963-d430-4d54-8d4d-a92e014af976
  • Create a New Organization-   https://northwestern.campuslabs.com/engage/actioncenter/branch/veracitymagazine/organizations/create
  • Event Submission- 
  • Resource Library- https://northwestern.campuslabs.com/engage/news

 

Let's make the most of your university journey together!

Best, SOA Team
summary: Student Organizations & Activities seeks to engage students in involvement opportunities that build a sense of belonging and community at Northwestern University.

name: 180 Degrees Consulting
description: Providing students with the opportunity to work on professional consulting projects while providing services to nonprofit organizations across the world helping millions of people
summary: Providing consulting services to nonprofit organizations across the world

name: 64 Squares
description: We are dedicated to promoting chess in the Northwestern community by providing a comfortable chess-playing environment. We also present students with the opportunity to play in Chicago’s only chess league and in intercollegiate matches.
summary: 64 Squares Chess Club is dedicated to promoting chess in the Northwestern community by providing a comfortable and fun chess-playing environment.

name: A&O Productions
description:
A&O Productions has provided Northwestern University undergraduates with top-notch large-scale programming since its inception in 1969. Interested in booking concerts or speakers, producing shows, screening films on a 35mm projector, promoting events across campus, working in the entertainment industry, or just want to be backstage? A&O is the group for you!

Advisor: K.Parker Hess
summary: A&O Productions, one of the largest student groups on campus, has provided Northwestern University undergraduates with top-notch large-scale entertainment programming since its inception in 1969.

name: Academy of Music and Arts for Special Education
description:
Academy of Music and Art for Special Education (AMASE) is a student organization that provides individuals with disabilities with music and art lessons. Every Saturday, Northwestern student volunteers meet with students from the local community on Evanston campus for individual music lessons and group arts and crafts activities. We work with children as young as 3 years of age to people who are in their early twenties. Through our partnership with other non-profit organizations in Evanston, such as Our Place and Transition House, we also organize weekly Community Music Choir on a weekday for other community members with or without disabilities to sing together. At the end of each quarter, we put on a concert for our students, their families, and volunteers to celebrate their hard work and talents. Through our Saturday Ensemble and Community Music Choir programs, we hope to provide our students, regardless of their cognitive abilities or age, a safe space to make music. AMASE volunteers are also dedicated to promote awareness and acceptance of neurodiversity to the greater campus community through various campaigns, fundraisers, and collaborations with other student organizations with similar interests.

For more information about volunteering, please visit our website or email us at amase@u.northwestern.edu.

To learn more about our students and get a taste of what we do, please follow us on social media--we keep active Facebook and Instagram accounts that documents our lovely students' weekly progress.
summary: Academy of Music and Art for Special Education (AMASE) teaches individuals with disabilities to engage with music and arts on Saturday mornings on campus.

name: ACEing Autism
description: ACEing Autism is a national organization whose goal is to help kids with autism grow and develop in a healthy way by promoting exercise and socialization through fun and affordable tennis practices. Volunteers will be matched with a participant and aid them throughout the duration of the one hour practice by guiding them through warm-ups,  movements with the racket, and playing games. By consistently spending time with a participant, significant friendships are formed and real improvements can be made in the lives of our participants. The sessions will be once a week for six weeks, twice a year - one in fall quarter and one in the spring.
summary: ACEing Autism aims to promote acceptance and inclusion between individuals with and without intellectual and developmental disorders by having volunteers teach kids with autism how to play tennis to help develop their motor and social skills.

name: Active Minds
description: Active Minds works to change the conversation about mental health. Through campus-wide events, we aim to remove the stigma that surrounds mental illness, and create a comfortable environment for an open conversation about mental health issues. NU Active Minds utilizes the student voice to reduce the stigma associated with mental illness through education, advocacy and awareness events.
summary: NUAM is the primary mental health awareness and advocacy group at Northwestern. We focus our efforts on informing students about mental wellness, creating a dialogue surrounding mental health and illness, and reducing the stigmas associated with it.

name: Advanced Degree Consulting Alliance
summary: ADCA introduces advanced degree students at Northwestern to alternative careers outside academia or their respective disciplines. We also provide tools and resources to help our members realize their professional career goals in consulting and beyond.

name: Advancement for the Undocumented Community
description: Advancement for the Undocumented Community (AUC) is a student organization dedicated to providing resources and support for undocumented student and students with undocumented family members. Through events, workshops, and organizing we hope to raise awareness, uplift, and advocate for this community. We hope to work with a number of local and national organizations to find resources and foster an inclusive environment.
summary: Advancement for the Undocumented Community (AUC) is a student organization dedicated to providing resources and support for undocumented student and students with undocumented family members.

name: African Graduate Student Association
description:
The African Graduate Student Association at Northwestern University is a student-led organization committed to supporting and empowering African graduate students on campus. Our mission is to provide a space for African graduate students to connect, share experiences, and support each other academically and professionally.
Through our events, programs, and initiatives, we aim to promote diversity and inclusion, foster global engagement, and enhance the academic and professional experiences of African graduate students at Northwestern University.
We organize events such as networking events with African professionals and alumni, professional development workshops and seminars, cultural events and celebrations, and advocacy and support for issues affecting African graduate students on campus.
Our association offers an opportunity for African graduate students to connect with others who share similar experiences, learn from one another, and create lifelong connections. Join us to connect with other African graduate students, share your experiences, and help shape the future of our community!
summary: AGSA is an organization committed to supporting and empowering African graduate students on campus. Our mission is to provide a space for African graduate students to connect, share experiences, and support each other academically and professionally.

name: African Students Association
description:
ASA is a student organization that provides a community for African students and promotes African awareness to the greater Northwestern community. Our main goal is to create and sustain community for those interested in engaging African culture. In the Fall, ASA hosts Afropollo, a mock of Showtime at the Apollo, where various students perform for a prize. Jabulani, the annual Cultural Show is hosted in the Winter which entertains all with a fashion show, skits, and performances. Throughout the year, we have community bonding events and hold discussions on topics related to Africa and also partner with various on-campus and off-campus organizations on relevant programming.

 

2024-2025 Executive Board:

Melissa Yankson Nyarko - President

Claribel Osei - Vice President

Jadon Ampadu - Treasurer

Olawale Olatunde - Treasurer

Riana Tadonki - Secretary

Freda Ganiyu - Events Coordinator

Imani Munene - Events Coordinator

Nzube Nwafo - Events Coordinator

Shalom Okeleke - Marketing Director

Timi Suleiman - Community Engagement Chair

Comfort Opafola - Ambassador

Murisi Zimbwa - Parliamentarian

Bezawit (Beza) Abate - Creative Director
summary: ASA is a student organization for African students and those interested in African culture. Our main goal is to create and sustain community for those interested in engaging African culture.

name: Afrothunda Dance Troupe
summary: African Dance Troupe at Northwestern University to promote African culture by showcasing traditional and modern African-themed dance moves and music.

name: Ahana Dance Project
description:
Ahana (अहना) in Sanskrit describes the first rays of sun that we see at dawn and represents the inner light present within all.

To us, Ahana means a new beginning. Grown from our passion for dance, our desire to create, and our vision to bring ideas and people together, we proudly present Ahana Dance Project.

We are a non-competitive South Asian fusion dance team that hopes to provide dancers with a space to learn, grow, and create. By fostering a community that celebrates its strengths, we will encourage dancers to share themselves through their ideas, choreography, and presence. In doing so, we hope to build a platform that supports individual growth and collaboration, and continues to spread our love for South Asian culture.

With lots of love and light,

Ahana Dance Project
summary: We are a non-competitive South Asian fusion dance team that hopes to provide dancers with a space to learn, grow, and create. By fostering a community that celebrates its strengths, we will encourage dancers to share their ideas and choreography.

name: AI Graduate Student Group
description: At Northwestern, artificial intelligence research extends far beyond the Computer Science Department to reach every corner of the university. The Northwestern AI Graduate Student Group was founded to serve this diverse group of students and foster a campus-wide AI student community. The board members of this organization consist of a representative from six schools at Northwestern, including Feinberg School of Medicine and Pritzker School of Law. Together we seek to connect AI enthusiasts across the campus and surface opportunities for collaboration among students and organizations.
summary: The Northwestern AI Graduate Student Group was founded to serve this diverse group of students and foster a campus-wide AI student community.

name: AIESEC
description: When you join AIESEC, you join the world's largest youth leadership organization, full of globally minded young leaders. People who want to grow their own potential while contributing to something much greater than themselves. We give you immediate responsibilities so you can learn by doing, step outside your comfort zone and experience both success and failures amongst a supportive community. If you’re keen on activating your potential and contributing to a mission that changes the world, then we’re looking for you. We can’t promise you that it’ll be easy, but it sure will be worth it.
summary: Present in over 2400 universities in 127 countries and territories, AIESEC's international platform enables young people to explore and develop their leadership potential for them to have a positive impact in society.

name: Aikido
description:
The Northwestern Aikido Club (officially Zanshinkan Dojo) was founded in 1974 by Fumio Toyoda Shihan. At 46 years old, NU Aikido is the oldest martial arts group on campus! Our members include students – undergraduates and graduate students, faculty, and staff.

Aikido is a dynamic, powerful and graceful martial art developed in the early 20th century by Morihei Ueshiba. Aikido training teaches both defensive techniques (strikes, throws, join locks) and the use of traditional weapons (long sword, staff and knife).

Anyone who is interested in learning more about Aikido is welcome to join us any time during the academic year.

Practices:

Please contact us directly for the link to Zoom practices as well as any other information.

Wednesdays  (HCSP Studio 1B)
6 - 7 pm   Aikido Practices- new members welcome!

Fridays  (HCSP Studio 1B)
6 - 8 pm   Aikido Basics - for all members

Weather Dependent  (outdoor-precise location TBA)
TBA   Aikido Practice - outdoor practice

*We practice at the Henry Crown Sports Pavilion in non-Covid years.  This year we will be attempting a hybrid model as permitted by the Health and Safety Department.
summary: The Northwestern Aikido Club is a student club dedicated to the study of the martial art, aikido. Aikido is a dynamic, powerful and graceful art founded early in the 20th century. Aikido training teaches both defensive techniques and traditional weapons.

name: AKPsi
description:
As Northwestern's leading business fraternity, Alpha Kappa Psi strives to build a supportive community of engaged and driven individuals who share a common interest in business. We exist to offer a place where people can meet, learn, and grow together, while accomplishing great things along the way.

Fundamentally, AKPsi is both a social and pre-professional organization, where people can make lasting friendships and memories throughout their undergraduate years, while receiving mentorship and furthering their careers.

Application rounds are open in the fall and spring quarters, and are open to freshmen and sophomores of all majors and fields of business interest.
summary: Northwestern's premier business fraternity.

name: Alexander Hamilton Society
summary: The Alexander Hamilton Society (AHS) is an independent, non-partisan, not-for-profit, membership organization dedicated to promoting constructive debate on basic principles and contemporary issues in foreign, economic, and national security policy.

name: Algorithmic Trading Club
description:
We are always accepting new members! Either send a membership request on Wildcat Connection or email algo@u.northwestern.edu to join. No other application process is needed.

Northwestern Algorithmic Trading Club. We explore various topics in Algorithmic Trading, Fintech, and general Finance. We encourage members to learn through working on projects with others in the club, including trading competitions, paper trading, etc.

This spring quarter we'll continue holding meetings where we introduce members to various topics in quantitative finance, as well as organize more guest talks.
summary: The Algorithmic Trading club is dedicated to teaching members fundamental concepts related to generating alpha, managing risk, executing trades, and more. If you are interested, please reach out to algo@u.northwestern.edu!

name: Alianza–The Latinx Student Alliance
description:
Alianza aims to unite and support its members towards an advancement of an empowered, productive Latino/a/x presence at NU; Alianza is a cultural, educational, and social programming body. 

Latine GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/40156620/vdcD6P

Sign up for the Latine Listserv: https://gmail.us2.list-manage.com/subscribe?u=46ee92d0dfde6dc4da09929a8&id=85ce585451
summary: Alianza is the oldest and largest Latine-run student organization on Northwestern's campus. Alianza aims to unite and support students towards the advancement of an empowered Latine presence at NU

name: All In
description:
All In at Northwestern is dedicated to advancing health equity and community welfare in the greater Chicagoland area in partnership with Community Health Chicago, a free clinic. Our mission is to:

  1. Foster Professional Growth and Volunteerism: Community Health Chicago offers volunteer roles to pre-health students, such as dental assistant, medical interpreter, triage assistant, and lab technician, providing them with invaluable hands-on clinical experience. We ensure pre-health students have free and accessible transit to the clinic.

  2. Create Access to Health Education: We are developing a low-cost, low-cholesterol cookbook to help clinic patients improve their cholesterol levels and overall health through dietary changes.

  3. Empower Health Equity through Fundraising: Our goal is to bridge gaps in healthcare access by innovatively mobilizing resources. Through engaging initiatives like bake sales, merchandise booths at student events, and unique entertainment such as student-band concerts, we generate essential funds to support the operations of Community Health Chicago, ensuring continued care for those in need.

Why Join All In?
Join us in our mission to make a tangible difference in the lives of underserved populations while developing your professional skills in a supportive, mission-driven environment. All In is not just a club; it’s a community of proactive individuals committed to creating a healthier future for all.

Connect, learn, and lead with us. General meetings happen Tuesday from 7-8 p.m. For more details, join our GroupMe (linked on this page).
summary: All In is a partner organization of Community Health Chicago, a free, volunteer-based clinic serving those in need across the greater Chicagoland area.

name: All Paws In
summary: We are a volunteer based organization focused on giving back to our community through volunteer work at and alongside the animal shelters in the area. We are committed to bringing the Northwestern community together with animals.

name: Alliance BE REAL Ambassadors: Campus EDucators
description: Campus EDucators are college students with and without lived experience with eating disorders and/or weight stigma representing the National Association for Anorexia Nervosa and Associated Disorders (ANAD) and BE REAL USA. Through awareness initiatives and speaker events, Campus EDucators aim to educate their school community about the warning signs, symptoms, and impact of eating disorders as well as provide an inclusive space for those affected. In addition to raising awareness, Campus EDucators facilitate BE REAL's BodyKind curriculum to promote a healthy relationship with food and body image among students. They strive to make eating disorder treatment and prevention a priority within their university administration and surrounding communities as a step towards creating lasting change. Formerly known as the NEDA Campus Warriors in 2023.
summary: Campus EDucators is a program in collaboration with ANAD and BE REAL USA empowering students across the country to promote awareness, support, and advocacy for those affected by eating disorders and weight bias.

name: Alliance of Evanston Minority Students
description:
Frozen October 2024

We are the Alliance of Evanston Minority Students (AEMS), a student-led club on campus geared towards creating intersectional spaces for graduate students that center people of color (POC) and other marginalized racial/ethnic identities. Our goal is to foster relationships between POC/minority students across campus and to host events that celebrate the diversity of cultures represented by Northwestern’s graduate student body.

We want to better support minority students' feelings of inclusion on campus by creating a diverse community of graduate students and making it easier to find a community that represents their intersectional identities.
summary: AEMS is a student-led club on campus geared towards creating intersectional spaces for graduate students that center people of color (POC) and other marginalized racial/ethnic identities.

name: Alpha Chi Omega
description:
Gamma Chapter, Northwestern University

"Together let us seek the heights."

To learn more about Alpha Chi Omega Sorority you may visit their headquarters' website at http://www.alphachiomega.org.
summary: Alpha Chi Omega is a national women's fraternity that aims to enrich the lives of both collegiate and alumnae members with opportunities for friendship, leadership, learning and service.

name: Alpha Psi Lambda National, Inc.
description:
Alpha Psi Lambda National, Inc. is a Co-educational Fraternity geared towards the goals of Latino students on university campuses. As the fastest-growing minority in this country, it is becoming more important that the Latino community continues their education. This trend has also brought with it a need for cultural awareness, cultural identity, and cross-cultural understanding. As a result, there is an increased need for Alpha Psi Lambda for which we try to assist in the identity of our culture. This trend has been demonstrated by the growth of our Fraternity. 

While we are a Latino interest Fraternity, Alpha Psi Lambda has welcomed people from a broad spectrum of cultures across the globe. Alpha Psi Lambda stands as one family united. Our strength lies in our ability to focus on our culture while appreciating the diversity of others. 

The Latino culture is very strongly rooted in family and support, which allows Alpha Psi Lambda to provide those same roots to our Fraternity. Alpha Psi Lambda, to its members, is more than just a Fraternity. It creates that true family support, brotherhood, and sisterhood, at a time in a student's life when it is most important. Our membership prides itself on the values of Familia, Culture, Academics, Service, and Leadership.
summary: Alpha Psi Lambda was established on February 11, 1985, at The Ohio State University.  We believe in the concept of Familia to create a support network for our members while creating the real-world experience of working with anyone.

name: American Foundation for Suicide Prevention
description: The American Foundation for Suicide Prevention club’s mission and purpose is to serve as a college chapter of the American Foundation for Suicide Prevention. This organization will serve as a space on campus that prioritizes mental health and fundraises for AFSP through various events including culminating each year with the American Foundation for Suicide Prevention club event: the Out of Darkness Walk on Northwestern’s campus. The club will provide a safe space for both people who have lost someone to suicide and those who empathize with the cause and would like to fundraise.
summary: The American Foundation for Suicide Prevention club’s mission and purpose is to serve as a college chapter of the American Foundation for Suicide Prevention.

name: American Medical Women's Association
description:
AMWA is a national organization whose aim is to foster an enriching environment for women in science and the medical professions. We hope to inspire women to pursue medicine and give back to the community through a variety of informational, volunteering, or networking events. We seek to provide a plethora of resources to aspiring healthcare workers while fostering an empathetic and welcoming environment. 

Our Objectives:  

…to provide peer mentorship to undergraduate students pursuing careers in science and medicine

…to spread awareness about women’s health issues on campus and the community at large

…to create a positive space for women pursuing careers in science and medicine

...to empower women interested in medicine by providing resources and networking opportunities
summary: The American Medical Women’s Association (NU Chapter) seeks to empower and inform students interested in pursuing medicine. In our organization, we uphold networking, volunteering, friendship, feminism, and community.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
summary: We are the Northwestern student chapter of the American Society of Civil Engineers, a professional society dedicated to advancing members' goals and passions in civil engineering.

name: AMPED
description: AMPED is a weekend music mentorship program that connects Northwestern students with incarcerated young men at the Cook County Juvenile Temporary Detention Center. The “Saturday practice sessions” are offered for 2 hours every Saturday in the CCJTDC music lab to young men who show interest, talent, and desire to hone the skills they learn in their once a week classes. The sessions are facilitated by undergraduate & graduate mentors from Northwestern University, who provide musical assistance and support for the students. AMPED aims to help develop the students’ music composition skills through the use of GarageBand software while offering structured and trusting mentorships between the NU students and CCJTDC students.
summary: We are a group of students that go down to the Juvenile Temporary Detention Center (JTDC) in Chicago to mentor residents through creating hip-hop music through Garage Band.

name: APIDA Pre-Law Association
description: The APIDA Pre-Law Association is a campus community of Asian Pacific Islander Desi American (APIDA) students interested in learning about the legal field or pursuing law as a career, offering educational, mentorship, and service opportunities to encourage members to pursue and succeed in achieving professional goals.
summary: The APIDA Pre-Law Association is a campus community of Asian Pacific Islander Desi American (APIDA) students interested in learning about the legal field or pursuing law as a career, offering educational, mentorship, and service opportunities.

name: Applause for a Cause
description:
Applause for a Cause is a philanthropic, student-run film production company. Our members annually write, produce, direct, and edit a feature length film (~90 minutes) to premiere in May and to raise donations for the chosen beneficiary of that year. In the past we have worked with local and national organizations including the Make-A-Wish foundation, the Howard Area Community Center, and Connections for the Homeless.

Our production timeline starts in the summer with screenplay submissions. Then once a script is chosen, we hold crew petitions and casting auditions when the school year starts in fall. Production occurs during the winter, and finally we edit and roll out the red carpet in the spring.

We are completely student run, and encourage students of all studies and film backgrounds (even if you have none at all) to join our organization so that you can experience college film-making and meet some great people along the way.

Not a student? We accept year-long support, advice, and donations and if you are in town, we invite you to attend the premiere.

It takes a village to make a feature, so we encourage you to participate in any way you can!
summary: Applause for a Cause annually produces and premieres an original feature-length film, in support of an annually-selected philanthropic organization.

name: Arab Graduate Student Association
description: ArGSA is a community of Arab and Arab American graduate students at Northwestern University. Our mission is to provide a space for Arab graduate students, regardless of nationality, to connect, share experiences, and support each other academically, professionally, and interpersonally. We aim to create a space for students to fell welcome and represented. Through our events, programs, and initiatives, we aim to promote diversity and inclusion, foster global engagement, enhance the academic and professional experiences of Arab graduate students and Northwestern University, and educate the wider community about issues that affect the Arab community.
summary: ArGSA is a community of Arab and Arab American graduate students at Northwestern University. Our mission is to provide a space for Arab graduate students, regardless of nationality, to connect, share experiences, and support each other.

name: Arab Gulf Student Association
description: The Arab Gulf Student Association is a group that aims to provide recourses that tend to the needs of students from the Arab Gulf. It also aims to build a community, of students and alumni from the Arab Gulf, that is engaged with the Northwestern community. The Arab Gulf Student Association also aims to engage with other groups on campus so that constructive dialogue can take place and we can learn from each others perspectives.
summary: The Arab Gulf Student Association is a group that aims to provide resources that tend to the needs of students from the Arab Gulf.

name: Arch Society
description:
The Arch Society is a group of student ambassadors sponsored by the Northwestern Alumni Association (NAA). Members will support and develop university-related programs and community-wide events to strengthen the relationships between alumni, students, faculty, and staff and to foster a sense of pride and community. Meetings occur once a month during the academic year with additional meetings or program support occurring as needed.

Candidates are chosen and serve as representatives throughout their time at Northwestern. Each member of the Arch Society will serve on one of the four committees:

(1) Class Programming Committee shall be responsible for class-specific programming to increase NAA touch points with current students between Wildcat Welcome and Senior Year Programs (i.e., Last Lecture, Leave Your Print)
(2) Marketing & Outreach Committee shall be responsible for social media presence/content creation and outreach to student groups on campus (i.e., supporting social justice and mental health initiatives)
(3) Traditions Committee shall be responsible for promoting current traditions and initiating new traditions (i.e., NU Day @ Wrigley, Northwestern Night at the Art Institute)
(4) Homecoming Committee shall support Homecoming programs, recruitment, and student-alumni engagement initiatives (i.e., Homecoming Social)
summary: Arch Society members are student ambassadors of the Northwestern Alumni Association. We support and develop university-related programs and community-wide events to strengthen the relationships between alumni, students, faculty, and staff.

name: Archery Club
description: We're a co-ed sports club practicing recreational and competitive archery for educational fun. People of all experience levels are welcome. We provide training for beginners, 1-on-1 coaching for serious competitors, and equipment & an archery range for all university-affiliated members!
summary: We're a co-ed sports club practicing recreational and competitive archery. People of all experience levels are welcome. We provide training for beginners, 1-on-1 coaching for serious competitors, and equipment & an archery range for all!

name: Area Councils
description:
Area Councils are student-led organizations that serve as the hall-governance for Northwestern’s residential communities. If you are craving an opportunity to be a leader on campus and play a pivotal role in organizing in-person social programs and initiatives for the next academic year.

Area Councils are composed of four elected officer positions and selected building representatives. Elected officers will work closely with the members of the area leadership team through their advisors, who will ensure council activities comply with university policies and procedures. 

We encourage you to be a part of our Residential Services Area Councils!
summary: Area Councils are student-led organizations that serve as the hall-governance for Northwestern’s residential areas.

name: Armenian Student Association
description: TBA
summary: Cultural interest group for Armenians and friends of Armenia

name: Art Union
description:
Art Union is Northwestern’s premier art club.

We aspire to promote and develop a community of Northwestern artists, helping to nurture relationships, artistic skills, and various talents from a diverse network of students, through intentional art-making and sharing of the love of art practice. AU seeks to emphasize the art practice of artists at Northwestern, using their passion and skills for art to create a strong community within NU. From sketching and drawing nights, painting sessions, trips to the museums, and chill hangouts at local areas to simply get together and make art, we want AU to nurture the general public’s love for art practice, creating an art-loving family for all.

For updates, join our Groupme: https://groupme.com/join_group/62920933/1IS5tV4A
summary:
Art Union is Northwestern’s premier art club.
We aspire to promote and develop a community of Northwestern artists, helping to nurture relationships, artistic skills, and various talents from a diverse network of students, through intentional art-making.

name: Artificial Intelligence Graduate Group
description: Northwestern graduate students have a broad range of technical skills and research interests. The AI Graduate Student Group seeks to connect AI enthusiasts across the campus to realize opportunities for collaboration and build community among graduate students. The group is not limited to computer scientists, rather the members range across many disciplines and schools. So if you are an active AI researcher or just interested in learning more about AI, please submit the form to join our community!
summary: The AI Graduate Student Group seeks to connect AI enthusiasts across the campus to realize opportunities for collaboration and build community among graduate students.

name: Arts Alliance
description:
Arts Alliance is the oldest and largest student arts group at Northwestern University. Founded in 1974 by a collection of arts groups looking to share space, Arts Alliance has grown to become one of the largest student arts organizations in the country. Its annual season consists of three mainstage shows and two Mee-Ow Improv & Sketch Comedy shows. Arts Alliance Executive Board consists of twenty three members committed to bringing accessible, exciting, and entertaining theatrical experiences to the Northwestern and Evanston communities.

All Arts Alliance programming revolves around the three pillars of our mission statement: art that is easy to relate to, art that is easy to speak with, and art that is easy to reach. We tell stories that resonate with our community members by delivering theatre that they want to see without physical or financial barriers. 

Arts Alliance is a non-profit student arts organization committed to providing diverse, accessible, and fulfilling artistic experiences to enrich the Northwestern University community. We continue to seek out new artistic opportunities while providing a positive atmosphere in which students can learn and work alongside a committed and supportive board to create professional-quality productions.
summary: Arts Alliance creates art that reflects, deepens, and challenges the Northwestern community. We provide a positive atmosphere in which students can learn and work alongside a committed and supportive board to create professional-quality productions.

name: Ascend
summary: Organization dedicated to giving every Northwestern student an opportunituy to consult with real company founders and to gain experience in a field that they are interested in.

name: Asian American InterVarsity Christian Fellowship
description: Asian American InterVarsity is a group of students on campus who grow together to love God, His Word, His people and His purpose in the world. Students work together to find genuine friendship and a real community through small groups, one-on-one discipleships, and large group gatherings.
summary: Asian American InterVarsity is a group of students and staff who strive together to grow in love for God, his Word, his people, and his purposes in the world. We seek to be a witnessing community to the Asian and Asian American community at NU.

name: Asian American Student Journalists
description: Affinity space for Northwestern's Asian American student journalists to build community and access resources such as mentorship, opportunities to interact with industry professionals, updates on internships/jobs, and more
summary: Affinity space for Northwestern's Asian American student journalists

name: Asian Pacific American Coalition
description:
In recognition of the heterogeneity of Asian America and Pacific Islander identity, the Asian Pacific American Coalition is a sociopolitical group that provides a space for students who identify as Asian/American and/or Pacific Islander to connect, learn, organize, and ultimately resist systems of domination. We recognize that our liberation must be collective because of the interconnected nature of oppressive power structures. From our unique position as similarly racialized peoples, we build community and raise awareness of historical, social, and political issues that affect all marginalized communities through the following:
  • Collective learning
  • Support systems
  • Programming, both educational and advocacy-based
  • Coalition-building
APAC’s history began in 1991, under the name of Asian American Advisory Board (AAAB). It started out as an umbrella organization for all of the Asian/American student groups to coordinate and guide their different activities. In 1992, the AAAB gained A-status from the Northwestern’s Associated Student Government. The organization began to make headlines in 1995 when APAC organized a successful hunger strike fighting for the creation of an Asian American Studies Program. The hunger strike garnered public support from universities all over the country and inspired a new generation of young Asian/Americans. In 2003, AAAB officially changed its name to The Asian Pacific American Coalition (APAC). The new name was intended to be more expansive and reflective of the diversity, heterogeneity, and differences in experiences of Asian/American and Pacific Islander communities. Along with the name change, APAC remade its constitution and redefined its mission statement towards a more holistic outlook, making commitments to community building by addressing political, cultural, and social issues both within our own communities and with the rest of NU’s student population through various programs and events. In the past, APAC has hosted events such as Asian American History Month, a screening of Better Luck Tomorrow with cast and director Justin Lin, Espresso Expressions, and speakers such as actor George Takei, music video director Joseph Kahn, SuChin Pak of MTV, Japanese Breakfast, Alok Vaid-Menon, and author R.F. Kuang.
 
APAC strives to achieve the following:
  • Commit to accessibility, inclusion, and the decentering of East Asian communities/supremacy
  • Recognize differences in lived experience and racialization within the umbrella of Asian, Asian American, and Pacific Islander peoples, as well as the experiences of multiracial peoples
  • Engage and mobilize the student community through activism and advocacy
  • Undertake intersectional grassroots activism and advocacy in the larger Northwestern community, Evanston, and Chicago
  • Foster collective healing from historical, intergenerational, and individual trauma
  • Empower individuals with marginalized identities

If you are interested in joining APAC, interest forms are open near the beginning of every quarter -- keep an eye out on the FB page and Instagram, or send our presidents Abhi Nimmagadda (AbhiNimmagadda2025@u.northwestern.edu), Hamin You (HaminYou2026@u.northwestern.edu), and Kiran Bhat (KiranBhat2026@u.northwestern.edu) an email!
summary: From our unique position as similarly racialized peoples, Asian Pacific American Coalition builds community and power, in addition to raising awareness of historical, social, and political issues that affect marginalized communities at large.

name: Associated Student Government
description: ASG is the Associated Student Government of Northwestern, a group of undergraduate students devoted to working on behalf of the Northwestern community. ASG discusses and acts on ways to make Northwestern an overall better experience for students. We work with Northwestern administrators to bring change to the university, but we also provide services and funding directly to students. ASG is comprised of a student Senate – broadly representative of the varied student interests at Northwestern – an executive board, and 10 working committees that address issues affecting student life, academics, relations with Evanston, student groups, and more. ASG believes in building a better Northwestern.
summary: ASG is the Associated Student Government of Northwestern, a group of undergraduate students devoted to working on behalf of the Northwestern community.

name: Association for Women in Mathematics
description: • The purpose of the Association for Women in Mathematics is to create a community in which women and girls can thrive in their mathematical endeavors, and to promote equitable opportunity and treatment of women and others of marginalized genders and gender identities across the mathematical sciences. We, on a chapter level, share the same mission as that of the national Association for Women in Mathematics organization. Our goal is to create an inclusive mathematical community at Northwestern.
summary: Our goal is to create an inclusive mathematical community at Northwestern.

name: Association for Women in Sports Media
description: The Northwestern Chapter of the Association for Women in Sports Media is a network and advocacy group for women in the sports industry. Open to both males and females, AWSM at Northwestern aims to prepare its members for their professional careers through group meetings, guest-speaker insight, industry connections, and other unique opportunities.
summary: Network and Advocacy Group for Women in the Sports Industry

name: Association of Kenyan Students
description: TBA
summary: TBA

name: Association of Latino Professionals for America
description:
ALPFA (Association of Latino Professionals For America) was the first national Latino professional association in the United States, established in Los Angeles in 1972. Throughout our 47-year history we have experienced steady growth and demonstrated consistent value. ALPFA continues to build upon its proud legacy with a mission: To empower and develop Latino men and women as leaders of character for the nation, in every sector of the global economy.

More than 92,000+ professional and student members across the country – a number we plan to grow aggressively to 100,000 within the next two years. In addition to networking and professional leadership development opportunities, our members have access to 50,000 paid summer internships through hundreds of Fortune 1000 corporate partners. We aspire to be the business partner of choice for companies seeking to hire and develop Latino talent.
summary: ALPFA (Association of Latino Professionals For America) is the first national Latino professional association in the United States, established in Los Angeles in 1972. =

name: Asterik A cappella
description: Asterik is Northwestern's premier all male range voices a cappella group, founded in 1994.
summary: Asterik is Northwestern's premier all male range voices a cappella group, founded in 1994.

name: Athletes in Action
description:
Athletes in Action is a interdenominational Christian ministry. We desire to provide every athlete at Northwestern an opportunity to hear how they can know God through Jesus Christ. We are committed to do this as a community of believers laboring together in unity.

If you are curious about this relationship with God, or want to learn more about Christianity a great resource is Knowing God Personally. Also check out EveryAthlete.com
summary: Athletes in Action is a interdenominational Christian ministry. We desire to provide every athlete at Northwestern an opportunity to hear how they can know God through Jesus Christ. We are committed to do this as a community.

name: Athletic Club of Economics Students
description: ACES's goal is to promote team sports within university facilities, fostering connections and well-being among economics graduate students.
summary: ACES's goal is to promote team sports within university facilities, fostering connections and well-being among economics graduate students.

name: Athletics & Recreation
description: The Department of Athletics and Recreation: Fitness, Aquatics and Recreational Sports oversees the sport clubs on campus.
summary: The Department of Athletics and Recreation: Fitness, Aquatics and Recreational Sports oversees the sport clubs on campus.

name: Aviation Club
description: Aviation Club helps aviation enthusiasts at Northwestern explore their interest in and expand their knowledge of aviation, including, but not limited to, general, commercial, and military aviation. It fosters interests in students new to aviation by offering a place to explore it in a supportive, knowledgeable, and connected environment. We help Northwestern students connect with fellow aviators to expand their network of colleagues on campus, providing more opportunities to share and participate in their passion with others. We also help Northwestern students interested in a career in aviation connect with advisors and opportunities to pursue their goals during and after college. Aviation is a very cost-prohibitive activity. We want to make it available to all Northwestern students in as many ways as possible. This can be achieved in many forms, and we are excited to build on our current plans to bring it to Northwestern students as best as possible.
summary: Aviation Club is Northwestern's only student organization dedicated to the social aspects of aviation. Dedicated to helping people expand their knowledge of and interest in aviation, we host a variety of meetings, events and trips each quarter.

name: Ayers Residential College of Commerce and Industry
description:
Thomas G. Ayers Hall is on north campus and houses 170 students in mostly double-
occupancy and a very limited number of single-occupancy rooms, arranged in a traditional-style layout. Men and women live in separate wings of the same floor. This building is available to all undergraduate students.
summary: Thomas G. Ayers Hall is on north campus and houses 170 students in mostly double-occupancy and a very limited number of single-occupancy rooms, arranged in a traditional-style layout. Men and women live in separate wings of the same floor. This building

name: B. Burlesque
description: B. Burlesque is a dance interest group that serves to teach burlesque dance to students of color and showcase their performances in the annual burlesque show. Promoting body and sex positivity, B. Burlesque offers workshops during fall quarter led by Chicago based burlesque dancers.  Winter quarter is dedicated to working with a professional burlesque dancer on rehearsing large group dances.  On their own time dancers will chreograph their own unique small group or solo dance to be perfromed at the showcase.
summary: A dance interest group for students of color to learn and perform burlesque dances!

name: Badminton Club
description:
Come join us at Blomquist Recreation Center every Tuesday & Thursday from 8-11! 

Please join our GroupMe and follow us on instagram @nu.badminton. Announcements will primarily be sent using GroupMe, so it's important to join the groupchat! To allow everyone a chance to play, matches are one game to 21 (rally scoring) with a brief warm-up period. Rackets and birdies are readily available to borrow, but we encourage you to bring your own rackets due to the sheer demand. There are quarterly dues of $10 so that we can continue to purchase high-quality shuttles and rackets. Due to space restrictions, doubles is usually played.
summary: Club Badminton provides court space for undergraduates, graduate students, professors, and the general public of all skill levels to play badminton. We strive to be an open, encouraging space for individuals to connect and have fun through the sport.

name: Baja SAE
description: The Baja SAE team's goal is to enrich the Northwestern experience through the production of a competitive off-road racing vehicle by inspiring creative automotive design, forging marketable leadership and technical skills, and building a close-knit engineering community. The one-man, dune buggy type car must be prepared to face the most rugged terrain—rocks, creeks, snow, jumps, and mud—and keep their driver safe through all driving conditions including a roll over. The team consists of students, mostly from McCormick, who simply like to build cars and play in the mud.
summary: The Baja SAE team is a team of undergraduate students who design, build and race an all-terrain vehicle each year. Students are challenged to pull all their engineering knowledge and skills together to engineer a safe, functional racing machine.

name: Balkan Student Association
description: It is difficult to meet people from our region on campus - we come from small countries and are not very well represented at NU. Balkan Student Association is a community where everyone is welcome, where we can connect far away from home. The common language for us in/from the Balkans is English. Although similar, we are also different - but coming from the same part of Europe, sharing several aspects of our cultures (such as food or dance) and histories, and facing common political situations and problems is what helps us connect and understand each other more easily. Our purpose is to promote the cultures and languages of the Balkan countries by creating a space where students who originate from the Balkan Peninsula and students who are interested in our region can connect easily and build a community.
summary: The goal of BSA is to promote the cultures and languages of the Balkan countries by creating a space where individuals who originate from the Balkan Peninsula and individuals who are interested in our region can connect easily and build a community.

name: Ballroom Latin and Swing Team
description:
BLAST (Ballroom, Latin, and Swing Team) is an undergraduate partner dancing community. Our club is split into two main seasons - a competition season (fall - early spring) and a showcase season (mid-winter - spring), interspersed with social dance classes and workshops. New members are welcome to join at any point of the year, with no prior experience necessary to participate in either season. The dancesport dance classes are open to anyone and begin in the fall shortly after new student week. Auditions for the showcase occur early in Winter quarter and then subsequent rehearsals will occur throughout the year until showtime in Spring.  Classes, workshops, and social dance opportunities range in style from social dances like swing or salsa, to more formal ballroom dances like waltz and tango.

Our goal as an organization is to create a partner dancing community here at Northwestern. All dancers are welcome to attend our classes, workshops, and social events.

If you want to get more involved, the best ways to become a formal member of BLAST is to regularly show up to dancesport classes and actively participate in either the competitive or performance seasons.
summary: BLAST, short for “Ballroom, Latin, and Swing Team”, is a partner dance organization committed to creating a community for all levels of dancers. We provide quality instruction alongside competitive, social and performance dance opportunities

name: Bangladeshi Students and Scholars Association
description: The Bangladeshi Students and Scholars Association (BSSA) is committed to fostering a strong and inclusive community for Bangladeshi students, postdocs, and scholars at Northwestern University. As a TGS-affiliated organization, BSSA primarily serves graduate students and postdocs, while also welcoming undergraduate participation. The association aims to support the academic, professional, and social well-being of its members while promoting Bangladeshi culture and heritage within the broader Northwestern community. Through cultural events, networking opportunities, and academic discussions, BSSA provides a platform for members to connect, collaborate, and celebrate their shared identity.
summary: The Bangladeshi Students and Scholars Association (BSSA) is committed to fostering a strong and inclusive community for Bangladeshi students, postdocs, and scholars at Northwestern University.

name: Belegarth Medieval Combat Society
description: The Medieval Combat Society serves as a space for individuals to safely engage in medieval-style combat, in appreciation of pre-1600’s martial arts, also known as Historical European Martial Arts (HEMA, and fantasy culture. We practice at minimum once a week, except in exceptional circumstances, in which students can engage in drills and sparring with one another to hone the principles behind the use of weapons such as longswords, shields, spears, and more; on occasion, we host or attend larger events as well in collaboration with other local Belegarth organizations. The sport uses padded weapons to ensure the safety and cost-efficiency of the sport.
summary: The Medieval Combat Society serves as a space for individuals to safely engage in medieval-style combat.

name: Bienen Student Advisory Board
summary: An organization that helps connect the student body of Bienen to the faculty!

name: Bienestar: Latinx Service Club
description:
We aim to service the marginalized and socioeconomically disadvantaged Latinx communities many of us come from; including but not limited to ethnicity, FGLI status, uninsured, and Spanish speaking communities. We will do this by bringing together students from all backgrounds with the common goal of servicing Latinx communities  to create several group initiatives, such as: college admissions talks at public high schools in predominantly Black/Brown communities, Spanish interpretation/translation at free health clinics, work within uninsured populations, and promoting youth health via creative initiatives (i.e teaching guitar to students without financial barriers).

A secondary goal just as important to us is to create a community among Latinx pre-health students which we have found Northwestern to lack in. From personal experience, many of us have experienced isolation at Northwestern or become overwhelmed by academic and social demands, which for many of us, are new experiences as first-gen students. This club will be a space for many to network and empower one another through our academic journeys, although not Bienestar's exclusive goal.
summary: Bienestar is an identity-based service club. Our mission is to unite Latinx students and allies to service the communities many of us come from not limited to race, class, or socioeconomic status. We aim to support Latinx wellness on campus and beyond.

name: Billiards Club
description:
Billiards Club is a community dedicated to enjoying billiards (pool) as a hobby. We welcome anyone in the Northwestern Community and are open to all skill levels. Throughout the year we hold workshops to help you learn fundamentals and for those interested in a more competitive setting we also host tournaments. 

 

Most importantly we foster a fun place to socialize and meet new people. This friendly environment allows you to join games at any point during our meeting. No commitment is needed simply come join us when you are available!

 

We meet weekly on Thursdays at 7 p.m. in the Norris Game Room (Underground Level of Norris). Many times you can find one of our executive players in the Game Room to play with on any given day if you can't make Thursdays. 

 

Please join our Listserv to get news and information regarding workshops, meeting days, and tournaments. Note: Please type your Full Name (First & Last), click the regular subscription type, and follow the instructions sent to you in a confirmation email.
summary:
A community for all those interested in billiards (pool) regardless of skill level! Great place to meet new people and have a fun time!

Casual Games! Workshops! Tournaments! 

Location: Norris Game Room (Underground Level) 
When: Thursdays at 7 p.m.

name: BinaryHeart
description: BinaryHeart is a student organization that refurbishes retired technology for donation to students, schools, programs, and other underserved groups in need of these devices.
summary: BinaryHeart is a student organization that refurbishes retired technology for donation to students, schools, programs, and other underserved groups in need of these devices.

name: Biokind Analytics
description: Biokind Analytics will bring together undergraduate data scientists and statisticians across NU to apply classroom learning for meaningful, positive societal impact in their communities. We will contact non profits in the Chicagoland area to conduct free data analysis for these organizations and present our findings. Our faculty advisor is Arvind Krishna.
summary: Biokind Analytics will bring together undergraduate data scientists and statisticians across NU to apply classroom learning for meaningful, positive societal impact in their communities. Our faculty advisor is Arvind Krishna.

name: Biology Students Association
summary: Biology Students Association (BSA) at Northwestern seeks to connect students with a common interest in biology, provide a forum for discussion, and bridge the gap between textbook knowledge and real-world science.

name: Biomedical Engineering Society
description: The Biomedical Engineering Society (BMES) at Northwestern University offers the chance to establish leadership skills, networking, and career development opportunities. BMES also facilitates community among biomedical engineering undergrads through social events and opportunities to interact with faculty in a casual setting. Members can participate in BMES through attending club events, joining committees, and competing in biomedical-focused design teams.
summary: The Biomedical Engineering Society at Northwestern University brings together biomedical engineering students and faculty members to promote academic, professional, and social collaborations.

name: Biomedical Informatics and Data Science
description:
The purpose of the Biomedical Informatics and Data Science (BIDS) Student Group is to create a community for biomedical informatics and data science students across Northwestern University’s Evanston and Chicago campuses. The main goals of the group are as follows:

  • Develop an internal and external network for BIDS students and alumni.
  • Foster an environment for discussion related to BIDS work at Northwestern University and beyond.
  • Increase awareness of current BIDS research and promote cross-program collaborations between the domains of informatics, data science, medicine, health and biology.
  • Create an avenue for inter-institutional collaborations with other BIDS student groups across Chicago.
  • Promote additional opportunities for BIDS students through lectures and workshops.
  • Serve as an informal advisory board to BIDS Faculty in curriculum planning and program development.

The main benefits of joining the BIDS student group are to:

  • Develop professional and leadership skills.
  • Network with other BIDS students, alumni, and faculty.
  • Gain a deeper knowledge and understanding of BIDS research, educational programs, and outside projects.
  • Have fun with other BIDS students at Northwestern University!
summary: The purpose of the Biomedical Informatics and Data Science (BIDS) Student Group is to create a community for biomedical informatics and data science students across Northwestern University’s Evanston and Chicago campuses.

name: Biotech Club @ Northwestern
description:
Frozen October 2024

The purpose of Northwestern Biotech Group is to build a community of graduate students and postdoctoral fellows who aspire to become successful professionals and leaders in the life sciences and biotechnology industry. Our intended goals are to provide professional development, networking and support for the broader Northwestern graduate and postdoctoral community and bridge the gap between academia and industry. We will serve as a platform to connect students and trainees with companies and alumni to expose them to the life sciences sector.
summary: The purpose of Northwestern Biotech Group is to build a community of graduate students and postdoctoral fellows who aspire to become successful professionals and leaders in the life sciences and biotechnology industry.

name: Black Formal
description: A collective of students, representative of members that both do and do not hold formal leadership positions in Black student organizations, pulled together to identify an event based on a community need. This event was intended to mirror other Northwestern-funded student formal affairs- with students dressing up and going to an off-campus venue for an evening of entertainment, food, and fun. This formal is unique because it is not affiliated with any student organization in the community and aims to create an inclusive space. This formal is in celebration of the Black community, but people of all identities are welcome.
summary: Black formal organizes a yearly formal in celebration of the Black community, and seeks to fulfill the needs expressed in the Black Student Experience Report.

name: Black Graduate Student Association
summary: Founded in 1996, the Black Graduate Student Association (BGSA), a graduate student organization within The Graduate School (TGS), aims to support the academic, professional, and social growth of graduate students of color from underrepresented groups.

name: Black Mentorship Program
description: The Black Mentorship Program (BMP) is a mentorship program for Black undergraduate students at Northwestern. Our mission is to improve the undergraduate experience of Black students at Northwestern through mentorship, community building, racial identity development, and engagement with community resources. During its pilot and subsequent years, the program has held weekly meetings throughout the school year with topics ranging from study skills and getting acclimated to college life to what it means to be Black at Northwestern. We are student led and hope to be an asset to the broader Northwestern community by creating a strong Black community and producing confident Black student leaders.
summary: BMP's mission is to improve the undergraduate experience of Black students at Northwestern through mentorship, community building, racial identity development, and engagement with community resources.

name: Black Poetry Society
description: tba
summary: The Black poetry society aims to promote community amongst Black students on campus and give Black poets the space and opportunity to share their poetry. It is a space to write, perform poetry, and connect with the Chicago poetry scene.

name: Black Pre-Law Association
description: Our organization aims to be the central home to all of Northwestern's Black Pre-Law students. Our goal is to support every member of the Black Pre-Law community socially, academically, and pre-professionally.
summary: Our organization aims to be the central home to all of Northwestern's Black Pre-Law students. Our goal is to support every member of the Black Pre-Law community socially, academically, and pre-professionally.

name: Black Screens
description: Black Screens is a community space for black students on campus interested in radio/television/film and theater where we can all connect and excel in our careers together. The intended goals of this organization are to provide black students on campus interested in radio/television/film and theater with a sense of belonging, networking opportunities, and career development.
summary: Black Screens is a community space for black students on campus interested in radio/television/film and theater. This organization is a space where we can all connect and excel in our careers together.

name: Blackboard Magazine
description: This student-led publication aims at reporting on issues concerning the Black community both on Northwestern's campus and in the United States, more broadly.
summary: BlackBoard Magazine is Northwestern University’s historically Black student publication.

name: Blockchain Group
description: Founded in 2017, Northwestern Blockchain Group focuses on fostering an active blockchain & cryptocurrency ecosystem at Northwestern University. We work on research and software development projects to promote blockchain education on campus, and we collaborate with leading Web3 companies to deliver engaging speaker events for students.
summary: NU Blockchain Group is an undergraduate group aiming to foster a healthy ecosystem surrounding Blockchain technology on campus through a series of workshops, speaker events, and development projects.

name: Blush
description:
Frozen October 2024

Blush is an inclusive, supportive, and barrier-free NU beauty and wellness organization devoted to encouraging fellow NU students to cultivate holistic well-being and recognize that beauty transcends the superficial and, instead, exists within. It shall be the mission of Blush to offer an array of services, including an engaging online blog and a vibrant social media presence, to actively connect with and empower our Northwestern community on their journey to holistic well-being and inner beauty. Blush's objectives are:

1. Creating an inclusive and supportive Northwestern community where members feel empowered to express themselves authentically and without judgment.

2. Educating members of the Northwestern student body that beauty is not merely superficial but rather a reflection of inner qualities.

3. Fostering self-acceptance and confidence, promoting holistic well-being by offering resources, workshops, and events that address physical, mental, and emotional health.

3. Cultivating an engaging online community by maintaining a strong social media presence across platforms and sharing diverse perspectives on beauty, wellness, and self-expression.

4. Fostering diversity, equity, and inclusion within the Northwestern community through diverse programming, events, and outreach efforts.

5. Promoting the exchange of beauty and skincare personal insights.
summary: Blush is an inclusive beauty + wellness org for NU students to foster holistic well-being and recognize inner beauty. Through a vibrant social media presence and engaging blog, Blush empowers the NU community on their journey to well-being.

name: Boomshaka
description: Boomshaka is Northwestern University's premier drum, dance, and rhythm ensemble. Entirely student written, directed, and produced, Boomshaka has established a reputation for consistently delivering energetic and inspiring performances. We combine dance styles such as hip-hop and contemporary with various forms of percussion, using everything from sticks, poles, and buckets to our hands and feet. Our performers come from a variety of backgrounds and have a number of areas of artistic expertise, ranging from classical music performance and formal dance training to free style rapping and break dancing. We blend our talents and experiment with new mediums of expression to create unique performances! Check us out on Youtube, Facebook, Instagram, or on our website.
summary: Boomshaka is Northwestern's premier drum, dance, and rhythm ensemble. Entirely student written, directed, and produced, Boomshaka has established a reputation for consistently delivering energetic and inspiring performances.

name: Bowling Club
description: The Bowling Club is a welcoming and low-pressure environment where students can come together to enjoy recreational activities, with a primary focus on bowling. We aim to offer a casual and stress-free outlet for students to relax, connect, and engage in physical activity by organizing 2–3 bowling outings per academic year. These events will be open to participants of all skill levels. In addition to bowling, the club may pursue secondary events, hosting other casual recreational events, such as a ping pong tournament or a friendly game of kickball.
summary: The Bowling Club is a welcoming and low-pressure environment where students can come together to enjoy recreational activities, with a primary focus on bowling.

name: Brazilian Student Association
description:
The Brazilian Student Association (BRASA) aims to establish prolific and long-lasting relationships between American universities and Brazil, thereby creating a platform through which Brazilian students in the United States – and non-Brazilian students willing to be involved – can act to improve Brazil. In addition, BRASA aims to promote the understanding of Brazilian culture, professional opportunities, and the Portuguese language.

A Associação Brasileira de Estudantes (BRASA) visa estabelecer relações prolíficas e duradouras entre universidades americanas e o Brasil, assim criando uma plataforma através da qual estudantes brasileiros nos Estados Unidos, e estudantes não brasileiros que desejam se envolver, podem atuar para melhorar o Brasil. Adicionalmente, a BRASA tem como objetivo promover o entendimento da cultura brasileira, oportunidades profissionais e da língua portuguesa.
summary: BRASA strives to engage the Brazilian community at Northwestern, integrate it with the community of Brazilians studying abroad, as well as to promote Brazilian culture, social life and pre-professional opportunities on campus.

name: Bridge Club
description:
Welcome to the world of Bridge! Bridge is a game of practically infinite possibilities and one of the world’s greatest card games. You’ll love Bridge if you enjoy puzzles or cards or if you’re just looking for a fun social game to participate in. Players of any experience level are welcome to join us at our weekly meetings at the Norris Student Center -  whether you’re a bridge master or have never touched a playing card in your life, we hope to see you there! Participation is open to all members of the Northwestern and Evanston community.

 

For new players there will be mentors available to teach and offer tips about the game. The atmosphere is always very casual and geared towards learning. For experienced players, it’s a great place to meet other bridge enthusiasts, forge partnerships, and get in some good practice. Chances are you’ll meet someone to partner with at local bridge games both down in Chicago and nearby in Evanston.

 

Looking for some serious bridge competition? We also participate in the ACBL Collegiate Bridge Bowl qualifiers each winter and spring. We have a strong history in this event and have qualified for nationals several times in recent years, which typically means a travel package to the NABC in the summer!

 

For more information, stop by our booth at the activity fair this Fall or email Eli at elicarroll2025@u.northwestern.edu
summary: Northwestern's Bridge Club. Come play with us! All levels welcome!

name: BridgeUSA at NU
description: Frozen October 2024
summary: N/A

name: Brown Sugar
description: Brown Sugar is the nation's premiere South Asian-interest co-ed a cappella group. Founded in 1999 by two Northwestern students looking to embrace their South Asian roots, Brown Sugar performs a refreshing mix of South Asian and Western music, ranging everywhere from Bollywood classics and Punjabi pop to American rock and R&B. The group performs around the Chicagoland area and around the country. Check us out on Instagram, TikTok and Youtube!
summary: Brown Sugar is the nation's premiere South Asian-interest co-ed a cappella group.

name: Buffett Institute for Global Affairs
description: Our mission is to foster meaningful engagement across borders of all kinds. We create opportunities for dialogue across academic disciplines—the humanities, social sciences, business, law, medicine, the arts and more—and catalyze connections between researchers and experts from government, industry, nonprofit and nongovernmental organizations worldwide.
summary: Northwestern University’s Roberta Buffett Institute for Global Affairs brings together the brightest minds from across Northwestern University and around the world to address critical global challenges...

name: Business in Healthcare Club
summary: The Business in Healthcare Club aims to educate, inform, and expose undergraduate students at Northwestern to the intersections of business and healthcare. From wanting to learn more about healthcare, to searching for a job, this is the club for you!

name: Campus Inclusion and Community
summary: DCIC is dedicated to collaborating with students, faculty, and staff with the aim of making Northwestern University an inclusive community for all Wildcats. DCIC's primary goal is to cultivate a community of respect at NU.

name: Campus Sleep Network
description: CSI is a group of students dedicated to improving the sleep health of the Northwestern community. Reach out to us at campussleepinitiative@gmail.com!
summary: The purpose of CSI is to promote and incentivize wellness in sleep health and hygiene for students, and to educate the community about the importance of sleep and the dangers of sleep deprivation.

name: Cantonese Club
description: Cantonese Club is a welcoming community for anyone interested in exploring the vibrant language, history, and culture of Cantonese. Whether you are just interested in picking up a new language, a fluent speaker, or looking for other students interested in learning Cantonese, our club offers engaging activities open to all levels. Our mission is to cultivate interest in Cantonese culture, create a supportive community, and preserve and carry on the unique Cantonese language and traditions. No prior knowledge of Cantonese is required! Join us in our journey to explore Cantonese language and culture.
summary: Cantonese Club is a welcoming community for anyone interested in exploring the vibrant language, history, and culture of Cantonese.

name: CaribNation
description: CaribNation is a community and support system for students of Caribbean descent, and individuals with a love and appreciation for the culture. Our organization fosters community through various events that will promote and educate others about Caribbean culture. Our primary goal is to provide students with access to a community of shared beliefs and origins. This is a space where they can be their authentic self and connected to their culture.
summary: Northwestern's Caribbean Student Alliance is an organization for students of Caribbean descent, and members of the NU community who are interested in the dynamic culture. We promote the diversity of the culture through various activities and events.

name: Cartography Club
description: The Cartography Club is a community for students interested in cartography and/or geography. We aim to increase understanding of the topological and sociological makeup of our globe. The club is a fun and interactive space with trivia nights, Geoguesser competitions, analysis of historical maps, and trips to archives in Chicago.
summary: The Cartography Club is a community for students interested in cartography and/or geography. We aim to increase understanding of the topological and sociological makeup of our globe.

name: Catholic Student Association
description:
The Catholic Student Association (CaSA) is the student group representing Catholic students and their friends at Northwestern University. We are affiliated with the Sheil Catholic Center and provide opportunities to engage in fellowship, education, prayer, and service. 

 

CaSA welcomes all students to participate in our events, including retreats, lectures, discussions, service trips, bible study, small faith groups and many other ministries and activities. During the school year, Sunday Mass is celebrated at 9:30 a.m., 11:00 a.m., and 5:00 p.m. and daily Mass is celebrated on weekdays at 5:00 p.m.

 

Whether you identify as Catholic, Christian, or are seeking a spiritual path, we're here to walk with you on your spiritual journey. Come with your questions. Come as you are. You belong here!
summary: CaSA gathers Catholic students and their friends to grow in friendship with each other and Christ. We invite students to explore faith, serve others, and enjoy each others' company. Our home is the Sheil Catholic Center.

name: Cats Who Compost
description: CWC aims to engage the Northwestern community in reducing food waste through composting. Northwestern’s Hillel Center oversees free, publicly available, industrial composting bins provided by Collective Resource, Evanston’s industrial composting service. These bins are also located behind Sheil Catholic Center and University Christian Ministry. While 'Cats Who Compost is secular, it works with religious sites on campus because Northwestern waste contracts prevent Collective Resource from collecting on campus property. CWC shares additional composting resources, like countertop compost bin liners, and shares knowledge about composting to make it a more environmentally conscious space. We host social, educational, and volunteer events, and we're happy to welcome you to our community!
summary: CWC's goals are to share knowledge about composting and engage the Northwestern community in composting around campus!

name: Center for Awareness, Response & Education
description:
CARE provides services for survivors of past or recent trauma, and friends of survivors, including confidential support and advocacy services, education programming, and referrals.
