Please note that this page is not kept active.  If you wish to know more about CARE, feel free to email care@northwestern.edu, follow us on Facebook, or stop by our office in Searle Hall.
summary: The mission of CARE is to create a culture of healthy sexuality at Northwestern University where sexual violence, relationship violence, and stalking are not tolerated

name: Center for Civic Engagement
description:
Northwestern University's Center for Civic Engagement promotes a lifelong commitment to active citizenship and social responsibility among all students. Through an integration of academics with meaningful volunteer service, research and community partnerships, the Center supports students, faculty, staff and alumni as they enhance their own academic experiences while contributing to stronger communities and a more engaged university.

The Center for Civic Engagement utilizes a variety of strategies to meet this mission, building in part on the successful programs that are already underway in various halls of the institution. The Center for Civic Engagement:

    • Promotes meaningful community involvement and a culture of active civic engagement among all members of the university community (pre-college students, undergraduates, graduates, alumni, faculty and staff)
    • Develops and promotes cohesive curricular strategies that integrate service and hands-on experience with academic learning in a variety of disciplines
    • Connects and coordinates related efforts among individual faculty and staff as well as programs and departments throughout the university
    • Serves as a clearinghouse to collect and disseminate information on civic learning opportunities
    • Facilitates mutually-beneficial partnerships with community organizations locally and across the globe
    • Provides training and technical assistance to faculty, staff, and student leaders who facilitate service-learning projects and community partnerships
    • Conducts and publishes research on civic engagement and service-learning
    • Evaluates programs and disseminates information on best practices for academically based centers on civic education
summary: Northwestern University's Center for Civic Engagement promotes a lifelong commitment to active citizenship & social responsibility. CCE integrates academics with meaningful service, research and community partnerships to promote a more engaged university

name: Center for Health Equity Transformation
description: tba
summary: tba

name: Center for Leadership
description:
Northwestern is one of only a few academic institutions that boast a university-wide research center dedicated to the study of leadership and dissemination of the latest and best practices.

The Center for Leadership offers opportunities for all students to improve their skills, in an environment that nurtures experimentation and innovation. We also encourage staff and faculty to get involved by contributing their expertise, by embedding assessment tools into the curriculum, and through consultations with the Center’s directors.

For Undergraduate students, we offer programs like Leadership Coaching, the Paradigms and Strategies of Leadership class, and the Leadership Certificate. We encourage everyone to learn more at lead.northwestern.edu.

By engaging with the Center for Leadership, you will build your leadership resilience through our robust assessment tools and deep expertise.
summary: The Center for Leadership provides programming for undergraduates, graduate students, and faculty that enhances leadership and teamwork. We start from a "questions first" approach and help leaders grow authentically by building on their experiences.

name: Central Asian Students Hub
description: A cultural student organization dedicated to celebrating and promoting the diverse traditions, languages, and histories of Central Asia.
summary: A cultural student organization dedicated to celebrating and promoting the diverse traditions, languages, and histories of Central Asia.

name: Challah for Hunger
description:
If you like to bake, eat, or volunteer, Challah for Hunger is for you!! Challah for Hunger (C4H) raises awareness of and money for hunger and disaster relief through the production and sale of challah bread. Volunteers gather weekly on afternoons at Hillel to make challah, which we sell to the Northwestern community. Challot come in a variety of flavors: plain, chocolate chip,  cinnamon sugar, and flavor of the week! Fifty percent of each CfH chapter's profits are donated to the national CfH cause-- Swipe Out Hunger. The remaining 50% goes to The Ark, an organization that supports Jews in the Chicago area.

Although we are a Hillel student group, Challah for Hunger is open to all students!! 

 

Contact Julia Schnipper at juliaschnipper2025@u.northwestern.edu for more information!
summary: If you like to bake, eat, or volunteer, Challah for Hunger is for you!! Challah for Hunger (C4H) raises awareness of and money for hunger and disaster relief through the production and sale of challah bread.

name: Chapin Hall
description:
Frozen October 2024

Chapin Hall is the Humanities Residential College. It houses 65 students in mostly double-occupancy and a very limited number of single-occupancy rooms, arranged in a traditional-style layout. Men and women live on separate floors. This building is available to all undergraduate students.
summary: The Humanities Residential College.

name: Chi Alpha Campus Ministries
description:
XA's focus is in three parts: 
 
God • Bringing you to a place where you can grow closer to God
Community • Building a community of people you can rely on, spend time with, and grow with
Mission • Pursuing the mission of helping others find the joy of experiencing God and community
 
Everyone of all backgrounds and identities are welcome in XA!
summary: Chi Alpha (or XA) is a Christian campus ministry focused on allowing students to grow together in their experience with God, community, and mission.

name: Chi Omega
description:
Xi Chapter, Northwestern University
1870 Orrington Ave

Installed: 1901

Chapter President: Emma Brick

You may visit the Xi Chapter's website at www.chiomegaxi.com

Organization Facts

National Founding Date: April 5, 1895

Colors: Cardinal, Straw

To learn more about Chi Omega you may visit their headquarters' website at http://www.chiomega.com/.

Awards

NU's Greek Order of Omega Awards 2013-2014

Xi was proud to accept the following three awards at the Order of Omega Awards:  

  • Outstanding Academic Programming for the Panhellenic Community
  • Sarah Suh received the Undergraduate Service Award
  • Christine Daly received the Outstanding Chapter President for the Panhellenic Community

NU's Greek Order of Omega Awards 2012-2013

Xi was proud to accept the following two awards at the Order of Omega Awards:  

  • Outstanding Philanthropy Event
  • Carrive Von Hoff received the Oustanding Chapter Advisor/Alumnus for the Panhellenic Community!

Chi Omega 2012 Convention Awards

  • Kelsey O'Hearn received the Jobelle Holcombe Panhellenic Award
  • Xi Chapter won the Excellent Financial Management Award
  • Nancy Jacobs, Xi Alumna, won Woman of Achievement Award

NU's Greek Order of Omega Awards 2011-2012

Xi was proud to accept the following three awards at the Order of Omega Awards: 
  • Outstanding Philanthropic Efforts and Initiatives
  • Outstanding External  Relations
  • The Community Spirit Award
We also congratulate Kelsey O'Hearn for winning the Greek Leadership Award and Sinead Flood for winning the Omega Society Senior Honor Roll
summary:
Xi Chapter, Northwestern University 1870 Orrington Ave
Installed: 1901
Chapter President: Emma Brick

You may visit the Xi Chapter's website at  www.chiomegaxi.com
Organization Facts
National Founding Date: April 5, 1895
Colors: Cardinal, Straw

name: Chicago Graduate Student Association
description:
For more information check out our website: http://sites.northwestern.edu/cgsa/

 

The Chicago Graduate Student Association (CGSA) is a student organization composed of graduate students from programs on Northwestern University’s Chicago Campus, including, but not limited to,  the Driskill Graduate Training Program in Life Sciences (DGP), Medical Scientist Training Program (MSTP), Northwestern University Interdepartmental Neuroscience (NUIN), Biomedical Engineering (BME), Clinical Psychology, Genetic Counselling and Physical Therapy.  The CGSA aims to serve in the best interest of all graduate students on the Chicago campus. We work to achieve this goal by advocating for and promoting students’ rights, academic and professional development, social interaction and community outreach. We also strive to bring together students from both the Chicago and Evanston campuses, whether through academic or social events. Our mission is to represent the diverse interests of Chicago graduate students and facilitate The Graduate School’s objective of funding and supporting graduate students’ academic and career goals.
summary: For more information check out our website: http://sites.northwestern.edu/cgsa/

name: China and Sinophone Studies Society
description: China and Sinophone Studies Society (CNSS) is an academic organization that seeks to engage the Northwestern community in research, discussion, and writing on contemporary China and the Sinophone world through the lens of culture, art, politics, economics, and history. It aims to provide a space for open, genuine intellectual debates in studies of China and the Sinophone cultural sphere, including the Chinese diaspora. Issues and implications less documented by the U.S. mainstream media will be of particular interest.
summary: China & Sinophone Studies Society is an academic organization seeking to engage the Northwestern community in research, discussion, and writing on contemporary China and the Sinophone world through culture, art, politics, economics, and history.

name: China Care
description: China Care is a cultural philanthropy group who helps to fund surgeries for Chinese orphans with disabilities and provides programming for adopted Chinese children who live in the Chicagoland area. Please check out the NU China Care website here!
summary: China Care is a cultural philanthropy group who helps to fund surgeries and education for Chinese orphans with disabilities and provides programming for adopted Chinese children who live in the Chicagoland area.

name: Chinatown Health Initiative
description:
What we do:

  1. Volunteer - You will work with medical students and health professionals to provide free primary healthcare to the indigent public at health fairs and clinics. Interpreting is a further way get more involved clinical experience (conversational proficiency in Mandarin or Cantonese required as translator).
  2. Education - CTHI hopes to foster greater education of health-related issues in Chinatown. In Chinatown, we are restarting outreach initiatives to connect to the public collaborating with medical schools in the greater Chicagoland area (no proficiency in Mandarin or Cantonese required).
  3. Public Events - We hold speaker events on campus to raise awareness of different public health issues. This has included speakers from Chinatown, Northwestern, Feinberg School of Medicine, and Doctors Without Borders in the past.
  4. Fundraising - In the past, we hand-made and sold Chinese dumplings, tanghulu, and other pastries to raise funds for our socials and health initiatives. New initiatives always welcome! No cooking or language experience needed
  5. Socials - We hold all types of socials, including dim sum outings, movie nights, game nights, etc! Come meet fellow CTHI members and have a good time.
  6. Apply to join the student board as a Junior Executive (Fall) and Executive Member (Spring). Our executive board coordinates volunteering activities and fundraisers. This is a great way to gain event planning experience and build communication and leadership skills!

If you're interested, please contact chinatown@u.northwestern.edu. We will reply within 1-2 days!
summary: Welcome! We are a student group that supports health initiatives in Cermak Chinatown. Get involved by volunteering as an interpreter or joining the student board.

name: Chinese Students Association
description:
The Chinese Students Association is one of the first established, and largest Asian-American student groups at Northwestern. We aim to promote the awareness of Chinese culture on campus and elsewhere while encouraging a social environment for students of all backgrounds. Furthermore, we strive to bring service to our community. Our events are geared towards bringing together the Asian community while sharing our culture with others. Some annual events include: Celebrasia, Dumpling Night, Cultural Festivals, dances, dinner parties, and hosting famous guest speakers.

Please follow us on instagram to stay up to date with events and general news: northwesterncsa 

Please email csa@u.northwestern.edu for questions, and if you are interested in joining! 

Warning: We do not use this page very often, so please refer to email and instagram!
summary: The Chinese Students Association is one of the first established Asian-American student groups at Northwestern. We aim to promote the awareness of Chinese culture on campus and elsewhere while encouraging a social environment for students of all

name: Christians on Campus
description:
Christians on Campus is a campus group comprising of believers in Christ from various backgrounds. We warmly welcome all Christians in Christ, and reach out to the greater Northwestern community.

Our Faith:

We hold the faith which is common to all believers (Jude 3):

  • The Bible is the complete divine revelation inspired word-by-word by God through the Holy Spirit (2 Peter 1:21, 2 Timothy 3:16)

  • God is uniquely one, yet triune - the Father, the Son, and the Spirit (1 Timothy 2:5a, Matthew 28:19)

  • God became a man, the man of Jesus Christ (John 1:1, John 1:14)

  • Christ died on the cross for our sins, shedding His blood for our redemption (1 Peter 2:24, Ephesians 1:7a)

  • Christ resurrected from among the dead on the third day (1 Corinthians 15:4) and ascended to the right hand of God to be Lord of all (Acts 1:9, Acts 2:33)

  • When a person repents to God and believes in the Lord Jesus Christ, he is regenerated (born again) and becomes a living member of the one Body of CHrist (Acts 20:21, John 3:3, Ephesians 1:22-23, Romans 12:5)

  • Christ is coming again to receive His believers to Himself (1 Thessalonians 2:19)

 

Events:

  • Weekly Bible study on campus to pursue the Lord and the truth in God's word with other students
  • Each Friday during the school year, Christians on Campus hosts fellowship meetings at several homes in the community adjacent to Northwestern. These times generally include a home cooked meal, singing hymns, and mutual Christian fellowship through reading the Bible and reading excerpts of spiritual books.
  • In addition to the Friday night fellowship meetings, we regularly come together with other Christians from the NU Medical Center, the Law School, UIC, and the University of Chicago.
  • Campus ministers are available throughout the week to meet with students at the students' convenience for prayer and fellowship, reading the Bible, or simply to talk.
summary: Christians on Campus is a campus group comprising of believers in Christ from various backgrounds. We warmly welcome all Christians in Christ, and reach out to the greater Northwestern community.

name: Chronic Illness Alliance
summary: Chronic Illness Alliance aims to foster a supportive campus environment around chronic illnesses and address issues that affect those living with chronic illness.

name: Church With Me
description: From Bible Studies to traveling to church together in Chicago, this organization prides itself on building a community without the restriction of location and cost.
summary: From Bible Studies to traveling to church together in Chicago, this organization prides itself on building a community without the restriction of location and cost.

name: Circle K
description: Circle K's motto is Live to Serve, Love to Serve. Circle K International is dedicated to promoting democratic values, fostering leadership skills, and serving both campus and community. We encourage active participation, academic excellence, and camaraderie while nurturing qualities like assertive citizenship and service spirit. Emphasizing human and spiritual values, we advocate for the Golden Rule and high ethical standards. Through our club, we cultivate enduring friendships, altruistic service, and community betterment. Overall, Circle K International strives to uphold ideals that contribute to righteousness, justice, patriotism, and goodwill in society. Circle K International focuses its work on four major areas: homelessness and food insecurity, childhood development, mental health and environmental justice. Our club will spearhead service opportunities in all of these areas.
summary: Circle K's motto is Live to Serve, Love to Serve. Circle K International is dedicated to promoting democratic values, fostering leadership skills, and serving both campus and community.

name: Club Baseball
description:
To express interest in playing and/or to learn more, please contact president noahhughes2025@u.northwestern.edu

– If you're looking for a way to continue playing the game you love, but not quite at the rigor you did in your glory days, club baseball is for you. 

– Tryouts will be held within the first two weeks of school.

Our schedule is usually something along these lines:

– 1 - 2 hour practices twice per week throughout the year (we have a practice facility with a batting cage)

– Division: NCBA Great Lakes West

– Fall Season: 3 3-game conference series (October - Early November)

– Spring Season: 3 3-game conference series after spring break (April-May), 1 3-game non-conference series, opportunity to advance to NCBA playoffs and World Series
summary: NU Club Baseball is the best way to continue playing competitive baseball after high school. We practice two times a week in the fall and spring (1-2 in winter) and have games in the fall and spring. Tryouts will occur either week 1 or 2 of fall quarter.

name: Club Field Hockey
description: Club Field Hockey provides students of Northwestern the opportunity to play high quality field hockey in a fun environment. The recreational, yet competitive nature of the club enables members to enjoy the sport through outdoor practice and games in fall and spring, in addition to regular training games with the Chicago Field Hockey Club and tournaments at nearby universities. Additionally, the club fundraises and organizes social events to further bond the team. The club contributes to the growth of the field hockey community at Northwestern and the greater Chicago area.
summary: Club Field Hockey is open to athletes of any skill level. The recreational, yet competitive nature of the club will enable members to enjoy the sport through training sessions, away games, tournaments, and bonding events.

name: Club Figure Skating (NUFSC)
description:
Founded in 2004, the Northwestern University Figure Skating Club (NUFSC), is home to the Northwestern Synchronized Skating Team ("The Purple Line") and Intercollegiate Freestyle Team. The club provides skaters of all levels with the unique opportunity to continue their figure skating journey during college, competing in both local and sectional competitions.

NUFSC is recognized as a club sport at Northwestern University and is registered as an Open Collegiate Synchro Team and Intercollegiate Team with U.S. Figure Skating (USFSA). 

The team is made up of passionate athletes who have a deep love for skating, with backgrounds in freestyle, synchro, ice dance, theater on ice, pairs, and showcase. Our diverse team includes skaters from all around the world and represents a variety of academic disciplines at Northwestern.  

Beyond the ice, we create a supportive and tight-knit community that thrives on teamwork, perseverance, and the love of the sport. Being part of the team means gaining lifelong friendships, valuable competition experience, and the opportunity to represent NU. We’re more than just a team — we’re a family, and we’re always excited to welcome new members who share our passion for skating.

https://vimeo.com/943642102
summary: The Northwestern Figure Skating Club consists of the Synchronized Skating Team and Intercollegiate Team. Members have the opportunity to continue figure skating in college and experience collegiate-level competition.

name: Club Golf
description: Club golf was brought back to campus during the 2013-2014 school year.  The club was created in order to provide a place for students to play competitvely, in regional tournaments in the spring and fall, as well as recreationally.
summary: Club Golf was re-formed during the 2013-2014 school year.  We play at local driving ranges and golf courses for practice, and we also participate in regional tournaments for players looking for competition.

name: Club Softball
description: Northwestern Club Softball is a competitive, collegiate fastpitch softball team. We play teams generally located in the Midwest region. 2019 was our 1st appearance at the national tourney.
summary: Northwestern Club Softball is a competitive, collegiate fastpitch softball team. We are a member of the National Club Softball Association and play teams generally located in the Midwest region. 2019 was our 1st appearance at the national tourney.

name: Club Tennis
description:
Fill out the form below by noon on September 18 to sign up for tryouts!
https://forms.gle/t3TgW8r7i1T4aCED9 

Our team is close to 130 people. Based on tryouts, we will select Men's A-team, Men's B-team, Women's A-team, and Women's B-team. All teams practice twice a week including a co-ed practice. The top members of the team sometimes compete against other schools in the USTA Tennis on Campus format.

Practices are held at night, a variation of times between 8pm-11pm, at the Combe Tennis Center inside the Henry Crowne Sports Pavilion. 

Follow us on instagram @nu.clubtennis

Please feel free to reach out with any questions at nuclubtennis@gmail.com

 

Please don't request to join the roster yet. Thank you!
summary: Club Tennis is a group for students who want to continue playing tennis at a competitive level, but in a more recreational environment.

name: Cognitive Systems and Artificial Intelligence Student Group
description: Frozen October 2024
summary: TBA

name: College Democrats
description: The Northwestern University College Democrats serves as a campus hub for progressive thought and action. In an effort to increase student involvement in politics and voter turnout among college-aged students, we host events with distinguished keynote speakers every academic quarter, sponsor colloquia on campus, and organize a multitude of canvassing and outreach opportunities in Evanston and the greater Chicago area. In addition, we hold weekly meetings on campus, where we discuss current events, debate progressive ideals and policy, and, most importantly, build a strong community.
summary: The NU College Democrats serves as a campus hub for progressive thought and action. We hold weekly meetings on campus, discussing current events, debating progressive ideals and policy, and, most importantly, building a strong community.

name: College Feminists
description: College Feminists is the premier feminist and pro-choice organization on the Evanston Northwestern campus. We frequently partner with the Women's Center and have events ranging from cookie decorating to attending protests together. We believe that everyone should be able to make their own decisions regarding abortion and birth control and we seek to educate students and the community at large about their rights to such decisions. We also work to counteract negative images of women and work against gender stereotyping. We believe in a progressive, informed, and open-minded community. We hope to accomplish these goals by rallying, supporting and educating one another in the Northwestern Community and also by creating a network with other college campuses nationwide.
summary: College Feminists is a premier feminist and pro-choice organization on the Evanston Northwestern campus. Email nufeminists@gmail.com to find out when we meet. New members are always welcome!

name: College Republicans
description: The Northwestern College Republicans is committed to upholding virtuous beliefs and values held by Americans that are currently silenced by the dominant campus culture. We host high-profile speakers to expound common sense opinions, participate in campus debates and activities to challenge the myth of leftist consensus, and provide an open forum where people of different political backgrounds may speak their minds and disagree without being disagreeable.
summary: NUCR is Northwestern's politically conservative and republican student organization. We host high-profile speakers, participate in campus debates, and provide a forum for political discussion that is open to diverging opinions.

name: Colloquium on Indigeneity and Native American Studies
description: The Colloquium on Indigeneity and Native American Studies is an interdisciplinary academic endeavor that aims to promote and stimulate conversations on indigeneity and indigenous populations in the Americas and the transnational imaginary at Northwestern University.
summary: The Colloquium on Indigeneity and Native American Studies is an  academic endeavor that aims to promote and stimulate conversations on indigeneity and indigenous populations in the Americas and the transnational imaginary at Northwestern.

name: ColorStack
description:
Frozen October 2024

ColorStack at NU is a supportive organization created to uplift and empower Black and Latinx students at Northwestern interested in technical careers and support them in pursuing their degrees. ColorStack is doing crucial work for creating a more diverse and inclusive tech workforce, addressing the skills gap, promoting economic empowerment, and driving innovation and creativity in the industry.
summary: ColorStack at Northwestern is a chapter of the ColorStack National non-profit organization. We are created to uplift and empower a community of Black and Latinx students at Northwestern interested in pursuing technical careers and opportunities.

name: Communications Residential College - East Fairchild
description: East Fairchild is a residential college south campus and houses 109 students in single- and double-occupancy rooms, arranged in an open suite-style layout. This building is available to all undergraduate students.
summary: East Fairchild is on south campus and houses 109 students in single- and double-occupancy rooms, arranged in an open suite-style layout. Men and women live in suites clustered by gender. This building is available to all undergraduate students.

name: Community Health Corps
description:
CHC recruits new members in the Fall and Winter. Find us at the fall org fair or Please e-mail nucommunityhealthcorps@gmail.com if you are interested in joining!

 

The Northwestern Community Health Corps (CHC) is a grass roots student run organization that aims to empower individuals to better manage their health in Evanston and Skokie area.

CHC members organize and lead a number of projects and initiatives aimed to increase access to accurate health information in Evanston and Skokie. We partner with community organizations such as Evanston Public Library and Erie Family Health Center. We aim to help community members navigate through their health related questions and connect individuals to existing health information and resources. In addition, through a unique partnership with the American Red Cross, CHC recruits and trains students to be trainers in CPR/First Aid/AED courses in order to provide these trainings free of charge to underserved populations in our community.
summary: Community Health Corps is a student group dedicated to improving health outcomes in the Evanston and Skokie area by increasing access to health information and resources.

name: Community Singers
description: Community Singers is a student-run community service show choir dedicated to raising awareness for important causes and bringing quality musical performances to locations around the community. This club is perfect for anyone who is looking to have a place to sing, regardless of experience level.
summary: Community Singers is a student-run, barrier-free show choir that is focused on making music, having fun, and doing good in the community.

name: Competition for Undergraduate Regenerative Engineering
description: We work in small, interdisciplinary teams to research new solutions to healthcare problems with the help of faculty and professionals from Northwestern and beyond. The teams compete to create the best solutions before presenting their results at a final symposium in the spring. CURE provides a collaborative, peer-led environment for students to get involved in cutting-edge research.
summary: CURE (Competition for Undergraduate Regenerative Engineering) is an engineering student research group designed to tackle medical challenges with regenerative engineering solutions. We use model wound systems and hands-on methods for applicable research.

name: Comunidad Latinx
description:
Frozen October 2024

The objects of this organization shall be to foster community oriented around Latinx identity, culture, and history; to provide a safe space for its members; to give back to the local Latinx community; and to educate the wider university community and public about Latinx affairs.
summary: Latinx Graduate Student Group

name: Concrete Canoe
description: Every year we build a canoe out of Concrete, defying the laws of common sense. If you like to see the full life of a project from conception to use, than this is the club for you! If you like to get your hands dirty and think of solutions on the fly, then you will have great time. You can be involved as much or as little as you want, and everyone is welcome.
summary: We build and race a Concrete Canoe!

name: Consult Your Community
description: Consult Your Community (CYC), a nonprofit organization dedicated to community empowerment, engages college students in quarter-long consulting projects that leverage their knowledge, digital expertise, mentorship connections, and consumer understanding. With a primary focus on empowering small business owners and driving positive community change, CYC provides strategic consulting services that foster a mutually beneficial partnership between academia and local businesses. This collaboration allows students to gain practical experience while contributing to the success and growth of small enterprises.
summary: Consult Your Community (CYC), a nonprofit organization dedicated to community empowerment, engages college students in semester-long consulting projects that leverage their knowledge, digital expertise, mentorship connections, and consumer understanding.

name: Consultants Advising Student Enterprises
description:
Started in the spring of 2008, CASE is a student organization at Northwestern University dedicated to providing pro-bono consulting advice to Northwestern-based organizations and beyond. We seek to harness our intellectual capital to help organizations become more efficient, active, and impactful.

The way we approach this mission is two-fold.

First, we provide measurable results to our clients. We stand behind our work 100%, and we follow through with our clients to ensure our recommendations are fully implemented.

Second, we provide the best possible learning experience for our consultants. Through ‘hands-on’ consulting experience, we develop our members to be well-rounded, analytical minds.

How to join:
CASE formally recruits twice per year (during fall and winter quarters). We will send out applications, after which there will be two rounds of interviews composed of both behavioral and consulting case style questions.
summary: Started in the spring of 2008, CASE is a student organization at Northwestern University dedicated to providing pro-bono consulting advice to Northwestern-based organizations and beyond.

name: Coptic Orthodox Christian Fellowship
description: The purpose of COCF is to create a vibrant community centered around faith, culture, and service. We aim to engage members in Bible study and discussions to deepen their spiritual understanding, while also sharing the unique cultural heritage and traditions of the Orthodox Church. Our goals include providing opportunities for service to the local community, embodying our faith through acts of love and charity. Additionally, we seek to be a welcoming space for newcomers to explore and learn about Christianity, inviting them to experience the beauty of the Orthodox faith and its teachings.
summary: We engage members in Bible study and discussions to deepen their spiritual understanding, while also sharing the unique cultural heritage and traditions of the Orthodox Church.

name: Cosmia Opera Collective
description:
Cosmia Opera Collective is an undergraduate student-run collective providing resources for the funding, rehearsal, and performance of new operatic works by historically-marginalized (female/gender non-conforming, POC, and LGBTQ+) composers at Northwestern University.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AN8MNeRnBP8
summary: Cosmia Opera Collective is an undergraduate student-run collective providing resources for the funding, rehearsal, and performance of new operatic works by historically-marginalized (female/gender non-conforming, POC, and LGBTQ+) composers at NU.

name: CPS Financial Literacy Outreach
description: Chicago Public Schools Financial Literacy Outreach (CPS FLO) is a personal finance program for minority-dominant, low-income, and limited education Chicago neighborhoods. In targeting Education and Economic Stability social determinants of health, we intend to improve health outcomes for these vulnerable populations. Students will lead 30-60 minute biweekly Zoom sessions with our partnered high schools. Sessions are divided equally between students presenting material and hosting Kahoot questions to the high schoolers. Upperclassmen are invited to train a small team of students through regular prep meetings prior to the biweekly sessions. All positions provide students with opportunities for growth in leadership, productive client interactions, and application of previous course material, furthering students' academic and professional skills. CPS FLO hosts an end-of-year field day on Northwestern’s campus to introduce interested high school students to college life. Events include: Northwestern Overview Tour of Northwestern’s campus Lunch (with catering from local businesses) Mini-lecture from Northwestern faculty Panel discussion with Northwestern students to discuss college applications, academics, and social strategies.
summary: Chicago Public Schools Financial Literacy Outreach (CPS FLO) is a personal finance program for minority-dominant, low-income, and limited education Chicago neighborhoods.

name: Crescendo for a Cause
description:
The Northwestern chapter of Crescendo for a Cause (C4C), an officially registered 501(c)(3) non-profit and Presidential Volunteer Service Award (PVSA) certifying organization, is dedicated to creating music together and sharing the joy of music with the Evanston community.

C4C brings student musicians together to perform uplifting, therapeutic music for residents and patients in local nursing homes and hospitals. Any instrument, from sax to voice, is welcome, and members can play their favorite pieces in small ensembles or solo. We also use our musical talents for fundraising concerts. Additionally, C4C aims to eliminate barriers to music education for students in Chicagoland public schools through our mentorship program. Our mentors foster these students’ interests in music by providing free music lessons and assisting in elementary music classes.

We are open to any NU student that is interested in music and service regardless of music experience and can't wait to get to know you!
summary: We are the Northwestern chapter of Crescendo for a Cause, and we are dedicated to increase access to music education and uplift our community through volunteer music performances in local nursing homes, hospitals, schools, and businesses.

name: Crochet for a Cause
description: Crochet for a Cause was established to help support women in need across the Evanston and Chicago area. Because so many students at Northwestern enjoy crochet, we hope to foster a connection between like-minded students who want to use their skills to give back to the community. We are planning on partnering with a broad range of shelters to help as many women as possible. Students with any crochet experience are welcome and will be supported in learning how to create goods.
summary: Crochet for a Cause provides students with a fun environment to learn or hone their crochet skills. Our mission is to donate the scarves, hats, and other items we make to women's shelters in the Evanston and Chicago area.

name: Crossword Team
description: Northwestern Crossword Team (NUXW) is the premier crossword team on campus. We are a co-ed team equally focused on improving our crossword skills and building a friendly, supporting community on campus. We work to facilitate a welcoming environment for students while also completing crosswords together. Meetings take place Monday nights, and application opportunities will be available at the beginning of each quarter. Students of all crossword abilities are welcome to apply!
summary: NUXW is a co-ed team equally focused on improving our crossword skills and building a friendly, supporting community on campus.

name: Cru
description:
What is Cru about?

 

Real faith. Real relationships. Real life.

 

Northwestern Cru is a community where the gospel captures hearts, transforms lives, impacts the world and launches men and women into a lifelong adventure with Jesus Christ. We’re glad you’re here. In short, we are about students becoming world changers. God has you in one of the most exciting places of your life—the campus. Unlike any other place (or likely any other time in your life) the world is literally all around you. Take a look around you, I’m willing to bet that within a few minutes of where you’re sitting right now you can meet someone from a different country, a different culture, and a different family setting than your own. And you may not be able to see it right now, but within a number of years these very people that you are sitting around in class and living next to right now in your dorm, house or apartment building will be people who give shape to the culture, the community, the nation and the world! The campus is a pretty amazing place!

 

What’s more, amidst this amazing setting we see that God is doing something big on the campus. Have you ever been confused about God? Have you ever felt disconnected from Him? Have you ever wondered if he was really real at all? Or maybe you believe that He is there, but you just don’t feel close to Him or you are trying to figure out this faith thing. Well, our experience tells us that these are common questions for today’s generation—and for those who wonder, more are discovering something real and fresh in a personal relationship with God! In fact if you’ve ever wondered some of these things,take a few minutes now and email one of us. We may not have all the answers but would love to hear your story.

 

Cru is a movement of people across the globe that are growing in and helping others grow in a personal relationship with God–one filled with increasing purpose, hope, honesty, & intimacy. So why did you come to college? What will you leave with? What’s your purpose in life? We hope that you’ll find us, find God, and find the adventure that is waiting for you!

 

If you are curious about this relationship with God, or want to learn more about Christianity a great resource is Knowing God Personally from EveryStudent.com
summary:
What is Cru about?
Real faith. Real relationships. Real life.
Northwestern Cru is a community where the gospel captures hearts, transforms lives, impacts the world and launches men and women into a lifelong adventure with Jesus Christ.

name: CRUSH Magazine
description: CRUSH Magazine is Northwestern's queer-interest publication, providing an inclusive space for LGBTQ+ identifying students to share their creative voices and visions. In our quarterly issues, we publish news, essays, op-eds, creative writing, art, and everything in between.
summary: CRUSH Magazine is Northwestern's queer-interest publication, providing a inclusive space for LGBTQ+ identifying students to share their creative voices and visions.

name: CTF Team
description:
The CTF Team aims to foster a healthy and active ecosystem in Northwestern and Chicago, and solidify our community on the map as a competitive organization for CTF challenges. We work towards this vision by doing the following:

  • Provide a gateway for interested students to learn about different cybersecurity skills related to CTF challenges
  • Connect students with professors to engage students in cybersecurity related research
  • Help our students with networking and career opportunities in the cybersecurity industry 
  • Collaborate with enterprises to empower our ecosystem
summary: CTFs (capture-the-flag) are widely recognized as one of the best ways to train and evaluate cybersecurity skills. Our ultimate goal is to give Northwestern students the opportunity to compete in and join the CTF community.

name: Cubing Club
description:
We aim to teach Northwestern students how to solve the world-famous Rubik’s Cube and other twisty puzzles. At club meetings, members will learn new solution methods, improve their solving speed, and complete challenges such as one-handed and blindfolded solving. Additionally, we plan to organize cubing competitions which will be officially sanctioned by the World Cube Association.

Join our GroupMe for meeting details and club updates!

https://groupme.com/join_group/105905026/JQWJprvO
summary: The cubing club will provide for the intellectual advancement of Northwestern students through the activity of puzzle solving. The club will provide an all-inclusive atmosphere that allows students to socialize and exchange ideas related to speedsolving

name: Dale Duro Latin Dance Company
description: Dale Duro Latin Dance Co. (DDLDC), Northwestern’s Latin dance team, is an inclusive dance group started in 2018 composed of Latin culture appreciators of all backgrounds and skill levels. We teach free workshops of varied Latin dance styles throughout each quarter, host community bonding events like karaoke nights and movie nights, and perform in our BIG annual spring show where we showcase our talents in salsa, bachata, merengue, flamenco, cumbia, reggaeton and more. We hope to continue fostering a supportive environment where we can all grow as Latin dancers, all while enriching and strengthening our perspective on Latin culture.
summary: Dale Duro is looking to create an inclusive community for all students searching to enrich their appreciation for Latin culture through dance.

name: Data Journalism Collective
description: DJC is a space for students interested in data-driven storytelling, coding, and writing to learn from and with each other. We educate students about data journalism, organize data journalism projects, and foster an academic and professional community. DJC is open to all, regardless of prior experience or major!
summary: DJC is a space for students interested in data-driven storytelling, coding, and writing to learn from and with each other.

name: Data Science and Analytics Club
description: Bringing students together to explore real-world datasets related to finance, healthcare, sustainability, and more, learn industry-standard tools, and collaborate on impactful projects while building a supportive community for both beginner and advanced data scientists.
summary: Bringing together students to explore real-world datasets related to finance, healthcare, sustainability, and more.

name: Deeva
description:
Deeva is a nationally-recognized, all-female competitive dance team that blends the best of Indian classical, Bollywood, folk, contemporary, bhangra, and hip-hop. Founded in 2001, the team has a unique mission: to generate and participate in an intercultural dialogue through dance. Each dancer brings a unique background from diverse regions and a knowledge of a variety of dance styles from all over the world. No experience is necessary to try out! By choreographing dances that mix cultures, Deeva is excited to not only bring increased cultural awareness to the student body, but to have a cultural exchange through dance. Deeva is also apart of the Desi Dance Network and is actively competing in this circuit. 

Deeva's Advisor is Dr. Sarah McFarland Taylor from the Religious Studies Department.
summary: Deeva is a nationally-recognized, all-female competitive dance team that blends the best of Indian classical, Bollywood, folk, ballet, lyrical, and hip-hop, founded in 2001.

description:

Organization Facts and Awards

name: Delta Sigma Pi
description:
Delta Sigma Pi is Northwestern's premier co-ed business fraternity. Our activities focus on three aspects: Professional, Social, and Community Service.

 

Through Delta Sigma Pi, you will develop lasting relationships with brothers who share a common passion for business in diverse fields and industries - from consulting and finance to technology, startups, and social impact organizations. Our members form a community and support each other in professional and academic endeavors.  We hold several social events per quarter and perform community service in the Evanston/Chicagoland area.


We develop our members professionally through a wide range of activities and career related workshops.  Past events have included consulting panels, Northwestern-wide stock competitions, prestigious guest speakers, site visits, and courses on resume building, interviewing, and networking. We also provide opportunities for leadership positions and a connection to the vast alumni base of the national organization of over 295,000 initiated members.  

We recruit in the Fall and Winter - please see our website for recruitment events and deadlines.
summary: As the world’s leading professional fraternity for men and women, Delta Sigma Pi develops principled business leaders for the future by providing a lifetime of opportunity for members.

name: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc
description:
Theta Alpha Chapter, Northwestern University

Installed January 9, 1971

"Intelligence is the Torch of Wisdom."

Organization Facts

National founding date: January 13, 1913

Colors: Crimson, Cream

Nickname: Deltas

You may view the Delta Sigma Theta national website at http://www.deltasigmatheta.org.
summary: The Theta Alpha chapter has been serving the Northwestern Community since January 9, 1971. Since that date, women of the Theta Alpha chapter have taken a vow to serve their Northwestern community using the same founding principles of the Sorority.

name: Delta Tau Delta
summary: TBD

name: Design for America
description: Design for America (DFA) is an award-winning nationwide network of interdisciplinary student teams and community members using design to create local and social impact. DFA teaches human-centered design to young adults and collaborating community partners through extracurricular, university based, student-led design studios to look locally, create fervently and act fearlessly. DFA currently tackles national challenges in education, health, economy and environment. We look forward to welcoming you all!
summary: Design for America is a student network of community-focused innovators. At NU, we strive to create local and social impact through human-centered design. The studio encourages the innovation of meaningful solutions with diverse and unique perspectives.

name: Destino
description:
Destino is an ethnic ministry at Northwestern designed to be a safe space for Latinx-identifying students to continue or initiate a relationship with Jesus Christ. We explore Latinx Northwestern students' ethnic identity in the context of Christianity and want to build up Latinx leaders. We are a welcoming and loving community who seeks to guide Latinx students in their spiritual walk, while also helping students to create friendships and resources that enhance their experience and life at Northwestern University. 

Get in contact: Destino Interest Form

To join GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/38734694/15POYe
summary: A spiritual resource for Latinx-identifying Northwestern students seeking to know God and develop or enhance their relationship with Jesus.

name: Develop + Innovate for Social Change
description: Through hands-on projects and mentorship, we empower students to build software for social good. Our teams work directly with community partners, gaining real-world experience while making a meaningful difference. Having grown to over 300 members, we're proud to foster an environment where technical innovation meets social responsibility, creating technology solutions that serve our community's needs. DISC has also become a stepping stone for many of our members towards successful careers in the technology industry, with alumni working at leading tech companies and startups. This organization is open to all enrolled Northwestern students. See what we're up to here: www.discnu.org
summary: DISC empowers students to build software for social good.

name: Diaspora Writers Society
description: The Diaspora Writers Society is a space for creative writers on campus to participate in peer-led writing workshops focused on diaspora writing, run book club discussions about diaspora/ethnic literature, and network with established diaspora writers. As an organization, our main initiative is to facilitate connections between writers. The Diaspora Writers Society has a unique focus on cultural diversity and multicultural storytelling but is not limited to a specific ethnic group or identity. Our primary goal is to provide writers with the opportunity to meet other diaspora writers and gain insight into the publishing industry as it relates to diaspora literature. We will ensure that all students feel welcome in this space and hope to have a diverse community of writers/readers join.
summary: The Diaspora Writers Society is a space for creative writers on campus to participate in peer-led writing workshops focused on diaspora writing, run book club discussions about diaspora/ethnic literature, and network with established diaspora writers.

name: Disabled Student Union
description: The student-led DSU provides three main opportunities for students with disabilities: A community space to meet other disabled students through fun events and casual hangouts; Peer support to help you request ANU accommodations, seek local healthcare options, and understand insurance; Opportunities to connect with disability-oriented career-advancement services such as NCA, LimeConnect, DisabilityIN, and federal jobs.
summary: A community space to meet other disabled students through fun events and casual hangouts.

name: Diverse Abilities in Business
description: The primary focus of Diverse Abilities in Business (DAB) is to create and foster a community for neurodivergent and disabled students in business at Northwestern. Alongside this community aspect, DAB will hold informational sessions about current economic trends, employers in financial fields, networking events, and guides to recruitment in various financial industries. Furthermore, DAB will hold a speaker series from neurodivergent and disabled professionals in business-related fields to expose members to prominent figures in business who have overcome challenges related to identifying as neurodivergent and/or disabled. Finally, DAB seeks to increase awareness and support neurodivergent and disabled students in business as well as educate allies about how to advocate for their neurodivergent and disabled peers and thrive in business upon graduation from Northwestern.
summary: The primary focus of Diverse Abilities in Business (DAB) is to create and foster a community for neurodivergent and disabled students in business at Northwestern.

name: Driskill Graduate Student Council
summary: The DGP Student Council brings the concerns of students to the DGP administration and fills programming gaps in the areas of mentoring, social and research activities.

name: Each One Teach One
description: Each One Teach One (EOTO) is a nonprofit organization committed to supporting underprivileged and orphaned children by providing them with access to essential resources such as healthcare, housing, education, nutrition, and supplemental income. Our purpose is to break the cycle of poverty by addressing the foundational needs that allow children to survive, thrive, and pursue their dreams. We work both locally and internationally to ensure that children receive customized support tailored to their unique circumstances. A key part of our mission is also to educate and empower students by involving them directly in this work—raising awareness about global inequality, fostering a sense of social responsibility, and giving them the opportunity to engage in hands-on service. Our goals include delivering direct financial and material aid, building long-term relationships with the communities we serve, and operating with transparency so that the majority of every donation directly benefits a child. We partner with local organizations and conduct in-depth interviews with each child to understand their needs and provide the tools necessary for long-term success. At EOTO, we believe that a small group of dedicated individuals can make a lasting impact.
summary: Each One Teach One (EOTO) is a nonprofit organization committed to supporting underprivileged and orphaned children by providing them with access to essential resources such as healthcare, housing, education, nutrition, and supplemental income.

name: East African Alliance
description: The East African Alliance is a student-led organization dedicated to celebrating and uplifting East African heritage, culture, and perspectives. We provide a space for students of East African descent and those interested in the region to build connections, share experiences, and deepen their understanding of East Africa's rich histories and traditions. Through cultural events, educational discussions, mentorship opportunities, and advocacy initiatives, we aim to foster community, promote allyship, and create meaningful engagement with East African voices on campus and beyond.
summary: The East African Alliance is a student-led organization dedicated to celebrating and uplifting East African heritage, culture, and perspectives.

name: EEqual
description: The EEqual chapter is part of a network of chapters across the nation who are committed to the unified mission of combating student homelessness and increasing the accessibility of higher education through mentorship, acts of community service and education awareness campaigns. We intend to increase the Northwestern undergraduate student engagement in the lives of youth facing homelessness through education and local advocacy in the Evanston community. As a student organization, we will provide opportunities to inform our undergraduate community on the intersection of student homelessness and higher education, while addressing the stigma surrounding the state of experiencing homelessness. We additionally hope to leverage our specific age group to connect with individuals to offer direct peer-to-peer guidance to students experiencing poverty and homelessness at the institutional level. Through connections with local organizations and resources, our organization will provide the opportunity to take direct action in the Evanston community to make a tangible difference in the intersection of the issue of student homelessness and higher education. We believe that establishing an organization like EEqual to increase access to higher education in students experiencing homelessness will introduce new perspectives and formulate impactful and meaningful discussions among our undergraduate population with the intent and aim of translating to direct action.
summary: The EEqual chapter is part of a network committed to the unified mission of combating student homelessness and increasing the accessibility of higher education through mentorship, acts of community service and education awareness campaigns.

name: Effective Altruism Club
description:
Join our listserv here: 

https://bit.ly/eanu-signup

 

Check out our website for ways to get involved:

-Website

 

 

Are you a Northwestern undergrad hoping to learn about the most productive ways to combat the world's most pressing issues? Join Northwestern Effective Altruism Club!

From engaging with researchers focused on increasing efficiency of poverty alleviation, to interacting with professionals who work high-paying jobs to increase donation potential, to discussing high-impact careers with other students, Northwestern EA nurtures a community of students who aim to promote positive impact effectively.

 

Feel free to reach out to us with any questions at effectivealtruism@u.northwestern.edu!
summary: Effective altruism is a philosophical and social movement that uses evidence and reasoning to determine the most effective ways to do good.

name: EighthDay Theatre Club
summary: Eigthday Theatre is established for the purpose of promoting Chinese theatre culture at Northwestern, providing a safe space for students from all backgrounds who are interested in Chinese theatre to discuss and engage with Chinese theatre production.

name: ELEL
description: Elel is an Ethiopian and Eritrean dance club that aims to spread cultural awareness on Ethiopian and Eritrean cultures through dance.
summary: Elel is an Ethiopian and Eritrean dance club that aims to spread cultural awareness on Ethiopian and Eritrean cultures through dance.

name: Emerging Coders
description:
1. To provide mentorship and support for underrepresented groups within Computer Science.

2. To provide pathways for students to develop their skills in technology.

3. To promote equality and a social environment where each other can learn from one another
summary: Community for FGLI (First-Gen Low-income) students who are interested in Tech!

name: Engage RP
description: Engage RP connects Northwestern students with volunteer opportunities in the Rogers Park neighborhood of Chicago.
summary: Engage RP connects Northwestern students with volunteer opportunities in the Rogers Park neighborhood of Chicago.

name: Engineers for a Sustainable World
description:
Engineers for a Sustainable World at Northwestern (ESWNU) is an action-based group, mobilizing students through education, training, and hands-on projects related to environmental sustainability. Membership is not limited to engineers, but rather includes students from all schools at Northwestern, and entails working in student teams with faculty and professional advisers to further sustainability both locally and internationally. Our projects range in focus from renewable energy to urban agriculture, environmental technology, and much more. Check out our project blogs, website, and join our Slack channel here. For more information, please contact us at esw@u.northwestern.edu.

*NOTE: Wildcat Connection membership is for existing members only, please join our Slack channel or send us an email if you would like to join ESWNU

https://youtu.be/Aae99SjBvGE
summary: ESW empowers students to take on leadership roles in applying the principles of sustainability to their present and future work through action-based engineering projects and the education of ourselves and our community on sustainability topics.

name: Engineers Without Borders
description:
Frozen October 2024

Engineers Without Borders-Northwestern works cooperatively with international communities to add engineering expertise to community-driven projects that address the community's most prevalent problems. Members of EWB-NU work throughout the academic year to brainstorm, research, survey, design, and prototype solutions to these problems, while working closely with a group of professional engineering mentors, an NGO, and most importantly, the partner community. These designs range from household necessities, to sanitation needs, to civil works, and beyond. Approximately once a year, EWB-NU sends a group of students and mentors to the community we work with to conduct further research and/or implement a phase of the project while building relationships with the members of the community.

EWB-NU also engages with the Northwestern, Evanston, and Chicago communities in to promote sustainability, engineering education, and stronger bonds between Northwestern and our surrounding communities. We do this through regular on-campus events, mentorship of local Chicago middle schools, and our developing service projects. We are always looking to increase the outreach that we do, so if you have ideas or a connection you would like to make, please reach out!

EWB-NU welcomes students of all academic backgrounds who are interested working toward making a positive impact on the world. Students gain experience in project management and leadership, design, teamwork, cross-cultural communication, fundraising, and interdisciplinary problem solving.
summary: The mission of EWB-NU is to engineer and implement solutions that enrich the lives of communities and meet basic human needs through the completion of educational and collaborative projects — both locally and abroad.

name: Environmental Engineering Undergraduate Society
description: The Environmental Engineering Undergraduate Society (EnvEUS) at Northwestern gives students the opportunity to meet other Environmental Engineering students, participate in career development events, and apply technical skills outside of the classroom.
summary: EnvEUS fosters community within the Environmental Engineering department at Northwestern.

name: EPIC
description:
Sign up for EPIC's email list here: https://forms.gle/YX18PND4cm6SPEhj6

Our Instagram 

 

EPIC is the premier entrepreneurial student org that aims to connect aspiring entrepreneurs with each other and with different entrepreneurial opportunities on/off campus. EPIC not only plans and creates amazing events and programs but also has internal teams that work on entrepreneurship focused projects and exposes students to the world of startups. Read below for brief descriptions on each team and/or program: 

General Membership: General membership is an opportunity for students to try out entrepreneurship in an open and supportive environment. They get exclusive access to EPIC events ranging from kick-ass speaker panels, design pop ups, product demos, and more. All internal and external EPIC members are also General Members.

 

Launch: Launch is a quarter-long student-led incubator program where you will learn everything from ideation to execution and turn napkin sketches into real, scalable businesses. Learn the ins and outs of an entrepreneurial mindset, then embark on an entrepreneurial journey (often for the first time) through an action-driven curriculum. Participants will form teams to start new companies that they build throughout the quarter, culminating in a Demo Day.  In the last 2 years we’ve helped 100+ students start over 25 new companies; you could be next.

 

Sprout: A week-long trip to the Bay Area with Workshops, Speakers, Site Visits, Networking, Startup Challenge, Food, Fun over Spring Break.
summary: EPIC is Northwestern's interdisciplinary hub for entrepreneurship. From networking with alums and startup-building workshops, to our annual trip to Silicon Valley, to our accelerator, EPIC is the great starting place for entrepreneurship at Northwestern.

name: Esports Club
description:
This club is dedicated to providing a forum for the Northwestern community to discuss, practice, and compete in video gaming. We currently field teams for Overwatch 2, VALORANT, League of Legends, Super Smash Bros., Rocket League, Apex Legends, osu!, and CS 2, and our Discord server and general meetings serve to foster discussion of video games and develop connections amongst the esports community.

Join our Discord: https://discord.gg/NUEsports
summary: This club is dedicated to providing a forum for the Northwestern community to discuss, practice, and compete in video gaming. We currently field teams in 8 competitive video game titles and foster community in many others.

name: Essence: For Us, By Us
description: Essence was established for the purpose of creating a community among Black Women identifying students, while fostering an environment of determination, self love, and accomplishment. Essence values the thoughts and desires of Black Women identifying students attending a Predominantly White Institution. With collaboration with each other, we strive to create a more inclusive and comfortable learning experience for Black Women identifying students at Northwestern University.
summary: Essence will be established for the purpose of creating a community among Black Women identifying students, while fostering an environment of determination, self love, and accomplishment.

name: Ethics Bowl
description: Ethics Bowl strives to spark interest in and awareness of societal issues involving ethics at Northwestern by providing a space for students to collectively learn and have dialogues about ethics as they prepare to compete in the Intercollegiate Ethics Bowl competitions.
summary: Ethics Bowl strives to spark interest in and awareness of societal issues involving ethics on campus.

name: Ethiopian and Eritrean Student Association
description: Northwestern EESA is a community organization and affinity space for Ethiopian and Eritrean students on campus, as well as anyone else who is interested in engaging with the culture!
summary: Ethiopian and Eritrean Student Association

name: Ethnic Studies Graduate Student Colloquium
description:
Frozen October 2024

The Northwestern Ethnic Studies Graduate Student Colloquium (ESG) began in 2010 as an informal group of graduate students who were studying different disciplines, but doing similar work in the field of ethnic studies, an interdisciplinary field “that starts from the assumption that race and racism have been, and continue to be, profoundly powerful social and cultural forces in American society and in modernity at large” (as described by the Ethnic Studies Department at UC Berkeley). Although students were in programs as varied as African Studies, African-American Studies, American Studies, Anthropology, Asian-American Studies, Communication Studies, History, Musicology, Performance Studies, Religion, Rhetoric, and Sociology, they were all connected by an interest in similar authors, theories, and methodologies.

The ESG was created to provide an intellectual and social home for interested graduate students. Our programming activities, such as our quarterly potlucks and end-of-the-year workshop, are geared toward helping students achieve personal and professional goals as it relates to their work as Scholars of Color and scholars working in ethnic studies.
summary: The Northwestern Ethnic Studies Graduate Student Colloquium (NU ESG) provides an intellectual and social home for graduate students doing work in the field of ethnic studies.

name: Extreme Measures
description:
Extreme Measures is a student-directed co-ed a cappella group, with nineteen diverse members. Majors and interests range from Theatre to Neuroscience, from community service to creative writing. In a very short time, the group has made incredible strides, performing shows every quarter, recording three studio-produced albums, and performing at events around Chicago! Our latest album, Electric Blue is on iTunes and Spotify, and a new album is in the works this school year!

First and foremost, EM is a group of friends who happen to make music together! We take great pride in our musical talents, and in our ability to have fun with performing and rehearsing. In addition to producing its own shows, EM performs at charity and fundraising events on campus and in the greater Chicago area.  We have even traveled as far as Montreal to sing and tour around!

EM owes all of its success to the dedication of its members; group members arrange all of the music we perform and serve on the executive board to manage finances and show production. Our repertoire is incredibly diverse, and includes artists ranging from Billy Joel to Rihanna to Lady Gaga.

Our goal is to continue growing and maturing as much as we have over the past couple of years, and we hope to see you at our next show and at auditions in the fall!
summary: Extreme Measures is a co-ed a cappella group founded in 2007. In addition to producing its own shows and performing around campus and Chicago, EM has recorded three full studio albums, all available on iTunes and Spotify!

name: Faith-Hope-Love Fellowship
description:
We are a community for Chinese-speaking students who follow Jesus Christ as Savior and/or who are interested in studying the Bible. We hold weekly fellowships during which we worship God, read the Bible, and pray together. Every year, we also hold special events to celebrate Chinese New Year, to explore nature, to welcome new students, and to serve the neighborhood. 

Fellowship Location: Tech (2145 Sheridan Rd), L361

Time: Every Friday 6:30pm - 9:00pm (dinner provided)

Subscribe to our monthly newsletter here!
summary: We are a community of Chinese-speaking students who follow Jesus Christ as Savior and/or who are interested in getting familiar with the Bible in order to witness the gospel of Jesus Christ.

name: Fencing
description:
Northwestern's Club Fencing Team is a recreational team open to both experienced and beginning fencers. We have a travel team open to all who are interested, that competes intercollegiately. We have a top notch coaching staff and equipment will be provided to those who need it. Currently, all of our practice take place in Patten Gymnasium. Our Practice schedule is as follows:

Tuesday 6:30pm-8:30pm Whole Team Footwork + Practice

Thursday 6:30pm-8:30pm Whole Team Footwork + Practice

Our instagram is @nuclubfencing. Contact alisha.shah@northwestern.edu or heatherhumbert2023@u.northwestern.edu with questions.
summary: Northwestern's Club Fencing Team is an intercollegiate competitive team open to both experienced and beginning fencers. We have a top notch coaching staff and equipment will be provided to those who need it. Practice is held in Patten Gym.

name: Fiedler Hillel Student Life
description: Welcome to Northwestern Hillel, the foundation for Jewish life on campus, where Jewish life on campus is vibrant, engaging and exciting. Hillel encompasses a vast number of student groups and initiatives, including Wildcats for Israel, JStreet, First Year Student at Hillel (FYSH) and the Mega Shabbat Committee. The Speakers Committee has helped bring entertaining speakers such as Sarah Silverman and Andy Samberg to campus. In addition, everyone is always welcome for services and free Friday Night Dinner and meals and services during the Holidays. Hillel also transports students around the world through trips to Israel on Birthright and ASB trips with destinations that have included Poland, Morocco, and Brazil. Visit our website at www.northwesternhillel.org.
summary: Welcome to Fiedler Hillel at Northwestern University, the foundation for Jewish life on campus, where Jewish life on campus is vibrant, engaging and exciting. Hillel encompasses a vast number of student groups and initiatives.

name: Financial Technologies Club
description: NU Financial Technologies Club explores the realms of business software, quantitative finance, and general fintech through speakers, simulating projects, and workshops. At present NUFT is developing a simulation hedge fund on an entirely in-house platform, as well as a variety of other initiatives to drive fintech education at Northwestern.
summary: NUFT provides pre-professional opportunities, hands-on collaborative projects, and general information to members of the Northwestern community interested in the Financial Technologies Space.

name: For Members Only
description:
For Members Only (FMO) serves as Northwestern University's premier Black Student Alliance. FMO serves to encourage political, social, cultural and intellectual unity and growth first and foremost within Northwestern University's Black community, but also within the broader Northwestern community as well. FMO is the representative and political voice of Northwestern University's Black undergraduate student body.

 

FMO sponsors a Freshman Mentoring Program, hosts a Spring Concert, Fall Speaker, and various other cultural and community-building events throughout the year! Come out and join us for General Meetings, events, and time at the Black House (1914 Sheridan Road).
summary: For Members Only (FMO) serves as Northwestern University's premier Black Student Union. FMO serves to encourage political, social, cultural and intellectual unity and growth first and foremost within Northwestern University's Black community.

name: Form & Function Marketing
description:
Form & Function, Northwestern University’s first student-run marketing agency, was founded in 2010 to support students’ marketing and advertising education in the absence of an official undergraduate marketing major. F&F gives students the opportunity to gain experience in a variety of marketing functions, building their strategic and creative skill sets, by working with clients in the Chicagoland area and beyond.

Our mission is twofold: to create professional campaigns that drive business impact for our clients and to provide Northwestern students with an applied marketing education and community.
summary: F&F Marketing is Northwestern University's first student-run marketing agency, providing all of the services and resources of a modern marketing agency to businesses in the Evanston/Chicago area and giving students valuable marketing work experience.

name: Freshman Fifteen
description: Northwestern’s premier all-bowling A Cappella group, Freshman Fifteen, is 25 years old and still growing!  Officially founded in 1998, the group does not consist of 15 members nor are they all Freshmen. And yet, the name struck a cord with the founders back in ’98 and has stuck ever since.  Around campus, the group is known for its energy and enthusiasm, as well as each individual member sporting a green bowling shirt with their own personal nickname embroidered on the lefthand pocket. Each member is dubbed a nickname during their first year in the group when they say something “stupid and memorable.” Nicknames range anywhere from “Hip-hoppopotamus” to “Neil Armstrong” to “Underpants.”  Freshman Fifteen has 7 albums; their latest, ILU, came out in 2018. Check them out on Apple Music or Spotify! They perform all over Northwestern's campus and the world, including as the entertainment for Universal Studios’ Holiday Festival in Florida, and in recent years in Punta Cana over winter break!
summary: Northwestern's premier all-bowling a cappella group that isn't all freshman and does not always have 15 people.

name: Freshman Musical
description:
The Freshman Musical works to provide barrier-free opportunities for any first-year student, with or without prior experience, to learn about the magic and power of theater. Through a production created entirely by the freshman class, we welcome and encourage students to explore their creative interests in any part of the production process. Our show aims to strengthen and celebrate the freshman community as well as the presence of performing arts on campus.

Freshman Musical is open to all Northwestern undergraduate students.
summary: As the musical production entirely produced, created, and performed by the freshman class, we work to provide barrier-free opportunities for any first-year student, with or without prior experience, to learn about the magic and power of theatre.

name: Friends of MSF
description: Friends of MSF (FoMSF) is a nationwide organization created by Médecins sans Frontières or Doctors without Borders that seeks to promote awareness of global health crises and medical humanitarianism. Our chapter's purpose is to get the Northwestern community engaged with MSF’s work and to promote its campaigns. We have 4 main goals: 1. Raise awareness and knowledge about MSF’s work in the field and about humanitarian issues 2. Encourage students to consider working with non-governmental organizations, such as MSF 3. Support MSF in advocacy campaigns and raise money through fundraisers 4. Invite speakers including physicians who have worked for MSF or other NGOs, human rights law specialists to share their experiences with global health crisis work. Anyone is welcome to join and participate regardless of major or global health experiences.
summary: Friends of MSF (FoMSF) is a nationwide organization created by Médecins sans Frontières or Doctors without Borders that seeks to promote awareness of global health crises and medical humanitarianism.

name: Fusion Dance Company
description:
Fusion Dance Company is a collegiate dance company at Northwestern University founded in 2003. Building on our common passion for dance and our diverse backgrounds, our members come together to grow as artists, a company, and a tight-knit family. Our choreography pulls from many styles and our goal is to foster community on campus while also participating in the Chicago dance scene. We dance for the love it spreads and we are so excited to share that with you!

 

Throughout the year, we perform at events like ReFusionShaka, our own Spring show, and various gigs. Fusion has our sights set on competing in the near future, having been accepted to Prelude Midwest 2020 (cancelled due to Covid). We take inspiration from many styles of dance in our choreography, including but not limited to hip-hop, popping, waacking, jazz, and contemporary.

 

If you have any questions please contact us through fusiondancecompany@u.northwestern.edu, message us on Facebook (Fusion Dance Company - Northwestern) or Instagram (@fusiondanceco_nu).

https://youtu.be/gfWnTKjzrnc
summary: Founded in 2003, Fusion Dance Company is a collegiate dance company composed of around 40 dancers, all of whom come from a variety of dance backgrounds and life experiences. We dance for the love it spreads and we are so excited to share that with you!

name: FutureDev
description: FutureDev's mission is to enhance the practical skill set of students in the technology sector and help prepare them for entry into the industry. By providing a collaborative learning environment for students interested in expanding their skills in programming languages, frameworks, and several other niches within the field of computer science, we hope to make learning in technology and computer science more accessible to Northwestern students.
