Under the guidance of experienced mentors, students will be able to learn alongside each other in areas of their interest, such as web development and cybersecurity, allowing them to deepen their expertise in these domains and excel in meeting the dynamic demands of the industry.
summary: FutureDev's mission is to enhance the practical skill set of students in the technology sector and help prepare them for entry into the industry.

name: Game Makers Society
description:
Anyone who wants to make games, should try to make games

[JOIN OUR DISCORD] --> here

 

Contact at colinschilf2025@u.northwestern.edu, or @cjschilf & @Btmodz on Discord
summary: The Game Makers Society is committed to making game development accessible to all students, recognizing the complexity of creating games and the continuous growth required to succeed.

name: Genetic Counseling Club
description: With the support of Northwestern University’s Graduate Program in Genetic Counseling (GPGC), this club aims to provide information and opportunities related to the field of genetic counseling for those who are just starting to explore and are interested in it as a potential career. As our club emphasizes exposure to the field, we plan on welcoming genetic counselors, current students, and genetic counseling assistants as guest speakers to share their paths and experiences within the field. Additionally, we plan to take advantage of our close relationship with Northwestern University’s GPGC, by having trips to Chicago for an open house, sit in on a journal club with current graduate students, and observe a portion of their Research Colloquium in March. We would also like to foster success with application workshops and celebrate the field by hosting DNA Day activities in April. Our organization will meet once a month during the academic calendar. 3 of these meetings will take place on the Chicago campus.
summary: This club aims to provide information and opportunities related to the field of genetic counseling for those who are just starting to explore and are interested in it as a potential career.

name: Get-A-Grip
description:
Frozen October 2024

Get-A-Grip is a project-based program that challenges middle school students to design and construct a prosthetic arm using low-cost, readily-available materials.
summary: Get-A-Grip is a project-based program that challenges middle school students to design and construct a prosthetic arm using low-cost, readily-available materials.

name: GH Foundation on Campus
description: GH Foundation on Campus strives to destigmatize gastro-health conversations among a younger group of individuals– college students. As a club, we will be working closely with the GH Foundation and striving to advocate and educate students about the importance of having these uncomfortable conversations. Being aware and having knowledge about your digestive system can help prevent future serious health consequences. We want to empower individuals to become their own health advocates and break the stigma around this topic. By breaking the silence and normalizing conversations about poop, colonoscopies, and everything in between, we can help individuals become proactive about their health and detect potential problems early on. We plan to attract members who are passionate about our cause and help spread awareness. We want to encourage others to start those uncomfortable conversations so that their families and communities can become their own best health advocates. GH Foundation on Campus will be providing volunteering and other educational opportunities for our members to become involved in.
summary: GH Foundation on Campus strives to destigmatize gastro-health conversations among a younger group of individuals.

name: Girl Gains
description: Girl Gains at Northwestern aims to empower women through strength training and fitness, providing a supportive community for women to pursue their fitness goals and build confidence in the gym and beyond.
summary: Girl Gains at Northwestern aims to empower women through strength training and fitness, providing a supportive community for women to pursue their fitness goals and build confidence in the gym and beyond.

name: Glioblastoma Initiative Outreach
description: The overarching mission of GLIO is to inform students and bring together the Northwestern and Evanston communities to support brain tumor patients and their families. Members will have opportunities to volunteer in support of brain tumor research and to provide resources and care for patients and their families. The organization will educate members about brain cancers by discussing underlying mechanisms of disease, current treatments, and effects on patients and their families. We will host researchers as guest speakers to share recent innovations in diagnosis and treatment methods, as well as to demonstrate how support for brain tumor research can make an impact.
summary: The overarching mission of GLIO is to inform students and bring together the Northwestern and Evanston communities to support brain tumor patients and their families.

name: Global Engagement Summit
description: The Global Engagement Summit brings together students interested in global affairs, entrepreneurship, current events, and social justice to plan a week-long innovation conference. The conference hosts 30 international entrepreneurs—from Nigeria, to Indonesia, to the Northwestern community itself— to work on their change-based projects that can tackle any social change from education to healthcare to sustainability. Previous projects include a solution to deforestation in Sudan and a mental health initiative for Armenian veterans. This org is a fun way to meet like-minded people who are passionate about making a difference in the world through entrepreneurship and creative problem-solving.
summary: The Global Engagement Summit (GES) is a student-led community of changemakers that values engaging with the world in meaningful ways. A core part of what we do is organize an annual social innovation conference for entrepreneurs from all over the world.

name: Global Medical Missions Alliance
description:
We are a group of students and healthcare professionals who seek to build one another up in Christ, and become better equipped for missions both in our local community and globally. 

We engage in activities such as:

  • Campus Outreach
  • Local Community Medical Outreach
  • Global Missions Outreach
  • Biannual National Conferences
  • Partnering with various chapters across the country
  • Partnering with medical professionals and hosting health panels and related mentorship events
summary: Our mission is to bring God’s healing to all nations. “Arise, shine, for your light has come, and the glory of the Lord rises upon you.” Isaiah 60:1

name: Global Research and Consulting
description: Global Research and Consulting Group (GRC) works with social impact businesses and NGOs across the globe to help them achieve their goals, while simultaneously inspiring students to give back to the global community and develop their skills. We have a presence across the globe, with chapters at Harvard, Stanford, Oxford, Cambridge, and Yale, among others. Here is the link to GRC Global's website: https://www.grcglobalgroup.com/
summary: Global Research and Consulting Group (GRC) works with social impact organizations and NGOs across the globe to help them achieve their goals while simultaneously inspiring students to give back to the global community and develop their skills.

name: GlobeMed
description:
GlobeMed was started at Northwestern University with the mission to enhance the way college students strive to improve the health and living conditions of poverty. Historically, grassroots leaders across the world have initiated projects aimed at increasing the standard of life in their communities. These leaders are born and raised in the communities they serve, possessing the knowledge, relationships, and vision necessary to foster impactful and lasting change. These initiatives, however, often lack crucial resources including funding, technology, research, and global recognition needed to realize their potential.

 

     The current partner of Northwestern GlobeMed is the Adonai Child Development Centre in Namugoga, Uganda. Adonai provides for the needs of children orphaned by AIDS and the extended civil unrest in the northern and eastern parts of Uganda. Through yearly fundraising campaigns, we supply financial resources for Adonai. Additionally, each summer three to four students are selected to participate on the GROW trip at the Adonai Centre. There, they collaborate with Adonai Executive Directors Aloysious and Abby Luswata along with members of the local community on sustainable, capacity building projects. Previous projects included the medical centre, safe water project, an electronic data system for their records, and a community needs assessment on existing health infrastructures, water and sanitation, and reusable energy needs of the community. 

 

     Back on campus, we hold weekly global health themed meetings to discuss current issues and allow our members to think critically in a low-stress, safe environment. Our mission is to create a sustainable impact on the health of our partner community as well as inspire and train the next generation of global health leaders. If you are interested in joining our community, recruitment is early Fall Quarter. For more information, check out our website (https://sites.northwestern.edu/globemed/), FB page (GlobeMed at Northwestern), instagram (@globemednu), email us at northwestern@globemed.org!
summary: GlobeMed presents an opportunity for students to contextualize healthcare inequity in a low-stress, non-academic setting. Uniting students from varying backgrounds, GlobeMed fosters a safe space for discussion on global health and social justice issues.

name: Graduate Christian Fellowship
description:
Our official website is here: http://studentorgs.northwestern.edu/gcf/

Please subscribe to our listserv or email gcf@u.northwestern.edu to receive updates about large groups, retreats, and other announcements. In addition, please fill out fill out this short Google form.

We are a community of graduate students encouraging faith-work integration; and spiritual exploration, dialogue, and development by engaging our campus and city.

We are an affiliate of InterVarsity Christian Fellowship, part of a worldwide network of Evangelical student organizations.
summary: The Graduate Christian Fellowship is a community of Christian graduate and professional students. We also provide spaces for skeptics to discuss religion and its connection to contemporary life. Our website is here: studentorgs.northwestern.edu/gcf

name: Graduate International Student Association
summary: To foster greater cross-cultural understanding by promoting interaction, providing information and ensuring inclusion for and by the graduate international community of Northwestern University.

name: Graduate Investment Club
description: GIC is a student group that enables graduate students to gain exposure to investing. GIC members are interested in various ways to allocate capital to create returns. GIC welcomes graduate students interested in public and private markets, wealth management and impact investing, and various other forms of investment. Pursuing its purpose, GIC engages in a broad range of activities including member presentations on various topics about investing and guest talks with industry leaders.
summary: GIC is a student group that enables graduate students to gain exposure to investing. Pursuing its purpose, GIC engages in a broad range of activities including member presentations on various topics about investing and guest talks with industry leaders.

name: Graduate Leadership Advocacy Council
description: The Graduate Leadership Advocacy Council's mission and purpose is multifold.  Our primary purpose is to act as a liaison between graduate student organizations and leaders, and The Graduate School (TGS) administration.  We also serve to open the lines of communication between graduate student groups across campus, serving as a forum for student leaders to share best practices.  GLAC also works to collect information from the graduate students represented by TGS to report on graduate student life to the Northwestern board of trustees.  Finally, GLAC serves at the NU representation at the National Association of Graduate-Professional Students (NAGPS).
summary: The mission of the Graduate Leadership and Advocacy Council (GLAC) is to advocate on behalf of TGS graduate students and graduate student organizations to the Graduate School (TGS) and Northwestern University.

name: Graduate Ski and Board Club
description: The Graduate Ski and Board club at Northwestern will serve as a leisure winter activity club for graduate students to enjoy the great snowy outdoors. Graduate students will be able to sign up to join a snow trip (there are multiple ski hills within a 2-3h driving distance of Evanston) to get their ski or snowboard legs on! The intended goal of the club is to build community around a sport people are passionate about (or for those interested in learning!). The club will plan to spend a day on the slopes whenever members are interested with the goal to get graduate students out of their labs and classrooms to enjoy the snow and slopes the United States has to offer!
summary: The Graduate Ski and Board club at Northwestern will serve as a leisure winter activity club for graduate students to enjoy the great snowy outdoors. The intended goal of the club is to build community around a sport people are passionate about.

name: Graduate Society of Women Engineers
description: The Society of Women Engineers (SWE) is a nationally recognized professional, educational, non-profit, and service organization dedicated to encouraging women to pursue STEM fields. The Northwestern SWE (NU SWE) chapter’s (est. 1976) original emphasis was on undergraduates. In the last decade, the need for a dedicated group targeting the interests of graduate women led to the establishment of GradSWE in 2012. As interest continued to grow, GradSWE formed an executive board of 8-10 people to coordinate social, professional development, and mentoring events to meet the needs of the graduate women community. GradSWE includes female and male graduate students across all STEM disciplines at NU who advocate for the participation and retention of women in STEM at all levels. We organize events such as quarterly lunch discussions and co-host networking events and monthly coffee hours to build support networks and community among graduate women and advocates. Moreover, benefiting from our position in the broader SWE network, we leverage our connections with professional organizations (SWE and Association for Women in Science) in Chicago and NU SWE undergraduates to encourage mentorship and networking between women at all careers stages.
summary: TBA

name: Graduate Student Association
description: The Northwestern Graduate Student Association represents and serves non-professional graduate students at Northwestern University in Evanston and Chicago, IL.  We build community at Northwestern and provide opportunities to serve the greater Evanston and Chicago communities.
summary: The Northwestern Graduate Student Association represents and serves non-professional graduate students at Northwestern University in Evanston and Chicago, IL.

name: Graduate Student Association for Latino and Spanish Activities
description:
Ever wonder how you can get involved in the Latino community at Northwestern?

G-SALSA (Graduate Student Association for Latino and Spanish Activities) was founded with the purpose of providing an  environment where students can meet and interact with other students to celebrate Hispanic and Latin American Heritage.

In other words, ...

We are committed to enjoy graduate student life!

We will work hard to make new friends!

We hope that you will join us in our quest to have fun while we learn in  the process!

Share: We help each other in whatever way we can.

Eat: We go out to restaurants or host events such as Argentinean parillada on the Summer.

Learn: Learn about the Latino American culture and other cultures.

Dance: Dance in salsa clubs.

Soccer: If you would like to play soccer, you can contact us. We organize practice, games and a team for the Intramurlas.

Outdoors: We have visited the Chicago Botanic Garden in summer or the Gardfield Park Conservatory in winter.
summary:
Ever wonder how you can get involved in the Latino community at Northwestern?
G-SALSA (Graduate Student Association for Latino and Spanish Activities) was founded with the purpose of providing an  environment where students can meet and interact

name: Graduate Student Karate
description:
Graduate Student Karate is a club dedicated to helping grad students train their bodies and minds while building community through traditional martial arts. Whether you're a complete beginner or an experienced martial artist, we welcome you to join us for high-energy training sessions that improve technique, stamina, and self-defense skills. Rooted in the Shotokan style, our practice emphasizes strong stances, precise strikes, and kata to develop both physical and mental resilience.

Come sweat, strike, and sharpen your spirit with us!
summary: Graduate Student Karate is a club dedicated to helping grad students train their bodies and minds while building community through traditional martial arts.

name: Graduate Women Across Northwestern
description: Graduate Women Across Northwestern (GWAN) is an interdisciplinary organization that seeks to create community building opportunities for women in the graduate and professional programs at Northwestern University in Evanston, Illinois. GWAN was founded in the fall of 2006 through the Northwestern Kellogg School of Management’s Women’s Business Association. We are funded by a Community Building Grant through The Graduate School.
summary: GWAN is an interdisciplinary organization dedicated to providing community building opportunities for women graduate and professional students at NU.

name: Graduate Womxn in Physics and Astronomy
description: GWIP aims to support and empower the physics and astronomy graduate student community of minority genders. We use the term “womxn” to broadly refer to the whole community of cisgender women, transgender women, non-binary people, and gender diverse people.
summary: GWIP aims to support and empower the physics and astronomy graduate student community of minority genders. We use the term “womxn” to broadly refer to the whole community of cisgender women, transgender women, non-binary people, and gender diverse people

name: Graffiti Dancers
description: Graffiti Dancers is Northwestern University's premiere and most eclectic dance ensemble. After over five decades of performing and choreographing, Graffiti continues to create exciting and boundary-pushing dance, attracting large audiences to entertain and inspire. Using the mediums and cross-sections of contemporary, jazz, ballet, and hip-hop, Graffiti has created innovative pieces ranging from elegant contemporary ballet to upbeat jazz. Graffiti continues to produce high-energy shows filled with all forms of dance, entertaining, provoking, challenging, and inspiring each member of our company and our audience. Graffiti Dancers is a completely student-run dance company, meaning we, and all that we do, are directed, produced, choreographed, and performed by students. We typically have 20-25 dancers, all of whom have a good deal of training and experience in ballet, jazz, modern, hip-hop, and tap. Though not everyone has experience in all of these dance forms, every dancer has their own strengths. These diverse strengths are what make us one of the most versatile dance groups on campus, providing the company with unique and individual voices that integrate into one community and allow us to connect with a broad audience through our performances.
summary: Graffiti Dancers is a not-for-profit, student-run organization, and the premier contemporary dance company at Northwestern University. After five decades, Graffiti continues to create unique choreographic work at NU.

name: Greek American Legacy Association
description: GALA is Northwestern University's leading community for students of Hellenic descent and Philhellenes.
summary: GALA is Northwestern University's leading community for students of Hellenic descent and Philhellenes.

name: Griffin's Tale Children's Theatre Repertory Company
description: We are Griffin's Tale, Northwestern University's only Children's Repertory Theatre Company!  We take stories and poems written by kids in the Evanston and Chicago areas and turn them into skits, sketches, songs, raps, dances, and more!  THEN we get to go to the schools these amazing students attend and perform their stories for them!  We've been doing it since 1989, and we love it!
summary: We are Griffin's Tale, Northwestern University's only Children's Repertory Theatre Company!  We take stories and poems written by kids in the Evanston and Chicago areas and turn them into skits, sketches, songs, raps, dances, and more!

name: Grounding for Public Service
description:
Grounding for Public Service (GPS) Club is a weekly learning and connecting space focused on social justice and equity education in Evanston and Chicago.  

GPS Club engages students in learning about the Evanston and Chicago community, including physical environment, local culture, history, and people. We focus on growing our knowledge of Evanston and Chicago through a racial justice and equity lens. The goal is to build a community of informed and engaged citizens. 

We meet every Tuesday at Parkes Hall from 5:00-6:30 pm for dinner and learning. Please visit our website for more information! All are welcome!
summary: Grounding for Public Service is a student-led group under LDCE dedicated to social justice education and building relationships with one another.

name: Happiness Club
description:
Do you think that if you make someone happy, you will be happy too?

Do you want to do more of what makes you happy?

What do you think of a club dedicated to puppies, candy, balloons, bubbles, sunshine, and smiley faces? Because that's what Happiness Club is, and that's what we do! We try to make everyone at Northwestern smile a little wider and a little more often by putting on fun, free events and creating the moments that make you think, "That just made my day!"

Join Happiness Club!
summary: Happiness Club is a student group at Northwestern University committed to increasing the happiness of members of the Northwestern community. How do we do this? Through fun and exciting activities!

name: Hawai'i Club
description: Hawai'i Club is a Northwestern student-run organization dedicated to promoting Hawaiian and Pacific-Islander culture through education and exposure. We aim to facilitate the weaving of lifelong bonds amongst students and establish a welcoming community that resonates with the aloha spirit of our homes and families. We host a variety of events throughout the year including our annual lu’au, relevant speaker panels, and community-bonding activities.
summary: Hawai'i Club is a Northwestern student-run organization dedicated to promoting Hawaiian and Pacific-Islander culture through education and exposure.

name: Heart Club
description: The purpose of our Heart Club is to promote cardiovascular health and awareness within both the Northwestern and greater Evanston community. By providing educational opportunities, advocating for healthy lifestyles, and putting on engaging events, we aim to empower students, faculty, and members of Evanston to take proactive steps toward preventing heart disease and stroke. We would like to further our impact outside just the Northwestern community by going to local Evanston elementary schools and providing education about heart health.
summary: The purpose of our Heart Club is to promote cardiovascular health and awareness within both the Northwestern and greater Evanston community.

name: Helicon
description: HELICON was the brainchild of three students in Mary Kinzie’s 1979 poetry sequence.  Lisa Getter, Christina Calvit, and Michael Steele wanted to provide Northwestern with a regularly published literary magazine that could showcase the artistic work of the student body. HELICON began in Chapin, the Humanities Residential College, and is funded by the Residential College Program through the Office of the Provost.  We publish twice a year, with a print issue in the Winter and a web issue (with online supplement) in the Spring. Throughout the year, we hold events including open mics and writing workshops.
summary: HELICON is Northwestern's premier literary and arts magazine. We publish the best in student art twice a year, with submissions ranging from poetry and prose to music and videos to comic books and visual art. Visit helicon.northwestern.edu for more info.

name: HerCampus
description: We publish weekly articles about entertainment, beauty, news, health and lifestyle as it pertains to college women. Members are encouraged to pitch ideas based on their interests and contribute to a female affinity space on campus.
summary: We are an online newsmagazine and digital brand tailored towards Northwestern women. We provide a platform for students to write online articles and get to know one another through bonding events. All are welcome, regardless prior journalism experience!

name: Hindu YUVA
description:
Hindu YUVA aims to organize activities for community service in the spirit of Sewa (Hindu conception of service to humanity) and celebrate Hindu festivals. In our weekly meetings, we play traditional team-building games, practice yoga and meditation, and study Hindu scriptures. 

The above initiatives will serve the purpose of creating a vibrant Hindu presence on campus, and will aid in providing a home-away-from-home feeling to incoming and existing Hindu students.
summary: Hindu YUVA aims to provide a platform to preserve, practice, promote, and protect Hindu Dharma by bringing together Hindu community on campus.

name: Hobart House
summary: Hobart House is on south campus and houses 50 women in single-, double-, and triple-occupancy rooms, arranged in a traditional-style layout. This building is available to all female undergraduate students.

name: Hong Kong Student Association
description:
Hong Kong Student Association aims to promote the vibrant and cosmopolitan Hong Kong culture on campus. Given that Hong Kong is one of the most culturally diverse and international cities in the world, we welcome students from all backgrounds to participate in our activities. 

 

Our social and cultural events held throughout the year range from celebrating cultural festivities to doing community service work for marginalized communities in Hong Kong… think dim sum get-togethers, canto karaoke, Wong Kar-wai movie marathons, and much more!

 

Not only are we here to provide support to HK students through peer support and alumni relations, but we’re also open to connecting with prospective HK students.
summary: The Hong Kong Student Association (HKSA) is a cultural organization focused on building a community around the shared love of our city.

name: Hospice Patient Care Club
description:
The purpose of the Hospice Patient Care Club (HPCC) is to provide a group of committed hospice patient care volunteers to the Evanston community, all the while supporting our volunteers to make sure students feel comfortable and confident during their interactions with patients. Through our efforts, we also hope to raise the awareness of hospice and palliative care, as well as hospice volunteering, at Northwestern and connect the student body to the surrounding community in a positive way.

Our primary goal is to see that all hospice patients in the Northwestern area get a friendly face if they want one. That being said, we want HPCC to be a win-win-win for the hospices we connect with, the students who are willing to generously give their time, and the hospice patients who truly benefit from having regular visits. To be Medicare-certified, hospices are required to have volunteer hours comprise at least 5% of their total number of patient care hours. Having a steady stream of volunteers also allows hospices to always provide a hospice patient with their own volunteer, improving the level of care these hospices can provide. From our members' perspectives, each student will be closely involved with their own patient(s). From these experiences, the student will learn both the healthcare responsibilities of being a team's official "Patient Care Hospice Volunteer" and lessons from a human being who naturally becomes a close friend. Regular club meetings will provide support and additional learning opportunities for the students: basic check-ins to make sure students are not stressing themselves out in their kind-hearted efforts, discussions on how they can best serve their patients, inviting guest speakers from the hospices to talk about intriguing and difficult issues in health care, and coverage of transportation costs through ASG funding if applicable.
summary: HPCC looks to provide a group of committed hospice patient care volunteers to the Evanston & greater Chicago community, all the while supporting our volunteers to make sure students feel comfortable and confident during their interactions with patients.

name: Housing Security Collaborative
description: The purpose of Housing Security Collaborative (HSC) is to work with community partners to combat homelessness and housing insecurity within the Evanston and nearby community. We intend to increase Northwestern student volunteering opportunities in the community by providing easy access to partners who are currently working on housing security initiatives. By working together with partners, our organization will be able to provide opportunities for students to receive adequate training and education to perform community service. Finally, Northwestern students will also be able to help Evanston organizations through advocacy, fundraising/drives, and educational campaigns. Ultimately, Housing Security Collaborative will increase the connection between Northwestern and our Evanston community so that students can become more involved outside of the campus community.
summary: The purpose of Housing Security Collaborative (HSC) is to work with community partners to combat homelessness and housing insecurity within the Evanston and nearby community.

name: IfYou'reReadingThis.org
description: The mission of IfYoureReadingThis.org is to serve as an open-ended university-wide support network to help students with mental illness feel comfortable talking to somebody, by identifying the people who want to listen, and by showing an outpouring of love and understanding. Our mission is to foster connections around mental health, support, and empathy. We host open letters written by students, faculty, and alumni on our website. These letters encourage people to connect and show that every story matters.
summary: The mission is to serve as an open-ended university-wide support network to help students with mental illness feel comfortable talking to somebody, by identifying the people who want to listen, and by showing an outpouring of love and understanding

name: Ignite Christian Fellowship
description: Ignite is a Northwestern campus ministry that believes college is the perfect time to explore big questions, navigate life together, and create meaningful community. At Ignite we welcome everyone to explore Christianity at any level while providing a chance for Christians to grow deeper in their faith through Bible studies, personal discipleship, retreats and getaways, and service opportunities. In any given week at Ignite, you might find yourself studying the Bible with your life group, talking through questions about your Christian faith, serving youth through camps and retreats, and attending church together on Sunday. In that same week, you might also find yourself eating Korean BBQ, bowling, venturing to Navy Pier, exploring the Midwest, or studying in Tech, all with the same community from Ignite. No matter where you are in your spiritual journey, we'd love to connect with you! Please reach out to us on our website: ignitenu.org
summary: Ignite Christian Fellowship is a space where students of all backgrounds can experience close community and learn more about the Christian faith.

name: Illustrators & Animators Club
description:
Frozen October 2024

Northwestern's #1 illustration club!

We provide educational opportunities about art and the art industry. Here you will find art tutorials, portfolio advice, watch parties, conversations with working professionals in the industry, and all sorts of art challenges like Drawtober and art swaps.

We welcome all levels of experience. Whether you love to doodle, want to start learning to draw, or just want to meet people who share your love of all things animation, this is the place for you!

 

NOTE: If you would like to get involved, please join our discord! This is where you will receive the most up to date information about the club. It is also a very active community :) join here -> https://discord.gg/jZg6xNVwYU
summary: The Illustrators & Animators Club aims to serve the Northwestern art community by supporting and promoting student artists. This club is open to all. No prior art experience necessary!

name: In Our Nature
description: In Our Nature aims to bring the environment to everyone. We look to express love for nature, highlight its importance, bring attention to environmental injustices and help readers learn more about environmental issues in the Chicagoland area and beyond.
summary: In Our Nature is a student-run, paperless magazine that aims to bring the environment to everyone, and to provide a platform for students of all majors to express care for nature.

name: Indian Graduate Students and Scholars Association
summary: IGSSA aims to serve and foster a sense of community among the the Indian (and larger South Asian) graduate and post-doc population at Northwestern University.

name: Indonesian Students' Association
description: IDSA creates a social environment for students of all backgrounds, and promotes Indonesian culture at Northwestern University. Our club aims to foster a stronger sense of community between our members, and form close relationships with other student organizations by celebrating and sharing the Indonesian identity. We hope that through IDSA, members will have access to professional opportunities and various student resources (e.g. consular services), and that the broader Northwestern community will have a place to learn about Indonesia. Finally, IDSA is a proponent for Northwestern University’s global initiatives, and aims to garner broader recognition as a prestigious institution for higher education among student communities in Indonesia.
summary: IDSA creates a social environment for students of all backgrounds, and promotes Indonesian culture at Northwestern University.

name: Institute for Student Business Education
description:
Northwestern Institute for Student Business Education (ISBE) provides unparalleled education and experiential business opportunities for its members. Visit our website: nuisbe.org

--

Our 7 product groups strive to provide real-world opportunities in consulting, finance, marketing, journalism, entrepreneurship and more. Members of ISBE can expect to learn tangible business skills through engagement with fellow students, ISBE alumni and clients from Evanston / Chicago.

--

All members from ISBE will also have the opportunity to participate in the workshops, office visits, exclusive company networking sessions and more. The members will not only hone their hard skills through projects, but also practice their interpersonal skills through the extensive ISBE alumni network.

--

Above all, with over 150 active members, our mission is to be the leading resource for all Northwestern undergraduates to discover the possibilities of a career in business.
summary: Established in 2005, ISBE is the premier and largest student business group at Northwestern. ISBE consists of 7 product groups and over 200 members. We offer a diverse array of educational and experiential business opportunities.

name: Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers
description: The Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers (IISE) strives to improve the IEMS student experience by fostering community, providing academic & career services, and serving as the department liaison between administration and students
summary: The Institute of Industrial and Systems Engineers (IISE) strives to improve the IEMS student experience by fostering community, providing academic & career services, and serving as the department liaison between administration and students

name: Interfraternity Council
description: The Interfraternity Council (IFC) consists of 12 nationally-recognized fraternities at Northwestern. For the past 150 years, IFC fraternities have enriched the lives of students at Northwestern through leadership development, social justice education, and various programming and social activities. Today, more than 1300 individuals are members of IFC fraternities at NU, sharing the sense of community, building long-lasting bonds, and unbreakable friendships.
summary: The Interfraternity Council (IFC) consists of 12 nationally-recognized fraternities at Northwestern. For the past 150 years, IFC fraternities have enriched the lives of students at Northwestern through educational programs and activities.

name: International Student Association
description: ISA seeks to promote interaction among students from different cultural and religious backgrounds and to build a cohesive community of students both from the United States and around the world. ISA serves as a platform to engage with global issues, spread cultural awareness, and voice the concerns and interests of the international and domestic student body.
summary: ISA promotes interaction among students from different cultural backgrounds, serves as a platform through which to engage with global and cultural issues, and hopes to be a "home away from home" for students both from the US and abroad.

name: International Studies Residential College - West Fairchild
description: West Fairchild is home to the International Studies Residential College (ISRC). Located on South Campus, West Fairchild is home to 106 students in single- and double-occupancy rooms, arranged in an open suite-style layout. While residents are organized into suites by gender, ISRC also has an all-gender suite on the 2nd floor. This building is available to all undergraduate students.
summary: West Fairchild is on south campus and houses 106 students in single- and double- occupancy rooms, arranged in an open suite-style layout. Men and women live in suites. This building is available to all undergraduate students.

name: International Studies Society
description: The International Studies Society (ISS) seeks to serve the NU undergraduate population by providing students with opportunities to learn about, explore, and engage in the professional world pertaining to the field of International studies. We work to spark and develop student interest in fields like diplomacy, international development, nonprofits with international focuses, etc. via social, networking, and informational events as well as mentorship programming that will ultimately prepare students for graduate school, job placement following graduation, and provide community among a group of like-minded individuals. Ultimately, our goal is to cultivate an inclusive and inspiring space where students with a passion for impacting our global community are supported, uplifted, and empowered.
summary: The International Studies Society (ISS) seeks to serve the NU undergraduate population by providing students with opportunities to learn about, explore, and engage in the professional world pertaining to the field of International studies.

name: Investment Management Club
summary: Interested in a potential career in investment management? Join a group that will give you broad exposure to the industry and its professionals, preparation for recruitment and interviews, and mentorship and hands-on experience!

name: Investment Management Group
description:
Northwestern University Investment Management Group (IMG) is a student run organization dedicated to those who have an interest in investing. The Group seeks to expose students to the investing world by simulating the operations of an asset management firm. Our mandate is to make long term investments in fundamentally great public companies with sustainable competitive advantages and high growth potential. IMG's emphasis on student-led investment education has allowed members to benefit from formal training on finance subjects, mentorship from upperclassmen and alumni, professional development workshops, speaker events, and internship opportunities. 

Please visit NUIMG.org or contact NorthwesternIMG@gmail.com to connect with the Executive Board.
summary: Please visit NUIMG.org

name: Iranian Student Association
description:
Frozen October 2024

The Iranian Students Association at Northwestern University is a student group dedicated to promoting social, intellectual and cultural activities for Iranian students and scholars at Northwestern University as well as interested members in the Northwestern community. Besides cultural and social activities, our organization strives to provide opportunities and benefits for its members such as information and support for incoming Iranian students, and connecting current NU students with Iranian NU alumni in order to enhance their experience in NU and expand their career opportunities in the future.
summary: The Iranian Students Association at Northwestern University is a student group dedicated to promoting social, intellectual and cultural activities for Iranian students and scholars.

name: Italian Association
summary: Open to all interested students, the Italian Association (ITA) aims to establish a foundation upon which to develop and manifest Italian language and culture within the Northwestern community.

name: J Street U Northwestern
description:
At J Street U Northwestern, we are committed to providing spaces for open conversations on Israel and Palestine with Northwestern community members from diverse backgrounds and perspectives. Whether you are staunchly pro-Israel or pro-Palestine, and whether you know a ton about the conflict or nothing at all, we want you to join our conversation. If you're looking for nuance and understanding, not name-calling and polarization, we are the group for you.

 

-------------------------------------

More on J Street U nationally:

J Street U is the student organizing arm of J Street, the political home for pro-Israel, pro-peace, pro-democracy Americans. We are a nationwide movement of campus chapters advocating and educating on colleges and universities, in our communities and on Capitol Hill for vigorous and sustained American leadership in facilitating a negotiated, two-state resolution to the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.

J Street U educates students about the Israeli-Palestinian conflict and America’s role in promoting peace. J Street U provides a home for students to hold nuanced views without compromising their pro-Israel values. We equip students to find their political voice and to take action on their college campuses, in their Jewish communities, and on Capitol Hill.

By educating, training, and mobilizing students, we are building the mainstream pro-Israel movement on college campuses nationwide. J Street U is the next generation of leaders who will advocate for American foreign policy that represents their core values.

 

----------------------------------

Why are we called J Street?

In Washington, D.C., the streets are named alphabetically, from A Street by the Capitol building all the way out to W Street. But one street is missing: J Street. We bring conversations that are totally missing from our national policy on Israel and Palestine.
summary: We are a nationwide movement of campus chapters advocating and educating on colleges and universities for vigorous and sustained American leadership in facilitating a negotiated, two-state resolution to the Israeli-Palestinian conflict.

name: Japan Club
description:
Northwestern Japan Club is an organization that fosters an open and friendly environment for the enjoyment of Japanese culture through a supportive network and in a community oriented environment. We are an active student group that holds numerous events throughout the academic year to celebrate and explore Japanese culture.


You don't have to be Japanese to join; you don't need to speak Japanese to join. If you have even the smallest interest in Japan or Japanese culture, we strongly encourage you to join! We hope to see you sometime at one of our meetings or events!

Feel free to email us with any questions!
summary: Northwestern Japan Club is an organization that fosters an open and friendly environment for the enjoyment of Japanese culture through a supportive network and community through events and occasions.

name: Japanese American Student Association
description:
JASA is established for the purpose of providing a community for Japanese Americans on campus. JASA provides a safe community for Japanese Americans for them to talk about contemporary issues and topics regarding the Japanese American identity. The organization also provides a platform for Japanese Americans and other interested parties to come together for informal networking. JASA is also meant to educate those within and outside of the Japanese American community about Japanese culture and contemporary issues.

Some events JASA hosts include meetings (discussion based and activity based), field trips, speaker events and co-hosting events with other organizations. 

All members, regardless of background, are invited to join JASA and foster the Japanese American community on campus.
summary: JASA is an organization that creates and fosters a community for Japanese Americans on campus. JASA is also meant to educate those within and outside of the Japanese American community about Japanese culture and contemporary issues.

name: Japanese Graduate Student Association
description: JGSA primarily serves the community of Japanese-identifying graduate students and postdocs alongside their family members across all disciplines and programs. The organization aims to enhance the quality of the graduate student life of this community by 1) Providing an emotional safe space in which Japanese-identifying students/postdocs can feel a sense of belonging, connect, and receive support in times of need, 2) Offering opportunities where members can help each other with academic/professional development to overcome the difficulties of navigating this space as a Japanese-identifying student, 3) Offering opportunities for social activities in which Japanese-identifying students/postdocs can enhance their overall experience in graduate school, and 4) Facilitating the communication between the school administration and the Japanese-identifying community.
summary: JGSA primarily serves the community of Japanese-identifying graduate students and postdocs alongside their family members across all disciplines and programs.

name: Jazz Club
description: The purpose of The Jazz Club is to allow everyone in the Northwestern community the chance to play and experience jazz, regardless of their background and knowledge. Furthermore, The Jazz Club also seeks to actively encourage and accept women, LGBTQ+, and minority jazz musicians (or those who seek to learn), who are often severely underrepresented in college-level jazz organizations. In fostering an inclusive environment, we hope to create a community where anyone can feel comfortable playing and making their voice heard. Jazz Club integrates itself into campus life by offering students opportunities to connect with one another, play and perform in music sessions, volunteer locally and more!
summary: The purpose of The Jazz Club is to allow everyone in the Northwestern community the chance to play and experience jazz, regardless of their background and knowledge.

name: Jewish Theatre Ensemble
description: The Jewish Theatre Ensemble is one of eight student theatre boards at Northwestern. While our name is the Jewish Theatre Ensemble, our members practice a wide variety of religions to a wide variety of degrees, and some of us practice no religion at all, but what unites us is a shared belief that faith is an exciting and productive lens with which to view theatre. There are many ways to get involved with JTE, including working on or performing in a production, petitioning to be a board member, pitching a special project, or talking to a few board members! Check us out on Facebook or Instagram @jewishtheatreensemble or www.jewishtheatreensemble.com.
summary: The Jewish Theatre Ensemble is one of NU's student theatre boards. We aim to operate at a level of artistic excellence and support creative endeavors that promote Jewish themes in the arts. We produce three shows a year with many ways to get involved!

name: Jewish Voice for Peace
description: We engage in pro-Palestinian activism from a Jewish perspective, educate students and community members on the genocide in Palestine, and build anti-Zionist Jewish community on campus.
summary: We engage in pro-Palestinian activism from a Jewish perspective, educate students and community members on the genocide in Palestine, and build anti-Zionist Jewish community on campus.

name: Journaling Club
summary: Journaling Club at Northwestern is a student organization open to all Northwestern undergraduates dedicated to fostering mindfulness and self-expression through the practice of journaling. We provide a supportive, friendly community and good vibes!

name: Juggling Club
description: A fun club to get together weekly and hang out to juggle! Welcome to those that already can juggle, and newcomers who wish to learn the skill. Feel free to show up as often as you wish, we just want to have an open space for people to be able to juggle!
summary: A fun club to get together weekly and hang out to juggle! Welcome to those that already can juggle, and newcomers who wish to learn the skill. Feel free to show up as often as you wish, we just want to have an open space for people to be able to juggle!

name: Jumpstart
description: Partnered with under-resourced preschools in the Evanston community, we work to provide children with the literary, language, and social-emotional skills to prepare them for kindergarten. We provide training and opportunities to work with family, faculty, and students in the community.
summary: Partnered with under-resourced preschools in Evanston, we work to provide children with literary & social-emotional skills to prepare them for kindergarten. We provide training & opportunities to work with family, faculty, and students in the community.

name: K-Dance
description:
Northwestern K-Dance, founded in 2018, is a student-led K-Pop dance group and general K-pop community open to all experience levels. 

K-Dance works to create a community for all to enjoy K-Pop. Members may enjoy one of two levels of membership: casual or auditioned. Casual general members are able to enjoy any of our middle pieces performances, bi-weekly dance workshops, K-pop social events, YouTube videos, or other events. Those particularly passionate in and interested in dancing are encouraged to audition for our quarterly performance teams in order to learn the full choreo to a K-Pop song, perform on stage, and have a music video filmed! Past performances have included our own Spring Show and guest appearances at Refusionshaka, CSA's annual Celebrasia and CISA's Lunar New Year event. Please feel free to follow us on Facebook and Instagram and subscribe on YouTube!
summary: Northwestern K-Dance, founded in 2018, is a student-led K-Pop dance group and general K-pop community open to all experience levels.

name: Kadima
description:
Kadima is the hub for Jewish social justice on campus! We provide a space for both Jewish and non-Jewish students to become involved with social action. Kadima runs volunteer opportunities, educational workshops, and speaker events based on various social justice issues, including climate change, mental healthcare, housing justice, and more.

 

Our organization strives to promote social justice causes and engage the student population while operating under Jewish values. We will both host events and promote other events with partner organizations to shed light on causes and organizations that deserve it. While we are a Jewish group, students of all faiths are welcome to attend and join in Kadima, understanding and respecting the Jewish identity held by the organization.
summary: Kadima is the hub for Jewish social justice on campus! We provide opportunities for both Jewish and non-Jewish students to get involved with volunteer opportunities, educational workshops, and speaker events based on various social justice issues.

name: Kaibigan: Philippine-American Student Association
description: Kaibigan is a Northwestern student-run organization dedicated to promoting Philippine culture through education and exposure. Kaibigan means "friend" in Filipino. The group fosters a sense of community providing a reliable and supportive environment for its members. We host a variety of events throughout the year such as Filipinx Fiesta, where we provide free Philippine food and teach attendees dances, as well as our biggest event Pinoy Show which is our annual cultural show that involves cultural and modern dances, skits, student-made videos, live music, and more.
summary: Kaibigan is a Northwestern student-run organization dedicated to promoting Philippine and Philippine-American culture through education and exposure. The group fosters a sense of community providing a reliable and supportive environment for its members.

name: Kappa Delta Chi Sorority, Inc.
description:
Purpose:
The purpose of Kappa Delta Chi is to promote the traditional values of Unity, Honesty, Integrity, and Leadership among women at colleges and universities. Through holding multifaceted events, KDChis learn more about themselves and their universities and communities. Then, they utilize what they have learned to become better leaders and offer optimal servitude to their surrounding areas. It's because of our members that Kappa Delta Chi is one of the strongest Latina-based sororities in the country and fosters the most powerful network among Latina leaders.

Upon graduation, members are encouraged to become part of the National Alumnae Association (NAA) and their local alumnae chapter, where they can continue serving their communities and mentoring collegiate sisters. KDChi also has a graduate/professional intake process for those who did not get the opportunity to join during their undergraduate career; to learn more about that, you can click here.
summary: Northwestern Colony of Kappa Delta Chi Sorority, Inc.

name: Kappa Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc.
description:
Nu Chapter at Northwestern University

Established November 23, 2002

"Sisterhood, Service, and Cultural Diversity."

 

Organization Facts

National founding date: March 9, 1995

PREAMBLE
We, the sisters of Kappa Phi Lambda Sorority, Incorporated, strive to strengthen Sisterhood amongst women by providing service and promoting cultural diversity to our members and our community, thus securing the bond of kinship for posterity. Kappa Phi Lambda exists to further educate individuals about Pan-Asian culture, while keeping our own identities. We aim for academic excellence, keeping in mind the importance of education and learning as the key to strength, awareness, and fulfillment.

Colors: Scarlet Red, White, Heather Grey,

You may view the Kappa Phi Lambda National website at www.kappaphilambda.org and the chapter's website at nukpl.weebly.com.

Instagram: @nu_kappas

name: Kappa Pi Beta
description: We are the ONLY asian-interest fraternity at Northwestern. We promote personal and social growth through cultural leadership and community. Our pillars are social, service and cultural awareness. Come see if you vibe with us as we're looking to grow our brotherhood!
summary: We are the ONLY asian-interest fraternity at Northwestern. We promote personal and social growth through cultural leadership and community.

name: Kappa Theta Pi - Kappa Chapter at Northwestern
description:
Frozen October 2024

KTP Northwestern is co-ed organization dedicated to providing a community for students interested in technology. We host events, workshops, and socials to help students learn and grow in their tech careers.
summary: KTP Northwestern is co-ed organization dedicated to providing a community for students interested in technology. We host events, workshops, and socials to help students learn and grow in their tech careers.

name: Kellog Management Studies Student Association
description: TBA
summary: Association of students enrolled in the MSMS program through Kellogg School of Management

name: Kesem at Northwestern
description:
Kesem at Northwestern University (also known as Camp Kesem) is a local chapter of a national organization (Kesem National) that supports children through and beyond their parent's cancer. CKNU hosts two weeks of free summer camp for over 150 children from the Chicagoland area who have been affected by a parent's cancer, in addition to other programming throughout the year.

Although our camps take place during the summer, becoming a counselor is not the only way to get involved in Kesem at Northwestern. Throughout the year, our general body members fundraise, outreach to find campers, plan events in committees, and, if applicable, get trained in order to prepare for the summer! Kesem is a great organization for any students interested in children or summer camp, or are interested in making a meaningful impact through service!

Watch this video to learn what we do here

Check out our Facebook page or Instagram to learn more!
summary: Kesem is a nonprofit that organizes a free summer camp for children who have been affected by a parent's cancer.

name: Keynotes
description: We are a graduate student a cappella group and come from all different programs and departments on campus. We welcome all graduate students who want to sing to audition at the beginning of the year or the beginning of each quarter, please email Michelle (President) for more information. We meet once a week for 2 hours and perform at least once per quarter.
summary: A graduate student a cappella group

name: Knitwestern
description:
Knitwestern is a learn-to-knit club for Northwestern students and community members. Knitwestern is dedicated to using knitting as a tool to build community among members of Northwestern's campus while making needed, practical winter items for donation to local organizations. Organizations we currently serve include Students Organizing for Labor Rights, Inspiration Corporation, and the Broadway Youth Center of Howard Brown Health.

 

Visit our Linktree for our social media!
summary: Knitwestern is a learn-to-knit club for Northwestern students and community members. Our mission is to teach knitting and then use those talents to knit warm winter items for Inspiration Corporation and the Broadway Youth Center of Howard Brown Health.

name: Korean American Student Association
description:
The Korean American Students Association (KASA) is an organization formed to provide social and culturally informative activities to all those who share a common interest in the Korean culture and Korean American issues. KASA also strives to promote awareness of Korean American students’ interests as well as intercultural understanding and cooperation. KASA does not discriminate against persons of a different race, color, religion, creed, or national origin. 

Are you interested in joining our organization? Check out our Instagram @northwesternkasa or reach out to an organization officer to stay up to date!
summary: The Korean American Students Association (KASA) is an organization formed to provide social and culturally informative activities to all those who share a common interest in the Korean culture and Korean American issues.

name: La Unidad Latina, Lambda Upsilon Lambda Fraternity, Inc.
description: The Beta Omicron Chapter of La Unidad Latina, Lambda Upsilon Lambda Fraternity, Inc. at Northwestern University was established on April 5, 2013 with the intent of engaging members around academics, culture, brotherhood, and service. Our fraternity consists of sixty-five (65) undergraduate chapters and fourteen (14) professional chapters across sixteen (16) states and the District of Columbia. We desire engagement from men and community members committed to "our ideas of Latino unity, culture and empowerment" as stated by Founding Father Hernando Londoño.
summary: The Beta Omicron Chapter of La Unidad Latina, Lambda Upsilon Lambda Fraternity, Inc. at Northwestern University was established on April 5, 2013 with the intent of engaging members around academics, culture, brotherhood, and service.

name: Lambda Strategy
description:
We are the first to have Kellogg MBA candidates and Northwestern undergraduates work together on consulting projects. Strategic partnerships with larger firms offer our consulting teams a pipeline of unique projects and clients. We work with high-growth, early-stage companies across various industries. Our scope, expertise, and depth of insight allow us to solve problems no other student organization can. 
 
Lambda Strategy operates at a highly professional level and maintains three initiatives: 
1. Create an environment of exceptional consultants by offering real-world experience to a group of motivated undergraduates 
2. Offer experience in project management, mentorship, and consulting to Kellogg students 
3. Offer high-impact deliverables to clients through innovation, best practices, and trustworthy relationships
summary: Lambda Strategy is a strategy consulting organization run by Northwestern University undergraduates, founded in 2017.

name: Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Inc
description:
Beta Psi Chapter, Northwestern University

Established March 4, 2001

"Latin by Tradition, not by Definition."

President: Magdalena Flores 

You may visit the Beta Psi Chapter's website at http://lta-betapsi.weebly.com

Organization Facts

National Founding Date: December 1975

The purpose of Lambda Theta Alpha is to provide a sisterhood based on Unity, Love and Respect; to provide social and cultural activities, and to carry on charitable and educational programs; to promote leadership, maintain high academic standards and to serve as a voice fo rthe Latino community.

Colors: Burgundy and Gray

You may visit the Lambda Theta Alpha Inter/national website at www.lambdalady.org.

Scholarships

The Beta Psi chapter of Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Incorporated gives out an academic scholarship, The Universal Woman Scholarship to a deserving freshman girl every year at their annual Brindis, a banquet that is held during our Founder's week to celebrate Beta Psi's presence on campus. The scholarship started in 2005. For more information on how freshmen girls can apply, email betapsi.lta@gmail.com.
summary:
Beta Psi Chapter, Northwestern University
Established March 4, 2001
"Latin by Tradition, not by Definition."
President: Magdalena Flores

name: Latin Music Ensemble
description: • Latin Music Ensemble hopes to share Latine culture with an aspect of Latinidad that is so influential to many of us, MUSIC! In this club, we will be learning and performing many genres of Latin music such as salsa, bachata, bossa nova, merengue, cumbia, and many many more. We will not only be learning how to play these styles, but we will also learn about the cultures, pioneers, dances, and history behind them.
summary: Latin Music Ensemble hopes to share Latine culture with an aspect of Latinidad that is so influential to many of us, MUSIC!

name: Leadership Development & Community Engagement
description:
Dedicated to nurturing the Social Justice fighter in you through leadership development opportunities, staff advisors, cohorts, trainings and opportunities to get engaged through our Neighbor2Neighbor initiative. 

 

NU students are invited to build relationships and become actively involved with their neighbors through unique opportunities to connect with community-based organizations in Chicsgo’s Rogers Park neighborhood (just 2 miles south of campus). Some of the N2N partners include preK-8 Gale Community Academy, A Just Harvest, and Howard Area Community Center, and more. Come to a one-time program or join an on-going project. Follow us on Facebook @NUneighbor2neighbor and IG @n2n_northwestern for up coming events and shout outs!

 

Leadership development opportunities through social justice advocacy training, workshops, cohorts and online training. Ongoing programming at campus include Social Justice talks and entering your Student Organization into the Community Engagement Coalition.
summary: Capacity building through creating mutually beneficial relationships with NU Students and community partners (both ongoing and one-time volunteer opportunities). Leadership development opportunities through workshops, cohorts and online training.

name: Learn To Be
description: The Learn To Be chapter at Northwestern University provides free, one-on-one virtual tutoring to underserved K-12 students seeking reliable academic support in the Chicagoland area as well as around the nation. Our tutors are qualified students from Northwestern University that are committed to compassionately supporting other students within their community, both on the academic and personal level. With our partnership with Learn To Be, we strive to create lifelong learners with the assistance of technology, as well as make tutoring and mentorship accessible to all.
summary: The Learn To Be chapter at Northwestern University provides free, one-on-one virtual tutoring to underserved K-12 students seeking reliable academic support in the Chicagoland area as well as around the nation.

name: Liberty in North Korea
description: NU LiNK is an independent chapter of the international organization Liberty in North Korea. The Rescue Team at Northwestern University was established for the purpose of changing the narrative surrounding North Korea and North Korean refugees by educating the Northwestern community and providing fundraising resources so that we may help the North Korean people achieve their freedom and develop self-efficacy during resettlement.
summary: Our Liberty in North Korea Rescue Team hopes to change the narrative surrounding North Korea by educating our community and providing fundraising resources so that we may help the North Korean people achieve their freedom.

name: Locket Cybersecurity
description: Formally known as Safe Security, its mission is to expand accessibility to cybersecurity across the Northwestern community.
summary: Formally known as Safe Security, its mission is to expand accessibility to cybersecurity across the Northwestern community.

name: Lumi Tutors
description: Lumi Tutors is an organization that connects select NU student tutors with underserved CPS high school students to provide a compassionate and tailored source of academic help and mentorship. Our organization provides resources for SAT test preparation to our tutors who then are responsible for creating weekly lesson plans to bring to their students. The service we provide is highly personalized to each individual student (1-1) and seeks to prepare them for higher education.
summary: Lumi Tutors is an organization that connects NU student tutors with underserved CPS high school students to provide a compassionate and tailored source of academic help and mentorship. We focus on SAT tutoring and life skills workshops.

name: Lutheran Campus Ministry
description:
LCMNU is a welcoming community experiencing, living, and sharing the love of God through scholarship, service, and faith.  LCMNU is a student-led ministry that welcomes all of God’s people regardless of race, color, national origin, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, parental status, marital status, disability, citizenship, or veteran status. As one of  180+ campus ministries of the Evangelical Lutheran Church in America (ELCA), LCMNU shares in the mission “to invite people in academic settings to engage more deeply with the teachings of Jesus Christ and the community that bears his name, so that they can discover and fulfill their vocation as disciples.”

We invite you to go to https://www.lcmnorthwestern.org/ to learn more about us, and we especially invite you to join us. During the fall 2023 quarter, we will worship at 11am every Sunday and gather every Wednesday at 6-6:45pm for Supper Worship. Welcome to LCMNU!
summary: Lutheran Campus Ministry is a welcoming community experiencing, living, and sharing the love of God through scholarship, service, and faith.

name: Marafiki wa Kiswahili - A Swahili Club
description: Marafiki wa Kiswahili at Northwestern is a student organization dedicated to fostering an inclusive community where individuals from all backgrounds can come together to explore and celebrate the rich cultural and linguistic heritage of the Swahili-speaking world. Our club provides a dynamic platform for students to enhance their Swahili language skills beyond the classroom while engaging with East African traditions and promoting cross-cultural understanding. Through interactive language lessons, cultural events, guest speakers, and community outreach, Marafiki wa Kiswahili seeks to deepen appreciation for Swahili as both a unifying language and a cultural bridge. We strive to raise awareness of the history, art, music, and traditions of Swahili-speaking communities, enriching the global perspectives of Northwestern students while celebrating diversity and inclusivity. Whether you are a beginner or a fluent speaker, we welcome you to join our journey of discovery, unity, and connection.
summary: Marafiki wa Kiswahili at Northwestern is a student organization dedicated to fostering an inclusive community where individuals from all backgrounds can come together to explore and celebrate the rich heritage of the Swahili-speaking world.

name: Mariachi Northwestern
description: Mariachi Northwestern is a student group that strives to expose our surrounding communities to Mariachi music and culture! We have performed at events ranging from campus activities to hired gigs all over the Chicagoland area. Any singer, violinist, guitarist, bass, accordionist, or trumpet player is welcome to join us, no Mariachi background or Spanish language prerequisite necessary.
summary: Northwestern's first and premier Mariachi group! Celebrating the culture and tradition of Mariachi music at NU. All NU students are welcome to join!

name: Masculinity, Allyship, Reflection, Solidarity
description:
We are a masculinity-focused organization dedicated to ending rape culture and "restrictive masculinity" on campus through self-work & peer-led discussions. MARS is a space to be vulnerable, genuinely learn about yourself, build connections with others, and be an ally for those impacted by various forms of violence on campus. You will learn skills in teaching, leading discussions, and developing activities aimed at exploring how re-imagining rigid societal expectations of masculinity can help lessen acts of violence as well as improve overall well-being. 


We are always accepting new members, just email us at mars@u.northwestern.edu!
summary: We are an all-masculine-identifying organization dedicated to ending rape culture & restrictive masculinity on campus through self-work & peer-led discussions. MARS is a space to be vulnerable, genuine, build connections & learn to support others.

name: Master of Science, Data Science (MSDS) Student Leadership Council
description: The Master of Science in Data Science (MSDS) at Northwestern University is one of the fastest growing and most established Data Science programs in the United States. The purpose of MSDS Student Leadership Council (SLC) is to help foster relationships among students in the program, alumni, and world-renowned industry professionals. The MSDS SLCcreates opportunities and events that allow students and alumni of the program to grow their knowledge of Data Science and their professional networks.
summary: The purpose of MSDS Student Leadership Council (SLC) is to help foster relationships a

name: Masters in Engineering Management Student Advisory Board
description: The Student Advisory Board for MEM is a group of MEM students that represent the student body. SAB hopes to foster community within MEM by hosting social events, increase relationships between MEM with other graduate programs, and to market the MEM brand. The greater goal is to increase the value of the MEM degree.
summary: The Student Advisory Board for MEM is a group of MEM students that represent the student body.  SAB hopes to foster community within MEM by hosting social events, increase relationships between MEM with other graduate programs.

name: Materials Science Club
summary: MatSci Club is the home for all undergraduate students interested in materials science and engineering.

name: Matriculate
description:
Matriculate leverages peer-to-peer relationships and networks to empower high-achieving, low-moderate-income high school students to enroll in their best-fit college or university. We train talented college students at high graduation rate institutions across the U.S. to provide the information, guidance, and support these high school students need to apply to, gain admission to, and excel in higher education.

Being a Matriculate Advising Fellow means making a commitment to guide and support high-achieving, low-income high school students across the country as they navigate the college admissions process. We’re looking for undergraduates who are passionate about helping others navigate the college process, have strong interpersonal skills, and above all, who want to make a difference in someone’s life.
summary: Matriculate’s mission is to support high-achieving, low-to-moderate-income high school students to make the leap to college. Our vision is that one day all students in the U.S. will have access to higher education regardless of socioeconomic status.

name: MaxForum
description:
Frozen October 2024

MaxForum is a student-run affiliation group created for the exchange of knowledge related to electronic music. Though we meet in the Bienen School of Music, our group welcomes students from across Northwestern with any musical or technological background. Our main focus is fostering an electronic music community by sharing ideas, tools, patches, and practices, and organizing community member-led teaching sessions. MaxForum will be made up of core and peripheral members. Core members, who agree to attend regularly throughout the quarter, are offered weeks throughout the quarter in which they can facilitate group discussion and learning sessions. Peripheral members are welcome to come as often as they choose, and may participate fully in any facilitated session. The main software of the club will be MaxMSP from Cycle ‘74, but over the course of this club other softwares like; Pure Data (PD), Csound, Ableton Live, Adobe Audition, will also become part.
summary: MaxForum is a student-run affiliation group created for the exchange of knowledge related to electronic music. Though we meet in Bienen School of Music, our group welcomes students from across Northwestern with any musical or technological background.

name: Mayfest Productions
description: Mayfest Productions is responsible for producing Dillo Day, the nation’s largest student-run music festival. Beyond Dillo, Mayfest hosts a series of special events throughout spring quarter including Battle of the Bands and Battle of the DJs which highlights student artists — the winners of our battle events earn a slot to perform on the Main Stage at Dillo Day. Along with our student artists, we also book A-list artists for our Main Stage and we collaborate with FMO, Northwestern's black student alliance, to book artists for our second stage - and it's all free to Northwestern students! That being said, Mayfest works year-round to ensure that Dillo Day is safe, fun, and inclusive for Northwesterners, their guests, and the greater Evanston community.Past artists include Daniel Caesar, Hippo Campus, A$AP Ferg, Rico Nasty, Regina Spektor, Kendrick Lamar, Steve Aoki, Chance the Rapper, 2chainz, The Mowgli's, Anderson .Pakk, and ScHoolboy Q. This year's lineup coming soon!
summary:
www.dilloday.com

Mayfest produces Dillo Day, as well as a series of special events throughout spring quarter. We book A-list artists for the largest student-run music festival in the country - and it's all free to Northwestern students.

name: McCormick Graduate Leadership Council
description:
The McCormick Graduate Leadership Council (MGLC) has three main goals:

  1. Build a graduate student community among the eight departments within the McCormick School.
  2. Organize social, academic, and professional activities
  3. Facilitate communication between McCormick graduate students and the McCormick Dean's office
summary: The McCormick Graduate Leadership Council helps graduate students connect through networking, professional development and social events.

name: McCormick Student Advisory Board
description:
The McCormick Student Advisory Board (MSAB) is composed of a diverse group of undergraduate engineering student representatives dedicated to representing the McCormick student body, identifying the needs of students, and working with the administration to unify the McCormick community and improve the McCormick experience. As such, MSAB serves as the student government within McCormick, acting as a liaison between other McCormick organizations, as well as with administration.

Through three committees (Project Development, Finance, and External Relations), MSAB has hosted various academic and social events for McCormick students, been directly involved in McCormick student organization funding, and developed strong relationships between students and faculty. Projects that MSAB has focused on in the past include hosting the annual Engineering Week (E-Week), a week-long celebration of engineering students with exciting events and free giveaways, as well as other quarterly social events.
summary: The McCormick Student Advisory Board comprises diverse undergraduate engineering students committed to representing the student body, identifying student needs, and working with administration to enhance the McCormick experience and foster community.

name: Mechanical Keyboard Club
description:
A club dedicated to fostering a community around the interest in mechanical keyboards. This is a platform for students to share their passion, learn about keyboard technology, and connect through events.
