Both keyboard enthusiasts and newcomers are welcome to engage in activities like meet-ups, workshops on keyboard assembly, and talks by experts in the field. 

 

If you love mechanical keyboards, join us and show us your precious collection!

 

If you are new, let us help you make your first perfect keeb!

 

Discord Server: https://discord.gg/f5Hvydb (most active!)

Email List: http://eepurl.com/iN0YEE

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/numechkeebs/
summary: An inclusive community built around the interest in mechanical keyboards.

name: Medical Makers
description: The purpose of Medical Makers is to innovate, design, and build medical devices that serve unmet clinical needs. Medical Makers aims to help members learn the prototyping process and acquire technical skills in medical device design.
summary: tba

name: Medill Undergraduate Student Advisory Council
description: MUSAC works to improve the student experience at Medill by listening to student feedback, organizing events and providing feedback on a number of initiatives. The council’s biggest event each year is the Student Showcase, where the best student work is displayed in the MTC Forum and Lobby for students, faculty and staff to enjoy.
summary: The Medill Undergraduate Student Advisory Council is a selection of freshmen, sophomore, junior and senior Medill students that represents the undergraduate community to members of Medill's administration on work with them collaboratively.

name: MEDLIFE
description: This organization is a chapter of the international organization, MEDLIFE. Our goals are to address global health issues to improve the overall health and wellbeing of communities in need. This is achieved through three pillars: Medicine, Education, and Development. Our chapter at Northwestern University hopes to foster a community of students passionate about promoting lasting developments in global health in both local and international communities. This is achieved through fundraising, volunteering, community building, and speakers events as well as service learning trips offered through the MEDLIFE organization.
summary: This organization is a chapter of the international organization, MEDLIFE. Our goals are to address global health issues to improve the overall health and wellbeing of communities in need.

name: Mee-Ow Improv and Sketch Comedy
description:
Founded in 1974, Mee-Ow Comedy is Northwestern University's premiere short-form comedy group combining sketch comedy, improv, and live music.  Mee-Ow has an impressive list of alumni, including Seth Meyers and Julia Louis-Dreyfus.  

Come audition for us, work with us, and check out our shows!  We can't wait to make something funny with you!
summary: Founded in 1974, Mee-Ow is Northwestern University's premiere short-form comedy group combining sketch, improv and rock n' roll.

name: Men's Basketball
description:
Frozen October 2024

The Northwestern Club Basketball Team has been competing against regional junior college and club teams for over 20 years. We serve as an outlet for the competitive desires of talented NU students, as well as a platform for exceptionally gifted players to make the next step up to the varsity team.

We are a proud member of the NCBBA league and compete in several games and tournaments throughout the school year. We also organize non-conference games with various schools within driving distance of NU. Anyone interested in playing is welcome to attend our tryout generally held the first week of October. 

If you have any questions or would like to learn more about the team, please feel free to contact us at cobygrant-krenz2025@u.northwestern.edu
summary: The Northwestern Club Basketball Team has been competing against regional junior college and club teams for over 20 years. We serve as an outlet for the competitive desires of talented NU students and as a platform for exceptionally gifted players

name: Men's Club Lacrosse
description:
MEN’S CLUB LACROSSE

The Men’s Club Lacrosse team is part of the Great Lakes Lacrosse League (GLLL). We play competitively against teams in and around the Chicago-land area. The fall season begins in September and runs to November. The spring season begins in February and extends to early May.

Games

We typically play anywhere between 2-4 games in the fall against teams in the Chicago-land area. The bulk of our schedule is in the spring which includes approximately 8-10 games during the regular season and a year-end bracket style tournament to determine the GLLL champions. We play all other teams in the “Chicago Division” of the GLLL: Northern Illinois, Western Illinois, University of Chicago, Lake Forest College, and Loyola University. We also play teams in the Central (e.g. University of Wisconsin – Madison) and Eastern (e.g. Notre Dame, School Craft) division during the regular season. We will occasionally travel on away games for regular season competition and the year-end tournament which is hosted on a rotating basis. Home games are hosted on the Lakeside field for an unforgettable experience. 

Practices

In the fall, we typically have 2 practices a week, usually on Monday and Wednesday. These practices are run by captains and are relatively flexible in terms of practice plan. The importance of these practices coincide with the game schedule, so after our final game in each quarter, players can focus on papers, finals, and whatever else may come up. 

In the winter, we reserve 2 practices in the University’s indoor turf facility. This allows us to keep high functioning practices in the bitter Chicago winter. 

In the spring, we practice 2 times a week, one inside and one outside by the lake. This allows us to get essential outside practices in the conditions we often play games in, while also preventing cold front or drastic weather from killing an entire weeks of practice.  

*Please note that the practice schedule may change according to our allocated field space and time each season.

Dues

All members are required to pay club dues, which are approximately $200. This is necessary to cover the league fees, referee fees, travel costs, apparel, and social events. Apparel will be ordered in the fall and arrive in the winter, additional apparel will be made available through the team shop. Members have an opportunity to order protective equipment (e.g. helmet, gloves) through the club at a discounted rate.

Tryouts

Practice will begin at the end of New Student week. We encourage players with any previous playing experience to join and truly welcome all to join. A percentage of the membership has not played lacrosse before arriving to Northwestern campus. In addition to playing lacrosse, the team arranges outings and gatherings in order to build a strong community for the players.

If you have any questions about the Men’s Lacrosse Club, please contact our president: 

Cole Soderlund

646-656-1527

colesoderlund2026@u.northwestern.edu
summary: The NU Men's Lacrosse Club welcomes all undergrads/grad students to join. Membership includes players with a wide range of skill from beginner to former NCAA athletes. We aim to have fun during practices and compete in the Great Lakes Lacrosse League.

name: Men's Club Soccer
description:
The NU Men's Club Soccer team is comprised of student athletes looking to continue playing competitive soccer without the restrictions and demands of a Varsity program during their undergraduate and graduate experiences. Our roster is brimming with talent, including former Varsity athletes and high school standouts, and we are exceptionally proud of the club's professionalism and level of play. In the past, our club finished twice in the Top 10 in the nation, we are extremely excited to continue to build on the club's recent successes.

Our fall season consists of 8 regular season games against other teams in the Metro division of the Midwest conference. Pending qualification, the Midwest regional tournament takes place in late October, and the national tournament takes place in mid-November. With practices two times a week and games on the weekend, the season is fast-paced and demands a sizable commitment, but does not conflict with class time.

The informal winter and spring seasons consist of occasional friendlies and two practices a week at the University's state-of-the-art indoor sports facility, Ryan Fieldhouse, as well as Martin Stadium.

THIS YEAR, TRYOUTS WILL BE SUNDAY, SEPTEMBER 18TH AND MONDAY SEPTEMBER 19th. THEY WILL BE LOCATED ON LONG FIELD, WHICH IS DIRECTLY NORTH OF ELDER. TIME TBD. Additional tryouts will occur depending on if a player is asked to return the following day. The roster tends to come in at around twenty-five to thirty athletes, and club members will be expected to pay a specified amount in team dues at the beginning of the season.

We practice and play our home matches at the fantastic Lakeside Field on the shore of Lake Michigan, and travel within the region to all of our away matches.

Although we are committed to success on the pitch, the very fabric of NU Men's Club Soccer is our commitment to camaraderie. Playing for the club is an awesome opportunity to continue to play competitive soccer, and provides a close-knit community for incoming students at the beginning of the academic year.
summary: The NU Men's Club Soccer team is comprised of student athletes looking to continue playing competitive soccer without the restrictions and demands of a Varsity program during their undergraduate and graduate experiences.

name: Men's Rugby Football Club
description:
Originally founded in the spring of 1972, the Northwestern University Men's Rugby club is a competitive Collegiate Division II rugby club in the National Collegiate Rugby organization.

Northwestern Men's Rugby's main season is during the fall, where it competes against teams such as Western Illinois, Eastern Illinois, Illinois State, Loyola, University of Chicago, etc. The squad, consisting of players with high levels of prior experience in rugby to players who have never heard of rugby, welcomes all players to join if they are interested in pursuing an active and new sport at the collegiate level.

Northwestern Rugby also competes during the spring, playing several Chicago area rivals as well as out-of-league squads. Northwestern Rugby are the 2007 CARFU Champions, the 2008 CARFU Runner-ups, and the 2009 CARFU Champions. More recently, NURFC finished the fall 2016 season as CARFU Champions and WISCARFU Championship Finalists. Additionally, Northwestern won the Chicago Cup in 2017. The team also competes every year in the Big Ten Sevens Tournament.

If interested, please contact Conlan Harrington (President) at conlanharrington2025@u.northwestern.edu. Feel free to visit our Instagram seen below for up-to-date practice and team information.
summary: Originally founded in the spring of 1972, the Northwestern University Men's Rugby club is a member of the NCR organization. Follow us on Facebook and Instagram for event information.

name: Men's Ultimate Frisbee
description:
Hi all!

We practice 2-3 times a week and attend 6-8 tournaments over the calendar year, competing in USA Ultimate's Division 1 College Series. All are welcome at our practices regardless of skill level or prior experience!

 

Some words from Chucktown (NUT ’03, Coach ’12-’13):

  • Learn how to play the right way and how to be the man you want to be in the world. The winning, the fulfillment of your hopes and dreams: it all comes in time. We can’t control when or where it comes. We can control how we show up and how much we choose to believe in the process. We CAN buy in.

Email captains@nutultimate.com with any questions! If you're interested in playing with us in 2024-25, please fill out this interest form. 

2024 Incoming Player Form

Best,

Captains
summary: Northwestern Men's Club Ultimate team.

name: Men's Volleyball
description:
Do you have high school varsity or club volleyball experience and want to continue playing at a high level in college? Northwestern Men's Club Volleyball is an opportunity for students to do just that. We practice twice a week and compete in several matches and tournaments per quarter. We are a member of MIVA (Midwest Intercollegiate Volleyball Association) and annually compete against Big 10 teams at various tournaments throughout the Midwest.

---

Join our men's volleyball team if you’re looking for thrilling competition and great brotherhood!

 

Here is our 2024 tryouts schedule:

Wednesday, October 2nd 9-11PM

Thursday, October 3rd 9-11 PM
summary: NU Men's Volleyball is an opportunity for players who played competitive volleyball in high school at the club/varsity level to continue playing on a high-level travel team with multiple matches and tournaments each quarter.

name: Menstrual Equity Activists
description:
Our three pillars:

1. Service: Provide free, safe, and accessible menstrual products to Evanston service partners (homeless shelters, free clinics, food pantries, LGBTQI+ resource centers, domestic violence shelters, schools, and other non-profits) to fulfill the needs of our community

2. Education: Educate our local community on menstrual health and equity to destigmatize menstruation

3. Policy: Advocate for systemic change through policy and legislation. Reach out to state representatives, canvass, and lobby

 

During the school year, our members work to implement a variety of community initiatives within Northwestern and beyond:

1. Sustainability: Raise awareness about sustainable menstrual products, such as menstrual cups and period underwear, and distribute them to college students and community partners.

2. Menstrual Products in Dorms: Advocate for the implementation of free menstrual products in dorms by organizing petitions, meeting with campus administrators, and creating student advocacy initiatives.

3. International Outreach: Fundraise for menstrual education projects around the world that distribute reusable, environmentally sustainable sanitary kits.
summary: We are a menstrual equity activism group that works to alleviate period poverty and period stigma through service, education, and policy reform in Evanston, the state of Illinois, and around the world.

name: Mental Health Mix & Mingle
description:
Frozen October 2024

Mental Health Mix and Mingle (MHMM) is a group for graduate students in the two programs housed within The Family Institute at Northwestern University (Counseling and Marriage & Family Therapy). The group is designed to bring together the students from both programs in social, professional, and educational events to foster connections and relationships among peers.
summary: Mental Health Mix and Mingle is a graduate student-based group that aims to connect the two graduate programs housed within The Family Institute (Counseling and Marriage & Family Therapy).

name: Mentorship Opportunities for Research and Engagement
description:
Frozen October 2024

MORE is a student organization that provides opportunities for Northwestern graduate students and postdocs to mentor and empower high school students interested in STEM. This mentorship takes a variety of forms, including one-on-one mentoring for independent research projects, group discussion panels on STEM careers and research, and MORE! We would love to have you on board with us to participate in outreach activities and help inspire the next generation of STEM students!
summary: MORE is a student organization that provides opportunities for Northwestern graduate students and postdocs to mentor and empower high school students interested in STEM.

name: Mewtwo Pokémon League
description:
This Pokémon League partakes in the following activities:

  • Provides opportunities for computer science, AI, and game design education as they relate to the Pokémon franchise.
  • Fields an interscholastic Pokémon eSports team.
  • Hosts social events that relate to the Pokémon franchise, such as game release parties, Pokémon movie nights, or gaming tournaments. 
  • Maintains a “Gym Challenge” experience that emulates the Gym system found in the Pokémon video games. 
  • Provides gameplay challenges for club members who are interested in the main-series Pokémon games.
summary: Love competitive Pokémon or the Pokémon franchise in general? We're the first Pokémon video game club at Northwestern University!

name: Middle Eastern North African Student Association
description:
We celebrate and aim to educate about the unique cultural identities of the MENA region through events centering cultural music, dance, and food. Everyone is welcome to our events!

In the past we have hosted many events such as: Bonfires, mixers, Shai (tea) nights, cooking cultural dishes nights (koshari and warak enab), dabke (traditional Levantine dance) workshops, Arab Heritage month programming such as Arab Expressions, Assyrian New Years event, Valentine’s day kickback, game night, karaoke night, and MENA Formal where we rented out a venue and brought a professional belly dancer, dabke group, and had a traditional MENA zaffe. 

We also advocate for the visibility of MENA students on campus, by demanding more representation on campus and advocating for human rights regarding issues in the MENA region. 

Please follow our Instagram @mena.northwestern to stay up to date with events; feel free to contact us through DM! Also please fill out our listserv form in order to receive email updates!
summary: The MENA SA is a community that serves as a home away from home for Middle Eastern North African (MENA) students on campus and allows for MENA students to represent and share their heritage with the greater NU community.

name: MiniChefz
description: MiniChefz is a non-profit organization comprised of college students. We have worked together to develop a curriculum based around nutrition, healthy eating, and a healthy lifestyle. Our volunteers dedicate themselves to traveling into neighboring communities and working with the youth population in order to help encourage healthier life choices. Nutrition is a key component to a healthy life. Our goal is to create healthy eating habits for youth to prevent many health issues that arise in adulthood.
summary: MiniChefz is an organization that looks to encourage healthy eating and nutrition among the youth of our city.

name: Minority Association of Pre-Med Students at Northwestern
description: The purpose of MAPS is to serve the minority pre-health students of Northwestern University, by providing valuable information that pre-health students need, prior to attending medical or graduate school, through meetings, workshops, events, etc. This organization works diligently to try to stimulate the interests, of pre-health students at Northwestern University by encouraging and promoting all minorities in medicine and other health professions. As the representative voice of Northwestern’s minority pre-health students, MAPS seeks to instill a consciousness in its members about contemporary issues and disparities of the medical world, especially those that pertain to minority groups. MAPS aims to prepare its members for the rigors of their health and science careers in an efficient manner.
summary: The purpose of MAPS is to serve the minority pre-health students of Northwestern by providing valuable information that pre-health students need, prior to attending medical or graduate school, through meetings, workshops, events, etc.

name: MIXED Student Association
description: Multiracial Identity Xperience Education and Dialogue (MIXED) Student Association, was founded in 2013 as the Mixed Student Coalition and renamed MIXED Student Association in 2020. MIXED was founded to provide a space for those who identify with a multiracial background or have interest in mixed race affairs. MIXED serves to create an inclusive community for people of all racial/ethnic backgrounds by hosting fun activities as well as generating discussions and bringing in speakers with an emphasis on the multiracial experience.
summary: MIXED serves to create an inclusive community for people of all racial/ethnic backgrounds by hosting fun activities as well as generating discussions and bringing in speakers with an emphasis on the multiracial experience.

name: Mock Trial
description: Northwestern Mock Trial (NUMT), a top 10 finishing team at Nationals the past six out of seven years, competes against the top programs across the nation in simulated trial tournaments where participants are scored for their advocacy skills as lawyers and performance as witnesses. The program offers team members the opportunity to improve their public speaking abilities, analytical skills, trial advocacy techniques and acting capabilities while experiencing the drama of a courtroom. Mock Trial is perfect for those who are competitive, enjoy public speaking or love to act. We take new members from all experience levels (more than half of our members joined with no mock trial experience!) and majors, whether you're interested in the law or just love performing in front of others. No matter your interests, there's a spot for you on NUMT!
summary: NUMT competes against the top programs across the nation in simulated trial tournaments where participants are scored for their advocacy skills as lawyers and performance as witnesses.

name: Mood Psychology
description:
Frozen October 2024

Mood Psychology is a peer support group mainly for the Chinese international undergraduate and graduate students at Northwestern University. Follow us on WeChat: 木禾心理NU to get the most updated activity notification!

As an official school chapter at NU of a larger NGO Muhe Health Support Group, we mainly have three goals:
1. Resources Sharing：
Link resources around psychological science, counseling services, mental health awareness, and self-growth to our targeted student group in a more accessible, peer-centered, and easy-to-understand way. We have compiled a mental-health resource list in Mandarin to our peers.

2. Community Building：
Through online and in-person activities, we want to strengthen the connections within Chinese students in NU and advocate for this group to connect with other communities in NU. We will provide peer-support by providing stress-reduction, connection-building, and student-led activities.

3. Career Networking:
Students will build connections with peers, mentors, professionals who have experiences already as mental health professionals. Also, students working as executive board will gain hands-on NGO-certified experience to help their future educational and occupational networking.
summary: Mood Psychology is a mental health support group mainly for the Chinese international undergraduate and graduate students at Northwestern University.

name: Morning Mentors
description: Frozen October 2024
summary: Morning Mentors aims to develop the graduate community by providing an opportunity for community service and bringing together students of different departments with similar goals and interests by facilitating a tutoring program for a local school.

name: Moroccan Student Organization
description: • Moroccan Student Organization provides a supportive community for Moroccan students and represents Moroccan culture on campus.
summary: This association provides a supportive community for Moroccan students and represents Moroccan culture on campus.

name: Multicultural Filmmakers Collective
description: The purpose of the Multicultural Filmmakers Collective is to nurture, promote, and ally multicultural student filmmakers at Northwestern University, as well as support student films that present multicultural concerns and experiences. We define multicultural as including, but not limited to, underrepresented races, ethnicities, and religions. In addition to general meetings, our programming includes film screenings, script workshops, and speaker events.
summary: The purpose of the Multicultural Filmmakers Collective is to nurture, promote, and ally multicultural student filmmakers at Northwestern University, as well as support student films that present multicultural concerns and experiences.

name: Multicultural Greek Council
description: The MGC is a unique community of fraternities and sororities; each of these member organizations’ foundations is founded upon celebrating cultural diversity and educating the community about its rich cultural heritage.  The MGC and the Greek-letter organizations are powerful media through which group members can express and share their cultural pride.  The members cherish the belief that the college experience is more valuable when it includes self-expression through the richness of diversity.
summary: The MGC is a unique community of fraternities and sororities; each of these member organizations' foundations is founded upon celebrating cultural diversity and educating the community about its rich cultural heritage.

name: Multicultural Student Affairs
description: Multicultural Student Affairs is committed to fostering an inclusive environment that promotes identity development, intercultural exchange, academic excellence, and leadership. While our departments primarily focus on traditionally under-served student populations, we strive to play an important role in creating an institutional climate that values diversity and educates the Northwestern community about cultural competence and social justice.
summary: Multicultural Student Affairs is committed to fostering an inclusive environment that promotes identity development, intercultural exchange, academic excellence, and leadership. While our departments primarily focus on traditionally under-served student

name: Music for Memory
description: We educate the student body about the physical, emotional, and mental effects of music in hopes to raise awareness and accessibility of music therapy on campus. By sharing knowledge, we strive to implement music therapy interventions for both students and individuals with Alzheimer’s disease.
summary: We educate the student body about the physical, emotional, and mental effects of music in hopes to raise awareness and accessibility of music therapy on campus.

name: Music Tasting Club
description: The Music Tasting Club is NU's premier music discourse group. Do you have a music taste you'd consider niche? Are you looking for an outlet to gush about your favorite artist or album? Are you sick of listening to the same artist(s) or song(s)? Are you generally looking for new music recommendations? Then the Music Tasting Club is the place for you! Imagine a weekly book club, but instead of books, we listen to albums and songs each week. Every week, different members of the club choose albums and songs (as of Spring 2024, members pick the Album of the Week, Runner Up Album of the Week, Song of the Week, and Runner Up Song of the Week) and one week later we come back to discuss. What do we discuss? Anything and everything that members thought about the album. From genres to specific instruments to artists to personal significance, everything is on the table for discussion. We'll have debates about different bodies of work, if they're good or bad and why. If we finish early, we play music related activities. For example, we play games like 'guess the blurred out album art' or 'guess the song' trivia. Each week we further the discourse about different musical bodies of work and expose people to different songs, EPs, mixtapes, and albums that they may not have otherwise have been aware of. Additionally, we give people the tools to critically analyze the music they listen to. If any of this sounded interesting to you, stop on by! Come with an open mind, and a willingness to share your opinions. Happy listening everybody!
summary: Do you have a music taste you'd consider niche? Are you generally looking for new music recommendations? Then the Music Tasting Club is the place for you! Imagine a weekly book club, but instead of books, we listen to albums/songs. Join today!

name: Music4Joy
description: NU Music4Joy is a community service organization at Northwestern University that works towards conducting live performances by Northwestern students in Evanston and Chicago hospitals, such as Trulee Evanston's Memory Care Floor (Alzheimer's & Dementia patients), NorthShore Evanston/Skokie Hospitals, and Institute of Therapy Through the Arts! Our primary goal is to utilize the power of music to foster supportive relationships between students and the ill/elderly while also creating a joyful/peaceful atmosphere within healthcare institutions. Current and past research by various credible institutions, such as Project CHROMA and the Cleveland Clinic, has proven that music serves as not only a form of entertainment but also a therapy for patients, bolstering their emotional, mental, and physical well-being. We hope that the different styles, genres, and timbres of music performed by students of diverse backgrounds and interests will create an environment in which all patients, hospital staff, and visitors experience an improvement in their overall health. These live performances will provide patients/visitors/hospital staff an opportunity to actively interact with the musician, allowing the two parties to form a unique bond forged with kindness and a love of music. Furthermore, since music therapy has been scientifically proven to help improve a patient’s quality of life, we aspire to encourage students to harness the power of their own music to support patients with this goal in mind. Music4Joy will support our healthcare community by organizing musicians to perform in nursing homes, patients’ rooms, lobby, and other areas in the hospital. We understand that by ourselves, we can not cure the physical illnesses of patients. However, we do hope to alleviate their pain/suffering and contribute to improving their overall well-being. Feel free to reach out if you have any questions!
summary: NU Music4Joy is a community service organization at Northwestern University that works towards conducting live performances by Northwestern students in Evanston and Chicago hospitals, specifically NorthShore Evanston and Skokie Hospitals.

name: Muslim Business Student Association
description: Muslims in Business is a student-led organization dedicated to empowering Muslim students to explore and thrive in the world of business. Our mission is to increase Muslim representation in business professions by equipping our members with the resources, mentorship, and community support they need to succeed—both academically and professionally. We aim to demystify the business world, provide insider knowledge, and help our members get ahead through shared experiences, curated opportunities, and practical guidance. By fostering a strong, tight-knit community rooted in mutual support and shared values, we help one another navigate the unique challenges and opportunities of business education and careers. In a landscape where Muslims are often underrepresented in business compared to fields like medicine, law, or engineering, Muslims in Business seeks to shift that narrative—paving the way for the next generation of Muslim leaders, innovators, and changemakers in business.
summary: Muslims in Business is a student-led organization dedicated to empowering Muslim students to explore and thrive in the world of business.

name: Muslim-cultural Students Association
description: The Muslim-cultural Students Association (McSA) is a community oriented organization that caters to the many different needs of Northwestern students. Our members are Muslim and non-Muslim alike. Even with such a diverse membership, our fundamental goals remain the same. Be it Discover Islam Week, philanthropy food drives, Ramadan dinners, or simply tutoring younger students, our primary motive is to promote the human values that Islam emphasizes: community, equality, justice, mercy, compassion and peace. In addition, by supporting other organizations or events, we hope to foster unity not only in McSA, but also among other NU student organizations, faculty and administrators.
summary: The Muslim-cultural Students Association (McSA) is a community oriented organization that caters to the many different needs of Muslim Northwestern students. Our motive is to promote the values that Islam emphasizes: community, equality, & justice.

name: National Alzheimer's Buddies
description: As the Northwestern University chapter of the National Alzheimer’s Buddies organization, our purpose is to build meaningful connections between students and cognitively impaired individuals affected by Alzheimer’s Disease and dementia. NAB is an intergenerational movement responding to the profound isolation and social disengagement experienced by people in the intermediate-to-late stages of Alzheimer’s Disease who are living in long-term care facilities. Weekly, our volunteers spend time with the residents of Trulee, Evanston, fostering meaningful bonds and supporting their days.
summary: The NU chapter of National Alzheimer’s Buddies aims to facilitate the formation of meaningful relationships between college students and cognitively impaired individuals, affected by Alzheimer's disease, who are residing in long-term care facilities.

name: National Arab-American Medical Association (NAAMA)
description: The organization’s purpose is to provide mentoring and advice to the younger Arab-American pre-medical students. One goal is to stay connected to the National Arab-American Medical Association (NAAMA) national chapter as this will allow us to expand opportunities and help the Northwestern students build their NAAMA network. Because most pre-med courses and commitments (research, shadowing, etc.) often start from a first-year’s first day on campus, we aim to build a student cycle where older students continue to feed and mentor the younger students as classes/research opportunities continue to evolve. By fostering a collaborative community, we aim to provide guidance, resources, and opportunities for success in their pre-medical journey.
summary: The organization’s purpose is to provide mentoring and advice to the younger Arab-American pre-medical students.

name: National Association of Black Journalists
description:
The National Association of Black Journalists serves as a resource and outlet for graduate and undergraduate students interested in pursuing a career in mass communications and journalism.
As an auspice, the Northwestern chapter seeks to uphold an organic atmosphere and community in which all of our members can grow professionally and personally. NABJ-NU also creates opportunities for students to develop meaningful relationships, harness impressive skills and build confidence in their careers.
NABJ welcomes students who are majoring in journalism and also those who are not.
summary: The NU chapter of the National Association of Black Journalists is a student organization that provides resources, professional development opportunities, and a community space for black students seeking careers in journalism or media.

name: National Association of Hispanic Journalists
description: The National Association of Hispanic Journalists (NAHJ) is dedicated to the recognition and professional advancement of Hispanics in the news industry. Established in April 1984, NAHJ created a national voice and unified vision for all Hispanic journalists. Northwestern's student chapter is the first of its kind in the nation and is looking for anyone who has any interest in Hispanic media affairs.
summary: The National Association of Hispanic Journalists (NAHJ) is dedicated to therecognition and professional advancement of Hispanics in the news industry.Established in April 1984, NAHJ created a national voice and unified vision for allHispanic

name: National Marrow Donor Program (NMDP)
description:
The Northwestern University chapter of National Marrow Donor Program (NMDP) aims to increase awareness of the need for more young registry members, educate others about the under representation of ethnic minorities in the registry, and hold campus-wide marrow donor registry drives to give the entire student body the opportunity to join in this global effort. 

We welcome any student to join! In National Marrow Donor Program (NMDP), we partner with other groups to host events, have fundraising activities, hold registry drives, host speakers, and more. Members will be involved in planning and holding events. For more information, feel free to contact us or check out our facebook page!
summary: Be the Match aims to register students and faculty for the national marrow donor program, and raise awareness of the minority ethnic groups that are underrepresented within the registry.

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council
description:
The National Pan-Hellenic Council (NPHC) at Northwestern University consists of seven of the "Divine Nine" historically black fraternities and sororities. Each of the NPHC organizations evolved during a period when African Americans were denied essential rights and privileges afforded to other college students. NPHC organizations maintain high positive standards for its member organizations and ensure the perpetuation of responsibility and privilege through engaging in service to the African American community at NU and the community at large.

 

The 2021-2022 Council of Presidents & Officers:

Council of Presidents

Marc Montgomery - Alpha Mu (Vice Chair)

Eden Strong - Eta

Samad Acklin - Theta (Chair)

Kamaryn Johnson - Theta Alpha

Officers

Director of Finance - Mychael Torres

Director of PR & Marketing - Jessica Hunter

Director of Justice Initiatives - Sam Anderson
summary: The National Pan-Hellenic Council, Inc. (NPHC) at Northwestern University consists of seven of the "Divine Nine" historically black fraternities and sororities.

name: National Residence Hall Honorary
description: NRHH strives to provide recognition for individuals who have contributed to the advancement of college and university housing. It ensures the advancement of member chapters through resource sharing, programming, and leadership development opportunities.
summary: Northwestern University's chapter of the National Residence Hall Honorary

name: National Society of Black Engineers - Northwestern Chapter
description: The National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE)'s mission is to increase the number of culturally responsible black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community. We host professional development workshops, company sponsored networking events, and community building activities. You do not have to be a member to attend any of our events, so come out and see what NU NSBE is all about!
summary: The National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE)'s mission is to increase the number of culturally responsible black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community.

name: National Student Speech-Language-Hearing Association-NU
description:
Frozen October 2024

The NU NSSLHA Chapter is a pre-professional association for graduate and undergraduate students interested in the study of communication sciences and disorders (CSD).  We are one of over 300 university chapters representing the greater national organization of NSSLHA, which is the only student organization recognized by the American Speech-Language-Hearing Association (ASHA), the national professional, scientific, and credentialing association for the communication sciences and disorders.
summary: The NU NSSLHA Chapter is a pre-professional association for graduate and undergraduate students interested in the study of communication sciences and disorders (CSD).

name: Naval Reserve Officers Training Corps
description:
The Northwestern NROTC unit was established as one of six original units in the United States. Today, the program develops midshipmen mentally, morally and physically and imbues them with the highest ideals of duty, and loyalty.

We welcome all freshmen and sophomores to contact Michael Jin for more information.
summary: The NROTC Program was established to educate and train qualified young men and women for service as commissioned officers in the Navy and Marine Corps.

name: Neuro Club
description:
The Northwestern University Neuro Club brings together a diverse set of students interested in any of the numerous fields of Neuroscience. We hope this club will not only inform the Northwestern community of the many facets of Neuroscience but also centralize the resources of both of the Northwestern Campuses to give a well-organized structure for education in Neuroscience. We hope to accomplish these things through events, community-service projects, and other ways.

The Community Service Committee works with local Evanston and Chicago schools, nursery homes, and organizations to plan community service events at least once per quarter. 

The Speaker Committee brings in (at least one) speaker from the field of Neuroscience per quarter.

The Public Relations Committee publicizes club events and helps recruit committed members who are willing to contribute to the organization.
summary: The Northwestern University Neuro Club is a student-run organization with the goal of furthering the education and understanding of neuroscience and related fields within and surrounding the Northwestern University community.

name: New Life Volunteering Society
description:
New Life Volunteering Society is a non-profit volunteering organization for students from medical schools at CMS, UChicago, Northwestern, Loyola, and UIC and undergraduates from Northwestern, Loyola, and UIC. The NLVS organization has a clinic in Little India in the Devon neighborhood where medical and undergraduate students can volunteer. At Northwestern, we are a health-oriented volunteering club that helps serve the Chicago and local Evanston communities. Some of our initiatives include designing and presenting health series at homeless centers, tutoring, and volunteering at local community centers, retirement homes, and health clinics, such as the NLVS clinic.

NLVS members are also connected with the NLVS chapter at Northwestern's Feinberg School of Medicine and we hold events with them. Please contact us with any questions!
summary: New Life Volunteering Society (NLVS) provides health-related service and leadership opportunities for Northwestern students.  We have ties to health clinics in the Chicago area, the Feinberg School of Medicine, and Evanston-based organizations.

name: New Movement Project
description: New Movement Project aims to enrich and enhance the dance experience of all actively motivated members of the Northwestern dance community. New Movement Project utilizes it resources as well as departmental, university, and community partnerships to provide students with elements of the dance experience not regularly provided by the curriculum. New Movement Project strives to facilitate the creative goals of dancers at Northwestern by: acting as a liaison between the Dance Program and students, offering performance and choreographic opportunities, and coordinating with the student dance community at NU.
summary: NMP's mission is to provide students with the resources and programming to support their artistic discoveries through dance, as well as act as a liaison between the NU dance community and the dance department.

name: New Student Organization Review Committee
summary: This group meets quarterly to deliberate on proposals for new registered student organizations to be recognized.

name: Nigerian Student Organization
description: The Nigerian Student Organization (NSO) aims to connect students to Nigerian culture through fostering unity and cultural awareness. Our goal is to create a supportive environment where students can connect, thrive academically, and celebrate their cultural heritage. Membership is open to all students who are of Nigerian descent or have an interest in Nigerian culture.
summary: The Nigerian Student Organization (NSO) aims to connect students to Nigerian and West African culture through fostering unity and cultural awareness.

name: Niteskool
description:
Frozen October 2024

Niteskool is the premier music management and video production group dedicated to finding and promoting the most talented musicians at Northwestern. We aim to bring these artists into the spotlight on campus and catapult them to success in the broader music industry. Niteskool Productions focuses on producing high-quality music videos to connect Northwestern students to their talented peers and provide otherwise unavailable opportunities to student musicians. Meanwhile, Niteskool Records focuses on manging student artists, with dedicated A&R members, a community marketing team, and giving artists opportunities to create their own videos, or create recorded performances at school events. Together, under the Niteskool banner, we serve to empower student artists and create a community where anyone can gain firsthand experience in the music industry!
summary: Niteskool is the premier music management/'record label' and video production group on campus. Whether through highly professional student-produced music content, or our dedicated teams for Marketing and A&R, Niteskool aims to engage with all NU artists.

name: No Fun Mud Piranhas
description: n/a
summary: An audition-free improv group providing a space for students to practice their skills, learn new techniques, and enjoy the art of improv in a supportive environment. Weekly meetings and quarterly shows.

name: Norris Box Office
description: The Norris Box Office serves as a ticketing agent for student organizations holding accounts with the Student Organization Finance Office or a University department.  Services include ticket printing and sales (on-site and on-line), individual stamp and envelope sales (cash only), and domestic fax send/receive.
summary: Northwestern University department within Student Affairs serving as primary ticket printing and sales agent for registered Northwestern University student organizations and departments. Located on the first floor of Norris University Center.

name: Norris Cashier's Office
description: The Cashier's Office works in conjunction with the Student Organization Finance Office to maintain a student organization banking structure that faciliates transaction-based requests.  It offers starting funds, depository and coin counting services, as well as notary services.
summary: An office within the Cash Operations Area that offers deposit and starting fund services for student organizations.

name: Norris University Center
summary: Home to an array of services, resources, and meeting spaces, Norris University Center is the center of campus life at Northwestern community.

name: North by Northwestern
description:
North by Northwestern Northwestern’s general-interest student magazine and website. Our website launched over the summer of 2006, and in spring 2008, we launched a print magazine. Both online and off, we report the truth, tell stories and have fun. This is a site where we can talk about what’s actually on our minds, share our passions and have a conversation with our readers.

We’ve been honored with 36 Mark of Excellence awards from the Society of Professional Journalists and won the 2008 National Online Pacemaker Awards.

North by Northwestern is proud to be a member of the Campus Progress journalism network.

Let us know if you want to get involved. We’re always looking for new writers, editors, photographers, videographers, multimedia producers, designers, social media managers, marketers and businesspeople.
summary: North by Northwestern is one of Northwestern University's best independent online publications, updated around the clock with stories about campus and culture. In Spring 2008, we launched a print magazine.

name: Northwestern Anime Club
description:
Welcome to NU Anime Club! Our group brings together students at Northwestern University for gatherings and events focused on all things Japan: anime, manga, video games and much more.

Check us out on Facebook and feel free to email us with questions! NU Anime Club FB 

Our group prides itself on having diverse interests and offering activities to promote them. Although each week you will find us meeting to watch the latest shows from Japan, we spend time as a group doing much more:

  • Art Group/Doujinshi Creation
  • Restaurant and Boba Trips
  • Manga Store Visits
  • Gunpla and Model Building
  • Video and Board Games
  • Karaoke
  • Cosplay
  • Guest Speakers
  • Local Conventions
  • Group Trips
  • Theater Showings

Join Our Discord!
summary: Come enjoy Anime, Japan's unique style of animation, with NU Anime Club. Experience the wealth of genres and styles that Anime has to offer and talk about it with us!

name: Northwestern Art Review
description:
Frozen October 2024

The Northwestern Art Review is an academic organization based at Northwestern University that seeks to promote art historical discourses at the undergraduate level. By publishing an annual journal of art scholarship and critique, the Northwestern Art Review strives to provide a forum for greater exchange among an expanding community of students who devote their time to studying, thinking and writing about art. Additionally, the Northwestern Art Review serves as a dynamic force on campus and within the Chicago Area promoting art and creatively influenced events and programming.
summary: The Northwestern Art Review is an academic organization based at Northwestern University that seeks to promote art historical discourses at the undergraduate level. By publishing an annual journal of art scholarship and critique, the Northwestern Art

name: Northwestern Bhangra Dance Team
description: Passion, dedication, and talent united to form Northwestern Bhangra in 2006. An enthusiastic co-ed university dance team known for its energy on and off the stage, the troupe performs bhangra, a traditional folk dance originating in the Indian state of Punjab. The team has performed at numerous campus events, imprinted its name all over the Chicagoland area, and entertained the bhangra community across the nation. Throughout the years, the team has performed at prestigious competitions such as Bhangra in the Bell, Bhangra Blowout, Bhangra in the Burgh, Nachte Raho, and many others. As they continue to inspire others with their dynamic style, the bhangra team shares and celebrates Punjabi culture with the Northwestern community. Northwestern Bhangra is a true family, and they always animate the stage with personality and originality while remembering their traditional roots.
summary: Founded in 2006, Northwestern Bhangra aims to promote awareness of Punjabi culture through dance. Since our founding, we have grown into a team that performs at events all around the Chicagoland area and at competitions across the nation.

name: Northwestern Career Advancement
description: The mission of Northwestern Career Advancement (formerly University Career Services) is to foster excellence in career development, preparation, and professional opportunity for undergraduate and graduate students and alumni by providing comprehensive services and programming and by promoting strong partnerships with employers, academic departments, and the university community.
summary: The mission of Northwestern Career Advancement (formerly UCS) is to foster excellence in career development, preparation, and professional opportunity for undergraduate and graduate students and alumni by providing comprehensive services.

name: Northwestern Climate Technology Club
description: CTC organizes student seminars, guest lectures, discussion groups, local company/startup/lab tours, and social outings for students. Our events focus on themes of climate tech, clean energy, environmental impacts, and resource management. We have about 10 officers on the board, and we are continually recruiting students to become members or join the board.  Please visit https://ctc.tgs.northwestern.edu/ to learn more.
summary: Climate Tech Club (CTC), formerly Northwestern Energy Technology Group, aims to advance the research and understanding of climate, energy, and sustainability technologies by providing educational and collaborative opportunities for Northwestern students.

name: Northwestern Club Swim Team
description:
Northwestern Swim Club was founded in 2008 providing a club swimming opportunity for undergraduate students at Northwestern. We practice three to four times a week at Norris Aquatics Center at SPAC. We typically go to two to three meets a quarter. We host our annual home meet during the beginning of winter quarter. Our competition season ends with a trip to CCS Nationals in early April. All swimmers are welcome to join, regardless of skill level!

 

Practice Schedule for Spring 2025:

Tuesday: 8:30PM-10:00PM 
Wednesday Dryland: 8:15PM-9:15PM
Thursday: 8:30PM-10:00PM 
Saturday: 4:00PM-6:00PM 

 

Tentative Practice Schedule for Fall Quarter 2025:

Tuesday: 8:30PM-10:00PM 
Saturday: 4:00PM-6:00PM 
Sunday: 4:00PM-6:00PM 

Weekly Dryland Session (Time TBD, depends on club members' availability)
summary: Northwestern Club Swimming was founded in 2008 providing a club swimming opportunity for undergraduate students at Northwestern. We practice three to four times a week at Norris Aquatics Center at SPAC and attend meets throughout the year.

name: Northwestern Community Ensemble
description: Founded in 1971, NCE is Northwestern's premier gospel choir who performs music in the Black Christian tradition. We seek to provide Northwestern's African American community as well as the broader Northwestern community opportunities to engage with Black culture, gospel music, and Christian faith through our concerts and other performances. Check out our most recent winter concert, featuring 2-time Grammy-award-winning gospel superstar Tye Tribbett, here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SRTK3SvOS8o&t=2711s
summary: We are Northwestern's premier gospel choir founded in 1971 performing spiritual, gospel, and anthem music from the Black Christian tradition.

name: Northwestern Crew
description:
Northwestern Crew is a co-ed club sport that competes with other club and varsity programs throughout the county. We are an active team throughout the entire year with our main seasons in fall and spring and indoor training in winter. Practices take place at the Dammrich Rowing Center in Skokie during fall and spring season and at the Henry Crown Sports Pavilion during winter season.

While learning to row/cox and working on technique and speed, novice (new) rowers and coxswains will participate in 1 - 2 races in fall and 3 - 4 races in spring. The team takes a spring training trip during Spring Break to prepare for spring racing in Oak Ridge, TN.

Novice practices will take place weekday mornings and on selected weekends at the coaches' discretion. Racing takes place on the weekends as well.

Experienced rowers and coxswains are welcome to practice and race at varsity level. All are welcome!

Please contact Maya Kraidy: vwomenscaptain@northwesterncrew.com for all women’s team inquiries and Benjamin Spinrad: vmenscaptain@northwesterncrew.com for all men’s team inquiries.
summary: Northwestern Crew is a co-ed club sport that competes with other club and varsity programs throughout the county. We are an active team throughout the entire year with our main seasons in fall and spring and indoor training in winter.

name: Northwestern Economics Tournament
description:
The purpose of Northwestern Economics Tournament (NET) is to give high school students opportunities to explore the field of economics before entering college. We aim to introduce high school students to economics in a welcoming environment through competitions and interactive speaker events. NET also encourages the participation of Northwestern students, who plan and organize the event. 

We’re looking for students from all backgrounds who are passionate about economics and can think creatively about adapting NET to a permanent hybrid format as we look forward to a post-pandemic landscape. We also welcome students interested in high school education and event planning.
summary: We are a small, close-knit, and collaborative group of Northwestern students who organize an annual economics tournament for high school students from the Chicago area and around the country.

name: Northwestern Emergency Medical Organization
description:
Details & the application for our Winter 2020 Basic Emergency Medical Technician (EMT-B) training course can be found here: https://tinyurl.com/nemo-app-2020. Applications have closed for this year! For the 2022-23 EMT-B Course, please check back in next fall. For more information and questions, please email nemo@u.northwestern.edu.

Northwestern Emergency Medicine Organization serves to promote a greater visibility for the broad-range applications of emergency medicine on campus and within the Greater Chicago area. Whereas other health organizations on campus attract pre-health students exclusively, NEMO teaches life-saving knowledge and skills to the entire Northwestern community.
summary:
Interested in Emergency Medicine?
Want to learn more about how to deal with medical emergencies? We offer an EMT-B course and other opportunities to learn and teach life-saving knowledge to the NU community!

name: Northwestern Energy and Sustainability Club
description: The Northwestern Energy and Sustainability Club (NESC) brings together undergraduates passionate about energy and sustainability. As a pre-professional organization, NESC offers members opportunities to connect with industry experts, participate in recruitment events, attend talks from leading professionals, and explore various industries through field trips. Our mission is to provide students with the experience, education, and networks necessary to create meaningful change in the energy and sustainability fields. Students from all academic backgrounds are encouraged to join! To get involved and learn more, refer to our Linktree.
summary: A community of students passionate about the energy and sustainability industry, providing educational, professional, and networking.

name: Northwestern Formula Racing
description:
Formula SAE

Formula SAE (FSAE) is an intercollegiate engineering design competition organized by the Society of Automotive Engineers (SAE), where student teams design, build, and race formula-style cars. FSAE began in the USA in 1978, and now includes hundreds of international teams that compete in eight competitions around the world.

 

The FSAE series is internationally regarded as one of the most rigorous collegiate design competitions. Each year, teams spend 8 to 12 months preparing for competition.

 

The concept of the competition is for students to design, build, and race a prototype formula-style vehicle. The prototype is marketed towards the non-professional, weekend autocross racer. It must be high-performance, low cost, easy to maintain, and suitable for manufacture at 1000 units per year. Students manage all aspects of the project like a small business, from the technical design to the management of an interdisciplinary team.

 

Engineering

Teams have overwhelming freedom in the design of their cars. This is evident in the variety and ingenuity presented at each competition. Technical restrictions only exist to ensure safety, and do not limit creativity. Students engage a panel of industry judges to defend their design decisions with supporting analysis. Student drivers compete against the clock in a series of driving events to demonstrate real-world performance of their vehicles.

 

Business and Marketing

Building a racing car is not all engineering. Complex projects require strong leaders and a cooperative environment to succeed. In addition to managing the project, students must present a business case to a prospective manufacturer as well as a detailed manufacturing and cost analysis, showing complete understanding of what it takes to bring a product to market.

 

Perspective

FSAE converts classroom knowledge to real world experience. Involvement in the complete design process is a unique educational opportunity.
summary: McCormick Student Group

name: Northwestern Geoclub
description: Northwestern Geoclub is a coalition of students passionate about the outdoors, science, and inclusion. We strive to make our club accessible members both inside and outside the field of geology, creating a diverse and like-minded community!
summary: Northwestern Geoclub is a coalition of students passionate about the outdoors, science, and inclusion. We strive to make our club accessible members both inside and outside the field of geology, creating a diverse and like-minded community!

name: Northwestern Ice Hockey
description: We're NU's Club Hockey Team. We hold practices on Monday and Wednesday and typically have 2 games/weekend, during fall and winter quarter. Please visit https://www.northwesternicehockey.com/
summary: We're NU's Club Hockey Team. We hold practices on Monday and Wednesday and typically have 2 games/weekend, during fall and winter quarter. Please visit https://www.northwesternicehockey.com/

name: Northwestern News Network
description: We are the only broadcast news station on Northwestern's campus. We produce, shoot and report for three weekly newscasts specializing in campus news, sports and politics. Our staff members shoot, edit, and anchor all of our content, and we often travel to cover news in Chicago and beyond. The shows are created for students, by students. The network has won many accolades including student news Emmys and Best All-Around Newscast from the Society of Professional Journalists.
summary: NNN is the only broadcast news station on campus. We produce, shoot and report for three weekly newscasts specializing in campus news, sports and politics, in addition to our online platforms.

name: Northwestern News Network-Sports
description: We are the sports department of Northwestern's student television station. Our group produces our own weekly sports TV show on Northwestern’s varsity teams. Our staff members shoot, edit, and anchor all of our content, and we often travel to road games.
summary: We are the sports department of Northwestern's student television station. Our group produces our own weekly sports TV show on Northwestern’s varsity teams. Our staff members shoot, edit, and anchor all of our content, and we often travel to road games.

name: Northwestern NuTango
description: NuTango is a Northwestern University student-run organization for Argentine Tango. We host lessons, practices, and dance events. NuTango is dedicated to provide a fun and friendly environment for beginners to start in Tango, as well for more experienced dancers to continue dancing. In our events, we strive to create an open environment that encourages members to explore the role of lead and follow. Additionally, we embrace musical variety by playing a wide range of songs from traditional Tango to modern. We are open to everyone in the Northwestern community as well as general public of Chicago area.
summary: NuTango is a Northwestern University student-run organization for Argentine Tango. We host lessons, practices, and dance events.

name: Northwestern Political Union
description:
The Northwestern University Political Union (PU) is a community of students united by their common interest in discussion of political and global affairs. Bringing together students of various backgrounds, ideologies, and political persuasions, the Political Union aims to foster a revival of intellectualism, sincerity, and sophistication in Northwestern’s campus political discourse.

PU holds weekly meetings in a variety of formats, including student debates, lectures from faculty, discussions over dinner, and special guest speakers. All undergraduates are welcome at PU meetings and there is no membership fee to attend our events.
summary:
The Northwestern University Political Union is a community of students united by their common interest in discussion of political and global affairs.

Visit http://www.nupoliticalunion.org/ for more information.

name: Northwestern Public Health Review
description:
Frozen October 2024

See more information at our website (https://sites.northwestern.edu/nphr/) and on our blog (https://nphr.wordpress.com/).
summary: NPHR is a student-run academic journal. Our goal is to take you behind the scenes of public health and provide a simpler platform for students and faculty to share their public health perspectives, ideas, and original research.

name: Northwestern Quest Scholars Network
description: The Northwestern Quest Scholars Network (NUQSN) is Northwestern University's only low-income and first generation advocacy group. NUQSN serves over 20% of the Northwestern population as both a community and advocacy group for low-income and first generation students and their allies. NUQSN also serves as a resource for the Chicagoland grade school and high school communities, both mentoring underprivledged youth and educating low-income students about scholarship and educational opportunities at Northwestern and beyond.
summary: NUQSN aims to create an engaged and supportive community for low-income and first-generation students as well as their allies in addition to ensuring equal opportunities for all low-income and first-generation students on Northwestern's campus.

name: Northwestern Raas
description: Northwestern Raas has been blending its passion for dance with a dedication to promoting awareness of South Asian culture since its founding in 2014. As the University’s premier co-ed Raas and Garba team, a dance form that originates in the Indian state of Gujarat, Northwestern Raas is known for its strong sense of community and enthusiasm both behind the curtain and on stage. The team has delighted audiences across the nation in multiple national competitions per year including Raas Royalty, Nasha, Raas Mania, and Pittsburgh Raaz. Northwestern Raas is more than just a college dance team — it’s a family, with a network of alumni, that continues to inspire, educate and engage those around them in the beauty of South Asian culture.
summary: Northwestern Raas is the University's premier co-ed Raas and Garba team, a high-energy folk dance originating from the Indian state of Gujarat.

name: Northwestern Sketch Television
description: NSTV (Northwestern Sketch Television) is a College Emmy-award winning sketch comedy group from Northwestern University. The team, which includes over eighty students working in various positions, writes, directs, shoots, edits, promotes and acts in a wide variety of sketches throughout the year. In addition to making content for the Internet and an annual feature-length campus premiere and NSTV produces and performs in live shows. Check out our work on our youtube!
summary: NSTV (Northwestern Sketch Television) is a College Emmy-award winning sketch comedy group from Northwestern University. Including over eighty students working in various positions, we write, direct, shoot, promote, and act in a wide variety of sketches.

name: Northwestern Swing Dance Syndicate
description:
NSDS is an organization run out of the community building grant of the graduate school to promote stronger community via swing dancing - lindy hop, blues, balboa, and a range of other dances. We host lessons throughout the year, as well as organizing all-Chicago dance events, with nationally renowned swing bands. We are open to everyone in the Northwestern community, from beginners to advanced dancers. 

There are several ways to connect with us and the community of dancers that we have.

1) Facebook group is our most active mode of communication among our group within NU and broader Chicago area.

2) Our group has an email listserv where we send out information about dance classes and events on campus that we organize. Subscribe by sending an email to listserv@listserv.it.northwestern.edu with no subject and SUBSCRIBE NSDS in the body, or contact us directly and we'll help you out.

3) The club's official email address is nuswingdance@gmail.com. Send us requests, questions, suggestions, etc.

Hope to see you dancing with us!
summary: Lindy Hop, East Coast, Charleston, and Balboa are what we do. We focus and promote social dancing with (new) friends by providing weekly dancing classes, quarterly dances with live band, and occasionally dance workshops.

name: Northwestern Taekwondo
description:
Northwestern Taekwondo Club is a community of students who love to kick! Our members are of all skill and experience levels--from black belts with 10+ years of experience to some who have never heard of Taekwondo before joining--we are open to all. We offer weekly practices, hold belt tests, attend competitions, and perform at the annual KASA show. We are more than a team; we are a family :)
 
Our practices are mainly led by our experienced student captains, and are supported by a WTF-based external dojang. Practices involve a variety of activities including fitness training, sparring, poomsae, and demonstration prep. Join us Mondays 6:45-8:45pm, Wednesdays 6:45-8:45pm, and Saturdays 12:45-2:45pm in the Henry Crown Studio 1AB.
summary: As the premier martial arts club on campus, we're dedicated to providing a friendly, motivating environment for members of all abilities. We compete in tournaments and perform in shows year-round. Come learn the world's most popular martial art with us!

name: Northwestern Triathlon Club
description:
The Northwestern Triathlon Club strives to make all members more confident in their triathlon abilities! We train for local races through our conference, the MECTC, national competitions, or just for fun if racing isn't for you. We have weekly swim, bike, and run practices, all of which are optional. No tryouts required! We aim for a strong social bond outside of training, hosting larger social events 1-2 times a month, and smaller events every week. The club is open to people of all ability levels and currently has 90+ members. Whether you are a competitive athlete who is looking to improve, or somebody who wants to stay in shape while in college, this is the right club for you!

Join our GroupMe with this link! https://groupme.com/join_group/96728519/k77Bruhj
summary: NU Triathlon competes at the highest level of collegiate triathlon and welcomes students of all abilities and backgrounds to join us. Already an elite triathlete? Ran or swam in high school? Don't know what a triathlon is? There's a place for you here!

name: Northwestern Undergraduate Law Journal
description: Northwestern Undergraduate Law Journal (NULJ) provides a platform for the submission and discussion of law-related topics for students at Northwestern University. Our mission is to encourage legal and political awareness in the Northwestern community. Our website is thenulj.org
summary: Northwestern Undergraduate Law Journal (NULJ) provides a platform for the submission and discussion of law-related topics for students at Northwestern University. Our mission is to encourage legal and political awareness in the Northwestern community.

name: Northwestern Undergraduate Research Journal
description: The Northwestern Undergraduate Research Journal is an annual peer- and faculty-reviewed publication of research articles written by Northwestern undergraduates from all academic fields.  NURJ provides students the opportunity to experience writing and submitting original research.  The goals of the Journal are to encourage the dissemination of ideas, increase undergraduate involvement in research, and recognize the impressive work being performed by undergraduates.  NURJ staff members participate in finding peer and faculty editors, compiling the articles for the annual edition, designing the annual edition, and spreading the word about NURJ to the NU community.
summary: The Northwestern Undergraduate Research Journal (NURJ) is an annual student-produced journal that showcases outstanding research done by Northwestern undergraduates from across all academic fields.

name: Northwestern University American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description: ASME allows students to interact with practicing mechanical engineering professionals through quarterly roundtables or panels, providing the opportunity to network, develop skills, and learn together. At Northwestern, students learn from peers and faculty in skill workshops and a mentorship program, creating a community around mechanical engineering. Additionally, ASME serves to inform and assist students in their pursuit of career opportunities directly related to the field of mechanical engineering. Participation in design projects such as the ASME Student Design Competition provides outlets for hands-on project-based learning and skill development.
summary: The Northwestern University chapter of the American Society of Mechanical Engineers

name: Northwestern University Community for Human Rights
description:
The Northwestern University Community for Human Rights (NUCHR) is a voice for global human rights on campus.

Through informed dialogue, general member meetings, educational excursions, social events, and year-round programming, we bridge connections between undergraduates and international discussions on current human rights issues. Our group is oriented to assist students in grappling with complex histories and inequities in order to develop deep critical thinking abilities and to mobilize inspired action.

Each winter, NUCHR explores a distinct topic in the field of human rights while hosting the largest student run, student attended human right's conference in the nation.
summary: Welcome to the NU Community for Human Rights! Our aim is to spark dialogue, foster activism, and improve awareness surrounding global and local human rights challenges.

name: Northwestern University Dance Marathon
description:
Overview

Now in its 50th year, Northwestern University Dance Marathon (NUDM) is one of the largest independent student-run philanthropies in the nation. Our mission is to unite the Northwestern, Evanston, and Greater Chicagoland communities through philanthropic fundraising, service, and education. A year-long effort culminating in a dance marathon, NUDM strives to create an inclusive and enriching environment for the NUDM student body, our rotating primary beneficiary, and our longstanding secondary beneficiary, the Evanston Community Foundation (ECF). Since 1975, NUDM has raised more than $23 million for more than 35 different charitable organizations.

Our History

In 1975, Northwestern University Dance Marathon, the philanthropic child of Alpha Tau Omega fraternity and the Associated Student Government, was born on the Northwestern University Evanston campus. It was initially called the “Dance to Give Them a Chance” marathon, and it benefited the American Epilepsy Foundation and the Arc. Fifteen couples made it through the 52-hour marathon, raising $9,100 at Blomquist Memorial Gymnasium.

 

This past year, NUDM raised $434,455 for Little Heroes League– a nonprofit organization striving to improve the care of the most medically complex babies born in Chicagoland and beyond by providing care coordinators who advocate for the family and child– and the Evanston Community Foundation – which funds a variety of local grants to deserving organizations with the goal of helping Evanston thrive.

Where We Are Now

This year, NUDM is thrilled to partner with Ronald McDonald House Charities of Chicagoland and Northwest Indiana (RMHC-CNI). RMHC-CNI is a nonprofit organization that houses and supports families with sick children. Ronald McDonald House Charities provide families with a “home away from home,” allowing children to access the highest-quality medical care with their families nearby.
summary: NUDM is a year-long effort culminating in a dance marathon that unites the Northwestern, Evanston, and Greater Chicagoland communities through awareness, service, education, and philanthropic fundraising.

name: Northwestern University Model United Nations
description:
Please see our website numun.org for more information!

The Northwestern University Model United Nations conference originated in 2004 when a group of dedicated Northwestern undergrads sought to fill a void in the community’s extracurricular department. In the years since, the conference has almost tripled in size, but its dedication to delegate experience has never changed. NUMUN is typically hosted every year at the Norris University Center on campus, directly adjacent to Lake Michigan.

Our mission is to be provide a premier high school conference and field a top tier competitive travel team. Our high-quality, dedicated secretariat and staff create a personal, professional, and passionate simulation experience. We seek to broaden delegates’ knowledge of worldwide politics, increase interpersonal skills, and create impactful experiences.
summary: Our mission is to offer a premier Model UN conference for High School students from around the world, while fielding a top flight competitive travel team that can compete on the national American circuit.

name: Northwestern University Native American and Indigenous Student Alliance
description: NAISA is dedicated to supporting, cultivating, and inspiring the Native American and Indigenous community and allies on Northwestern University’s campus and beyond. Our mission is to uplift voices and increase the visibility of Native American and Indigenous cultures through the discussion of issues NAI students and community face. We open our group to anyone willing to better understand the historical and modern circumstances of Native Americans and to experience and celebrate NAI cultures worldwide. We strive to form alliances and partnerships with other students, student groups, and universities, while elevating initiatives to sustain future generations of NAI students and allies at Northwestern.
summary: NAISA is dedicated to supporting, cultivating, and inspiring the Native American and Indigenous community and allies on Northwestern University’s campus and beyond.

name: Northwestern University Outdoors Club
description:
NOTE: Please visit our website Home | NU Outdoors Club (nuoutdoorsclub.org) and fill out the contact form to join our mailing list! Trip invitations and club updates will be exclusively sent out to our Listserv, so if you want to join the club, complete the contact form on the website or email us at nuoutdoorclub@gmail.com.

 

NUOC is a group of students who love the outdoors! Everyone and anyone is welcome to join, lead, or plan trips. Prior experience is rarely necessary. During our 50+ years of existence on the Northwestern campus, we have gone backpacking, canoeing, hiking, climbing, camping, skiing, snowshoeing, spelunking, and more. We also provide free gear rentals to club members. The next adventure is up to you! Send us an email at nuoutdoorclub@gmail.com to join our listserv!
summary: Providing your outdoors fix since 1965.

name: Northwestern University SACNAS Chapter
description: Frozen October 2024
summary: SACNAS is a society of scientists dedicated to fostering the success of Hispanic/Chicano and Native American scientists—from college students to professionals—to attain advanced degrees, careers, and positions of leadership in science.

name: Northwestern University Sailing Team
description:
Past History:
