The Northwestern Sailing Team can trace its history back to the late 1950's when teams practiced in downtown Chicago, with the NU boathouse constructed on campus in 1974. Since that time the NU sailing team has ranged from a relaxed learn-to-sail program to a nationally competitive team attending numerous national championships and graduating two college all Americans: Roslyn Rea who received sailing's top honor 1987 and Scotia Ryer in both 1995/1996. Today Northwestern continues to be a top school in the Midwest Collegiate Sailing Association.


Schedule:

Competition occurs from early September through Thanksgiving and starts back up in late March continuing through early June with regattas most weekends. The Northwestern sailing team attends upwards of 25 events each year. Practice is held in the fall and spring Monday thru Thursday from 3-6 out of the NU boathouse. Team members are asked to attend practice at least twice a week. Single-Handed and Sloop Nationals occur in the fall while Co-ed, Women's, and Team Race Nationals are held in the spring.

League Affiliation:

College sailing features over 200 schools and is governed by the Inter-Collegiate Sailing Association (ICSA). The ICSA is broken down into seven regional districts, with the Midwest Collegiate Sailing Association, of which Northwestern is a member, stretching from Minnesota to Ohio.


Club Dues:

200 dollars per year. Team members are also required to purchase proper sailing gear their first year.


Tryout Schedule:

Try outs are held each fall.


Practice Locations:

NU Sailing Center, located on south beach which features a fleet of 12 420's and 8 lasers.


Competition Locations:

Schools all over the country, however most events are in the Midwest. Frequent locations are Wisconsin, Minnesota, Ohio State, and Michigan, however this past year we attended events in Seattle, Larchmont, and Dallas.
summary: We are a student-run, student-led club sport team at Northwestern, practicing on Lake Michigan out of the NU Sailing Center and competing in regattas on both a local and national scale.

name: Northwestern University Science Olympiad
description: We are a group of undergraduate and graduate students at Northwestern, committed to serving the Science Olympiad community. Most of us are alumni of Science Olympiad, having competed in Division B and/or C. We host an annual Invitational-level Science Olympiad Division C tournament for over 600 students in 40 teams from across the nation. We also serve as volunteers and event supervisors for other Invitational, Regional, and State level tournaments in Illinois.
summary: A volunteer-based organization committed to hosting a Science Olympiad invitational for Division C teams and coordinating volunteers for Illinois Regional and State tournaments.

name: Northwestern University Space Technology and Rocketry Society
description:
Our Objectives: 

  1. To compete in the NASA BIG Idea competition (Space Technology, “ST”);
  2. To compete in the NASA Student Launch (NSL) Competition (“Rocketry”);
  3. To lead Northwestern students to obtain Level 1 High Power Rocketry Certifications;
  4. To engage students and educators at Northwestern and in the Evanston and Chicago communities in learning about space technology and rocketry.

Please visit nustars.org or email us to learn more!
summary: NUSTARS is the premier aerospace organization at Northwestern dedicated to rocketry, aerospace engineering, and experimental space technology. We compete in the NASA Artemis Challenges to give students hands on aerospace engineering experience.

name: Northwestern University Special Olympics
description: The purpose of Northwestern University Special Olympics is to integrate individuals with developmental disabilities in our community through athletic competition, inclusive programming, and events to raise awareness. The objective of Northwestern University Special Olympics shall be to partner with Evanston Special Olympics in joint programming. Northwestern University Special Olympics coordinates over 100 student volunteers and coaches for Special Olympics athletes in the community, special events on campus, and awareness and educational outreach about intellectual disabilities.
summary: Northwestern University Special Olympics is the oldest and largest collegiate Special Olympics chapter. Our mission is to integrate individuals with developmental disabilities into our community through inclusive programming.

name: Northwestern University Women Filmmakers Alliance
description: The Northwestern University Women Filmmaker's Alliance is a student-run film board dedicated to supporting women and LGBTQ+ in the entertainment industry. We provide grants, mentorship events, industry panels, and many other exciting events to the RTVF community.
summary: Uplifting women and non-binary people in film and entertainment, on and off campus.

name: Northwestern Wildside
description: Northwestern Wildside is the university's official athletic student section, which unites students at Northwestern University through their passion for athletics. The group is known for its energetic and enthusiastic support of the university's athletic teams, particularly at home games and events. As a society shared between NU Student Affairs and NU Athletics, Northwestern Wildside provides an inclusive space for students to come together, show their school spirit, and engage with their fellow peers. Through their collective efforts, Northwestern Wildside embodies the university's spirit of community and collaboration, helping to foster a sense of belonging among students from all walks of life.
summary: The Student Section of Northwestern University

name: Northwestern Women's Club Basketball
summary: We practice 2 times a week and travel the Midwest for games throughout each quarter :)

name: NSSLHA-Undergraduate
description:
The purpose of our organization is i) to encourage professional interest among undergraduate students in the study of Human Communication Sciences and Disorders, ii) to provide opportunities to gain practical and applicable experience and knowledge related to a future career in CSD, iii) to serve as a welcoming, open, and tolerant space to kindle CSD-related discussion by those pursuing or interested in the field of study from all identities and backgrounds, iv) to spread awareness of CSD causes to our general student body members and peers and v) to develop and sustain a meaningful and mutually beneficial connection between NSSLHA-Undergraduate and NU NSSLHA Graduate as well as faculty and leaders in the CSD Department. The intended goals for the organization are primarily based in the furthering of the bond within the CSD undergraduate community and in providing adequate and useful support, guidance, and opportunities to successfully achieve their professional goals with ease. 

More information on the overall mission and purpose can be found from the national NSSLHA organization.
summary: NSSLHA-Undergraduate creates an open, inclusive, and equitable space for undergraduates interested in the study of Human Communication Sciences and Disorders to come together in pursuit of academic, social, and professional opportunities in this field.

name: NU Asian Magazine
description: NU Asian Magazine is Northwestern University’s Asian American-interest magazine. We publish at least twice a year and cover anything and everything related to the greater Asian American community.
summary: NU Asian Magazine is Northwestern University's Asian American-interest magazine. We publish at least twice a year and cover anything and everything related to the greater Asian American community.

name: NU Club Gymnastics
description:
WANT TO JOIN? USE THESE LINKS BELOW!

     1.) Join our GroupMe

     2.) Register through our Website

     3.) Follow our Instagram

 

Want to view our most recent trip across the country? Santa Monica - Muscle Beach
summary: Welcome to our page for NU's Gymnastics Club!  This is an inclusive and supportive environment where students can explore and grow in the sport of gymnastics, regardless of their skill level. If you're interested, please don't hesitate to reach out :)

name: NU Curling
description:
Frozen October 2024

NU Curling is a community-building graduate club dedicated to teaching and playing the great winter sport of curling. Our FREE events take place at the Chicago Curling Club, located a short carpool ride away in Northbrook. From fall to spring we host weekly lessons, games, and broomstacking (post-game drinks and snacks) and would like all of you to join in. Newcomers are highly encouraged to come try it out, as are any curling pros.

In addition to these weekly events, we have also sent teams to Regional and National Curling Tournaments and have held the Northwestern Bonspiel to end our past six seasons.
summary: A community-building graduate club dedicated to teaching and playing the great winter sport of curling. Our FREE events take place at the Chicago Curling Club, located a short carpool ride away in Northbrook. From fall to spring we host weekly lessons.

name: NU Heights
description:
The volunteers involved with NU Heights have the opportunity to tutor and mentor local students in elementary, middle, and high school. The subjects include math, science, and reading comprehension, among others. There are morning and afternoon shifts each day throughout the week to fit your schedule! 

Tutors working in a classroom during the school day may assist the teacher with various tasks, work one-on-one with a student, or help a small group of students independently. The responsibilities vary depending on the teacher. After school, tutors may offer homework assistance, mentor, or do various fun activities with the students. There are also opportunities to work in Spanish since many of our students are ESL and use Spanish at home.
summary: We connect Northwestern student volunteers with various local elementary, middle, and high schools to tutor in-person either as classroom assistants or afterschool homework helpers.

name: NU Nights
description:
NU Nights organizes substance-free, late night programming activities for Northwestern students every weekend and unites our campus community through these activities. Programming includes a variety of on-campus and off-campus events, catering to the needs and interests of our student body, usually between 8p-11p on Fridays. Events include, but are not limited to, arts and crafts, game nights, excursions, and student group collaborations.

 

See our Linktree for a form to join our listserv and get notifications about meetings, events, how to join, and more! https://linktr.ee/nu_nights
summary: NU Nights organizes substance-free, late night programming activities for Northwestern students and unites our campus community through these activities.

name: NU Orthodox Christian Fellowship
summary: N/A

name: NU Photonics/SPIE
description: We welcome people from the fields of engineering, materials science, chemistry, and others. We promote the science of light at Northwestern, as well as foment social and professional interaction of our student members and faculty.
summary: We welcome people from the fields of engineering, materials science, chemistry, and others. We promote the science of light at Northwestern, as well as foment social and professional interaction of our student members and faculty.

name: NU Robotics
description:
NU Robotics is a student-run organization that is open to any enrolled Northwestern student. We sponsor a multitude of engineering focused projects for Northwestern University. Our mission is to develop a core community of undergraduate student builders interested in robotics and unifying them across majors. We offer a place for students to express their creative and innovative side as they work on student-created projects and apply the knowledge learned in class to gain practical experience in a fun way. The projects range from technical research projects to start-up ideas to yearly competitions. We welcome every type of student and hold workshops during the fall to teach and provide hands-on experience to newcomers, so students from all over can join the build!

We strongly encourage interested members to join our slack workspace- this is where we do most of our communication!  We'll send out reminders about events and give updates on what's happening in the club as well.
summary: NU Robotics Club is an open interdisciplinary group working on robotics competitions and design projects in teams of undergraduate students. We are always looking for new project ideas, feel free to reach out. All skill levels are welcome!

name: NU SIAM Student Chapter
description:
Frozen October 2024

The aim of the Northwestern Student Chapter of SIAM is to provide a platform for students involved in science to interact with one another. We organize social and scientific events that bring together students from all areas of science as SIAM is a strong promoter of interdisciplinary collaboration. Students that use mathematical, computational, statistical, or visualization methods for their research are strongly encouraged to become a member and participate in this organization!
summary: NU's official student chapter of the Society for Industrial and Applied Mathematics. We organize events to promote interaction between students involved in different areas of science.

name: NU Solar Car Team
description:
The Northwestern University Solar Car Team (NUsolar) is an undergraduate student-run organization that designs, builds, and races solar-powered electric vehicles. We are one of the most diverse student organizations within the McCormick School of Engineering. Our members come from all areas of expertise: software, electrical, mechanical, and chemical engineers, as well as economics, music, journalism, and film majors.


NUsolar’s mission is to prepare Northwestern students for careers in engineering, manufacturing, marketing, and leadership positions, by providing the opportunity for them to engage in real-life, out-of-the classroom projects. Our members learn how to function in a specialized team and work within budgets and deadlines. They make connections with their peers and with our advising professors, as well as with potential employers in all sorts of fields.

Each summer, our team participates in two nation-wide solar car races, the Formula Sun Grand Prix (FSGP) and the American Solar Challenge (ASC). Both races are endurance-type races in which we compete against teams from other universities, driving our car for up to a week on only the power we can get from the sun.

In addition to racing our car, we also participate in numerous outreach events in the wider Chicago area. We frequently take our car to elementary schools and community service events to promote an interest in STEM education and increase awareness for “green” vehicle alternatives within our community.
summary: We are an undergraduate student team at Northwestern that builds solar powered electric vehicles, which we race in a number of national competitions against other university teams. We are also active in the Chicago community.

name: NU Syllabus Yearbook
description:
Syllabus, founded in 1885, is the official undergraduate yearbook of Northwestern University. Published by SPC (Students Publishing Company) and run by Northwestern students, Syllabus seeks to include memorable events and highlights of each year as well as campus groups, organizations, and living units. All seniors are invited to have their portrait taken for inclusion in our senior section. Parents and friends of graduating seniors may purchase a special ad. Syllabus yearbooks are delivered on campus in May of each year.

 

The Syllabus is created by students, for students. There are opportunities for photography, design, and copy writing ranging from academics, to sports, to clubs and organizations within Syllabus.
summary: Syllabus, founded in 1885, is the official undergraduate yearbook of Northwestern University. Published by Students Publishing Company, Inc. Syllabus features the memorable events that make up the collective Northwestern experience.

name: NU Thrift Store
description: We are a student-run thrift store currently organizing pop-ups at the end of every quarter. Our mission is to bring affordable and sustainable clothing to the Northwestern community, while donating our proceeds to social/environmental justice groups. Our permanent location is in Parkes Hall.
summary: We are a student-run thrift store located in Norris. Our mission is to bring affordable and sustainable clothing to the Northwestern community, while donating our proceeds to social/environmental justice groups.

name: NU World Cup
description:
• Northwestern University World Cup is the largest student-run athletic tournament on campus. It is an organization seeking to bridge cultures and bring students from all backgrounds together to play the global game of soccer. Fundamentally, NU World Cup believes in the unifying power of sport to bring students together. Our vision is to create a campus where all members of the Northwestern community, regardless of background, experience the passion and energy of soccer.
  • We recognize the power of our diverse backgrounds and cultures and aim to promote intercultural exchange between Northwestern students as well as our community partners. We want students to realize that while we all come from different places and have different experiences, we are all unified through Northwestern. Through soccer tournaments, school-wide events, and volunteering, World Cup brings together students of all backgrounds to learn and play with one another.
  • World Cup also aims to support the local Chicago community through our close partnership with TOPSoccer Chicago, an organization dedicated to providing equitable access to soccer for children with disabilities.
summary: NU World Cup (NUWC) is Northwestern’s largest student-run sports and philanthropy organization, bringing together over 750 students from all backgrounds through the love of the beautiful game of soccer and its power to drive change.

name: NUcleus
summary: NUcleus is a student-run magazine focused on producing and writing about STEM culture and research in a way that goes beyond the obvious and mundane.

name: Off The Ball
description: Off The Ball is a club for soccer/association football fans on campus to meet each other and watch matches from both men's and women's leagues together! People from all levels of soccer knowledge and enjoyment are welcome.
summary: Off The Ball is a club for soccer/association football fans on campus to meet each other and watch matches from both men's and women's leagues together! People from all levels of soccer knowledge and enjoyment are welcome.

name: Office of Fraternity & Sorority Life
description:
Vision Statement:

We aim to create a socially just and responsible fraternity and sorority community.

Core Values:

  • Wellness: the physical, mental, and emotional well-being of the chapter.
  • Accountability: the knowledge of, commitment to, and willingness to accept responsibility for policies and expectations.
  • Chapter Development: the ongoing individual and collective development of members.
  • Social Justice: the holistic understanding of privilege, power, and oppression and a commitment to action.
  • Campus & Community Partnership: the contribution to campus and surrounding communities through the development of sustainable and purposeful partnerships.
summary: The Office of Fraternity & Sorority Life provides support for the vibrant community of affiliated students at Northwestern University.

name: Office of Student Enrichment Services
description: Student Enrichment Services (SES) works with low-income and/or first-generation students to enhance their academic success, personal development, and professional growth. Through campus-wide partnerships, SES builds an inclusive Northwestern community by engaging students and their allies with programming and dialogue around the low-income and/or first-generation experience.
summary:
Hear from students, staff, and faculty about their first-generation and/or low-income experience at Northwestern.
http://www.northwestern.edu/enrichment/about-us/we-are-here-video/index.html

name: Omega Delta Phi Fraternity, Inc.
description:
Alpha Alpha Chapter, Northwestern University

Installed November 25, 1997

"One Culture, Any Race."

Omega Delta Phi's Signature Events: Founders' Month Celebration, Cesar Chavez Day of Service, and Silver Rose Scholarship Banquet

You may visit the Alpha Alpha Chapter's website at http://www.nuodphi.com/.

 

National Organization Facts

National founding date: November 25, 1987

The purpose of this Brotherhood, a Service/Social fraternity dedicated to the needs and concerns of the community, is and shall be to promote and maintain the traditional values of Unity, Honesty, Integrity, and Leadership. This Brotherhood was founded in order to provide, to ANY man, a diverse fraternal experience which coincides with a higher education.

You may view the Omega Delta Phi national website at www.omegadeltaphi.com.
summary: The Alpha Alpha Chapter of Omega Delta Phi was installed at Northwestern University on November 25th, 1997. The brothers of Omega Delta Phi make it their organization's goals to graduate its members and to serve its surrounding communities.

name: Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc.
description:
Psi Sigma Chapter, Northwestern University

"Friendship is Essential to the Soul"

President: Lawrence Price III 

Organization Facts

National founding date: November 17, 1911

Founding Institution: Howard University

Colors: Royal Purple, Old Gold

Nickname: The Ques

Cardinal Principles: Manhood, Scholarship, Perseverance, Uplift

You may view the Omega Psi Phi national website at http://www.oppf.org.
summary:
Psi Sigma Chapter, Northwestern University
Re-Chartered: April 5, 2013
National founding date: November 17, 1911
"Friendship is Essential to the Soul"
Colors: Royal Purple and Old Gold
President: Lawrence Price III

name: One Book
description: Over the summer, copies of this year's book will be delivered to the homes of every incoming first-year undergraduate and distributed to hundreds of members of Northwestern’ s faculty, staff and graduate student communities as well as community partners beyond our campus.
summary: Nate Silver’s improbably entertaining book on statistics and forecasting, The Signal and the Noise: Why So Many Predictions Fail — but Some Don’t, is Northwestern University’s One Book One Northwestern all-campus read for the 2016-17 academic year.

name: Open Democracy Undergraduate
description: Our purpose is to educate community members about the importance of being a citizen and uplift their voices to create a more equitable society.
summary: The purpose of Open Democracy is to educate community members about the importance of being a citizen and uplift their voices to create a more equitable society.

name: Opera Projects for Undergraduate Singers
summary: Opera Projects for Undergraduate Singers (OPUS) is an ensemble of undergraduate singers and actors at Northwestern University who seek to direct, produce, and perform student-driven opera.

name: oSTEM (Tuesday, East Lawn, 30)
description: oSTEM is a community for queer STEM students. Through offering academic support, pre-professional development, speaker series, and social events, oSTEM strives to form a community for queer STEM students at Northwestern!
summary: TBA

name: oSTEM (Undergraduate Chapter)
description:
We aim to address the need for an LGBTQIA+ group with a STEM-specific focus within the Northwestern University undergraduate community. While our primary mission is to create a safe community for LGBTQIA+ individuals, we are also strongly committed to intersectionality as an organizing principle, and we therefore aim to actively center those who have been historically underrepresented in STEM and STEM-adjacent fields due to their gender identity, sexual identity, racial, ethnic, and socio-economic backgrounds, or any other part of their identity, both established and questioning.

This organization is open to all currently enrolled Northwestern Undergraduate students.
summary: TBA

name: Out in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (Graduate chapter)
description: Frozen October 2024
summary: The goal of this oSTEM chapter is to empower LGBTQIA+ people in STEM to succeed personally, academically, and professionally by cultivating environments and communities that nurture innovation, leadership, and advocacy.

name: Outdoor Volleyball Association
description: The OVA was founded in January 2022 with about 20 members and, since then, has grown to become a community of over 350 people. The group's current programming includes weekly open gyms throughout the academic school year. Furthermore, it holds select events such as a mentorship program and tournaments to provide opportunities for player/team growth and development. We hope to see you join the OVA community soon!
summary: The OVA is dedicated to giving volleyball players of all skill levels the ability to play in casual and competitive environments. Events are ideally outdoors but occur inside as needed (due to weather, demand, etc.).

name: Overtime
description: Overtime's purpose and intended goal is to bring a fresh take on improv sports, sketch, and stand up to Northwestern's campus! You know the riff-off in Pitch-Perfect? Now picture that but with improv comedy, now doesn't that sound like a good, silly, little time? Let's bring friendly competition back to comedy. Chicago has an incredibly abundant comedy scene, which includes ComedySportz Chicago, so we know the comedy sport model works! We want to allow people to engage with comedy on this campus in a way they’ve never been able to before, and Overtime will accomplish that mission! Overtime’s improv performance format is inspired by ComedySportz, an organization based out of Wisconsin that has since become popular nationwide. Two teams of 4-5 players compete in improv games and scenes to earn points, which are awarded based on the audience’s vote. There is also a referee overseeing the games, providing interstitial commentary and calling fouls when necessary (ex. If a player is not adhering to the rules of the game, if a player is too slow to answer, etc). As is the case with most sports, the team who has the most points at the end of the game wins!
summary: Overtime's purpose and intended goal is to bring a fresh take on improv sports, sketch, and stand up to Northwestern's campus!

name: PAC.Solutions
shortName: (Public speaking, Activity with current events, Collaboration with peers)
description: Each week, a current topic/problem will be presented with either a brief article or a summary of the issue at the start of the week. On Friday, after getting familiar with the topic, students will form an opinion on the issue and be prepared to explain their perspective to the other members. Students are allowed/encouraged to engage in other articles to back their stance. In each meeting, after hearing everyones input, we will come together to brainstorm possible solutions to the overarching issue.
summary: Each week, a current topic/problem will be presented with either a brief article or a summary of the issue at the start of the week.

name: Pakistani Students Association
description:
PSA aims to represent a home away from home for international students, a way for the diaspora to connect with their roots, and a means to project a positive image of the country amongst the Northwestern community. Through programming and collaborations with different organizations, both within Northwestern and outside, we hope to offer the following along with many other:

  • Cultural gatherings e.g. for Eid, Independence Day etc. 
  • Collaborative literary and political speaker events with other interest groups like SASA, the Subcontinent Project etc.
  • Networking opportunities with Pakistani professionals in Pakistan and the US
  • Fundraising and awareness for community uplifting in Pakistan and for South Asian communities in the Chicago area 

It is difficult to represent every group under an umbrella which is why we hope to offer a unique space for those who find they do not completely fit in present structures, but without drawing any restrictions on membership. With the aforementioned programming we hope to offer members a sense of security, belonging and opportunities to engage with the identities they hold dear, hence allowing them to thrive in their time at Northwestern. 

Instagram: psa.nu
summary: The organization intends to be a space for international and diaspora Pakistani students and all those interested in learning more about and connecting with the communities and traditions of Pakistan.

name: Pan African Students' Union
description: The Pan-African Students Union (PASU) is a student organization that is dedicated to developing a political and ethical consciousness among students who are committed to the advancement of the African continent. Inspired by the historic vision of Pan-Africanism, we engage in meaningful programs such as reading groups and discussion series to help us build an understanding of the challenges facing Africa today.
summary: PASU is a student organization that is dedicated to developing a political and ethical consciousness among students who are committed to the development of Africa.

name: Pan-Asia Medicine Club
description: The Pan-Asian Medicine Club aims to introduce and foster a deep understanding and appreciation of Pan-Asian medicine within the Northwestern community. We acknowledge the rich cultural heritage and medical efficacy of Asian medicine for whole-body wellness and aim to provide students with opportunities to experience and investigate this field.
summary: The Pan-Asian Medicine Club aims to introduce and foster a deep understanding and appreciation of Pan-Asian medicine within the Northwestern community.

name: Panhellenic Association
description: The Northwestern Panhellenic Association (PHA) consists of 7 nationally-recognized chapters. Each sorority has a chapter house on the south end of Northwestern's campus. All of the organizations have a long-standing (inter)national history and traditions as many of the Northwestern chapters are more than 100 years old. Nearly 800 women of all backgrounds and interests are members of PHA sororities at NU. Panhellenic women are leaders and participants in campus organizations, serve the NU and Evanston communities, and excel in the classroom.
summary: The Northwestern Panhellenic Association (PHA) consists of 12 nationally-recognized chapters. Each chapter has a sorority house on the south end of Northwestern's campus, and all of the organizations have a long-standing history at NU.

name: Parliamentary Debate Union
description:
Weekly Meetings for debate.

All are welcome!
summary: Parliamentary debate is a form of extemporaneous debate in which two teams of two people go against each other. You can debate about almost anything and no prior experience is needed!

name: Partners In Health Engage
description: A social justice organization focusing on expanding health equity locally and beyond to best help underserved areas and people gain access to high-quality, affordable healthcare. Partners In Health Engage is an offshoot of the nonprofit organization, Partners In Health. PIHE focuses on fostering grassroots movements using three pillars of organization: advocacy, fundraising, and community building.
summary: A social justice organization focusing on expanding health equity locally and beyond to best help underserved areas and people gain access to high-quality, affordable healthcare.

name: Personal Finance Student Association
description: The purpose of our organization is to assist Northwestern students in managing their personal finance. We hope to educate our fellow students in responsible financial practices and a healthy understanding of the five branches of personal finance (taxes, budgeting, credit, long-term planning/investing, and insurance), in addition to helping them navigate and utilize the resources Northwestern already offers. We also want to develop a community that understands the difficulties and supports the unique perspective of FGLI students, as well as advocate to the University in regards to the financial needs of all students.
summary: Our organization's purpose is to provide financial education to students at Northwestern.

name: Phi Alpha Delta
description: The fundamental objective of Phi Alpha Delta is to assist undergraduate students to make an informed choice in selecting law as a career, in deciding which law school to attend, and in preparing for the rigors of law school. Phi Alpha Delta is an international coed professional fraternity, and it remains the only Law Fraternity with a national Pre-Law Program committed to meeting the needs of undergraduate students interested in the law.
summary: Phi Alpha Delta is an international coed professional fraternity, and it remains the only Law Fraternity with a national Pre-Law Program committed to meeting the needs of undergraduate students interested in the law.

name: Phi Delta Epsilon
description: The Illinois Gamma chapter of PhiDE International Medical Fraternity at Northwestern University provides its members with mentorship, leadership and volunteering opportunities, social activities and scientific education. Our international philanthropy is Children's Miracle Network Hospitals, which we fundraise for every year. PhiDE brings high-profile speakers to NU's campus to discuss important issues in the medical field, such as healthcare systems, the role of research and funding in medicine, and even speakers that advise pre-medical students on their path into medical school. Our recruitment of members occurs in the fall and spring quarters.
summary: Co-ed International Medical Fraternity that provides pre-medical students with mentoring, networking, leadership development, scientific education, volunteering opportunities, and a supportive community.

name: Phi Gamma Delta
description:
Pi Chapter, Northwestern University
2331 Sheridan Rd.

"Friendship, the sweetest influence."

Chapter President: Alex Conway

Organization Facts

National Founding Date: May 1, 1848

Colors: Royal purple

You may visit the national website at www.phigam.org.
summary:
Phi Chapter, Northwestern University 2331 Sheridan Rd.
"Friendship, the sweetest influence."
Chapter President: Justin Erb
Organization Facts
National Founding Date: May 1, 1848
Colors: Royal purple
You may visit the national website at

name: Phi Gamma Nu (PGN)
description: Phi Gamma Nu (PGN) is Northwestern's newest business fraternity, committed to cultivating a diverse and dynamic community of students with a shared passion for business and entrepreneurship. Our fraternity is a hub where ambitious minds meet to exchange ideas, grow professionally, and make a lasting impact. At the heart of PGN lies a dual focus on professional development and personal growth. We provide a supportive environment where members can build meaningful relationships, foster a spirit of entrepreneurship, and prepare for diverse career paths in business. Through our unique approach, we strive to nurture leaders who are ready to innovate and excel in various industries.
summary: Phi Gamma Nu (PGN) is Northwestern's newest business fraternity, committed to cultivating a diverse and dynamic community of students with a shared passion for business and entrepreneurship.

name: Phi Mu Alpha Sinfonia
description:
Iota Chapter, Northwestern University
630 Emerson St.

Installed: April 29th, 1910

Chapter President: Ronnie Glas

Organization Facts

National Founding Date: October 6, 1898 (New England Conservatory)

Object: The object of this fraternity shall be for the development of the best and truest fraternal spirit, the mutual welfare and brotherhood of musical students, the advancement of music in America, and a loyalty to the Alma Mater

Colors: Red, Black and Gold

Philanthropy: Mills Music Mission

You may visit our local website at: http://iotasinfonia.org

You may visit the national website at http://www.sinfonia.org
summary: The object of this fraternity shall be for the development of the best and truest fraternal spirit, the mutual welfare and brotherhood of musical students, the advancement of music in America, and a loyalty to the Alma Mater

name: Physicians for Human Rights Student Program
description: The Physicians for Human Rights Student Program is a chapter of a national student program committed to advocacy for health and human rights violations on undergraduate and medical school campuses.
summary: The Physicians for Human Rights Student Program is a chapter of a national student program committed to advocacy for health and human rights violations on undergraduate and medical school campuses.

name: Pickleball Club
description: Northwestern University's Pickleball Club promotes pickleball for students of all levels, fostering community through regular play, events, and tournaments. Join us for this exciting sport!
summary: Northwestern's Club Pickleball team! Join us for the fastest growing sport in the United States!

name: Plant Futures
description: Plant Futures Northwestern is a pre-professional organization accelerating the transition to a sustainable, plant-based food system by empowering the next generation of leaders through career development, education, environmental advocacy, and community. As a chapter of the Plant Futures Initiative (PFI), a global nonprofit, we provide members access to exclusive networking opportunities, recruitment events, educational programs, field trips, and a global community of passionate students. Students from all academic backgrounds are welcome to join! Visit our Linktree to get involved and learn more.
summary: Plant Futures Northwestern's mission is to accelerate the transition to a sustainable, plant-based food system by empowering the next generation of leaders through environmental advocacy, education, career development, and community-building.

name: Plant-it Purple Graduate Garden
description:
Plant-it Purple Graduate Gardens is a community garden located on the patio of the Technological Institute on Northwestern’s Evanston campus.  The garden provides a place where graduate students, staff, and others can interact and relax outside of their labs and offices.  Working in and utilizing the garden fosters relationships between graduate students from many different disciplines by bringing them together to cultivate and enjoy a living project.  The garden is a benefit and an outlet to students, staff, and the community.

 

Our organization is most active through our listserv and Facebook page (which is linked at the top right). Come join us, we'd love to have you!
summary: We are a community garden located on the patio of the Technological Institute on the Evanston campus.  The garden is a place where graduate students, staff, and others can interact and relax outside of their labs and offices.

name: Platinum Valuation Group
description:
The Platinum Valuation Group (PVG) is an undergraduate student organization dedicated to equipping its members with a comprehensive understanding of how to use quantitative and qualitative business valuation techniques to make strategic investments. PVG combines technical training with investment pitch projects to support members in developing valuation and investment expertise while collaborating with peers. PVG aims to prepare students with the knowledge necessary to value and invest in businesses in a variety of careers, including investment banking, private equity, asset management, consulting, and more.

 

The goals of PVG include:

1. Education: Provide members with foundational knowledge in industry-standard valuation and investing methods, covering concepts like discounted cash flow (DCF), comparable company analysis, precedent transactions, and more.

2. Practical Experience: Enable members to work on investment pitch projects for actual, publicly traded businesses, helping them apply theoretical knowledge to real scenarios.

3. Professional Development: Enhance career readiness through targeted workshops on topics such as resume writing and interviewing, while providing mentorship for post-graduation success.
summary: The Platinum Valuation Group (PVG) is an undergraduate student org dedicated to teaching its members how to apply quantitative and qualitative businesses valuation methods to make strategic investments in businesses.

name: Platypus Affiliated Society
description:
We are the Northwestern Chapter of the Platypus Affiliated Society. Platypus is a project for the self-criticism, self-education, and, ultimately, the practical reconstitution of a Marxian Left. The Platypus Affiliated Society, established in December 2006, organizes reading groups, public fora, research and journalism focused on problems and tasks inherited from the “Old” (1920s-30s), “New” (1960s-70s) and post-political (1980s-90s) Left for the possibilities of emancipatory politics today.

Reading Group: Tuesdays, 6-9pm, Fisk Hall 114

Coffee Break: Sundays, 1-3pm, Norris 1st Floor
summary: We are the Northwestern Chapter of the Platypus Affiliated Society. Platypus organizes reading groups, public fora, research and journalism on the history and present tasks of the Left and Marxism.

name: Polerize
description: One of North America's first collegiate pole dance organizations, Polerize seeks to empower and educate the student population through pole dance classes and performances.
summary: To create a safe space for body movement that allows students to explore the sensuality, strength, and physical fitness of their own bodies  — while defying historical narratives of gendered oppression.

name: Polish American Student Alliance
description: The Northwestern University Polish-American Student Alliance (PASA) was founded in the fall of 1996. Since its establishment, PASA has grown tremendously. PASA's mission is simple: to promote cultural awareness of all things Polish. The organization does not restrict membership solely to those of Polish descent. It welcomes students with any interest in Polish history, society, and culture. Its dual goal is to teach as well as to learn. We have accomplished this in the past by advocating for Polish language education, hosting bonfires, and holding an annual Polish Culture Night.
summary: The Polish American Student Alliance (PASA) seeks to connect the Northwestern community with Chicagoland's rich Polish traditions. PASA commits itself to building a strong community among its members and working with other cultural student groups.

name: Polo Club
description:
The primary goal is to introduce students to the sport of polo by providing opportunities to ride and play. We also aim to educate participants about all things horsemanship ranging from horse care to stable maintenance. Polo is often—rightfully—perceived as an inaccessible and obscure sport. We hope to revitalize this dying sport and make it more approachable and accessible.

Our club will strive to provide opportunities for all levels of riders, including lessons and non-competitive scrimmages to practice the game for beginners. It is our hope to raise awareness of the sport for non-riders by facilitating opportunities to watch games where possible as well. Additionally, our aim is to create a competitive team that can participate in USPA Intercollegiate tournaments in the future. The goal is to foster inclusivity, passion, and skill development in this historic sport. Participation at the competitive level will require a certain degree of skill as well as tournament fees.
summary: We introduce students to the sport of polo by providing opportunities to ride and play. We also aim to educate participants about all things horsemanship ranging from horse care to stable maintenance.

name: Prairie Cats Ecological Restoration Club
description: The Chicago region is home to a stunning diversity of plants, animals, and other creatures; everything from tiny orchids to huge bur oaks, weird mushrooms and legless lizards. Although a large amount of land has been set aside for protection in our region, the battle is not over. Invasive species lurk around every corner and invade ecosystems whose natural processes have been disrupted by human activity. Through ecological restoration, we can prevent further damage and reverse it by restoring natural processes and returning species which have been in decline. Only this way, we can ensure a livable future for us and all of the other creatures we share the land with. Please join us at our fun outdoor workdays, workshops, speaker events, and more.
summary: We are an action-oriented organization committed to preserving and restoring nature in our area.

name: Pratham Education Foundation on Campus
description: Pratham Education Foundation on Campus intends to connect students on campus with learners in India to support Pratham’s greater purpose. Pratham focuses on learning enhancement programs, vocational training, research, and advocacy for underprivileged children in rural and urban India. Students on campus will volunteer to advocate for Pratham initiatives, and directly tutor some students in English, basic sciences, and digital literacy. Students on campus will have the opportunity to engage in cultural exchange activities as this club will promote global thinking and cross-cultural collaboration. We will to help design high-quality educational programs and look toward a world where every student has the opportunity to pursue their dreams.
summary: Pratham Education Foundation on Campus intends to connect students on campus with learners in India to support Pratham’s greater purpose.

name: Pre-Dental Society
description: Northwestern Pre-Dental Society (NUPDS) strives to unite NU pre-dental students and serves as a resource as students explore careers in oral healthcare or prepare to enter dental school.  NUPDS is focused on educating students about career options in dentistry and offering support to undergraduates navigating pre-dental preparations including course requirements, volunteer opportunities, and DAT test preparation.  NUPDS also aims to strengthen networking connections with dental professionals and students in the greater Chicago area as well as within the NU community.
summary: The pre-dental society is a group dedicated to pre-dental preparation, education and service in the field of oral healthcare.  We strive to provide resources and a community for students pursuing pre-dental studies.

name: Pre-Surgical Society
description: The Pre-Surgical Society is committed to fostering an environment where students with a keen interest in surgery can thrive. Our mission is to provide resources, mentorship, and opportunities for hands-on experience to help members build a strong foundation for their future careers in the medical field. Our society organizes a variety of activities and events throughout the academic year to enrich the educational experience of our members. From informative workshops like suture clinics to panel discussions featuring experienced surgeons, we ensure our members are well-informed about the latest developments in the field.
summary: The Pre-Surgical Society is committed to fostering an environment where students with a keen interest in surgery can thrive.

name: Pre-Veterinary Society at Northwestern University
description: The Pre-Veterinary Society at Northwestern University is a group dedicated to students who are pursing a pre-vet agenda on their path to veterinary school. The objectives of the Pre-Veterinary Society shall be to educate students interested in a career in veterinary medicine through guest lectures, meetings and community service. The society will aid students in the admissions process, promote connections between pre-veterinary students, and provide resources and guidance through the support of the University Academic Advising Center.
summary: The Pre-Veterinary Society at Northwestern University is a group dedicated to students who are pursing a pre-veterinary agenda on their path to veterinary school.

name: Professional Ethics Club
description: Professional Ethics Club seeks to provide a community promoting awareness and understanding of professional ethics and to foster a culture of ethical decision-making and responsibility in our future careers. We will provide opportunities for learning and discussion about various ethical topics and issues relevant to different professions and develop skills and competencies in ethical reasoning and analysis.
summary: Professional Ethics Club seeks to provide a community promoting awareness and understanding of professional ethics and to foster a culture of ethical decision-making and responsibility in our future careers.

name: Project MED
description:
Project MED (Medicine, Exposure, and Development) is an organization that focuses on educating and preparing high school students for careers in healthcare, particularly in underserved communities. We have two primary goals:

  1. Expose high school students to the breadth of opportunities in the healthcare field
  2. Prepare students for a successful pathway to a career in healthcare
summary: Project MED (Medicine, Exposure, and Development) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit and student organization that focuses on educating and preparing high school students for careers in healthcare, particularly in underserved communities.

name: Project Sunshine
description: Project Sunshine is a nonprofit organization focused on bringing joy to pediatric patients and their often forgotten caregivers! We work on delivering "the healing power of play" to children in hospitals through interactive events such as Teleplay, care packages, and arts and crafts. We also actively fundraise for our cause in order to raise money to support the national Project Sunshine headquarters, as well as strive to embody the organization values of SHINE (Safe, Healthy, Inclusive, Nurturing, Engaging).
summary: Delivering the healing power of play to pediatric patients!

name: ProjectProd
description: ProductProd focuses on the emerging field of product management, a role that is not widely explored on campus. Our goal is to introduce students to this dynamic career path, offering resources and opportunities to help them build a solid foundation in product management. By connecting students with industry professionals and offering hands-on experiences like case study competitions, ProductProd equips members with the practical skills needed for success in the tech world, particularly in a niche role that bridges technology, business, and design.
summary: ProductProd focuses on the emerging field of product management, with our goal is to introduce students to this dynamic career path, offering resources and opportunities to help them build a solid foundation in product management.

name: Psi Chi
description: Psi Chi, the International Honor Society in Psychology, was founded in 1929 with a mission to encourage excellence in scholarship and advance the science of psychology. More than three-quarters of a million lifetime members have joined including Distinguished Members such as Drs. Albert Bandura, B. F. Skinner, and Phillip Zimbardo. A few popular Membership Benefits include international recognition for academic excellence, as well as access to psychology-related publications and more than $400,000 in annual awards and grants. Lifetime Membership is open to undergraduate and graduate students, transfer students, full-time faculty members, and alumni. Individuals become members by joining their local Psi Chi chapter. See our specific membership requirements. After potential members are approved by a local faculty advisor, all 1,150+ Psi Chi chapters process their new members on our online system. The Psi Chi Central Office, located in Chattanooga, Tennessee, manages the Society under the direction of the Board of Directors, who are Psi Chi members elected by the chapters to guide the Organization. Psi Chi is a member of the Association of College Honor Societies and is an affiliate of the American Psychological Association (APA) and the Association for Psychological Science (APS). Our sister honor society is Psi Beta, the national honor society in psychology for community and junior colleges.
summary: Recognizing and promoting excellence in the science and application of psychology.

name: Public Affairs Residential College
description:
The Public Affairs Residential College (PARC) is a south campus living community at Northwestern University focused on integrating public affairs concepts into the living environment. The building houses 70 students in single and double-occupancy rooms. Floors are co-ed, with students from different genders living on different sides of each floor. This building is available to all undergraduate students.

As a Residential College, PARC has special access to university resources and faculty which make our living experience particularly unique!  As a PARCer you can participate in Firesides with professors, an amazing yearly formal, weekly Munchies and Cookies and Conversation, trips to Chicago and cool places on campus, philanthropy events, Intramural Sports and much, much more!

At PARC, almost everything you need is right next door, if not in PARC itself. But for a Res College to be truly great, it has to be more than a pretty building in a convenient part of Campus; it has to be a community. What really sets PARC apart is the people. Even though our name says “Public Affairs,” the students who live here are diverse in their areas of study. While we have our fair share of SESP and Medill students, each of Northwestern’s six schools is generously represented in PARC’s student population, even McCormick (the walk to Tech from NMQ is really not that bad). Your fellow PARCers will help you grow both academically and personally. Not only will you learn from PARC’s college staff and guest speakers; you will also learn from your fellow PARCers themselves. Students in PARC hold leadership positions in countless organizations on campus, whether for student-run publications, engineering societies, student government, and more. PARC fosters dialogue between students so that ideas can flow freely between us. At PARC you won’t just find a dorm; you’ll find a home.
summary: The Public Affairs Residential College (PARC) is a south campus living community at Northwestern University focused on integrating public affairs concepts into the living environment. The building houses 70 students in single and double-occupancy rooms.

name: Purple Crayon Players
description: Purple Crayon Players is a student-run theatre and community service organization that produces professionally written theatre for young audiences within our mainstage season, as well as special projects and events by, with, and for youth. This year, you can use theater to engage, inspire, and educate audiences of all ages as an actor, director, designer, storyteller, teaching artist, team member or project leader.
summary: Purple Crayon Players is a student-run theatre company on the Northwestern University campus committed to creating high quality productions, events, and educational materials regarding all aspects of theatre for young audiences.

name: Purple Haze
description:
Formed in 1996, Purple Haze has been singin', dancin' and lovin' for more than 25 years! Completely run by students, we rehearse on a weekly basis and put on a killer show every quarter. In the fall, we invite other groups from nearby colleges to perform in our Best of the Midwest concert. In the winter, we put on a show with some of our ICCA competition music if we compete that year or other new music we have been working on! In the spring, we celebrate our beloved seniors through a concert that features each of them with a solo as well as other new music from our repertoire.

Aside from campus shows, Purple Haze also performs throughout the Chicagoland area.  In the past, we have even sung on ABC Chicago News! Purple Haze has released nine studio albums, with many songs recognized on national a cappella compilation CDs. Mouth Off, an a cappella podcast, called us “the best you’re going to hear out of the whole midwestern United States” and “the group that everyone wants to party with!” Our music is available on iTunes and Spotify.

Purple Haze goes on tour every year, alternating between domestic and international trips. We have recently flown to Lima, Peru and are thrilled to announce that we will be going to Costa Rica this year. Past tours also include Havana, Cuba, Montreal, Ft. Lauderdale, and Amsterdam. We also compete biennially in the International Championship of Collegiate a cappella, making it to the ICCA Semifinals in 2016, 2018, and most recently 2023! But most importantly, Purple Haze is a group of friends who love to sing together!
summary: In the vibrant Northwestern a cappella community, Purple Haze prides itself on maintaining the highest standards of musicality while keeping the fun and love alive!

name: Quantum Game Club
description: As a community, we define problems and find unique ways to solve them using the quantum toolbox available to us. We will work with leaders in the quantum space to interact with the state-of-the-art virtual and physical layers of the future quantum system
summary: As a community, we define problems and find unique ways to solve them using the quantum toolbox available to us. We will work with leaders in the quantum space to interact with the state-of-the-art virtual and physical layers of the future quantum system

name: Queer Media Association
description: QMA is an affinity space for queer journalists and members of the media, with an emphasis on queer media members of color, to build community and speak their truths. We will also provide resources to Northwestern’s queer journalist and film communities, including guest speakers, job opportunities, conference information and mentorships.
summary: QMA is an affinity space for queer journalists and members of the media, with an emphasis on queer media members of color, to build community and speak their truths.

name: Quizbowl
description:
Northwestern Quizbowl participates in academic, buzzer-based competitions primarily held in the Midwest, including novice tournaments, regular events, and national championships.

We frequently travel to the University of Chicago, the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, the University of Michigan, Michigan State University, and Washington University in St. Louis, the University of Michigan, among others.

We also host middle school, high school, and collegiate tournaments over the course of the academic year. We have also hosted 2005 ACF Nationals, the 2011 PACE NSC, and the 2012 College National Championships of the National History Bowl.
summary: Northwestern Quizbowl participates in academic buzzer competitions against other colleges.

name: Rainbow Alliance
description:
Check out our website:https://sites.northwestern.edu/rainbow/

Our purpose is to create a safe space at Northwestern University that encourages personal development for Northwestern’s 2SLGBTQ+ community and coalition building among our intersecting communities. To this end we:

  • Develop programming to actively address and solve issues surrounding gender and sexuality in the Northwestern community
  • Provide a supportive and fun social environment that creates a sense of community!
  • Fight homophobia, biphobia, transphobia, heterosexism, and genderism and the intersecting oppressions experienced within our diverse community
summary: Our purpose is to create a safe space at Northwestern University that encourages personal development for Northwestern’s 2SLGBTQ+ community and coalition building among our intersecting communities.

name: RAISO
description:
The Responsible Artificial Intelligence Student Organization (RAISO) is Northwestern University’s largest student organization dedicated to artificial intelligence and its ethical impacts. We're a growing community of 65 members making AI work better for everyone.

RAISO currently consists of 5 committees:

  1. The Projects committee organizes machine learning workshops and projects every quarter. Members have built everything from a stock prediction model to a computer vision AI gym trainer.
  2. The Education committee hosts weekly discussions about the latest developments in tech and their ethical implications, as well as movie nights and socials.
  3. The Newsletter committee regularly publishes articles and opinion pieces about tech ethics read by over 450 subscribers, as well as fun contests.
  4. The Events & Partnerships committee hosts speaker events and workshops with Google, IBM and other industry leaders in AI.
  5. The Business & Finance committee helps publicize RAISO initiatives and ensures responsible management of our funds as we advance the cause of responsible AI.

Members can join any committee and may rotate between committees every quarter. Whether you're technical or not, come discuss and build the future of responsible AI at RAISO.
summary: RAISO is Northwestern's largest student organization dedicated to artificial intelligence and its ethical impacts.

name: Red Cross Club at Northwestern University
description: We, as the Northwestern branch of the American Red Cross, work to provide relief to victims of disaster, strive to raise awareness for all of those in need, hold quarterly blood drives, raise funds to support the Red Cross' various initiatives, and support the Red Cross principles of humanity, impartiality, neutrality, independence, voluntary service, unity, and universality.
summary: Northwestern University Chapter of the American Red Cross.

name: Red Dot: Singapore Student Association
description: The Red Dot: Singapore Student Association aims to promote Singapore's rich and diverse culture on campus. As there are few students from Singapore at Northwestern, our primary goal is to foster a sense of community and provide support for students who are interested in Singaporean culture or have a Singaporean background. We welcome students from all backgrounds to join our activities and events throughout the year which are designed to foster friendships and connections, ranging from cultural celebrations and culinary events to community service projects that give back to marginalized communities in Singapore.
summary: The Red Dot: Singapore Student Association nurtures a vibrant community, inviting interest in Singaporean culture. We curate diverse, year-round activities to foster connections and cultural appreciation.

name: Reflections Repertory Company
description: The purpose of Reflections Repertory Company is to choreograph new works of dance and create a community for those who want to train, rehearse, and perform classical and contemporary ballet together. We aim to provide a space for all levels of dancers to grow and continue their training with regular open classes. We also offer a performance division of dancers that perform regularly on-campus and in the Evanston community. This group of dancers creates and learns repertoire through weekly rehearsals, providing a space for dancers to showcase their artistry to a wider audience.
summary: The purpose of Reflections Repertory Company is to choreograph new works of dance and create a community for those who want to train, rehearse, and perform classical and contemporary ballet together.

name: Reformed University Fellowship
description:
Reformed University Fellowship (RUF) is a Christian community at Northwestern seeking to reach out to people from diverse backgrounds with the message of the Gospel and to help one another live our lives in response to who Jesus is and what he has done for us. We meet every Tuesday night at 8:00pm in Vail Chapel for our large group meeting and offer a number of small groups that gather throughout the week to study the Bible, discuss a book, pray together, and build relationships with one another. We also host numerous social events and service opportunities throughout the year. Regardless of your beliefs or doubts, we would love to have you join us.

For more information on RUF and to be added to our mailing list, please visit www.rufnorthwestern.org
summary: Reformed University Fellowship (RUF) is a Christian community at Northwestern seeking to reach out to people from diverse backgrounds with the message of the gospel and to help one another live our lives in response to who Jesus is and what he has done

name: Refresh Dance Crew
description:
To stay up to date on Refresh, follow us on Instagram at @refreshdancecrew!

ReFresH Dance Crew was born in 2005 from a group of beginner dancers who wanted to learn how to dance. Since then, Refresh has grown into a large family of freestylers and choreographers with different styles that range from popping and breaking to modern hip hop choreography. We're structured in a way that promotes the growth of beginner dancers through our free choreography classes and open sessions. Although many of our members only started dancing in college, all of us have learned to love dance and the strong community we've built around it.
summary: Refresh Dance Crew was born in 2005 from a group of friends who wanted to learn how to dance. Since then, Refresh has grown into a large family of dancers with different styles that range from popping and breaking to choreography.

name: Relay For Life
description:
Relay for Life is the largest nationwide fundraiser for the American Cancer Society, raising hundreds of millions of dollars for cancer research and advocacy. Here at Northwestern, we have raised over $100,000 for 4 of the last 7 years and host an annual event with hundreds of participants. Throughout the year, we focus our efforts on advocacy, fundraising, and outreach, culminating in our final event in May!

Sign up for Relay at https://secure.acsevents.org/site/STR?fr_id=109781&pg=entry

Watch a Relay promo video here: http://youtu.be/RF17W0cXe2A
summary: Relay for Life is the largest nationwide fundraiser for the American Cancer Society, raising over $400 million for cancer research and advocacy. We regularly raise over $100,000 and host an annual event with hundreds of participants.

name: Religious & Spiritual Life
description:
The primary mission of the Office of the University Chaplains is to promote spiritual growth and nurture at Northwestern University.

Spiritual maturity entails

    * reflection on the meaning and purpose of human existence
    * appreciation for the depth and expanse of life
    * articulation of beliefs
    * development of spiritual resources to sustain one through good times and bad
    * a willingness to rethink values and to apply them to life
    * a concern for the welfare of humankind and a capacity to act with confidence upon that concern

Spiritual maturity is enhanced by critical dialogue and community with members of one's own tradition and through encounter with differing traditions.

Northwestern is privileged to enjoy a rich and diverse religious life, which supports and challenges the intellectual pursuits of the University.

We are delighted to introduce you to some of the organizations and programs at Northwestern that provide occasions for religious reflection and opportunities for worship, service, and action. Through the various religious traditions represented on campus, students may seek moral and spiritual insights into the meaning and value of human life and experience.

We invite you to explore the rich and varied options available to Northwestern students. Our office is open to all inquiries from students, their parents, and campus visitors. Campus religious leaders and advisors are always ready to help students broaden their faith.

It is our hope that together we can build a community of conscience and spiritual awareness and support the University's search for and celebration of "whatsoever things are true."
summary: The primary mission of the Office of Religious & Spiritual Life is to promote spiritual growth and nurture at Northwestern University.  Spiritual maturity entails      * reflection on the meaning and purpose of human existence

name: Remote Area Medical Volunteer Corps
description:
The core purpose of this organization will be to give Northwestern University students the chance to participate in Remote Area Medical (RAM) trips focused on providing free services to those who are unable to afford medical care themselves and for those who do not have access to hospitals or healthcare facilities in their area. Through this organization, members will have the opportunity to volunteer and travel to RAM clinics across the nation. This will be an especially valuable experience to aspiring healthcare professionals. This organization will aspire to promote quality healthcare both locally and nationally to the fullest of its abilities.

Students will work to fundraise and plan trips to RAM clinics around the United States. At these clinics, volunteers will assist in a variety of fields, some of which include vision testing, patient registration, dental assisting, or making glasses. The Northwestern RAM chapter will also assist in disaster relief efforts, such as through hosting food and clothing drives. 

Click this link to join our groupme: https://groupme.com/join_group/86543501/5IgQOZ1K

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OEQA8RSvfbs
summary: The Northwestern Chapter of Remote Area Medical (RAM) allows students to volunteer in pop-up medical clinics around the country in order to assist with providing free dental, vision, and medical care to those without access to healthcare.

name: Renaissance Singers
description: The Renaissance Singers is a student-led vocal ensemble that performs diverse repertoire from the Renaissance and Baroque eras, focused on the promotion of early music and, occasionally, commissions from student composers.
summary: The Renaissance Singers is a student-led vocal ensemble that performs diverse repertoire from the Renaissance and Baroque eras, focused on the promotion of early music and, occasionally, commissions from student composers.

name: Repro at NU
description: NORJ aims to demystify abortion and provide information about services near campus in order to improve access for the Northwestern community.
summary: NORJ (NU Repro for short) aims to demystify abortion and provide information about services near campus in order to improve access for the Northwestern community.

name: Residential College Board
description:
We serve as an advisory body for the residential colleges here at Northwestern University. Our membership is composed of the presidents of the ten residential colleges and eight executive officers. Each of the seven vice presidents specializes in an area specifically relevant to the residential college community: academic affairs, financial affairs, inter-college relations, philanthropic affairs, public relations, and social affairs. The president presides over the entire Board and coordinates long-term planning.

We hold general meetings every week in which we deal with problems and issues that arise during the week in individual colleges, plan activities for the entire residential college community, and discuss items for presentation to the University. In addition to these weekly meetings, the Executive Board meets every week to guide the Board's agenda.

The Residential College Board is responsible for a number of large-scale social events every year, including the Fall Formal, Winter Quarter Quiz Bowl, and the annual RCB Field Day.
summary: RCB is comprised of the 10 Residential Colleges at Northwestern. Our motto, a tradition of living and learning, exemplifies our goal: to bring an enhanced sense of community to your dorm life and to help students learn outside the classroom.

name: Residential College of Cultural & Community Studies
description:
WELCOME! The Residential College of Cultural and Community Studies (CCS) is one big family of diverse and dedicated students. Its intimate setting and small population easily allow it be one of Northwestern’s best kept secrets. CCS houses 42 women, men, and all else on or off the gender spectrum in single, double, and triple-occupancy rooms. 

Students from as far away as China and as close as the western suburbs live together in CCS, one of Northwestern University's oldest residential colleges. Our majors range from RTVF to psychology to engineering, but we share a love of diverse cultures and concern for our local and global communities.

When not out enjoying the social scene around us on North Campus, we work with our fellows and outside organizations to put on activities like firesides, community service projects, and IM sports. For trips into Chicago, the Noyes El stop is conveniently close. Favorite outings include trips to ethnic neighborhoods, musical and dramatic performances, and camping.
summary: The Residential College of Cultural & Community Studies (CCS) is one big family of diverse and dedicated students. Its intimate setting and small population easily allow it be one of Northwestern's best kept secrets.

name: Residential Life
description: University Residential Life (URL) is a Student Affairs department. Our vision is "Engage and educate: Creating communities of learners, leaders, and responsible citizens. "Through our mission we strive to promote a sense of community and encourage the growth and development of each student in a variety of living environments. Our program seeks to complement the academic mission of Northwestern University by providing students opportunities for learning in a safe, diverse, and respectful environment.
summary: University Residential Life (URL) is a Student Affairs department. Our vision is "Engage and educate: Creating communities of learners, leaders, and responsible citizens. "Through our mission we strive to promote a sense of community and encourage the

name: Residential Services
summary: Residential Services at Northwestern is committed to providing an environment that fosters the academic and personal success of every student.

name: Reverie Literary Magazine
description: We aim to inspire and celebrate imaginative prose and poetry in the undergraduate community, providing a unique haven for dreamers. Whether you're a seasoned wordsmith or a budding apprentice, join us in forging with the magic of storytelling. Expect two editions per year: winter and spring.
summary: Reverie Literary Magazine exclusively nurtures and showcases undergraduate works in the realms of fantasy, science fiction, and speculative fiction.

name: Ritmo de Mis Ancestros: Ballet Folklórico
description:
Previously known as Ballet Folklórico Mexicano: Ritmo de Mis Ancestros.

Ritmo de Mis Ancestros: Ballet Folklorico, was established in 2012 by 6 freshmen Chicanas to create a space at Northwestern that made them feel comfortable enough to share and embrace their artistic side, their culture, and their roots. We strive to critically preserve our Mexican culture by showcasing folklórico at Northwestern and the surrounding communities and to increase awareness of Mexican ancestry and to serve as a catalyst for others to take pride in their own backgrounds. We are currently expanding to other folk dance across Latin America.
summary: Ritmo de Mis Ancestros: Ballet Folklórico (Rhythm of My Ancestors) is a traditional Mexican dance group dedicated to spreading folklore music and dance on Northwestern's campus and the surrounding community.

name: Rock Climbing Club
description:
Our goal is to create an environment that both introduces students to climbing as a sport and offers experienced climbers a collaborative space to help improve their skill level. We have club climbs regularly throughout each quarter at Chicago climbing gyms, offering some free day passes for many of our climbs. These are laid-back events, where people come to climb and hang out with each other. Climbing is in many ways a team sport and we hope to highlight the collaborative and fun spirit that defines the best climbing communities.

*Please view connected socials and website to join the club*

https://www.rccnu.com/
summary:
The Rock Climbing Club does climbing. Please view our instagram or this link for more info: 
https://www.rccnu.com/

name: Rotaract
description:
Rotaract clubs bring together people ages 18 and older to exchange ideas with leaders in the community, develop leadership and professional skills, and have fun through service. In communities worldwide, Rotary and Rotaract members work side by side to take action through service. Rotaract emphasizes a strong commitment to promoting world understanding and peace through the development of leadership among those ages 18 – 30. Today, there are more than 8,000 Rotary-supported Rotaract clubs with more than 175,000 members in 164 countries around the world.This NU club is committed to promoting world understanding and peace through young adult leadership development. A variety of projects and activities are organized that fall into three categories: professional development, leadership development, and service project development. NU Rotaract dedicates spring break to international service, connecting with Rotaract chapters abroad and working on a week-long project.

 

Join our slack!! https://join.slack.com/t/rotaract-5rb6114/shared_invite/zt-1snpdbmf5-e4o23eYHoPE86We31vJBRA
summary: As a sub-organization of Rotary International, Rotaract club brings us together to exchange ideas with leaders in the community, develop leadership and professional skills, and have fun through service. We also focus on international travel and community

name: Rug Tufting Club
description: Rug Tufting Club is here to provide the students of Northwestern with a new, fun, and creative way to relax and recuperate from the stress of their day to day lives. At Rug Tufting Club, students will have the opportunity to design, draft, and create their own personalized rugs over the course of the quarter (or however long it takes them to complete their project). The club is open to all, regardless of previous experience, and there will be plenty of chances to learn and improve their skills during the regularly scheduled meetings of the club. Let's get Tufting!
summary: Rug Tufting Club is here to provide the students of Northwestern with a new, fun, and creative way to relax and recuperate from the stress of their day to day lives. Come join us to learn about rug tufting and create your very own personalized rug!

name: Rural and Small Towns Collective
description: • An organization for Northwestern students from rural or small-town areas (generally a population of 50,000 or less). We put together fun community-building events as well as put together resources that help alleviate the transition into college and living in the Chicago area!
summary: An organization for Northwestern students from rural or small-town areas (generally a population of 50,000 or less).

name: Russian Club
description: Our purpose is to give Russian students of all levels and even students with no Russian knowledge an outlet outside of class to learn more about the Russian language and culture in an informal setting.
