We want to host fun events that improve students speaking ability (something not often focused on in class in favor of grammar and reading comprehension) and their knowledge of Soviet Russian and modern Russian food and culture.
summary: Our purpose is to give Russian students of all levels an outlet outside of class to learn more about Russian language and culture. We want to host fun events that improve students speaking and their knowledge of Soviet and modern Russian food and culture

name: Scene+Heard
description: Scene+Heard is purposed to connect artists and audiences and chronicle culture, on-campus and off. It was created on the basis of making arts more accessible and engaging to a broader network of students, rather than the more insular theater and music communities that often comprise the majority of performance audiences at Northwestern. The website, sceneandheardonline.com, will make information about performances more accessible to students through the prolific publication of new artistic and cultural events and happenings in our community.
summary: Scene+Heard is Northwestern's official online arts and culture publication. Through dynamic content, exclusive interviews, and first-hand coverage of the best events on-campus and Chicago, we're transforming the way students interact with culture.

name: Scholars of Finance
description:
Frozen October 2024

We seek to solve the world’s largest problems by investing in undergraduate students through leadership development, mentorship, and the active support of hundreds of purpose-driven finance executives and investors.

Our members join top firms, funds, and a life-long support community that empowers them to spend their careers serving the greater good using the biggest lever in the world: finance.
summary: Scholars of Finance is a rapidly growing organization on a mission to inspire character and integrity in the finance leaders of tomorrow.

name: Science Policy Outreach Task Force
description: The Science Policy Outreach Task Force (SPOT) is a non-partisan organization of Northwestern University researchers focused on advocating for science, evidence-based reasoning, and scientifically-sound policy to the voting-aged public and policymakers. Members have the opportunity to learn and put effective science advocacy into political action through events to educate voters, meetings to educate and collaborate with policymakers, educational seminars to learn about the interface of science and policy, and activities with external science policy entities. As a group, our combined efforts allow for a greater impact toward the advocacy of science in politics than any one of us acting alone.
summary: Advocating for science and evidenced-based decision making to policymakers and the voting public.

name: Seesaw Theatre
description:
Seesaw Theatre produces highly interactive, multi-sensory theatre for autistic and otherwise disabled individuals. Founded with the belief that all individuals should have the opportunity to experience theatre in a welcoming, safe environment, we offer autistic and otherwise disabled individuals and their parents the ability to experience theatre together free from societal expectations of what behavior should look like in a theatrical setting. Our theatre spaces are used in innovative ways to create an entire world to absorb, full of features that engage a variety of senses. Seesaw Theatre is produced solely by undergraduate students and continues to provide theatre free of charge.

For more information about our work please visit http://www.seesawtheatre.org. 

To get involved email us at seesawtheatre@gmail.com.
summary: Seesaw Theatre strives to enrich the lives of autistic and otherwise disabled individuals by increasing their access to theatre and fostering the use of performance as a channel for expression.

name: Segal Professional Bridge
description:
Frozen October 2024

Segal Professional Bridge is an organization aimed at connecting students, faculty members, and industry professionals who are passionate about innovation and entrepreneurship.

Highlights include:

  • Presenting students with networking opportunities through industry visits, speaker series, professional and societies panels
  • Helping students to get connected with relevant industry-level projects to enhance their skillset 
  • Exposing students to events hosted by the Northwestern and Chicago communities
  • Providing students with opportunities for professional development through mentorship programming
summary: Segal Professional Bridge is an organization aimed at connecting students, faculty members, and industry professionals who are passionate about innovation and entrepreneurship.

name: Self Care Sunday
description: At Self Care Sunday we offer a support group as well as wellness experiences via a hybrid format. Our primary clientele are Black Womxn who feel empowered to live more balanced lifestyles.
summary: At Self Care Sunday we offer a support group as well as wellness experiences via a hybrid format.

name: Sexual Health and Assault Peer Educators
description:
SHAPE is a Northwestern student organization affiliated with NU's Center for Awareness, Response, and Education (CARE) that combats rape culture and works to create a culture of healthy sexuality through education, public events, and an open dialogue on sexual health, sex positivity, and sexual assault. SHAPE's mission is to increase students’ comfort about sexuality, encourage them to learn and adopt healthy behaviors and help them recognize and address unhealthy and dangerous behaviors and attitudes regarding sexuality and relationships.

Members of SHAPE receive training from current SHAPE educators and Director of CARE, along with Northwestern's Title IX Coordinator and Northwestern- or community-affiliated doctors, nurse practitioners, advocates, counselors, and other experts in the fields of sexual health and sexual assault prevention. Peer educators are trained on a range of topics including:

  - Consent & Communication

  - Sexual Assault

  - Supporting Survivors

  - Safer Sex & STI’s

  - Intersectionality

  - LGBTQ+ sexual health and inclusivity

  - Healthy Relationships

  - Reproductive anatomy & birth control

  - Title IX

  - & More!

 

After training, peer educators provide presentations, workshops, and trainings to students, organize outreach events & tabling, collaborate with other student organizations, and serve as a resource for individual students looking for information about sexual health and sexual assault.

For membership inquiries or any questions please DM us on Instagram @nushape or email shape@u.northwestern.edu
summary: SHAPE is a Northwestern student organization affiliated with CARE that combats rape culture and works to create a culture of healthy sexuality through education, public events, and open dialogue on sexual health, sex positivity, and sexual assault.

name: Shepard Residential College
summary: Shepard is a multithematic residential college that houses 74 students, mainly sophomores and freshmen. It is located on south campus, at the center of the sorority quad. Its residents refer to themselves as "sheep."

name: Shia Muslim Association
description: SMA's mission is to educate Shia Muslims and the broader Northwestern community by upholding and promoting the teachings of the Sublime Qur'an, Prophet Muhammad, Lady Fatimah, and the Twelve Imams from his Household. We actively participate in fostering dialogue, promoting cooperation, and encouraging engagement within the larger Muslim and non-Muslim communities at Northwestern University. Our aim is to enrich the religious and spiritual life on campus by creating a platform for a comparative understanding of Shia Islam, other Islamic denominations, and various religions through the organization of lectures, workshops, and symposia.
summary: SMA provides an educational forum for the intellectual development of Shia Muslims student community on campus and aims to spread the knowledge of the Quran, the Prophet Muhammad, the Lady Fatimah, and the Twelve Imams through lectures and symposia.

name: ShireiNU A Cappella
description: ShireiNU A Cappella is an open-minded, welcoming, and collaborative community of all backgrounds and identities. We are first and foremost a group of friends passionate about singing together and having fun. As Northwestern’s premier Jewish a cappella group, we arrange and sing a variety of musical genres, including pop, indie, and alternative, and a number of Hebrew pop songs per year. We host a show once per quarter and perform at a variety of venues and events throughout Northwestern, the Chicagoland area, and on tour in cities across the country! Check us out on Instagram and YouTube (@ShireiNUaCappella)
summary: ShireiNU A Cappella is Northwestern's premier Jewish a Capella group, founded in 2001. With both Jewish and non-Jewish members, we sing English and some Hebrew music all over campus and throughout Chicagoland!

name: SIG Theater
description: Frozen October 2024
summary: Special Interest Group in Chinese Theater (SIG Theater) is a TGS-sponsored theater club at Northwestern. Our goal is to broaden student life by hosting theater-related events.

name: Sigma Alpha Epsilon
description:
Illinois Psi-Omega Chapter, Northwestern University
2325 Sheridan Rd.

Installed 1894

Chapter President: Shuhey Wada

You may visit the Illinois Psi-Omega Chapter's website at www.saeilpo.com

Organization Facts

National Founding Date: March 9, 1856

Colors: Royal purple, old gold

You may visit the national website at http://www.sae.net/

SAE Nationally:

227 total chapters + 19 colonies = 246 total groups
Approximately 13,000 undergraduates
Average chapter size, including pledges and actives, is 51 men.
Sigma Alpha Epsilon has initiated more than 305,000 men since badge sequences were first recorded.

The Fraternity’s largest chapters are located at the University of Texas at Austin, with 142 members, and the University of Colorado at Boulder, with 138 members.

There are approximately 190,000 living alumni in the Fraternity. 

The average colony size is 32 men, and the average colony GPA is 3.1.

Awards

NU's Greek Awards 2011-2012

  • Greek Leadership Award, JP McCleary

SAE National Awards 2011-2012

  • Zeal Award for National Chapter of the Year
summary:
Illinois Psi-Omega Chapter, Northwestern University 2325 Sheridan Rd.
Installed 1894
Chapter President: Shuhey Wada
Illinois Psi-Omega Chapter's Instagram https://www.instagram.com/northwesternu_sae/
National Founding Date: 3/9/1856

name: Sigma Alpha Iota
description:
Sigma Alpha Iota is an international music fraternity for women, open to female and nonbinary identifying students of all majors as an opportunity to continue their musical involvement after high school. We offer performance opportunities, music related philanthropies and social events and outings for all of our members. Our House is located in the Sorority Quad, right next to the Willard Residential College. As SAI is outside of the PanHellenic system, students are able to be in both SAI and a PHA sorority if interested. 

Visit our full website or visit our Facebook Page to learn more details!

___________________________________

Sigma Alpha Iota is an organization which promotes interaction among those who share a commitment to music. Members of SAI are active in all areas of campus music and campus life, working closely with faculty, administration, campus and community groups, music professionals and patrons. In addition to personal encouragement and support, members may receive scholarships, loans and awards in many areas and at all levels of music-related study. Sigma Alpha Iota has long been recognized as a leader in the field of music and provides a lifetime of fraternity contact.

The SAI Alumnae Association allows alumnae members to remain active and supportive of the ideals of SAI.

Members of Sigma Alpha Iota, in collegiate and alumnae chapters, devote their talents and energies to many national and international projects including those of Sigma Alpha Iota Philanthropies, Inc.:

  • The SAI quarterly, PAN PIPES, comprehensively lists American premieres and works-in-progress in each Winter issue, and is included in the Library of Congress Archives.
  • The People-to-People Music Committee, Inc., sends music, instruments, and educational materials to deserving musicians world-wide.
  • The triennial Inter-American Music Awards (IAMA) and commissions recognize and encourage the finest in creative composition throughout the western hemisphere.
  • Undergraduate, Graduate, and Doctoral Scholarships and Loans, Summer Music Scholarships, and Chapter Outreach Grants are available to SAI members and chapters.

Sigma Alpha Iota accepts with pride the responsibilities and privileges of being a leader in the pursuit of music creation, performance, and scholarship.

To visit our national website, click here.
summary: Sigma Alpha Iota is an international music fraternity for women, open to female identifying students of all majors as an opportunity to continue their musical involvement after high school. Visit our website at http://sainorthwestern.wixsite.com/saibeta

name: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc.
description:
About Sigma

Sigma Gamma Rho was founded on and continues to be steadfastly committed to the tenets of excellence in Scholarship, Sisterhood and Service.  Sigma Gamma Rho has a proud legacy of providing positive and proactive community leadership and support when and wherever needed as indicated by the sorority's international slogan, "Greater Service, Greater Progress."

 

 

Foundation

Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. was organized on November 12, 1922, in Indianapolis, Indiana, by seven young educators: Mary Lou Allison Gardner Little, Dorothy Hanley Whiteside, Vivian White Marbury, Nannie Mae Gahn Johnson, Hattie Mae Annette Dulin Redford, Bessie Mae Downey Rhoades Martin and Cubena McClure. The group became an incorporated national collegiate sorority on December 30, 1929, when a charter was granted to Alpha chapter at Butler University.


Soaring To Greater Heights Of Attainment Around The World, Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc., as a leading national service organization, has met the challenges of the day and continues to grow through Sisterhood, Scholarship and Service.
summary: The Eta Chapter of Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc.

name: Sigma Lambda Gamma National Sorority, Inc.
description:
Tau Alpha Chapter, Northwestern University

Installed February 19, 2000

"Culture is Pride, Pride is Success."

President: Gabriella Rios

You may visit the Tau Alpha Chapter's website at www.tagammas.org. 

Organization Facts

National founding date: April 9, 1990

Mission Statement:

Through excellence in the organization’s five founding principles, Sigma Lambda Gamma National Sorority, Inc. provides opportunities for lifelong empowerment to its members, thereby positively influencing the global community.

Vision Statement: 

Empowered women leading the world.

Colors: Shocking Pink and Majestic Purple

You may view the Sigma Lambda Gamma national website at www.sigmalambdagamma.com.
summary:
Tau Alpha Chapter, Northwestern University
Installed February 19, 2000
"Culture is Pride, Pride is Success."
President:  Gabriella Rios
You may visit the Tau Alpha Chapter's website at www.tagammas.org

name: Sigma Nu Fraternity
description:
Sigma Nu recolonized the Gamma Beta Chapter at Northwestern during the spring of 2013!

A group of 45 men came together to recolonize Gamma Beta in the winter of 2013. Since then, 15 more have joined in an effort to create a fraternity from scratch, to mold it into what they believe a model fraternity should look like. This group of men strive to create an inclusive, friendly, and collaborative environment where everyone is welcomed and encouraged to take on leadership opportunities. 

Now, we are 80 members strong and growing. With a new house, strong motivation, and a close brotherhood, we invite you to see if you want to be part of something greater than yourself.

...
Visit the website for more info and fill out a prospective member questionnaire!
http://becomeasigmanu.org/prospective-member-questionnaire

If you'd like to make a membership referral, please click the link below.
http://becomeasigmanu.org/membership-referral
summary: Gamma Beta Chapter was founded in 1898 with a rich history and tradition at Northwestern and is committed to its principles of Love, Honor and Truth, and has stood in firm opposition to hazing since its founding in 1869 at the Virginia Military Institute

name: Sigma Phi Epsilon
description:
The Illinois Lambda Chapter of Sigma Phi Epsilon was founded at Northwestern University in 1989, and rechartered in 2016. As a chapter, we strive to provide the single best undergraduate Greek experience on campus. Please reach out if you would like to know more about us!

As a national organization, Sigma Phi Epsilon is the nation's largest fraternity with over 15,000 undergraduate members and more than 295,000 total lifetime members.
summary:
Illinois Lambda Chapter, Northwestern University 
2341 Sheridan Rd.
"Building Balanced Men"

name: Sigma Psi Zeta Sorority, Inc.
description:
Alpha Beta Chapter, Northwestern University

Installed May 10, 2008

President: Elaine Jiang

"True to our colors, red and gold. Sisters forever, heart and soul"

Organization Facts

National founding date: March 23, 1994

The fundamental goal of Sigma Psi Zeta Sorority is to promote awareness of the myriad Asian/Asian-American cultures. Our sorority addresses this issue through leadership, outreach, individual, community interaction, and most importantly, the bonds of sisterhood. The sorority also promotes development among its members, the students and the faculty at each of the campuses we represent. In order to achieve this goal, we partake in political, social, educational, and cultural activities. Each effort improves our environment, and instills within our sisters experiences that will follow them long after they've graduated. Through a network of like-minded yet highly individualistic women, the sorority lends structure and support to sisters that share this Sigma vision. We believe the strength of our foundation derives from the unity of sisterhood, one that was proudly established years ago. In addition, we work in collaboration with other Greek and non-Greek organizations to provide services for the local communities and the community at large. It is through our various participation in these cultural events, educational workshops, and philanthropic support that we make a difference. Nationally, regionally, and individually, we strive to embody the ideals of Sigma Psi Zeta.

Colors: Red and Gold

You may visit the Sigma Psi Zeta national website at www.sigmapsizeta.org.

name: SigO
description:
Founded in 1994, Significant Others is Northwestern’s treble voices a cappella group. SigO is a close community of friends who love to sing and make amazing music together! We are a completely student-run organization that puts on a unique show each quarter featuring a fun theme and setlist that we arrange.

While we were initially founded as a women’s a cappella group, we have since expanded our mission statement to include female, non-binary, trans, and any other non-cis identifying individuals. We strongly value inclusivity and creating a safe space for historically marginalized gender identities.

SigO puts on three different shows during the school year: a fall show, a winter show, and a spring show. Throughout the year, we sing at various gigs on campus and throughout the Chicagoland area, including charity events, opening acts for other groups, and new student week performances. We also travel to perform with a cappella groups at other universities throughout the midwest!

For more information, check out our website and Instagram @sigonu!
summary: Significant Others is Northwestern's premier a cappella group for treble voices, founded in 1994. We are a close community of female and non-binary folx who love to sing together! We are also avid Just Dance fans.

name: Simpli-Sci
description: Simpli-Sci works to increase science literacy and interest in science from underrepresented communities by presenting topics in nontraditional formats, specifically graphic novels. Our production process is split into three parts, the writing, design, and marketing team. Students at Northwestern make up these teams, many of them coming from a background in science. They are passionate about making science more equitable while showcasing their creative sides. These graphic novels catch the attention of young learners and motivate them to learn more about science, without the overwhelming blocks of text that come from science literature. Simpli-Sci creates graphic novels on a variety of subjects, including animals, how the body functions, and scientific discoveries or inventions. The wide range of topics means that anyone can find something they are interested in. In addition to these graphic novels, we hold speaker series with established scientists who share their paths into how they broke into science, further inspiring and showing learners to pursue science. In order for Simpli-Sci to distribute the materials in an equitable manner, all of our novels are posted to our website at no cost to the customer. For communities and underfunded school districts that might not have access to electronics and our website, we set aside a printing budget and bring hard copies to schools.
summary: Simpli-Sci works to increase science literacy and interest in science from underrepresented communities by presenting topics in nontraditional formats, specifically graphic novels.

name: Sit & Spin Productions
description: Sit & Spin Productions exists to expand the definition of theatre at Northwestern University by giving artists opportunities to explore unconventional, risk-taking works. We promote new approaches to existing material, adapted or original work, and site-specific theatre. We are dedicated to providing administrative, artistic, and production support for all aspects of our season.
summary: Sit & Spin Productions is a theatre board at Northwestern University that produces unconventional, risk-taking, and site-specific work.

name: Slivka Hall
shortName: Residential College of Science and Engineering
description:
The Science and Engineering Residential College at Slivka Hall provides a unique living and learning opportunity for undergraduates interested in science, engineering, and technology. College members take part in tutorials, firesides, and distinguished lectures on topics ranging from ethics in the lab to quantum teleportation to AIDS in America. They participate in community service projects and fundraisers, go to Chicago sports events, and organize camping and biking trips. The college sponsors an annual Thanksgiving dinner, and a barbecue is held in June to "toast" our graduating seniors.

Slivka is located on north campus just a short walk from the Technological Institute, the science and engineering library, the Henry Crown Sports Pavilion and Norris Aquatics Center, and Patten Gym.

Slivka houses 140 students in single- and double-occupancy rooms, arranged in a closed suite-style layout. Men and women live in suites clustered by gender. This building is available to all undergraduate students.
summary: The Slivka Residential College of Science and Engineering provides a unique living and learning opportunity for undergraduates interested in science, engineering, and technology. Slivka houses 140 students in single and double rooms.

name: Snapshot!
description: Hello! We are Snapshot! We are a barrier-free organization at Northwestern focused on providing a space for photographers of all skills! If you are interested in meeting other photographers, honing your craft, or just a social space come join us!
summary: Hello! We are Snapshot! We are a barrier-free organization at Northwestern focused on providing a space for photographers of all skills! If you are interested in meeting other photographers, honing your craft, or just a social space come join us!

name: Social Justice Education
summary: Social Justice Education (SJE) creates co-curricular educational opportunities in partnership with our student community that foster self-exploration, facilitate conversations across difference and support actions that create social change on campus.

name: Societe Francophone
description:
Frozen October 2024

The Societe Francophone is a weekly meeting space for students who want to practice and improve their french conversational skills. The club is open and welcome to all levels from complete beginners to fluent french speakers. We are also planning to have french cultural immersion events, such as visiting patisseries, french improv or watching a french movie.
summary: The Societe Francophone is a weekly meeting space for students who want to practice and improve their french conversational skills.

name: Society for Ecological Restoration (Northwestern Chapter)
description:
Frozen October 2024

The Society of Ecological Restoration creates and participates in restoration projects and programs, and facilitates opportunities for organization members to build essential skills in the science, practice, and policy of ecological restoration. Our organization will leverage existing partnerships with organizations committed to restoration and conservation (such as the Chicago Botanic Garden, the Forest Preserves of Cook County, the Chicago Park District, and the Illinois Native Plant Society) to foster a local restoration network within Northwestern University that delivers positive impacts in our communities.
summary: The Society of Ecological Restoration creates and participates in restoration projects and programs, and facilitates opportunities for organization members to build essential skills in the science, practice, and policy of ecological restoration.

name: Society for Psychedelic Studies
description:
Our mission is to advance the scholarly understanding of psychedelic medicines through interdisciplinary academic exploration, focusing on their historical, anthropological, and scientific significance.

We aim to:

- Examine the cultural and religious contexts of psychedelic medicines across societies

- Study current scientific research and clinical applications

- Promote informed academic discourse about public health approaches to psychedelic compounds

- Promote evidence-based education about harm reduction

- Explore the intersection of psychedelic research with issues of social justice and healthcare access
summary: Our mission is to advance the scholarly understanding of psychedelic medicines through interdisciplinary academic exploration, focusing on their historical, anthropological, and scientific significance.

name: Society of Asian Scientists & Engineers
description: The Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers (SASE) seeks to advance Asian-heritage scientific and engineering professionals and help achieve their full potential. SASE members help create and partake in a variety of cultural and professional development activities as well as volunteering work to help the surrounding community. SASE is open to enrolled Northwestern students of all majors, races, ethnicities, and genders, and is an organization where students can connect over an appreciation of Pan-Asian culture and support each other.
summary: SASE prepares Asian heritage scientists, engineers & technologists for success in the global business world, celebrates diversity & provides opportunities for students to contribute to their local communities.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
description:
SHPE History
The Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers (SHPE) was founded in Los Angeles, California, in 1974 by a group of engineers employed by the city of Los Angeles. Their objective was to form a national organization of professional engineers to serve as role models in the Hispanic community.
The concept of networking was the key basis for the organization. SHPE quickly established two student chapters to begin the network that would grow to encompass the nation as well as reach countries outside the United States. Today, SHPE enjoys a strong but independent network of professional and student chapters throughout the nation.

SHPE Mission
SHPE at Northwestern University promotes the advancement of all ethnicities, majors, and backgrounds in education, employment, and society. SHPE at Northwestern University serves the Northwestern Community and surrounding schools through academic workshops, community service, and student group collaboration.

Chapter History
In the mid-1990s, SHPE was founded as a source of encouragement for the underrepresented Latinx minorities in Engineering at Northwestern. As of this year, we have grown to over 60 active members. We are currently in the process of researching and maintaining our local chapter's history, as well as establishing connections with our alumni.

Vision
SHPE is the leading social-technical organization whose primary function is to enhance and achieve the potential of Hispanics in engineering, math and science.

Strategic Focus Areas
We will fulfill our mission by increasing educational opportunities, promoting professional and personal growth, carry out our social responsibility to be involved in education, business and government issues and enhancing pride within our organization and reinforcing our reputation as a vital Hispanic organization

Statement of Values
We are brought together by heritage, social responsibility, and desire to improve the equality of all people through the use of science and technology. We value excellence in education, professional pursuits and leadership. We obtain excellence through integrity, empowerment, achievement, diversity and continuous improvement.

Connect with us:

Join our groupme, where members can be updated on upcoming events and activities:

https://groupme.com/join_group/30690709/PO7uQlE8

Follow our instagram: @shpe_nu

If you wish to be added to our emailing list, please reach out to shpe@u.northwestern.edu
summary: Our mission is to promote the development of Hispanics in engineering, science, and other technical professions to achieve educational excellence, economic opportunity and social equity.

name: Society of Physics Students
description: The Northwestern University Society of Physics students seeks to connect students interested in physics, astronomy, and related fields. Whether you're looking for a pre-professional organization or just a community of STEM (well, science) enthusiasts, SPS is for you! We plan public lectures, demonstrations, and events open to the entire Northwestern community, as well as informal meetings to help students and professors get to know each other. We are also expanding our outreach efforts to local elementary and high schools to encourage younger students’ interest in science.
summary: SPS is a community for anyone interested in physics, astronomy, space science, engineering ... Whether you're seeking pre-professional experience or a community of like-minded enthusiasts, the Society of Physics Students welcomes you!

name: Society of Trans and Non-binary Students
description: Northwestern STANS aims to promote and develop a community of queer and gender non-conforming students, helping to nurture relationships, support networks, and various events from a diverse network of students, through informal get-togethers and info sessions.
summary: N/A

name: Society of Women Engineers
description: SWE hosts a variety of events to foster community and support for fellow undergrads and all gender minorities in STEM. Our five committees are Professional Development, Outreach, Social, Publicity, and HeforSWE. To receive more information about how you can get involved, join our listserv at https://nu-swe.weebly.com/contact.html!
summary: SWE’s mission is to create a supportive community of women and non-binary engineers through professional and social programming. We aim to empower our members to fulfill their full potential as engineers and leaders and inspire the next generation.

name: Sociology Collective
description: The Sociology Collective aims to create a community on campus for students who are interested in sociology or are currently majors or minors in sociology. Our goal is to provide peer-led resources, information, and advice about the sociology department so that more students can interact with this discipline, whether through major/minors or simply exploring their personal interests. The Sociology Collective also creates spaces for students who are passionate about sociology to connect with each other through fun social events and open, engaging discussions.
summary: The Sociology Collective aims to create a community on campus for students who are interested in sociology or are currently majors or minors in sociology.

name: Soka Gakkai International Buddhists for Peace
description: We are members of the Northwestern University community contributing to the creation of a culture of peace and nonviolence based on the teachings of the Nichiren school of Mahayana Buddhism as practiced in SGI-USA. Recognizing that the point of departure for lasting peace is the establishment of a firm inner self that will not be shaken by the most difficult of circumstances, we aim to cultivate a network of friendship to support and encourage other members of the university community to enjoy fulfilling, happy lives filled with hope for the future. Our organization primarily serves graduate students and postdoctoral fellows, while warmly welcoming participation from all Northwestern constituents.
summary: We are members of the Northwestern University community contributing to the creation of a culture of peace and nonviolence based on the teachings of the Nichiren school of Mahayana Buddhism as practiced in SGI-USA.

name: Songwriters Association at Northwestern
description:
SWAN aims to create a space on campus for songwriters at Northwestern University (NU) to create and share their work with fellow musicians. SWAN encourages collaboration and inclusion for musicians of all levels and creative goals.

Becoming a member of SWAN is free and open to undergrad and grad students, and you can join at any point during the year! 

Our Events:

  • open mics
  • songwriting prompts/challenges
  • studio sessions/music production workshops
  • SWAN Mixtape
  • music festivals like Kresgepalooza
  • Battle of the Bands
  • and more!

See our Linktree to join our listserv and see more from SWAN: https://linktr.ee/nuswan
summary: SWAN aims to create a space on campus for songwriters at Northwestern University (NU) to create and share their work with fellow musicians. SWAN encourages collaboration and inclusion for musicians of all levels and creative goals.

name: Soul4Real
description: Soul4Real A Cappella is Northwestern's premiere Black student a cappella group. Founded in 2009, S4R serves to expose the Northwestern community to the fullest spectrum of historically Black musical genres including R&B, Soul, Hip-Hop and more.
summary: Soul4Real A Cappella is Northwestern's premiere Black student a cappella group. Founded in 2009, S4R serves to expose the Northwestern community to the fullest spectrum of historically Black musical genres including R&B, Soul, Hip-Hop and more.

name: South Asian Students Alliance
description:
The South Asian Students Alliance (SASA) is the largest cultural student organization on campus that is dedicated to the celebration of South Asian culture. We proudly put on events that display our South Asian culture throughout the year, the largest of which is the annual SASA Show held in February. Other events include a Fall Speaker, a Garba/Raas/Bhangra, Holi, and student dialogues (among others). We dedicate time and effort to fostering a vibrant student community that engages with South Asian culture on the NU campus and in its surrounding community.

 

For any questions, comments, or concerns, please contact:

Co-Presidents Lavanya Subramanian and Aarav Raina at nusasaexec@gmail.com
summary: The South Asian Students Alliance (SASA) is the largest cultural student organization on campus that is dedicated to the celebration of South Asian culture.

name: South East Asian Alliance
description: The SouthEast Asian Alliance (SEA Alliance) is dedicated to fostering cross-cultural understanding and communication among various South East Asian cultural organizations. Through the organization of quarterly mutual events, the SEA Alliance aims to bring together individuals from different countries, promoting mutual appreciation of diverse cultures, languages, and traditions. Additionally, the coalition seeks to enhance collaboration among member organizations by facilitating the efficient sharing of resources and expertise. Acting as a unified voice, especially in high-stake events like senate meetings, the SEA Alliance advocates for support for South East Asian student organizations, thereby increasing their influence and visibility. By uniting people from different backgrounds and working towards common goals, the coalition addresses issues too large for individual organizations, providing a platform for idea exchange and relationship-building to benefit the region as a whole. Ultimately, the SEA Alliance strives to create a unified voice that brings about greater impact and improved support for South East Asian students and organizations on campus.
summary: The SouthEast Asian Coalition (SEA Coalition) is dedicated to fostering cross-cultural understanding and communication among various South East Asian cultural organizations.

name: Spectrum Theatre Company
description: Spectrum aims to inspire thought and dialogue both on campus and within the larger community. Spectrum is dedicated to recognizing the spectrum of beliefs, opinions, colors, creeds, and lifestyles of all people by producing thought provoking and challenging theater. We seek to collaborate with and empower the Northwestern community through theatre. Our seasons consist of scripted plays, devised work, and public happenings centered around a relevant politically and social conscious issue.
summary: Spectrum Theatre Company is dedicated to empowering the greater Northwestern community by raising awareness, inspiring dialogue, and affecting change through theatrical experiences based on relevant social and political issues.

name: Speech Team
description: Members of the speech team spend their year crafting immaculate performances for competitions around the country, all in the hopes of receiving useful feedback (and maybe a trophy or two). Revise. Memorize. Repeat. Since 1994, the Northwestern Speech Team has won five Team National Titles, 18 individual national championships, and placed in 91 national final rounds. We've also made a lot of friends along the way! With 11 different events to choose from, you are sure to find an avenue to explore your interests and develop your public speaking skills. While high school speech experience will translate, no previous forensics experience is required. The team is looking for students who are passionate about the activity, eager to learn, and ready to work.
summary: The Speech Team offers students a venue to participate in collegiate public speaking competitions. We also host showcases and tournaments on campus, such as the Tournament of Champions in Extemporaneous Speaking.

name: SPG Improv
description:
Frozen October 2024

We're Northwestern's only Graduate Student improv troupe!
summary: Graduate Student Improv Troupe

name: Splash
summary: Northwestern Splash is an outreach organization that allows Chicagoland high school students to take classes taught by members of the NU community.

name: Spoon University
description:
Spoon is on a mission to make food make sense. The Food Network for college students, our content is written by students, for students learning to use their kitchens for the first time. On on website or in our quarterly print magazine, you'll find everything food, lifestyle and culture, from family recipes passed down through generations to our favorite Evanston restaurants. Spoon University is here to simplify and celebrate the exploration of food as students embark on their own culinary journey.

We offer opportunities for involvement through writing, photography, videography, and marketing.  We publish editorial content daily and a yearbook-style print magazine each Winter and Spring.  Please visit  our chapter website, our national website, our instagram, or email us at spoonuniversitynu@gmail.com for more information!
summary:
The Food Network of the college world. 

Follow @spoon_nu on insta for recruitment info! Please don't request membership through Wildcat Connection or through the join button on Spoon's website. Our app will be linked in our insta bi

name: Sport Management Club
description:
Sport Management Club is committed to student advancement in the following five domains of the sport industry:

  1. Sport Business
  2. Sport Law
  3. Sport Analytics & Strategy
  4. Sport Media & Journalism
  5. College Athletics & Name, Image, and Likeness

Each domain will have an SMC committee: members of the club will be free to join committees in the as many fields as they are interested in, but will be housed in a primary committee of their strongest interest. Programming will include opportunities such as stadium trips, speaker panels, case competitions, and sport service endeavors.
summary: Sport Management Club is an inclusive, pre-professional undergraduate organization that empowers Northwestern students to discover and develop their prospects in sport management.

name: Sports Analytics Group
description: The Sports Analytics Group is a group of Northwestern undergraduates committed to strategic data analysis and outside-the-box thinking in the world of sports. Our organization serves as an outlet for Northwestern students interested in sports analytics, attracting those who are creative, have a dedicated passion for sports statistics, and use analytical thinking to solve problems. We offer our members the ability to bridge the gap between sports and data analytics by fostering the education and guidance necessary to produce content that provides valuable insight into all facets of sports (business, roster management, fantasy, in-game strategy, etc.).
summary: The Sports Analytics Group is a group of Northwestern undergraduates committed to strategic data analysis and outside the box thinking in all facets of sports (business, roster management, fantasy, in-game strategy, etc.).

name: Spotlight
description: Spotlight is a Food Condition Club. Our goal is to spread food condition awareness and education to college campuses nationwide. In the past year, we have hosted epinephrine auto-injector training sessions in Norris so Northwestern students know how to assist their friends during allergic reactions, hosted dialogues where students with food conditions can share their experiences navigating Northwestern, and have even painted pumpkins for food allergy awareness!
summary: Food Condition Club. Our goal is to spread food condition awareness and education to college campuses nationwide.

name: SPS MSA Student Organization
description:
Frozen October 2024

Successful networking can be an essential component for entry into the sports field. Northwestern University's MSA program provides a range of networking opportunities that provide MSA students and alumni with a competitive advantage as they begin and look to advance in their careers.

  • MSA faculty and staff have access to team executives, sports agents, corporate sports consultants and a broad range of other professionals in collegiate, professional and amateur athletics administration.
  • The MSA student board offers networking events that give students inside access to Chicago-area sports operations and provide opportunities to meet with and learn from team management and executives. See below for recent events.
  • Select MSA courses feature guest speakers from the sports industry who provide their insight and guidance.
  • MSA alumni hold positions in many areas of sports. As Northwestern University alumni, MSA graduates become part of an alumni population known nationwide for excellence and achievement.
summary: The School of Professional Studies offers a part-time Master of Arts in Sports Administration (MSA) program that focuses on the business of athletics. The MSA Student Board adds opportunities to meet and socialize with industry professionals.

name: SPS Northwestern University Prehealth Professional
description: Frozen October 2024

name: StageStrat
description:
Started in 2024, StageStrat is the first student-run consulting agency at Northwestern dedicated to the entertainment industry. Our mission is to foster a professional and inclusive environment for students to discover the intersections between business and the arts through mentorship, client work, and a shared passion for the arts. StageStrat will introduce students to business functions within the entertainment sector, which include investing, operations, marketing, and production.

Our focus on fostering our members' professional journeys enables us to deliver exceptional value to our clients. We provide our clients with tailored strategies by leveraging our members’ unique experiences in the arts and their unwavering passion for exploring the trends and challenges within the entertainment sector. By combining data-driven narratives, strategic insights, and a deep understanding of the arts industry, we deliver measurable and impactful results.
summary: StageStrat is Northwestern's premier student-run consulting agency dedicated to the entertainment industry.

name: Starlight
description: We are Starlight, an organization serving as a student-run artist management agency. Our mission is to create an organization that serves as a platform for up-and-coming musical artists to provide funding and materials needed to help artists develop their musical talents and take their careers to the next level. The funding for this club will go toward various mediums of marketing for student artists, whether online or through real-life events, to boost the popularity, fanbase, listener base, and other aspects of student artists’ personas. This club creates many opportunities for members to expand upon their passions and careers, or to begin pursuing their dreams in ways that would otherwise be unrealistic as a student at Northwestern. The first clear opportunity is for artists who otherwise would have no means of effectively marketing their music in necessary fields to have the ability to do so (i.e., low-income students who simply cannot afford the cost of online promotion). The second is résumé building; with official positions in the club similar to an industry-standard management label, club members will gain valuable experience working at the helm of a professionally structured organization, which will result in high levels of work experience for future endeavors through Northwestern or other adjacent vocational mediums, such as internships and other career-building opportunities. Ultimately, our mission as a club is to create an organization that functions as powerful marketing, promotion, and publicity for student musicians who otherwise wouldn’t have the means to pursue their passions. As previously mentioned, this club will focus on the promotion of student artists through a combination of social media and real-life marketing campaigns. To clarify, a social media campaign is a thorough set of promotional tactics specialized for an individual artist. A basic example of a social media campaign is as follows: promoted Instagram/TikTok posts through ad traffic, promoted songs on Spotify via playlist submissions, sponsorships/brand deals with small interested brands, and networking with popular artists/influencers in any given field. While this is a basic online promotional campaign, it encapsulates the multi-dimensional nature of an effective marketing strategy in the field of social media and will prove effective in furthering the career development of musical artists beyond their current financial capacity. As stated, Starlight will also focus on real-life marketing campaigns. This includes putting up flyers around campus (as permitted) promoting artists' musical endeavors, setting up tables to draw attention to the music of the promoted artists, and working with other on-campus student organizations to set up performance opportunities for Artists. Essentially, any form of publicity that can be attained through realistic and appropriate mediums of marketing and promotion will be utilized to build the following of affiliated student artists. Ultimately, this combination of online and real-life marketing will serve to create an organic fanbase for student artists at Northwestern. Furthermore, while artists can be as involved with the marketing side as they desire, the goal of these campaigns will be to most effectively market artists and organically increase their following without taking away unreasonable amounts of time from schoolwork or other obligations that student artists may have.
summary: We are Starlight, an organization serving as a student-run artist management agency.

name: Steam Heat Dance Company
description: Steam Heat is Northwestern's premier musical theatre dance company dedicated to representing the ever changing definition of musical theatre choreography. Steam Heat was founded in 2009 by Dance major Danielle Kipnis. Today we practice and perform several dance styles such as Musical Theatre, Jazz, Jazz Funk, Tap, hip-hop, and Contemporary. We perform at various events on and off campus throughout the year and have a main-stage show each spring complete with lighting, costumes, sets, props, and a live band! We have performed both pieces from shows like A Chorus Line, Sweet Charity, HadesTown, as well as original pieces choreographed by members.
summary: Steam Heat is Northwestern's premier musical theatre dance company dedicated to representing the ever changing definition ofmusical theatre choreography and inspiring new theatre dance. We perform dance styles including jazz, tap, contemporary, and more!

name: STITCH
description: STITCH is a fashion publication at Northwestern University. Since its creation in 2006, the student-run and funded magazine has distributed four to five print issues per year to the Northwestern University student body, the Evanston community and select stores in Chicago. Founded by Joyce Lee, Matthew Alfonso, Christopher Adamson, Nate Adkisson and Kevin Burke, STITCH is now comprised of 80+ staffers from among the Northwestern University student population. Our blog, www.stitchfashion.com, is updated daily, and our latest print issue can be found at https://issuu.com/stitchfashion/docs.
summary: STITCH is a fashion publication at Northwestern University. Our blog, www.stitchfashion.com, is updated daily, and our latest print issue can be found at https://issuu.com/stitchfashion/docs.

name: Streetside Health
description: Streetside Health’s purpose is to help provide unhoused populations with equitable access to health resources in order to improve the overall well-being of those residing in the Evanston and Chicago area. We aim to build trusting relationships with the community, reduce stigmatization, and aid the delivery of health and hygiene services directly to the patients in non-traditional clinical settings. Additionally, we hope to increase undergraduate students’ consciousness of the clinical and structural barriers that unhoused populations face such that those who hope to pursue a future career in health care can better understand the needs of diverse patient populations.
summary: Streetside Health’s purpose is to help provide unhoused populations with equitable access to health resources in order to improve the overall well-being of those residing in the Evanston and Chicago area.

name: Strive
description: Strive works to create a safe space for Black men and masc-identifying students to connect, network, and engage in conversation. Aside from general meetings and social gatherings, Strive will host workshops with other campus orgs and guest speakers to discuss salient topics within our community. We hope to host fun and engaging programming to foster responsible students that hold each other accountable.
summary: Strive is an affinity space for Black men and masculine identifying students at Northwestern. Strive holds general meetings, socials, and workshops throughout the quarter to build community with Black men on campus.

name: Student Admission Council
description: The Student Admission Volunteers work with the Admissions Department to assist prospective students when they visit Northwestern. Volunteers can host prospective students overnight, assist with Alumni Interviews, and be a campus leader during Wildcat Days among many other opportunities. By volunteering for the SAV, you have a chance to help convince prospective students that Northwestern is the best place on earth!
summary: The Student Admission Volunteers work with the Admissions Department to assist prospective students when they visit Northwestern.

name: Student Affairs
description: Student Affairs affects every Northwestern student. Nearly 30 departments make up the Division of Student Affairs, from housing to food services, career services to counseling, the student center to student org advising, and so much more. Student Affairs enriches the Northwestern experience outside of the classroom. This division engages students in activities and culture in both the campus and surrounding community. Student Affairs offers support to students in need and creates an environment that makes Northwestern students Wildcats for life.
summary: Student Affairs affects every Northwestern student. Nearly 30 departments make up the Division of Student Affairs, from housing to food services, career services to counseling, the student center to student org advising, and so much more.

name: Student Affairs Information Technology
summary: Student Affairs Information Technology - IT Support and Development for the Division or Student Affairs.

name: Student Affairs Marketing
description:
Place your marketing request here.

The Student Affairs Marketing department is responsible for establishing the Division of Student Affairs brand as well as assisting each department within the division with developing and implementing integrated marketing communication plans.
summary: The Student Affairs Marketing department is responsible for establishing the Division of Student Affairs brand as well as assisting each department within the division with developing and implementing integrated marketing communication plans.

name: Student Alliance Board School of Professional Studies
summary: The SAB is the overarching student government organization within the School of Professional Studies.

name: Student Branch of the Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
description:
The primary purpose of IEEE NU is to provide the means through which all students at Northwestern University may explore the EECS Department’s disciplines. It also serves the role of conveying the opinions and insights of the Department’s student body to the Department itself.

The secondary purpose is to foster a community for EECS students to meet people of similar interests and connect with others in the school. We want to help students create connections that will last them through their time at Northwestern and into their further careers.
summary: IEEE NU exists to provide all students at Northwestern University with an avenue for participating in events and activities that promote the disciplines within the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Science.

name: Student Counselors for Social Justice
description: The mission of Student Counselors for Social Justice is to provide community, connection, training, and empowerment for online students of the Counseling@Northwestern Master of Arts in Counseling program as well as other students across Northwestern who are interested in social justice and advocacy action. SCSJ members are advocates for increasing diversity and acceptance in the world and with their clients in accordance with the ACA Code of Ethics and the mission for Counselors for Social Justice. SCSJ members work to become aware of power and privilege and how it is embedded into systems of bias. They make space to support, inspire, motivate, and encourage each other to find ways to make both small and large changes in their spheres of influence, and to become more culturally sensitive and compassionate in their work with their counseling clients.
summary: The mission of Student Counselors for Social Justice is to provide community, connection, training, and empowerment for student counselors across Northwestern who are interested in social justice and advocacy action.

name: Student of Iranian Nationality Association (SINA)
description: SINA aims to foster a strong sense of community for Iranian students at Northwestern University. Despite the significant number of Iranians on campus, there is not an organization dedicated to connecting them. Graduate students at Northwestern benefit from a similar organization, IRSA, which brings together over 80 members, providing opportunities for mutual support and cultural connection. Recognizing the importance of cultural identity during college, SINA seeks to fill this gap for undergraduate students. Through cultural events, educational resources, and opportunities to explore Iranian history, culture, and language, SINA will bring Iranian undergraduates together. By doing so, the organization will strengthen each student’s cultural identity and foster a vibrant, supportive community on campus.
summary: SINA aims to foster a strong sense of community for Iranian students at Northwestern University.

name: Student Organization Finance Office
description:
The Student Organization Finance Office (SOFO) serves as the banking center for all student groups recognized by Northwestern University. As such, it facilitates financial transactions and offers an independent and verifiable record of all the activity that occurs on these organizational accounts.

Student organizations are financially accountable to their members and the student body at large. Though they are not recognized as legal entities outside of Northwestern, they do, at times, “represent” the University unofficially by the programs and activities they sponsor and make available to the general Evanston public. This association with the University brings them many benefits (e.g., exempt status from Illinois sales tax) and responsibilities (e.g., adherence to documentation requirements).

SOFO is committed to providing student organizations with a financial framework that helps student treasurers maintain accurate and detailed records while allowing them and student presidents the flexibility to pursue their organizations’ stated purposes and objectives.
summary: The Student Organization Finance Office (SOFO) serves  as the banking center for all student groups recognized by  Northwestern  University. As such, it facilitates financial transactions  and offers an  independent and verifiable record of all the

name: Student Organizations & Activities
description:
Students have the opportunity to participate in over 500 student organizations on campus or create one of their own. Our office provides support, advising, and training for students and their organizations. Through educational and programming opportunities we facilitate and nurture experiences in:

  • Leadership
  • Campus programming
  • Student community service
  • Social justice
  • Student organization development

We encourage students to visit us to find their passion, develop ideas, and think creatively about their student organizations.
summary: Student Organizations & Activities seeks to engage students in involvement opportunities that build a sense of belonging and community at Northwestern University.

name: Student Philanthropy Council
description:
Mission

The mission of the Northwestern Student Philanthropy Council (SPC) is to educate the current undergraduate student population about philanthropy at Northwestern University and inspire a lifetime connection to the University through giving back.

About The Council

Established in 2017, the Student Philanthropy Council (SPC) provides a student voice to philanthropy programming at Northwestern. The Council is comprised of representatives from each of the current undergraduate classes. The Council meets monthly to plan, discuss, and execute Student Philanthropy education and programming opportunities.

The Council is a vital component of the Student Philanthropy program. All members have a voice in the kind of programming that the Office of Alumni Relations and Development provides current students. Members actively engage and participate in shaping the conversation around philanthropy.

Some of the programming and events the Council hosts include:

  • Fall PhilanthroFest in October
  • Pie-lanthropy in March
  • I Heart Northwestern Week in April
  • Rooted in Giving in May
  • Quarterly SPC Awards for student group programming
  • Monthly newsletter, The Scoop
summary: The Student Philanthropy Council (SPC) provides a student voice to philanthropy programming at Northwestern. The Council meets monthly to plan, discuss, and execute Student Philanthropy education and programming opportunities.

name: Student Theatre Coalition
description: The Northwestern Student Theatre Coalition, or StuCo for short, is a collective of nine student theatre groups and two dance groups, each with its own executive board and mainstage performance series. StuCo exists to provide organizational support for the common practices of the student theatre community at Northwestern, including general auditions for shows, space allocation, and acquisition and maintenance of shared equipment.
summary: The Northwestern Student Theatre Coalition, or StuCo for short, is a collective of nine student theatre groups and two dance groups, each with its own executive board and mainstage performance series.

name: Students Consulting for Non-Profit Organizations
description: Students Consulting for Nonprofit Organizations (SCNO) at Northwestern University provides clients with meaningful strategic consulting services to achieve significant results in the community, while simultaneously fostering opportunities for students to develop professional skills and gain practical consulting experience.
summary: We are a community service organization that provides free strategic consulting services to local non-profits. See more information at our website: https://nuscno.com

name: Students Demand Action
description: Students Demand Action at Northwestern is a campus chapter of a national network of students organizing to end gun violence. Through gun violence education, voter registration, rallies, conversations & partnerships, lobbying, and campaign work, we connect with community organizations and elected leaders at various levels to advocate for common sense gun legislation and to end the uniquely American gun violence epidemic.
summary: SDA at NU is the Northwestern chapter of Students Demand Action under Everytown for Gun Safety, a national nonprofit organization advocating for gun safety and prevention against gun violence.

name: Students for a National Health Program (SNaHP)
description: We aim to engage and educate Northwestern students, faculty, and staff about the benefits of a single-payer healthcare system and the damaged state of our current healthcare system. We strive to connect with local and national resources to facilitate relationships between the communities we serve and the medical profession. Our advocacy for single-payer will strive to include and uplift the voices of those who suffer from lack of or inadequate access to healthcare. Our activities include a book club, a research journal club, speaker events, a possible trip to the SNaHP summit, and socials.
summary: We aim to engage and educate Northwestern students, faculty, and staff about the benefits of a single-payer healthcare system and the damaged state of our current healthcare system.

name: Students for Ecological and Environmental Development
description:
As Northwestern's oldest environmental student organization, Students for Ecological and Environmental Development (SEED)'s mission is to not only promote awareness of environmental issues, but provide opportunities for students to engage politically and through service at local, state, and national levels. SEED seeks to promote civic and community engagement throughout the Northwestern community by providing opportunities for local service, promoting environmental political participation, and presenting diverse perspectives on environmental issues. By forging connections between our campus, community, state, and country, we take genuine steps towards improving the welfare of people and the planet together to produce a healthier, more just, and more sustainable world.

Note: SEED is a non-partisan organization, but seeks to educate students on environmental policy issues and provide opportunities to engage in environmental political participation.
summary: SEED advocates for present and future leaders to preserve our planet by providing opportunities for local service, promoting environmental political participation, and presenting diverse perspectives on environmental issues.

name: Studio 22 Productions
description:
Studio 22 is a student-run, not-for-profit, film production company.

In addition to making short films, Studio 22 serves the community by
providing workshops on filmmaking as well as by bringing in industry
professionals to help transition our filmmakers from the university
setting to the professional world.
summary: Studio 22 is a student-run production company that exists to provide extracurricular filmmaking opportunities not offered in production classes. Studio 22 continually strives to promote education, ambition, and quality in Northwestern filmmakers.

name: Supplies for Dreams
description: Supplies for Dreams is a 501(c)3 nonprofit organization that provides underprivileged Chicago Public School students with critical resources and extra-curricular opportunities ranging from basic school supplies to field trips and social/academic mentoring. The Northwestern Chapter of SFD strives to give opportunities for NU students to help SFD do this, as well as bring awareness of the issues in education that exist.
summary: Supplies for Dreams is a 501(c)3 nonprofit organization that provides underprivileged Chicago Public School students with critical resources and extra-curricular opportunities ranging from basic school supplies to field trips and one-on-one mentoring.

name: Surf Club
summary: Surf Club hopes to build a fun-loving and supportive community of student surfers! We will strive to give access to surfing equipment to as many students as possible, regardless of skill level.

name: Survivor Advocacy Through Greek Engagement
description: SAGE is a student-run organization dedicated to preventing sexual violence and promoting a greater culture of consent and allyship within Northwestern Greek life. We lead meetings on violence prevention, risk management strategies, bystander intervention scenarios designed for Greek life contexts, speaker events featuring Northwestern professors about supporting survivors and relevant research studies on topics like intra-fraternity sexual violence and party rape, fundraisers and more for the entire Greek life community.
summary: SAGE is a student-run organization dedicated to preventing sexual violence and promoting a greater culture of consent and allyship within Northwestern Greek life.

name: Survivor: Northwestern
description: Survivor: Northwestern is an organization that aims to mimic the reality television show "Survivor" here on campus. It is completely recorded, edited, and produced by students at Northwestern, and a typical season runs from January to May, with pre- and post-planning.
summary: Survivor: Northwestern is an organization that aims to mimic the reality television show "Survivor" here on campus.

name: Swing Left
description:
Swing Left is a student group organizing Northwestern to help Democrats win back the House and keep control of the Senate and White House, as well as end Republican voter suppression.

We engage in direct voter contact by writing letters and making calls to Swing Left's Super States: Arizona, Colorado, Texas, Iowa, Wisconsin, Michigan, Ohio, Pennsylvania, Maine, North Carolina, Georgia and Florida.
summary: Swing Left introduces Northwestern students to the most effective voter contact opportunities to elect Democrats up and down the ballot in key states across the country.

name: Tabletop Club
description:
• You're invited to join our Discord! https:/discord.gg/c2UsqZTnRM
  • Extenuating circumstance notwithstanding, Tabletop Club will primarily be offering in-person events. Hybrid and virtual may be made available with significant demand.
  • We hold Vibe Night events, where people can play board games or just hang out! Drop to play Coup, Werewolf, and other favorites, or to talk about TTRPGs.
  • Our longer campaigns are great for those who would like to play an ongoing story with a consistent group of people. Anyone is welcome to run or join a campaign! Campaigns usually last a quarter, but they can be shorter or longer depending on the group. They are often a weekly commitment, but scheduling is quite flexible and is determined by every member of the campaign group. If you'd like to run a campaign, don't hesitate to join our Discord and start organizing!
  • We also have intermittent One Shot games. These are casual, single session games for people with any amount of experience with TTRPGs. These games are run in a variety of systems like Dungeons & Dragons, Honey Heist, Monster of the Week, and more! One Shots are a great opportunity to dip your toes in the water and learn about TTRPGs. They're also awesome for more experienced players who are looking to try out new systems, meet people interested in the hobby at NU, or get their dose of TTRPGs without needing to commit too much time!
summary: We aim to facilitate tabletop roleplaying at NU by providing everyone the opportunity to learn, explore, and enjoy the hobby in an inclusive, friendly environment. Join our discord! https://discord.gg/c2UsqZTnRM

name: Taiwanese American Students Association
description:
Northwestern University's premiere student group for those passionate about Taiwan and Taiwanese culture!

Previously known as TASC, we have rebranded to TASA!

The Taiwanese American Students Association (TASA) was established in 1992, and the Associated Student Government recognized TASA as a student organization in January of 1994. Since then, TASA has grown from a group of eight members to an Associated Student Government A-status organization with members from all backgrounds, Asians and non-Asians alike.

TASA was founded to provide a forum in which Taiwanese history, culture and current events could be openly celebrated and shared among all Asian Americans and the NU community at large. Our mission is two-fold: to build community amongst students interested in Taiwan, and to provide the larger Northwestern community the chance to explore and learn about the many facets of Taiwanese culture.
summary: A Taiwanese-American student interest group striving to promote cultural awareness through educational events like Taiwanese Night Market, Karaoke Night, Dragon Boat Festival, Celebrasia, and more.

name: Taiwanese Students and Scholars Association
description: TSSA is dedicated to sharing Taiwanese culture with students and scholars at Northwestern, especially those affiliated with TGS. We typically host several events throughout the year, featuring authentic Taiwanese cuisine and a variety of cultural activities. Through these events, participants can enjoy delicious food while gaining a deeper understanding of Taiwanese traditions and heritage.
summary: TSSA is dedicated to sharing Taiwanese culture with students and scholars at Northwestern, especially those affiliated with TGS.

name: TAMID Group
description:
TAMID is a nonprofit organization that develops the professional skills of undergraduate students through hands-on interaction with Israeli business.

Northwestern TAMID integrates undergraduates with Israel through pro-bono consulting for startups, capital market investment research, and a summer internship program in Tel Aviv. TAMID has no political or religious affiliations, meaning students of all backgrounds are encouraged to join.

Website

2024-2025 Interest Form
summary:
TAMID Group is a premier undergraduate consulting and investment club.

www.northwesterntamid.org

name: TBD
description:
Founded in 2015 in collaboration with the Chicago Neo-Futurists, TBD is Northwestern's premiere Neo-Futurism inspired performance group. TBD is an ensemble of students who strive to create non-illusory theatre that shares our thoughts and feelings as simply as possible so that it is more accessible to all audiences. We play ourselves without the normal conventions of theatre, telling our own truths onstage. We create an environment where the audience is as important as the people on stage, and we create a space for sharing the human experience as honestly as one can whilst performing.

Our ensemble members write and direct their own short plays, which can range in length anywhere from 1 second to 5 minutes! Our plays are wacky, serious, hilarious, political, sad, and anything in between. We perform on Northwestern's campus once per quarter in a fast paced, audience interactive showcase of new plays.
summary: A Neo-futurism inspired performance group

name: Team Awareness Combating Overdose
summary: The mission of the organization is to combat drug overdose among young adults using research-based education and accessible harm-reduction supplies like fentanyl test strips.

name: TEDxNorthwesternU
description:
TEDxNorthwesternU is an independently organized TEDx conference held annually at Northwestern University. The organizing team is comprised of undergraduate students representing all of Northwestern's undergraduate schools and colleges.

Every spring we host a TED conference on campus, bringing together students and speakers to discuss and share ideas. The board is broken down into three committees: Marketing, Curation, and Logistics/Finance, each with its own role in developing and planning content for the conference. 

Check out our website here, or look up any of our old TED talks on youtube!
summary: TEDxNorthwesternU is an independently organized TEDx conference held annually at Northwestern University.  The organizing team is comprised of undergraduate students representing all of Northwestern's undergraduate schools and colleges.

name: TGS Cross-Cultural
description:
Frozen October 2024

Northwestern University is a community devoted to producing successful and competent leaders for the modern workplace. Producing such leaders requires that graduate students have skills of cultural competence to successfully navigate the growing diversity of our global workplace. Northwestern University has an office of multicultural affairs, which contains programs related to diversity and cultural competence, demonstrating Northwestern’s recognition of the importance of instilling cultural competence and intercultural dialogue. To this end, a graduate student cross-­‐cultural communication committee can strengthen and further promote skills of graduate student cultural competence through advertising and co-­‐hosting programming aimed specifically at creating an inclusive atmosphere where all graduate students can interact and learn how to be successful communicators.
summary: We are student group aimed to promote cross-cultural dialogue, awareness of situations where cultural sensitivity is important, so that students from different backgrounds can have good conversations together.

name: Thai Student Association
description:
Thai Club is an organization comprised of Thai international students, Thai Americans, and anyone interested in Thai culture. Our activities include the famous Thai Night taken place in the spring (featuring classical dance, Thai sports, entertainment, good food, and more), SEA night and various cultural and social events throughout the year. Please visit our website: http://groups.northwestern.edu/thaiclub/

 

DM on ThaiSA instagram page to learn more!
summary: Thai Club is an organization comprised of Thai international students, Thai Americans, and anyone interested in Thai culture. Our activities include the Southeast Asian Night in winter quarter and Thai Night in the spring.

name: The Army Praise Dance Ministry
description: The Army encourages students to explore their adoration for Jesus Christ through the art of praise dancing.
summary: The Army encourages students to explore their adoration for Jesus Christ through the art of praise dancing.

name: The Bike Project
description: The purpose of The Bike Project is to promote cycling and casual bike riding as an accessible, sustainable, and social activity among students.
summary: The purpose of The Bike Project is to promote cycling and casual bike riding as an accessible, sustainable, and social activity among students.

name: The Bix
description:
THE BIX is Northwestern's premier musical improv group!! With the help of our music directors, The Bix improvises scenes, songs, and more. We are a goofy bunch of improvisers who refuse to take ourselves too seriously. Throughout the year, we put on shows using short form and long form skills culminating in a fully improvised musical. 

***Not a singer? Not a problem! The Bix prides ourselves on our diversity of skills. If singing or music isn't your thing, but you love being a goofball, The Bix just might be the perfect fit!***

 

Wanna know more? https://thebiximprov.weebly.com

 

Questions? Shoot us an email at thebiximprov@gmail.com
summary:
THE BIX is a comedy improv group at Northwestern University focused on, but not limited to, musical improvisation. 
Not a singer? Not a problem!

name: The Black Creative Collective
description:
Frozen October 2024

The purpose of this organization is to provide community, foster collaboration, and ground a specific focus of work in a space for critical feedback. Specifically, this organization aims to create a community amongst black graduate students at Northwestern who are either practitioners or in a field of study pertaining to the representation of people of African descent in film, video, and other media.

Our goals are derivative from TGS’ pillars of advocacy, connection, and cultivation but focused in a way that is specifically necessary for Black students to commune and thrive in the graduate school at Northwestern. We aim to advocate for an authentic space for culturally specific engagement with students' works. We aim to cultivate and foster a community that connects students with each other through the engagement of work and culturally relative discussion.
