This organization also aims to include the consistent and earnest engagement of Black faculty within the university and externally through alumni, artists, thought leaders in the community, and professionals in various fields of creative industries. In doing so, our overarching goal is to cultivate a thriving Black artist community within the graduate program whose work is confidently grounded in the rich cultural landscape of students’ place of origin, the Chicagoland area, and the larger world as understood by people of African descent.
summary: This organization aims to create a community amongst black graduate students at Northwestern who are either practitioners or in a field of study pertaining to the representation of people of African descent in film, video, and other media.

name: The Blackout
description: The Blackout, Northwestern's Late Night Show, infuses Northwestern humor and the late-night structure to create a unique show capturing the Northwestern experience in a satirical light. The crew of 100+ students write, direct, and produce filmed and live sketches, scout out and book local talent for musical and performance guests, and throw one live-show for the student body per quarter.
summary: The Blackout is Northwestern's premiere late-night comedy show. Drawing inspiration from "The Late Show with Stephen Colbert" & "Saturday Night Live", The Blackout utilizes the structure of a late-night talk show and infuses it with Northwestern humor.

name: The Campus Kitchen at Northwestern University
description:
SUBSCRIBE TO OUR EMAIL LIST: http://eepurl.com/iaoFZL

INSTAGRAM: @nucampuskitchen

EMAIL: campuskitchen@u.northwestern.edu

WEBSITE: http://tinyurl.com/cknuhome

 

Campus Kitchens collects over 3,000 lbs of excess food from Northwestern dining halls that would otherwise be thrown away. We package the food into healthy meals and deliver these meals to low-income Evanston residents for free. We are conveniently based on campus in the old Hinman Dining Hall at 1835 Hinman Ave. Shifts happen on Tuesday and Friday afternoons, and are led by student shift leads -- no prior experience required!

 

Come check us out! You can sign up once a quarter or do multiple shifts a week, and we would love to have you. 

 

Because there are so many ways to get involved with our organization, we ask that you subscribe to our mailing list, check out instagram, or email us rather than requesting info on wildcat connections. Thank you for your interest in food justice!
summary: We fight food waste and food insecurity at the same time by recovering food from dining halls, and then distribute leftovers in healthy meals to Evanston residents in need. We are a chapter of the Food Recovery Network!

name: The Club Ski Team
summary: We are a competitive alpine ski and snowboard team in the Midwest Division of the USCSA. We run events and raise funds to build a winter sports community on Northwestern's Campus. Join us this winter and get stoked!

name: The Daily Northwestern
description: One of the nation's most respected college newspapers, The Daily Northwestern has won dozens of regional and national awards. In 2017, The Daily was named Best All-Around Daily Student Newspaper in the nation by the Society of Professional Journalists. The Daily received 18 awards at the 2016 ICPA convention. In the last decade, The Daily has won two prestigious national awards, the Gold Crown Award and the Pacemaker, several times.
summary: One of the nation's oldest and most respected college newspapers, The Daily Northwestern has won dozens of regional and national awards and has been publishing since 1871. Both print and online publications are produced entirely by students.

name: The Deep End
description:
The Deep End is a filmed show produced each quarter that exists at the intersection of comedy and journalism. Each episode takes a deep dive into a topical issue at Northwestern, which is presented in a comedic monologue delivered by a host. Comedy writers work alongside our team of journalists to craft pieces that are both funny and informative. Our Impact Committee then works to ensure that each of our episodes has a tangible impact on the Northwestern community, whether that be through fundraising or advocating for change that will help make Northwestern a better institution.

2020 Organizations Fair Zoom link here: https://northwestern.zoom.us/j/95560917619
summary: The Deep End is a filmed show produced each quarter that exists at the intersection of comedy and journalism.

name: The Dolphin Show
description: The Dolphin Show is a non-profit student theatre organization that annually produces America's largest student-produced musical. Having evolved from a swim team fundraiser in the 1940's to an annual professional-caliber musical production, The Dolphin Show celebrates the diverse talents of the Northwestern University undergraduate community by providing opportunities to learn and excel in all artistic and administrative fields of non-profit theatre. The Dolphin Show additionally involves the greater Chicago area communities through outreach programming. Successful Dolphin Show Alumni include actors David Schwimmer, Warren Beatty and former Miss America Kate Shindle.
summary: The Dolphin Show is a non-profit student theatre organization that annually produces the nation's largest student-run musical.

name: The Film Festival
description:
Frozen October 2024

The Film Festival's purpose is to unify Northwestern undergraduate filmmaking within the Northwestern community, to raise awareness about Northwestern undergraduate films among Evanston, Chicago, and beyond, and to facilitate the transfer and appreciation of cinema between Northwestern and the rest of the artistic world. The Film Festival hosts two events annually: One inward-out Showcase each spring where The Film Festival promotes a week of end-of-the-year student premieres, such as the Studio 22 Premiere, Multicultural Filmmakers Collective Premiere, the Applause for a Cause Premiere, the Media Arts Grant premiere, the Senior Directing Premiere, the RTVF Sitcom Premiere, and any other premieres that may enter the scene. This initiative involves making promotional materials that can go across campus, social media, and other channels to spread the word about ALL the premieres, instead of each one competing for eyeballs and attendance. We would also provide a red carpet with stanchions that each premiere can use/share, easels for each premiere to use/share, and someday hope to welcome speakers, food trucks, and other attractions! The actual Film Festival will occur each Fall, where through submissions from across the country and a committee of student judges, we curate a slate of selected films. Additionally, we hope to host a premiere of the most celebrated student films from the previous academic year downtown in Chicago.
summary: The purpose is to unify Northwestern undergraduate filmmaking within the Northwestern community, to raise awareness about Northwestern undergraduate films among Evanston, Chicago, and beyond, and to facilitate the transfer and appreciation of cinema.

name: The Flipside
description:
Free Everywhere, $2.30 Canada

Like The Onion ? The Daily Show? The Colbert Report? The Flipside is a satirical publication and official student group at Northwestern University. Founded in 2008, we make ~1000 people laugh, chuckle, and smile to themselves daily. We publish articles and headlines online each day, with quarterly print issues and stand-up nights. All are welcome to contribute in any capacity possible. You can write, edit or just attend meetings to throw out some topics or ideas. Come participate in one of the most fun groups on campus!

The Flipside is a member of the Flipside News Network, which includes other satirical publications such as The Stanford Flipside, The USC Flipside, The Feinberg Flipside, The Deerfield Flipside, The Mizzou Flipside, The Flipside International and The NorShore News.

All names and quotes are made up unless a public figure is being satirized. Remember, these are all jokes.

 

Contact

If you would like to join The Flipside, recommend an idea, submit an article, or have any questions, please email zoekulick2025@u.northwestern.edu.

 

Links

Visit our official site to read past articles and find more information!

Check out our Instagram here, and be sure to follow us on Twitter @realnuflipside!
summary: The Northwestern Flipside is Northwestern University’s official satirical newspaper. We publish new content online daily, as well as monthly print issues distributed across campus. Visit our website at www.northwesternflipside.net for more information!

name: The Graduate School
summary: The mission of The Graduate School of Northwestern University is: to be a trusted, responsive, and visionary leader and partner in order to maintain and promote the highest quality master’s and doctoral education.

name: The Groove Street Dance Club
description: We want to create a street dance community and culture on campus. And nothing speaks more of street dance than freestyle. Together, we practice, cypher, and battle. We also organize public battles that welcome everyone, not just the dancers, to come watch and participate in.
summary: We want to create a street dance community and culture on campus. And nothing speaks more of street dance than freestyle.

name: The Impact Movement at Northwestern University
summary: We are a Black Christian ministry designed to equip Black students to become spiritual leaders in all areas of their lives. Impact is a loving space for Black students to seek community, while exploring and/or deepening their relationship with God.

name: The Jasmine Collective
description: The purpose of this organization is to create a diverse and inclusive space for all students on campus to engage in dialogue about social justice issues impacting the South Asian community. We are dedicated to creating a coalition of students who value speaking out about casteism and Islamophobia, among other challenges, in the South Asian community, as well as building solidarity across movements and groups.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to create a diverse and inclusive space for all students on campus to engage in dialogue about social justice issues impacting the South Asian community.

name: The Leukemia and Lymphoma Society Club at Northwestern
description: The Leukemia and Lymphoma Society (LLS) is a national organization at the forefront of the fight against blood cancers, supporting patients by funding research efforts, advocating for legislation which benefits patients, and providing financial assistance to current blood cancer patients. The Leukemia and Lymphoma Society Club at Northwestern aims to support the national organization’s goals primarily through fundraising campaigns such as Visionaries of the Year. Additionally, LLS@NU will spread awareness about blood cancers through monthly panel discussions with healthcare professionals. Finally, we will involve the school and local communities through partnerships with other clubs and local Evanston organizations and schools.
summary: The Leukemia and Lymphoma Society Club at Northwestern aims to support the national organization’s goals primarily through fundraising campaigns such as Visionaries of the Year.

name: The MSGH SLC
description:
Frozen October 2024

The Master of Science in Global Health (MSGH) Student Leadership Council (SLC) aims to promote a sense of community and collaboration among students in the MSGH program, while also providing opportunities to connect with alumni and renowned professionals in the field. The SLC provides a breadth of opportunities for students to participate in industry discussions, networking events, and gain valuable insights into the latest advancements in global health, including the latest research, policies, and innovations. Engaging with the Student Leadership Council is an excellent way to enhance your educational experience and develop valuable skills and connections that will benefit you throughout your career.
summary: The Master of Science in Global Health (MSGH) Student Leadership Council (SLC) aims to promote a sense of community and collaboration among students in the MSGH program, while also providing opportunities to connect with alumni and renowned professionals

name: The Open Democracy
description:
Frozen October 2024

We are a student-led chapter of Indivisible, a grassroots movement of thousands of groups with a mission to elect progressive leaders and rebuild American democracy. The goal of The Open Democracy Project is to provide students with action-oriented opportunities to develop organizing and leadership skills, and to meet and work with other students interested in addressing social issues like climate change and social justice. The Open Democracy Project is the organization supporting the first ever Participatory Budgeting process in Evanston.
summary: We are a student-led chapter of Indivisible, a grassroots movement of thousands of groups with a mission to elect progressive leaders and rebuild American democracy.

name: The Panini Players
description:
The Panini Players are a theatre ensemble who perform in the style of Commedia dell'Arte. We hold one feature performance per quarter, which includes a make-your-own panini dinner and a one-hour show. We mix lowbrow physical comedy with highbrow wit, which produces an interesting comedic dynamic.

https://vimeo.com/256685839
summary: The Panini Players are a theatre ensemble dedicated to bringing the classical style of Commedia dell'Arte to campus. Commedia is distinctive for its use of masks,  stock characters, physical comedy and improvisation.

name: The Set Up
description: The Set Up is a stand-up comedy club welcoming all who wish to join, regardless of their comedy experience. Our meetings feature a variety of activities including workshops to hone your jokes, comedy lessons from seasoned comedians and expert instructors, and engaging exercises to spark creativity. Additionally, The Set Up hosts multiple comedy shows, providing members with valuable opportunities to perform and refine their material in front of an audience. Join us to develop your comedic skills and share your humor with others!
summary: Standup comedy club open to everyone

name: The Subcontinent Project
description:
Frozen October 2024

The Subcontinent Project aims to foster meaningful conversations among the Northwestern and Chicagoland community by organizing events pertaining to South Asian issues including, but not limited to, democracy and human rights, gender and sexuality and nationalism, and situating these topics as a part of larger global conversations that are currently taking place.
summary: The Subcontinent Project aims to foster meaningful conversations among the Northwestern and Chicagoland community by organizing events pertaining to South Asian issues.

name: The Undertones
description:
Founded in 2001, the Undertones is a co-ed a cappella group that loves to travel, compete, and be silly together. We pride ourselves on maintaining a very high level of musicianship, and most recently placed first at the 2024 ICCA (International Championship of Collegiate A Cappella) Great Lakes Quarterfinals and received the “Outstanding Soloist,” "Outstanding Choreography," and "Outstanding Arrangement" awards. Tones have studied in every one of Northwestern's schools and hail from all over, creating opportunities for the group to tour nationally (The White House, NYC, San Francisco, Fort Lauderdale) and internationally (Edinburgh, Scotland and Barcelona, Spain). Although much of our time together is spent rehearsing, performing, and recording, the Undertones are also a close group of friends who love spending time with one another and keeping the fun alive in the work we do!

Check us out on Instagram and YouTube (@nuundertones) or on Spotify (Northwestern Undertones).
summary: Award-winning, competitive co-ed a cappella group founded in 2001. We sing while keeping the love and silliness alive!

name: The Women's Network
description: The Women’s Network (TWN) was created for ambitious women looking to grow professionally while being lifted by their community. TWN offers opportunities to gain exposure to the professional world, network with high-profile speakers, and meet peers around their campus and the country. TWN meets biweekly and holds speaker meetings, various networking events, resume, internship and LinkedIn workshops, alumni networking receptions, and interactive activity sessions to develop relationships with fellow members. Our workshops and guest speakers help prepare you for your next steps and may open your eyes to career paths you hadn't considered. TWN prides itself on its non-competitive, inclusive, and supportive community of high-achieving and ambitious members. This organization offers exclusive internship opportunities, workshops and support to help you build your LinkedIn/resume. More personally, TWN is a great place to meet new people within Northwestern, around the country and make friends from various majors.
summary: The Women’s Network is a networking organization that celebrates ambition and connects women to fellow high-achieving, noncompetitive peers on their respective college campuses and to seasoned female professionals in various fields.

name: The Youth Movement Against Alzheimer's
description: YMAA is a national non-profit organization that focuses on "curing" Alzheimer's through raising awareness of neurodegenerative diseases amongst youth, reducing stigma surrounding Alzheimer's, and alleviating caretaker/family stress. We work towards this mission by participating in national events, such as the Walk Against Alzheimer's, volunteer at local assisted living facilities, and fundraise for nursing homes in the area.
summary: YMAA is a national non-profit organization that focuses on "curing" Alzheimer's through raising awareness of neurodegenerative diseases amongst youth, reducing stigma surrounding Alzheimer's, and alleviating caretaker/family stress.

name: Theme Park Engineering & Design
description:
The Theme Park Engineering & Design Group (TPED) is a design club under McCormick School of Engineering. 


TPED provides theme park enthusiasts from ALL academic majors a fun multidisciplinary space to pursue their passion for themed entertainment. TPED is a platform for members to further their professional development and delve deeper into this unique field of interest by focusing on three main areas:

1. Collaborating on themed entertainment design projects and competitions.
2. Learning from and networking with professionals in the themed entertainment industry via talks, workshops, and conferences.
3. Creating opportunities to discover and hone industry-related skills.

If you're a designer, artist, engineer, musician, programmer, or literally any person who's interested in making memorable themed experiences, join us!
summary: Theme Park Engineering & Design Group (TPED) is a multidisciplinary design club for themed entertainment enthusiasts from all backgrounds and majors! Join us to connect with other theme park fans and participate in fun projects and competitions!

name: Think Grow Connect
description: We aim to create a welcoming space where students can nurture their mental well-being through movement and meaningful connection. Every Saturday morning, we will offer a different recreational activity on a rotating calendar. Whether members are looking to try something new, make new friends, or boost their physical and mental wellbeing, they can find a space to think, grow, and connect.
summary: We aim to create a welcoming space where students can nurture their mental well-being through movement and meaningful connection.

name: THUNK A Cappella
description:
THUNK a cappella is Northwestern University's oldest co-ed a cappella group. Since their beginning on campus in 1993, THUNK performs three shows every year on campus, while also performing at numerous external gigs and workshops on campus and in the Chicago area.

Follow us on Instagram for audition updates!

⬇︎⬇︎ Check out our Videos & Social Media below! ⬇︎⬇︎
summary: THUNK is Northwestern's premier co-ed a cappella group. They are the oldest group on campus singing and wearing hot black since 1993. They love regularly performing on campus, in the Chicago area, and anywhere around the world on their annual winter trip.

name: Tikkun Olam Makers
description: Tikkun Olam Makers (TOM) is a global movement that creates and disseminates affordable solutions for unmet challenges faced by seniors and people with disabilities. TOM: Northwestern aims to help individuals in Evanston and the greater Chicago area by connecting “Need Knowers” (people with disabilities who understand their needs) with “Makers” (innovative thinkers). Through initiatives like Makeathons, Build Parties and long-term projects, students will collaborate directly with partners from local rehabilitation and senior centers to create, customize, and manufacture products locally. This organization is open to any enrolled Northwestern student.
summary: Creating affordable open-source assistive technologies and solutions to improve the lives of people with disabilities.

name: TONIK Tap
description: Founded in 2001, TONIK Tap is Northwestern University’s premier tap dance ensemble. TONIK’s members hail from all over the country and collectively study a variety of majors, but we all have one thing in common – we love to create rhythm. We perform at various Northwestern and Chicagoland events, from Rhythm Nation and Eberhard's chemistry show in the fall, to Wildcat Days and Dance Marathon in the spring. We also produce two shows a year on campus, with original choreography and video interludes. TONIK's self-choreographed repertoire has been recognized and featured as part of Dance Chicago, a city-wide dance festival in downtown Chicago. We have tapped with soccer balls, an a cappella group, eggs, on contact explosives, and have recreated classic choreography by Gene Kelly. What can we say, tap goes with everything! Email us at toniktap@gmail.com.
summary: TONIK Tap is Northwestern's premier tap dance company. We come from all over but we all have one thing in common -- we love to make rhythm! As a completely student-run group, we perform all over campus and the Chicagoland area.

name: Track Club
description:
The NU Track Club is a group of runners from Northwestern University in Evanston, IL. The goals of the group are to:

  •  Promote the sport of running and track and field
  •  Provide a supportive group training environment
  •  Have fun!

    The club is composed of all types of runners, from those running to stay in shape to those seeking competition. The track club is also a great way to meet new friends and we are always accepting new members.

We meet for practice on weekdays at 5:30pm in front of SPAC.

For contact information and more about the team please visit our website: 

http://sites.northwestern.edu/runners/ . 

 

If you would like to join our Listserv (mailing list), email our President, Calvin! If you'd like to join our Slack (primary form of messaging), join here!

 

If you have any questions or concerns, email our President, Calvin!
summary:
Go check out our main website: 
 http://sites.northwestern.edu/runners/

name: Trading Card Game Club
description:
Helping Northwestern students find groups to play trading card games, help them develop as trading card game players, and to provide an inclusive environment in which new and seasoned players alike can play trading card games together.

Please feel free to join our Discord or follow our Instagram @tcgclubnu for details!
summary: Our goal is to help Northwestern students find groups to play trading card games, help them develop as trading card game players, and to provide an inclusive environment in which new and seasoned players alike can play trading card games together.

name: Transfer Student Organization
description: Transfer Student Organization (TSO) fosters a welcoming environment for Northwestern transfer students across all years. TSO facilitates social events every quarter which serve as a platform for transfers to meet, make friends, and access mentorship. In 2018 TSO launched the Transfer Mentorship Program–an initiative which pairs incoming transfer students with transfer mentors based on common interests. Given the self-guided mentor approach, the goal is to ease the process for new transfers. In addition, we promote inclusion and secure resources by voicing community perspectives to the Associated Student Government and university offices. All transfers are welcome to join!
summary: As the only Northwestern group of its kind, TSO aims to cultivate a dynamic and supportive community of transfer students. We hope to overcome any challenges standing in the way of transfer integration into Northwestern.

name: Transportation Club
description:
Frozen October 2024

Northwestern Transportation Club is a platform for all students interested in transportation at Northwestern. We connect communities, generate interest, and create space for transportation enthusiasts and career-seeking students to collaborate and celebrate their shared passion. We organize seminars, workshops, field trips, and networking events.

Some highlights are: visiting American Airlines flight tower in 2018, visiting United Airlines headquarters in 2019, holding online social events and game nights in 2020 during the pandemic, and holding online industry event with Amazon and DHL in 2021 in collaboration with Northwestern's Informs Club.

The goal is provide an environment for members and students to stay-in-touch outside of a course environment through relaxing and fun social events, advancing the experience of students through boots-on-the-ground visits and events, and providing networking and job opportunities through industry events.

Anyone who is interested in Transportation is welcome to join the club!
summary: The Northwestern Transportation Club is a graduate student club focused on students interested in Transportation. Undergraduate students are also welcome to join to club events.

name: Treblemakers
description:
Treblemakers is Northwestern’s premiere East-Asian interest a cappella group and welcomes all identities. We sing everything from mainstream American pop, to jazzy Asian ballads, to mashups featuring the best of both worlds. 

Apart from our quarterly shows, we have year-round gigs performing at Celebrasia, Homecoming, Christkindlmarket, CISA show and many others. Celebrating cultural exchange, we’ve had the opportunity to tour Hong Kong, Seoul, Beijing, and California, as well as collaborate with other special-interest a cappella groups for shows. 

Throughout the year, everyone has the chance to pitch songs, arrangements, show themes and whatever tickles their fancy. When we’re not making music with our mouths, you can catch us on a road trip around the Midwest, going out for a boba run, or just kicking back for a game night.
summary:
Treblemakers is Northwestern’s East-Asian interest a cappella group and welcomes all identities. We sing everything from mainstream American pop, to jazzy Asian ballads, to mashups featuring the best of both worlds.
Link: https://linktr.ee/Nutreble

name: Tufaan Entertainment
description: Tufaan Entertainment is Northwestern's premier Bollywood-fusion dance competition. It identifies itself as a student-led philanthropic entertainment organisation aiming to raise awareness of South Asian culture in the US and promote education through the arts abroad. Tufaan hosts esteemed teams from across the country as they compete for a national bid at the Bollywood America competition. Tufaan Entertainment is dedicated to supporting Shanti Bhavan, a K-12 school in rural India providing a world-class liberal arts education for socioeconomically disadvantaged children.
summary: Tufan Entertainment is a student led non-profit organization that presents talented South Asian performers from across the country in an intercollegiate dance competition to raise funds for a school in India.

name: Turkish Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of our organization, Turkish Graduate Student Association (TGSA), is to bring the large population of Turkish graduate students, Turkish faculty, and general graduate community together to create a safe, inclusive, social, and connective environment. We aim to create a welcoming and supportive environment for all students, Turkish and NU graduate community, to share their ideas, build a network, and enrich the graduate community at NU through cultural, educational and professional events. Along with contributing to Turkish community at NU, our organization aims to hold outreach events in both U.S. and Turkiye to promote and encourage networking and collaborations between the strong Turkish graduate research community.
summary: The purpose of Turkish Graduate Student Association (TGSA) is to bring the large population of Turkish graduate students, Turkish faculty, and general graduate community together to create a safe, inclusive, social, and connective environment.

name: Turkish Student Association
description: The Turkish Student Association (TSA) aims to gather Turkish students under one umbrella and create a supportive environment while introducing Turkish culture to Northwestern, thus creating a more diverse environment. Some of the events that TSA organizes are (but are not limited to): watching Turkish national team games (i.e., football [soccer], basketball), dinner organizations, national holiday celebrations, and many more. All Northwestern students are welcome to take part in the events.
summary: The Turkish Student Association (TSA) aims to gather Turkish students under one umbrella and create a supportive environment while introducing Turkish culture to Northwestern, thus creating a more diverse environment.

name: Typhoon Dance Troupe
description:
Typhoon Dance Troupe is a student organized dance group that highlights popular, classical, ethnic, and contemporary dances of East and Southeast Asia. Through the art of dance, we dedicate ourselves to displaying pieces of the regions' rich, diverse cultures and to promote more interest and appreciation in the community for those cultures.

Dances are open to people from any type of background, ranging from beginner to experienced, in many styles of dance, including but not limited to: Traditional Han and ethnic Dai Dance, Hip-hop and street dance, jazz dance, and kpop dance. We have weekly workshop and rehearsals and we also invite famous dancers all over the world as our "Master Class" program. We learn and practice for several performances in Northwestern-affiliated productions and off-campus events, most notably Celebrasia, the annual Chinese New Year celebration co-sponsored by CSA and TASC, which draws over 500 people annually. We also make dance videos to record our work and memorable time together. 

Examples of our dance:

"Caiwei" (Chinese classical dance) https://youtu.be/dHs60XSBxRY

"Peacock" (Chinese Dai dance) https://youtu.be/KK3Isi67XQc

"Flying Apsaras" (Chinese style popular dance) https://youtu.be/rAWcKl5P9TM

"Layin Low" (K-pop dance) https://youtu.be/w7Ir_F7sknc

"Moment" (Chinese style jazz dance) https://youtu.be/ZmtOqXIXrzA

"Grain in Ear" (Chinese style jazz dance ) https://youtu.be/HNUdvEhGTvA
summary: Typhoon Dance Troupe is a student organized dance group that highlights popular and classical dances of East and Southeast Asia. Through work of art, we dedicate ourselves to displaying pieces of the regions' rich and diverse culture.

name: Ukrainian Club
summary: Ukrainian Club provides an inclusive environment where students can explore Ukrainian identity and culture through educational and social events.

name: Ukulele Club
description: Uke Club provides another avenue for students to get involved in music at Northwestern without commitment or prior experience. Through weekly meetings, we will use the universal language of music to continue to grow the performing arts community at NU while also educating members about the instrument's significance to Asian Pacific Islander Desi American arts and culture. No commitment is required for general membership, and there will also be optional opportunities for members to perform at NU and in the local community.
summary: The purpose of Ukulele club is to teach people how to play ukulele, and to help people who already know how to play the ukulele improve. Additionally, our goal is to foster an accepting and welcoming environment where people can jam with friends.

name: Undergraduate Chemistry Council
description:
The UCC welcomes students of all majors who are interested in the science of chemistry. We hold quarterly chemistry-related events including UCC Pizza Talks with fellow undergraduates giving talks about their research. We also provide community outreach events for grade school-aged children in the Evanston and Chicago area. In addition, each year the UCC helps put on campus-wide chemistry shows that attract hundreds of people, the most popular ones being the Halloween and Eberhard Beach shows, and each spring we bring a distinguished speaker to come to Northwestern and speak about their research.

Join our listserv here!
summary: The UCC welcomes students of all majors who are interested in chemistry! We put together chemistry-related events each quarter as well as campus-wide chemistry events such as the Halloween show and liquid nitrogen ice cream social!

name: Undergraduate Economics Society
description:
Frozen October 2024

The Undergraduate Economics Society is a group for undergraduate economics majors, and others interested in economics and business.

Our mission is to increase resources for students interested in economics and business and further their academic and career goals. We strive to provide a variety of events to enrich students’ experiences at Northwestern and to continue the pursuit for excellence in the events we host and sponsor.

In the past, UES has hosted Career Workshops on topics such as Resume, Cover Letter, Recruitment, and Industry Overview. Also, UES holds multiple Speaker Events every quarter featuring renowned economists in Northwestern and Kellogg, Internship speaker panel, and Professionals.
summary: The Undergraduate Economics Society is a group for undergraduate economics majors, and others interested in economics and business. We hold a variety of events to increase resources and to enrich students’ experiences at Northwestern.

name: Undergraduate International Student Advancement Council
description: The International Student Advancement Council (UISAC), conceived as Project Atlas, is a new student-led organization aiming to address the critical and unique needs of international students at Northwestern University. We aim to empower students by integrating resources, fostering cultural interconnection, and addressing unique academic and administrative challenges. We are committed to building a supportive and inclusive community that celebrates diversity, advocates for equitable policies, and enhances the overall experience of international students on our campus.
summary: The International Student Advancement Council (UISAC), conceived as Project Atlas, is a new student-led organization aiming to address the critical and unique needs of international students at Northwestern University.

name: Undergraduate Latin American Law Association
description: Our organization is committed to fostering a community for Latino-identifying students interested in the legal field by providing a supportive platform for personal and professional growth that emphasizes cultural engagement. We aim to equip our members with the necessary tools to succeed in law school and beyond through various events, workshops, and meetings. We recognize the underrepresentation of Latinos in the legal profession and seek to create a place for our community in this field. Our mission is to empower and guide each other to achieve success and make a meaningful impact in the legal industry. By offering a supportive network of like-minded individuals, we strive to create a community where members can flourish.
summary: Our organization is committed to fostering a community for Latino-identifying students interested in the legal field by providing a supportive platform for personal and professional growth that emphasizes cultural engagement.

name: Undergraduate Premedical Society
description: The Undergraduate Premedical Society at Northwestern University (UPS) is an organization designed to provide premedical students with resources and opportunities as they plan to apply to medical school. Please contact premednu@gmail.com if you have any questions. For more information visit the UPS Facebook Page at www.facebook.com/NorthwesternUPS.
summary: The Undergraduate Premedical Society at Northwestern University (UPS) is an organization designed to provide premedical students with resources and opportunities as they plan to apply to medical school.  Contact: premednu@gmail.com

name: Undergraduate Prison Education Partnership
description: The Undergraduate Prison Education Partnership cultivates collaboration between Evanston undergraduates and incarcerated students in the Northwestern Prison Education Program through prison education initiatives. These initiatives include events, tutoring, workshops, and re-entry support for incarcerated stuxents in the NPEP program.
summary: Our student group works closely with the Northwestern Prison Education Program (NPEP) to develop prison education initiatives at Sheridan Correctional Center and Logan Correctional Center.

name: Undergraduate Radio/Television/Film Student Association
description: URSA is the Undergraduate Radio/Television/Film Student Association. It is made up of student representatives in the Radio/TV/Film department. We act as the liaison between the student body and the faculty. Our role is to relay students' issues, concerns, and complaints about the curriculum to the faculty so that these problems can be solved. Our co-chairs attend monthly faculty meetings and meet with the department chairs regularly in order to ensure that the students are being heard.
summary: URSA is the Undergraduate Radio/Television/Film Student Association. It is made up of 10 student representatives in the Radio/TV/Film department. We act as the liaison between the student body and the faculty.

name: Undergraduate Real Estate Club
summary: The Northwestern University Real Estate Club (NUREC) seeks to prepare undergraduates with the required skills to thrive in the real estate finance industry. Club members will undergo financial modeling training, take part in networking events, and more.

name: UNICEF NU
description: UNICEF NU seeks to engage Northwestern University students in global health issues and to advocate for the rights of children and other vulnerable populations worldwide. In the past, UNICEF NU has hosted a number of events, including a speaker panel about women's health, documentary screenings about human trafficking, and a water walk to raise awareness about the need for clean water across the globe.
summary: UNICEF NU is an organization that aims to educate, advocate and fundraise on behalf of UNICEF.

name: United Students for Veterans' Health
description:
Frozen October 2024

United Students for Veterans' Health strives to connect students with veterans by volunteering at local Veterans Affairs (VA) Hospitals. Our aim is to give back to the men and women who've given so much to us. Our volunteers organize all sorts of events such as pumpkin decorating in halloween and making valentines day cards in February. If you're looking to get some volunteering experience inside a hospital, then apply for a spot now!
summary: tba

name: UNITY Charity Fashion Show
description:
About Us:

Uniting communities through fashion and philanthropy.

UNITY Charity Fashion Show is a non-profit, student-run organization working to provide an outlet for students and those in the Greater Chicago area to use their love for fashion, style, and art for good. Each year, during Northwestern's spring quarter, UNITY hosts a fashion show featuring student performers, dancers, musicians, and artists with an audience of 200+ people. Proceeds from the show benefit a charitable organization in Chicago chosen by our own members. UNITY also hosts fundraising events throughout the year such as clothing drives, pop-up thrift shops, and model Q&As. In the past, we’ve partnered with Social Works and raised $10,000 towards their efforts for Chicago Public Schools. This year, UNITY partners with Arts of Life, which is a non-profit organization for artists with intellectual and developmental disabilities.

Any inquiries about involvement, press, or sponsorships, please contact unity.northwestern@gmail.com.
summary: UNITY Charity Fashion Show is an annual event sponsored by diverse student groups from across campus. Together, students from all backgrounds come together for their love of fashion and raise money for a different charity every year.

name: University Bible Fellowship
description: UBF is a nondenominational, evangelical fellowship with one-to-one and group Bible studies, reflection sharing, Sunday worship, and conferences. One-to-one Bible studies are flexible appointments; students can determine location and time. Email for more information.
summary: UBF is a nondenominational, evangelical fellowship with one-to-one and group Bible studies, reflection sharing, Sunday worship, and conferences. One-to-one Bible studies are flexible appointments; students can determine location and time. Email for info.

name: University Blood Initiative
description: UBI’s mission is to replenish a blood donor pool that is lacking both in numbers and diversity of blood type by engaging youth and local communities to become invested blood donors. UBI seeks to build lasting and sustainable relationships within communities to allow our nation’s blood supply to thrive and save lives. We do this by educating and spreading awareness of the serious blood shortages throughout our healthcare system. UBI therefore is distinct from other organizations that offer blood drives and similar activities. While blood donations and drives are only a portion of other organizations’ activities, UBI focuses on using grassroots efforts to reduce our nation’s significant blood shortages.
summary: UBI’s mission is to replenish a blood donor pool that is lacking both in numbers and diversity of blood type by engaging youth and local communities to become invested blood donors.

name: University Christian Ministry
description:
UCM is an ecumenical and queer-inclusive campus ministry at Northwestern University. We are sponsored by the United Methodist Church and the Presbyterian Church (USA). If you are looking for the Wesley Foundation or the Westminster House at Northwestern University, you have found us! All are welcome at UCM.

On Sundays at 5:00 PM we have a casual worship service in our living room. Stay afterwards for fellowship and let us get to know you better. Occasionally we share meals and have holiday parties. We also host guest speakers throughout the quarter who address a variety of current social justice issues. Annual retreats give us a chance to get away from campus, bond with each other, and find spiritual renewal.  

Discover how our campus ministry expresses God's love in action, compassion and justice on the campus of Northwestern University.
summary: Authentic community, queer inclusive, Jesus lovers, justice seekers.

name: Unscripted Love
description: Dating shows as well as the following lifestyle reality TV shows are an extremely crucial part of the entertainment industry. Shows like The Bachelor, Love Is Blind, LoveIsland all are immense providers of career opportunities to those interested in the unscripted side of the entertainment industry. Through our student organization, Unscripted Love, we hope to introduce the filmmaking community to this niche yet popular side of writing, developing, and producing a Dating Game Show style Reality TV Show, and by introducing this platform we hope to provide an opportunity for students at Northwestern to get the experience of participating in a student produced reality TV show and hopefully find love.
summary: Through our student organization, Unscripted Love, we hope to introduce the filmmaking community to this niche yet popular side of writing, developing, and producing a Dating Game Show style Reality TV Show.

name: Vantage Venture Group
description: Vantage Venture Group (VVG) is a student-run organization at Northwestern University that connects driven undergraduate students, promising startups, and venture capital firms. We are dedicated to preparing students for success in venture capital and entrepreneurship through hands-on opportunities such as deal sourcing, startup consulting, and diligence projects. Additionally, we work with early-stage ventures to support their growth strategies and product development efforts. Our mission is to build a vibrant community that bridges the gap between aspiring investors and innovators, fostering connections that drive the future of technology and business.
summary: Vantage Venture Group (VVG) is a student-run organization at Northwestern University that connects driven undergraduate students, promising startups, and venture capital firms.

name: Vertigo Productions
description: Vertigo is an executive student theatre board dedicated to producing new work by fellow undergraduate students.
summary: Vertigo is an executive student theatre board that produces 6+ shows a year that are written by fellow undergraduate students. Want to learn more? Check out our website at http://www.vertigoplays.org/ or reach out to rileyharrison2025@u.northwestern.edu

name: Vibrant Colors Collective
description: At VC², we color OUTSIDE the lines: VC² is a Northwestern theater organization that strives to approach and produce theatre through a multicultural lens. We are devoted entirely to producing theater for the communities that have been historically silenced- not just at Northwestern, but in the theater community as a whole. Our objective is to spark a conversation about the space and lack thereof marginalized communities have had in the theater community and PWI spaces. We aim to provide them the space to experience joy and positivity while celebrating their CULTURE and VIBRANCY on the stage.
summary: Northwestern's only multicultural student theatre board.

name: Video Game Music
description: We are a music-based organization centered around student-arranged video game music across different instruments! We support different instrumental groups, including a concert-style band, jazz band, and woodwind and brass choirs to practice and perform in an annual concert in spring quarter!
summary: We are a music-based organization centered around student-arranged video game music across different instruments! We support different instrumental groups to practice and perform in an annual concert in spring quarter.

name: Vietnamese Student Association
description: VSA is for everyone who has an interest in the Vietnamese culture. VSA exists in order to promote awareness of and appreciation for Vietnamese and Vietnamese-American culture and history within the student body as well as to form a mutual relationship between Northwestern University and the local Vietnamese community. We host a variety of social and cultural events, such as Pho Outings, Tet Night, and SEA Night, and attend different conferences with other VSAs in the area. We also do a lot of collaborated events with other VSAs, primarily at UIC, Loyola, and UIUC. You do not have to be Vietnamese to join!
summary: VSA exists in order to promote awareness of and appreciation for Vietnamese and Vietnamese-American culture and history.  You do not have to be Vietnamese to join! We are actively seeking new members, and currently offer junior exec positions!

name: Water Polo
description:
Practice schedule:

M-TH 8:30-10:00 at SPAC during the fall, Monday and Wednesday 8:30-10:00 at SPAC during offseason.


Competition schedule:

Generally three tournaments in the fall finishing with the Big Ten Championships / Then approximately one or two tournaments in winter and spring quarters / One home tournament a year (generally held in the winter) / Womens tournaments in spring


League affiliation:

Big Ten Water Polo


Club dues:

Club dues vary per year but are generally around $150 which includes travel, lodging, and entry fee to tournaments.


Tryout schedule:

We are always willing to accept new players. Please contact the president for further information.
summary:
Practice:
M-TH 8:30-10:00 at SPAC
Three tourneys in the fall finishing with the Big Tens Champ (Men's League)
Home tourney winter quarter
Three tourneys in the spring finishing with the Big Ten Champ (Women's League, tentative)

name: WAVE Productions
description:
WAVE Productions (WAVE) is a self-supporting, not-for-profit student theatre company operating primarily on Northwestern University’s Evanston Campus. WAVE Productions exists to open the door to a better future. We strive to produce stories the campus needs by empowering our peer collaborators in pursuit of their passions and growth.

To fulfill its responsibility to the Northwestern University campus and surrounding communities, WAVE sets for itself the following objectives:

-    To sustain and nourish a dynamic and progressive presence on campus in large part by fostering a diverse theatrical season.

-    To provide an environment in which students can educate and empower themselves and others through the art and business of theatre.

-    To create a supportive community and provide a safe environment in which creativity can flourish.

-    To encourage ongoing exploration, discovery, and reflection through theatre.

-    To engage and provoke response within our communities by striving to produce stories the campus needs to hear.

-    To inspire new theatre artists and audiences.
summary: WAVE Productions exists to open the door to a better future. We strive to produce stories the campus needs by empowering our peer collaborators in pursuit of their passions and growth.

name: We're In Physics
description: We're In Physics (WIP) is a community of gender minorities in physics and other STEM fields. We aim to aid in the retention and enrich the experience of woman-identifying and nonbinary undergraduate physics students by providing professional development support, mentorship and community building. We host and attend many events throughout the year, including the annual PUG (Professor, Undergraduate, and Graduate) Mixer, the national Conference for Undergraduate Women in Physics (CUWiP), and a mentorship program in collaboration with the Society of Physics Students (SPS). If you're interested in joining, please email our President at sherrywang2024@u.northwestern.edu or join our GroupMe using this link: https://web.groupme.com/join_group/71690695/pe9FZaLG
summary: Community for woman-identifying and nonbinary undergraduates in physics

name: Werewolves Club
description:
Frozen October 2024

Werewolf is basically poker, plus rationalization, minus cards and betting. During the game, each player needs to logically come up with convincing speeches and reasonable deductions within a very short period of time.


Many have found that they have become better at critical thinking and making impromptu speeches after participating. More importantly, they develop strong bonds among themselves while having great fun. This game is extremely popular among both undergraduate and graduate students (our first few trial events have attracted around 50 students each time). However, many students tend to stick to their own local groups and do not have the chance of meeting new players due to the lack of interaction between graduates and undergraduates. Also, it is very hard for them to find enough people each time. Our aim is to provide such
an opportunity to bring these excellent players together and help them meet new people, so they can share new strategies and improve their conversation and networking skills. Players of any experience level are welcome, and our members are ready and able to teach the game to any new players. We intend to host a weekly event for both undergraduates and graduates and participate in national tournaments (which are hosted sporadically). At this stage we mainly focus on Werewolves using Chinese, but ultimately we would like to include players from all of the world and introduce more strategic board games to our members.
summary: Our organization mainly hosts ‘The Werewolves of Millers Hollow’ (‘Werewolves’ in shorthand), which is a strategic board game involving 8 and 18 players debating.

name: Wild Roots
description:
Wild Roots Garden was founded at Northwestern University in the fall of 2009 through a collaboration between the One Book One Northwestern project, three undergraduate environmental student organizations (SEED, ECO, and ESW), Facilities Management, the graduate student garden, and Norris University Center. Today, Wild Roots is managed and rub by undergraduate students. During the fall and spring quarters we are out working in the garden every Sunday, rain or shine! The fresh produce we harvest is donated to community groups in Evanston. Throughout the school year we also host fun and educational events on campus and have a variety of field trips related to sustainability, the environment, and farming. We are located on the south lawn of Norris University Center (outside of ShakeSmart). Feel free to come by the garden and visit us!

Email us to join our listserv and group me!
summary: Wild Roots is a student run garden on campus that grows fresh produce for the Evanston community, hosts fun and educational events on campus, and offers a variety of field trips to the Chicago area.

name: Wildcat Connection Pages
description: Wildcat Connection is managed by representatives in Student Organizations & Activities as well as Student Affairs IT.
summary: Wildcat Connection is the directory for student organizations, campus departments, fraternity/sorority chapters, and more.

name: Wildcat Welcome
description:
Be a Part of Wildcat Welcome!

Freshmen, Sophomores and Juniors Can Apply to Be on the Wildcat Welcome Staff

The Office of New Student and Family Programs relies heavily on student volunteers to plan and execute Wildcat Welcome and Parents' Weekend. Freshmen, sophomores and juniors can apply for two positions within the Wildcat Welcome staff structure starting late Fall Quarter and ending mid-Winter Quarter. The positions include the Board of Directors, and Peer Advisers.

Requirements and Characteristics of Staff Members

Applicants should display exceptional communication, organizational and interpersonal skills,  a positive attitude and passion a for WW, Northwestern University and new students. If you want to be a staff member, we expect you to...

- Devote the majority of your time to Wildcat Welcome activities during the week of Wildcat Welcome

- Be motivating, spirited, and enthusiastic

- Have a positive attitude

- Demonstrate excellent communication skills

- Demonstrate exceptional interpersonal relationship skills

- Be organized, flexible, mature, and professional

- Be passionate about Wildcat Welcome, Northwestern University, and new students’ need

- Serve as a role model and mentor

- Be open-minded toward diverse cultures, ethnicities, lifestyles, and backgrounds

Duties of Wildcat Welcome Staff Members

- Attend all  trainings and meetings during Spring Quarter--an attendance policy will be in effect

- Maintain close communication with your Board liaison or freshmen over the summer for Wildcat Welcome updates, answers to questions, and concerns

- Attend One-Day Intensive Training, one day prior to the beginning of Wildcat Welcome

- Assist with new student move-in at assigned residence hall area. This includes check-in, moving boxes, conversing with parents and family members, and creating a welcoming environment

- Attendance at required assigned Wildcat Welcome events and activities

- Wildcat Welcome responsibilities associated with your particular school

- Assist with implementing the Essential NUs

- Attendance at all sessions, social activities, President’s Convocation, and various other Wildcat Welcome events

- Lead thoughtful and effective discussions after Essential NU sessions. Topics include diversity, safety, alcohol, etc.

- Answer all new students’ questions with honest and appropriate answers

- Coordinate solutions to problems, issues, or emergencies encountered during Wildcat Welcome

- Be prepared to devote the majority, if not all of your time during Wildcat Welcome to Wildcat Welcome events

- Assist with assigned Academic School’s Dean’s Convocation
summary:
Be a Part of Wildcat Welcome!
Freshmen, Sophomores and Juniors Can Apply to Be on the Wildcat Welcome Staff!
The Office of New Student and Family Programs relies heavily on student volunteers to plan and execute Wildcat Welcome and more!

name: Wildcats for Israel
description: Wildcats for Israel invites the Northwestern community into a deeper conversation about Israel through dynamic educational and cultural events. Wildcats for Israel finds speakers and organizations that promote a more well-rounded understanding of Israel's past, present, and future. It is a non-partisan organization welcoming participants of various ethnicities, religions, and backgrounds.
summary: Wildcats for Israel is Northwestern's pro-Israel group on campus that strives to advocate, educate, and discuss all topics concerning Israel including culture, politics, and history.

name: WildHacks
description: WildHacks is Northwestern’s overnight computer science Hackathon taking place in-person annually in the spring. As Northwestern’s largest hackathon, WildHacks is an opportunity for students to learn programming skills and develop a final project in a collaborative and inclusive environment. Through workshops, mentorship, and prizes, WildHacks aims to be an inclusive event that welcomes students of all skill levels, majors, and backgrounds.
summary: WildHacks is Northwestern’s largest overnight computer science hackathon, held annually during the spring quarter.

name: WNUR News
description: WNUR News is Northwestern's only dedicated audio news publication, creating high-quality programming by students, for students. We work to offer both online content through streaming platforms like Spotify and Apple Podcasts, as well as regular radio broadcasts on the WNUR 89.3 FM frequency. We are open to all students, from all schools and levels of experience. If you have a passion for audio and a willingness to learn, we'll help you learn and get your stories on the air!
summary: Northwestern's only dedicated audio news publication, combining online streaming with radio broadcast.

name: WNUR Student Radio
description:
WNUR is Northwestern's student radio, but it's much more than just college radio. WNUR is a non-commercial, listener-supported radio station broadcasting at a frequency of 89.3 MHz FM and a power of 7200 watts. The WNUR studios are located in Louis Hall on the Northwestern University campus in Evanston, IL, and the station produces a signal that can be heard by 3 million potential listeners throughout the Chicagoland area. WNUR also streams on the Web to listeners around the world.

Through its programming, WNUR strives to provide a forum for underrepresented music and ideas. By pursuing the cultural, intellectual, and artistic aspects of radio, WNUR promotes musicians, musical genres, news, public affairs issues, and athletic events often overlooked by major media outlets. Because it is completely independent of commercial pressures, WNUR has the opportunity to provide unique and challenging programming to the Northwestern, Evanston, and Chicago communities no matter how unpopular or controversial.

Although WNUR is student-run, its roster of DJs comprises both students and members of the WNUR community. These DJs are able to share their experience, knowledge and passion with the students, creating an incredible learning community. Programming ranges from jazz to all kinds of experimental rock to house music and everything in between. We host top notch talk radio, news reporting and sports broadcasts for talkers; techies join Airplay, our long standing in-studio performance segment which has previously hosted bands like OK GO, Jeff Tweedy of Wilco and the Smashing Pumpkins. With over 60 years of tradition, some of our famous alumni include Win Butler, Stephen Colbert, Steve Albini, and Derrick Carter. Students are always welcome to visit the studio and we accept apprentices year-round (although our fall kick off event is the best time to get plugged in!). To get a taste you can always tune in right now, at www.wnur.org.
summary: WNUR is Northwestern's student radio- but it's much more than just college radio. WNUR is a non-commercial, listener-supported radio station broadcasting at a frequency of 89.3 MHz FM and a power of 7200 watts. WNUR has broadcasted since 1950.

name: Women in Business
description:
Women In Business (WiB) is an open-entry organization established to provide business-minded female or non-binary identifying individuals with the background and network to support them in their professional endeavors. WiB's goal is to engage women across a wide variety of academic and professional affiliations on campus in order to catalyze the involvement of women in the Northwestern community, in the business world, and beyond.

For 2024 Spring quarter, please apply here: Application Form
summary: Women in Business (WiB) is an open-entry organization established to provide business-minded female or non-binary identifying individuals with the background and network to support them in their professional endeavors.

name: Women in Computing
description: Founded in 2012, Women in Computing (WiC) initially sought to disrupt Northwestern University’s male-dominant computer science major by creating a space for those who identify as women. Now, WiC has become a full-fledged community for women who share an interest in technology and code, and the primary advocate for women in the computer science major. WiC hosts weekly programming tutorials, organizes lunch-and-learn tech talks with corporate and academic professionals, and volunteers to teach middle school girls the principles of computer science. One of the hallmarks of WiC membership is attending the annual Grace Hopper Celebration for Women in Computing; WiC aims to send all interested members to this conference in order to promote professional development and WiC friendships. WiC is currently headed by internal president Laura Félix (laurafelix2026@u.northwestern.edu) and external president Irena Liu (irenaliu2026@u.northwestern.edu).
summary: WiC fosters a community for and promotes the professional development of women who share an interest in technology. WiC offers weekly hack nights, tech talks with corporate & academic professionals, volunteering opportunities, and a mentorship program.

name: Women in Music
description: As a chapter of Women in Music, our purpose is to empower women in a male-dominated industry. According to the official website, Women in Music is “an organization with a mission to advance the awareness, equity, diversity, heritage, opportunities, and cultural aspects of women in the musical arts through education, support, empowerment, and recognition.” At Northwestern, we aim to offer that space and the resources for music-involved women to connect, share their art, develop their craft, and navigate careers in music. Through this chapter, students can keep pursuing their passions and learn to integrate music into a multifaceted career. We provide direction and support, no matter what part of music and the industry they hope to be involved in.
summary: As a chapter of Women in Music, our purpose is to empower women in a male-dominated industry. We offer the space and the resources for music-involved women to connect, share their art, develop their craft, and navigate careers in music.

name: Women in Philosophy Workshop
description:
Frozen October 2024

The Women in Philosophy Workshop seeks to increase the visibility and relevance of women in philosophy by showcasing their work in a supportive and inclusive environment.  We welcome any and all professional women philosophers (graduate and faculty) in the Chicagoland area to attend.
summary: The Women in Philosophy Workshop seeks to increase the visibility and relevance of women in philosophy by showcasing their work in a supportive and inclusive environment.

name: Women in Science and Engineering Research
description:
Frozen October 2024

Are you interested in issues surrounding women in STEM? Are you a graduate woman in the sciences at Northwestern? Join our listserv (wiser@listserv.it.northwestern.edu) or like us on Facebook to hear about interesting articles, helpful events for skills & networking, and quarterly discussion socials and faculty seminars.
summary: Women in Science and Engineering Research (WISER) works towards building community among graduate women in STEM, advancing women's leadership in STEM, and developing professional skills through workshops & seminars for early-career women in the sciences.

name: Women's Club Lacrosse
description:
NU women's club Lacrosse is a fun and competitive atmosphere. Most of us played in high school and couldn't bear to give it up in college. Others are looking for a fun way to meet some awesome people and play a great sport.

We practice as much as possible, understanding that the quarter system and our academic schedules drive a busy NU career. This works out to practice twice a week in fall/spring and once a week in the winter. 

We play from 3-4 tournaments both at Northwestern and at other schools in the spring with other teams from the area and from schools in our league. 

Tryouts are informal and as inclusive as possible. This team formed because we love the game and simply love to play.
summary:
NU women's club Lacrosse is a fun and competitive atmosphere. Most of us played in high school and couldn't bear to give it up in college.
 
We practice about twice a week and play in 3-4 tournaments throughout the year.

name: Women's Club Soccer
description:
The Women’s Club soccer team is part of the Women’s Midwest Alliance Soccer Club (W-MASC) League. We play competitively against teams in the Great Lakes region. Our fall season begins in September and runs to the mid October. Our spring season begins around April and consists of a few scrimmages against local schools.

Games

In the Fall, we typically play 10 games against teams both in Chicago and neighboring states, and our competition includes teams like UW-Madison, UW-Milkwaukee, Michigan Tech, and other Wisconsin/Michigan teams. About half of our games are away and require traveling. Home games are at Lakeside Field on campus. In the Spring, we play fewer games, typically against teams in the Chicagoland area. 

Practices

In the fall, we will practice at least twice a week, focusing on skill work, play and fitness. We stress the importance of coming to practice, so please try to arrange your class schedule around practice times. 

Dues

All members are required to pay club dues, which will be around $250 for the year. This is necessary to cover facility costs, referees, travel costs, equipment, and social events. Members are encouraged to apply for the Student Activities Scholarship Fund to alleviate the financial burden of dues.

Tryouts

As a competitive team, we consist of around 25-30 total players who are available for practices and games throughout the school year. The number of players we take each year varies. Although most girls have played competitively in high school, we encourage anyone who wants to play to tryout.

Tryouts:

Fall tryouts are typically during the week of Wildcat Welcome on either Lakeside or Long Field -- CHECK OUT OUR INSTAGRAM FOR MORE INFO ABOUT TRYOUTS!

Please bring cleats, shin guards, a ball if you have one, one dark and one light shirt, and water. And let us know if you cannot make it! 

Aside from playing soccer, the team arranges outings and gatherings in order to build a strong community for the players. If you have any questions, please contact us at nuclubsoccer@gmail.com.

Be sure to check out our Instagram: @nuclubsoccer (Tryout info here!)
summary: The Women's Club soccer team is part of the Women's Midwest Alliance Soccer Club (W-MASC) League. We play competitively against teams in the Great Lakes region. Our competition season begins in September and ends at the end of October.

name: Women's Club Volleyball
description: The Women's Club Volleyball team is a competitive team opportunity for anyone who loves volleyball. Our team has anywhere from 15-20 members depending on positions and lineups. We take a full team to every tournament. Typically we travel to 3-4 tournaments per quarter. Based on team interest, scheduling and finances, our goal is to finish each season with a trip to the NCVF National Tournament in early April. This year it will be in Kansas City, MO! We practice three times a week on campus, in addition to participating in beach games and other opportunities when they arise. We pride ourselves on being a competitive squad, so we expect our level of play to reflect that every time we pick up a volleyball – in practices, tournaments, on the beach, and elsewhere. Being a part of the Women’s Club Volleyball team is a commitment – we expect every member to attend each practice, tournament and meeting, unless academic or emergency circumstances apply. We hold our team to a high standard of play, as well as a high standard of conduct. Work hard, play hard, and have fun!
summary: Join the Women's Club Volleyball to experience the thrill of the game, forge lasting friendships, and become part of a community that celebrates both individual growth and collective success.

name: Women's Rugby Football Club
description:
Northwestern Women's Rugby team welcomes players with all levels of rugby experience, athleticism and body types. We would love for you to join and give it a try!                                                                                                        

We are a member of the Great Waters and compete against other local women's collegiate teams. Our main seasons are in the fall and spring, where we compete against teams such as Loyola, DePaul, UIC, etc. During the fall, we compete in 15s matches and in the spring, we compete in 7s matches and also host tournaments. We incorporate a lot of touch rugby games into our practices.

You are also more than welcome to join us if you do not wish to play competitively.

We will put in our Fall practice schedule when available!

If interested please reach out to Katelyn Cai at katelyncai2027@u.northwestern.edu or at the official NU Women's Rugby email address - womensrugby.nu@gmail.com
summary: Northwestern Women's Rugby team welcomes members with all levels of experience, athleticism and body types. We would love for you to join and give it a try! The team competes in 15s matches during the Fall and 7s matches in the Spring.

name: Women's Ultimate Frisbee
description: Claw is Northwestern's women's Ultimate frisbee team open to women, non-binary, and genderqueer players. We welcome all skill levels! We have a competitive A team and a developmental B team. We have a large community of players on campus and would love for you to join!
summary: Claw is Northwestern's women's Ultimate frisbee team open to women and non-binary players. We welcome all skill levels! We have a competitive A team and a developmental B team.

name: Womxn In Economics
description:
Womxn in Economics (WiE) is a group of undergraduate students interested in and/or pursuing a degree in economics. 

WiE seeks to encourage female- and non-binary-identifying members of the community to pursue their interests in economics through a variety of events and programming that not only connect womxn with similar passions but also offer valuable mentorship opportunities. We aim to offer insight into the many unique ways economics can be applied in various fields and to provide an empowering environment for womxn at Northwestern within an often male-dominated field. Our hope is that all womxn will have the resources and support they need to see that economics is for everyone!
summary: Womxn in Economics (WiE) is a group of undergraduate students which seeks to encourage female-identifying members of the NU community to pursue their interests in economics, and to support them as they do so.

name: Womxn in Law
description: Womxn in Law was founded for female-identifying and non-binary students on campus who are interested in pursuing careers in legal fields. Its main purposes are to create a support system for students interested in law, provide them with the tools necessary to thrive in the field, and to address some of the most prominent issues women and gender minorities in the field often face. Womxn in Law aims to create a network of ambitious students who are passionate about their career aspirations. To join, email womxninlaw@u.northwestern.edu.
summary: Womxn in Law is an organization dedicated to empowering women and non-binary individuals on campus to pursue their career goals in the legal field.

name: World Affairs Group
description: World Affairs Group unites Northwestern students from all academic disciplines to create student-led content on international affairs for the public. The organization produces an annual journal, recurring podcast episodes, and other forms of content while prioritizing strong research, writing, and technical and creative production. Each year's work will culminate in an annual symposium, showcasing and celebrating the student content and bringing on speakers in the field. Finally, World Affairs Group hosts social events for its members throughout the year to foster tighter bonds among peers and lifetime networks. Founded in April 2024.
summary: World Affairs Group unites Northwestern students from all academic disciplines to create student-led content on international affairs for the public.

name: X-Factors
description:
The X-Factors were formed in 2001 with the goal of infusing amazing music with the passion and individuality of a diverse group of singers. Currently, we have 13 members who major in anything from theatre to neuroscience, and who each get a solo every year. We perform anything from Adele to Daniel Caesar, all while keeping the same spirit and energy that are so unique to this group. Though more than 20 years have passed since the formation of X-Factors, we still hold close to heart the mission of creating an artistic, eclectic group that shares amazing music in our performances in and outside of Northwestern.

We perform across campus every quarter, as well as take our fall set on tour in December of each year! Recently, we’ve visited Miami, Florida and San Francisco, California to sing in high schools and nursing homes, as well as run workshops for middle and high school students. 

We currently have three albums on all streaming platforms, including our most recent: Singing with Instruments! Find us @X-Factors to hear some of our songs.

UPCOMING EVENTS

Fall Auditions! TBA through NACCA after Wildcat Welcome :)

For more about the #FactorFam, check out:
-Website: xfactorsnu.wix.com/nuxfactors

-YouTube: www.youtube.com/xfactorsacappella

-Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/xfactorsnu

-Facebook: www.facebook.com/XFactorsNU
summary: The X-Factors are a co-ed group of singers who love to have fun, wear pink, and sing a cappella music. Every year, each member gets a solo in order to showcase their unique musical style. We are so excited to meet everyone at auditions in the fall!!

name: Young Americans for Freedom
description: We host high-profile conservative speakers and engage in on campus activism to promote ideas such as individual liberty, economic freedom, and strong national defense. We encourage students of all ideological backgrounds to attend events and meetings in order to facilitate productive political discourse.
summary: Nonpartisan conservative political activism.

name: Young Life
description: Young Life is a non-denominational Christian outreach to high school and middle school students. Young Life leadership is an opportunity for Northwestern students to serve as volunteer leaders with kids in the local area. NU students gather with students from Loyola University and adults in the community regularly for training, encouragement, and planning -- and all interested students are welcome to come see what we do! Please contact us and we'll shoot you an email with the times of our next leadership meeting!
summary: Young Life is a non-denominational Christian outreach to high school and middle school students. Young Life leadership is an opportunity for Northwestern students to serve as volunteer leaders and grow in community together.

name: Zen Society
description:
Hello!

 

Zen Club is a group of students who meditate and seek to build a great community of people where you can come to de-stress and feel welcomed! We would like to emphasize that anyone is welcome regardless of experience, and we have many first-timers. Each meeting consists of meditation, followed by tea, discussion (not only about meditation but really anything!), and the occasional guest speaker. Further, meetings usually occur on Thursdays in the Multi-Belief Space, Parkes Hall Room 204 at 7:30 PM.

 

Visit our friendly and open circle! :)

 

||
summary: We strive to offer you a community in which to meditate, talk about life, make friends, de-stress, feel welcomed, and be present!

Gamma Chapter, Northwestern University
